\title{PyCypher}

\pagestyle{normal}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{index::doc}}


\sphinxAtStartPar
PyCypher is a Cypher query engine that executes openCypher queries against
ordinary pandas DataFrames.  It is built on a Lark\sphinxhyphen{}based parser, a
Pydantic AST, and a BindingFrame execution layer — no graph database required.

\sphinxstepscope


\chapter{Zero to Hello World}
\label{\detokenize{hello_world:zero-to-hello-world}}\label{\detokenize{hello_world::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Get a working Cypher query in under 5 minutes.  No external files, no
databases — just Python and PyCypher.

\begin{sphinxcontents}
\sphinxstylecontentstitle{On this page}

\section{Prerequisites}
\label{\detokenize{hello_world:prerequisites}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Python 3.14+

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
PyCypher installed (see {\hyperref[\detokenize{getting_started::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Getting Started}}}} for installation)

\end{itemize}


\section{Level 1: Your First Query}
\label{\detokenize{hello_world:level-1-your-first-query}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Three lines of setup, one query, instant results:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{import}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pandas}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{as}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pd}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{Star}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Create in\PYGZhy{}memory data}
\PYG{n}{people} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}ID\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{3}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Alice}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Bob}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Carol}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{age}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{30}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{25}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{35}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Build context and execute}
\PYG{n}{context} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{from\PYGZus{}dict}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n}{people}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{star} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) RETURN p.name AS name, p.age AS age}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{result}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Output:}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
 name  age
Alice   30
  Bob   25
Carol   35
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{What just happened:}
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ContextBuilder.from\_dict()}} wrapped your DataFrame as a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Person}}
entity table.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Star}} is the query engine — call \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{execute\_query()}} with any Cypher
string.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The result is a standard \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pandas.DataFrame}}.

\end{enumerate}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_ID\_\_}} column is required — it uniquely identifies each row (like a
primary key).


\section{Level 2: Filtering with WHERE}
\label{\detokenize{hello_world:level-2-filtering-with-where}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Add a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WHERE}} clause to filter results:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) WHERE p.age \PYGZgt{}= 30 RETURN p.name AS name, p.age AS age}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Output:}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
 name  age
Alice   30
Carol   35
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Any comparison operator works: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{=}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{<>}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{<}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{>}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{<=}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{>=}}.
Combine with \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{AND}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{OR}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{NOT}}.


\section{Level 3: Multiple Entity Types}
\label{\detokenize{hello_world:level-3-multiple-entity-types}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Add more DataFrames to query across different entity types:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{products} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}ID\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{10}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{20}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{30}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{title}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Widget}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Gadget}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Gizmo}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{price}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mf}{9.99}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mf}{49.99}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mf}{24.99}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{n}{context} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{from\PYGZus{}dict}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n}{people}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Product}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n}{products}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{star} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Product) WHERE p.price \PYGZlt{} 30 }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN p.title AS product, p.price AS price ORDER BY p.price ASC}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Output:}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
product  price
 Widget   9.99
  Gizmo  24.99
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\section{Level 4: Relationships}
\label{\detokenize{hello_world:level-4-relationships}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Connect entities with relationships.  A relationship DataFrame needs three
special columns: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_ID\_\_}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_SOURCE\_\_}} (from\sphinxhyphen{}entity ID), and
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_TARGET\_\_}} (to\sphinxhyphen{}entity ID):

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{purchases} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}ID\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{100}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{101}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{102}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}SOURCE\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}     \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Person IDs}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}TARGET\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{10}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{20}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{30}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}   \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Product IDs}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{date}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{2024\PYGZhy{}01}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{2024\PYGZhy{}02}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{2024\PYGZhy{}03}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{n}{context} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}entity}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{people}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}entity}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Product}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{products}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}relationship}\PYG{p}{(}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{BOUGHT}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{purchases}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{n}{source\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}SOURCE\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{target\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}TARGET\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{build}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{star} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (person:Person)\PYGZhy{}[:BOUGHT]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(item:Product) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN person.name AS buyer, item.title AS product }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ORDER BY buyer, product}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Output:}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
buyer product
Alice   Gizmo
Alice  Widget
  Bob  Gadget
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\sphinxhyphen{}{[}:BOUGHT{]}\sphinxhyphen{}>}} arrow in the MATCH pattern follows the relationship
from source to target.


\section{Level 5: Aggregation}
\label{\detokenize{hello_world:level-5-aggregation}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{count()}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{collect()}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{sum()}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{avg()}}, and other aggregation
functions:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (person:Person)\PYGZhy{}[:BOUGHT]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(item:Product) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN person.name AS buyer, count(item) AS items\PYGZus{}bought, }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{       collect(item.title) AS products }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ORDER BY items\PYGZus{}bought DESC}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Output:}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
buyer  items\PYGZus{}bought        products
Alice             2  [Widget, Gizmo]
  Bob             1        [Gadget]
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\section{Runnable Example}
\label{\detokenize{hello_world:runnable-example}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
All the code above is available as a single runnable file:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
uv\PYG{+w}{ }run\PYG{+w}{ }python\PYG{+w}{ }examples/hello\PYGZus{}world.py
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxstepscope


\chapter{Getting Started}
\label{\detokenize{getting_started:getting-started}}\label{\detokenize{getting_started::doc}}

\section{Installation}
\label{\detokenize{getting_started:installation}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
PyCypher requires \sphinxstylestrong{Python 3.14+} and uses \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{uv}} for dependency management.
It is not yet published on PyPI — install from source:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Install uv (if not already installed)}
curl\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}LsSf\PYG{+w}{ }https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{p}{|}\PYG{+w}{ }sh

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Clone and install}
git\PYG{+w}{ }clone\PYG{+w}{ }https://github.com/zacernst/pycypher.git
\PYG{n+nb}{cd}\PYG{+w}{ }pycypher
uv\PYG{+w}{ }sync

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Verify}
uv\PYG{+w}{ }run\PYG{+w}{ }python\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}c\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}from pycypher import Star; print(\PYGZsq{}OK\PYGZsq{})\PYGZdq{}}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\section{Quick Start: Run Your First Query}
\label{\detokenize{getting_started:quick-start-run-your-first-query}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The fastest path to results — load a pandas DataFrame and execute Cypher:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{import}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pandas}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{as}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pd}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{Context}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{EntityTable}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{ID\PYGZus{}COLUMN}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} 1. Bring your data as a DataFrame}
\PYG{n}{df} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}ID\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{3}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Alice}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Bob}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Carol}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{age}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{30}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{25}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{35}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} 2. Wrap it in an EntityTable}
\PYG{n}{table} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{EntityTable}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{from\PYGZus{}dataframe}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{df}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} 3. Build a Context and a Star executor}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{relational\PYGZus{}models}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{EntityMapping}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{RelationshipMapping}
\PYG{n}{context} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Context}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{entity\PYGZus{}mapping}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{EntityMapping}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{mapping}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n}{table}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{relationship\PYGZus{}mapping}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{RelationshipMapping}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{mapping}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{star} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} 4. Execute Cypher — returns a pandas DataFrame}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) WHERE p.age \PYGZgt{} 25 RETURN p.name AS name, p.age AS age}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{result}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{}     name  age}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} 0  Alice   30}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} 1  Carol   35}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\section{Using ContextBuilder (Recommended)}
\label{\detokenize{getting_started:using-contextbuilder-recommended}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ContextBuilder}} is the friendliest API for building
a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Context}} from files or DataFrames:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{import}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pandas}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{as}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pd}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{Star}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{ingestion}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}

\PYG{n}{people} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{user\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{3}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Alice}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Bob}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Carol}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{age}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{30}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{25}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{35}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{n}{context} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}entity}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{people}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{id\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{user\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{build}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{n}{star} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) RETURN p.name AS name ORDER BY p.age ASC}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
You can also load directly from files:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{context} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}entity}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{data/people.csv}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}entity}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Product}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{data/products.parquet}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}relationship}\PYG{p}{(}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{BOUGHT}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{data/purchases.csv}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{n}{source\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{user\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{n}{target\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{product\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{build}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\section{One\sphinxhyphen{}Line Context Builder}
\label{\detokenize{getting_started:one-line-context-builder}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
When all your data is already in memory as DataFrames, use
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{from\_dict()}} to build a context in
a single call:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{import}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pandas}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{as}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pd}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{ingestion}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{Star}

\PYG{n}{people} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}ID\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{3}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Alice}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Bob}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Carol}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{age}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{30}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{25}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{35}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{products} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}ID\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{10}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{20}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{title}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Widget}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Gadget}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{price}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mf}{9.99}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mf}{49.99}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} One call — no chaining required}
\PYG{n}{context} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{from\PYGZus{}dict}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n}{people}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Product}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n}{products}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{star} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) RETURN p.name AS name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\section{Graph Queries: MATCH with Relationships}
\label{\detokenize{getting_started:graph-queries-match-with-relationships}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH (buyer:Person)\PYGZhy{}[:BOUGHT]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(item:Product)}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    WHERE buyer.age \PYGZgt{}= 30}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    RETURN buyer.name AS buyer, item.name AS product}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    ORDER BY buyer.name}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    \PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\section{WHERE Label Predicates}
\label{\detokenize{getting_started:where-label-predicates}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WHERE n:Label}} tests whether a node variable belongs to a specific entity
type at query time.  This is useful when MATCH scans multiple entity types with
an unlabeled pattern (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MATCH (n)}}) and you want to filter or branch on the
actual type:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Filter to only Person nodes from a heterogeneous scan}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (n) WHERE n:Person RETURN n.name AS name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Negation — exclude a specific type}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (n) WHERE NOT n:Product RETURN n.name AS name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Combine label predicate with property filter}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (n) WHERE n:Person AND n.age \PYGZgt{} 30 RETURN n.name AS name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Redundant but valid — labeled MATCH + WHERE :Label confirms the type}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) WHERE p:Person RETURN p.name AS name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Label predicates compose naturally with AND / OR / NOT and with all other
WHERE predicates.  The compound form \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{n:Label1:Label2}} applies AND semantics
(the node must have both labels simultaneously) — useful when an entity table
carries multiple inherited labels.


\section{Variable\sphinxhyphen{}Length Paths}
\label{\detokenize{getting_started:variable-length-paths}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Match paths of variable hop depth using \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{*min..max}} syntax on relationship
patterns:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Any number of KNOWS hops (1 or more)}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (a:Person)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS*]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(b:Person) RETURN a.name, b.name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Exactly 2–3 hops}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (a:Person)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS*2..3]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(b:Person) RETURN a.name, b.name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Up to 4 hops — find indirect connections}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (a:Person)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS*1..4]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(b:Person) RETURN a.name, b.name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\section{shortestPath and allShortestPaths}
\label{\detokenize{getting_started:shortestpath-and-allshortestpaths}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{shortestPath()}} to find the minimum\sphinxhyphen{}hop route between two nodes.
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{allShortestPaths()}} returns every path tied for the minimum length:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Shortest route from Alice to Dave}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH (a:Person \PYGZob{}name: \PYGZsq{}Alice\PYGZsq{}\PYGZcb{}), (b:Person \PYGZob{}name: \PYGZsq{}Dave\PYGZsq{}\PYGZcb{})}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH p = shortestPath((a)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS*]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(b))}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    RETURN a.name AS start, b.name AS end, length(p) AS hops}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    \PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} → one row: start=\PYGZsq{}Alice\PYGZsq{}, end=\PYGZsq{}Dave\PYGZsq{}, hops=2}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} All shortest paths (same minimum hop count)}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH (a:Person \PYGZob{}name: \PYGZsq{}Alice\PYGZsq{}\PYGZcb{}), (b:Person \PYGZob{}name: \PYGZsq{}Dave\PYGZsq{}\PYGZcb{})}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH p = allShortestPaths((a)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS*]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(b))}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    RETURN a.name AS start, b.name AS end, length(p) AS hops}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    \PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} → one or more rows, all with hops == minimum}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
If no path exists between the two nodes the result is empty (not an error).


\section{OPTIONAL MATCH — Left\sphinxhyphen{}Join Semantics}
\label{\detokenize{getting_started:optional-match-left-join-semantics}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{OPTIONAL MATCH}} preserves rows even when the pattern has no match.
Unmatched variables are bound to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{null}}:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH (p:Person)}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    OPTIONAL MATCH (p)\PYGZhy{}[:BOUGHT]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(item:Product)}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    WITH p.name AS person, item.name AS product}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    RETURN person, product}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    \PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} People who have not bought anything appear with product = null}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\section{WITH + UNWIND: List Explosion}
\label{\detokenize{getting_started:with-unwind-list-explosion}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WITH}} pipelines data between stages.  \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{UNWIND}} explodes a list
column into one row per element:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Collect all names and unwind into individual rows}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH (p:Person)}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    WITH collect(p.name) AS names}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    UNWIND names AS name}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    RETURN name}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    \PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Explode a list property}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH (p:Person)}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    WITH p.name AS name, p.tags AS tags}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    UNWIND tags AS tag}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    RETURN name, tag}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    \PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\section{Aggregation}
\label{\detokenize{getting_started:aggregation}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Count, group by department}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) RETURN p.dept AS dept, count(p) AS n}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Collect values into a list}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) RETURN collect(p.name) AS all\PYGZus{}names}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Order and paginate}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) RETURN p.name, p.age ORDER BY p.age DESC LIMIT 5}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Null placement: NULLS FIRST puts null\PYGZhy{}valued rows before non\PYGZhy{}null rows.}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Default (no keyword) is NULLS LAST, matching Neo4j 5.x behaviour.}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (n:Person) RETURN n.name, n.score }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ORDER BY n.score ASC NULLS FIRST}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\section{WITH * — Pass\sphinxhyphen{}Through Projection}
\label{\detokenize{getting_started:with-pass-through-projection}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WITH *}} forwards all variables from the preceding MATCH into subsequent
clauses without requiring an explicit alias list:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Access any matched variable after WITH * — useful for filtering then projecting}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) WITH * WHERE p.age \PYGZgt{} 28 RETURN p.name AS name, p.age AS age}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Sort and cap rows before explicit projection}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) WITH * ORDER BY p.age ASC LIMIT 3 RETURN p.name AS name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\section{UNION — Combining Query Results}
\label{\detokenize{getting_started:union-combining-query-results}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{UNION}} merges the results of two or more queries, deduplicating rows.
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{UNION ALL}} keeps duplicates:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Names from two entity types (deduplication applied)}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH (p:Person) RETURN p.name AS name}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    UNION}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH (c:Company) RETURN c.name AS name}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    \PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Keep all rows including duplicates}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH (p:Person) WHERE p.dept = \PYGZsq{}Eng\PYGZsq{} RETURN p.name AS name}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    UNION ALL}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH (p:Person) WHERE p.age \PYGZlt{} 30 RETURN p.name AS name}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    \PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\section{Mutating the Graph — CREATE, MERGE, DELETE, REMOVE}
\label{\detokenize{getting_started:mutating-the-graph-create-merge-delete-remove}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
PyCypher supports the full Cypher write path.  All mutations are
\sphinxstylestrong{atomic}: changes are buffered and only committed when the query
succeeds.  An exception mid\sphinxhyphen{}query rolls back all pending changes.


\subsection{CREATE — Insert New Nodes and Relationships}
\label{\detokenize{getting_started:create-insert-new-nodes-and-relationships}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Create a single node}
\PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{CREATE (p:Person }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name: }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Dave}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{, age: 28\PYGZcb{})}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Create a relationship between existing nodes}
\PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH (a:Person \PYGZob{}name: \PYGZsq{}Alice\PYGZsq{}\PYGZcb{}), (b:Person \PYGZob{}name: \PYGZsq{}Bob\PYGZsq{}\PYGZcb{})}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    CREATE (a)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(b)}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    \PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{MERGE — Upsert Semantics}
\label{\detokenize{getting_started:merge-upsert-semantics}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MERGE}} either matches an existing pattern or creates it.  Use
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ON CREATE SET}} and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ON MATCH SET}} to apply different updates:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Find or create a Person with this name}
\PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MERGE (p:Person }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name: }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Eve}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZcb{}) SET p.created = true}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Track first\PYGZhy{}seen vs update\PYGZhy{}time}
\PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MERGE (p:Person \PYGZob{}name: \PYGZsq{}Alice\PYGZsq{}\PYGZcb{})}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    ON CREATE SET p.created\PYGZus{}at = timestamp()}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    ON MATCH  SET p.updated\PYGZus{}at = timestamp()}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    \PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
When the entity type referenced in a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MERGE}} pattern is not registered in
the context, MERGE treats it as \sphinxstyleemphasis{absent} and creates the pattern — this is
expected Cypher semantics for bootstrapping a new entity type.  If a genuine
runtime error occurs during the match phase (e.g. a programming error, not a
missing type), the error propagates as a
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.exceptions.GraphTypeNotFoundError}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{GraphTypeNotFoundError}}}}} so bugs are never
silently swallowed.


\subsection{SET — Update Properties}
\label{\detokenize{getting_started:set-update-properties}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Set a single property}
\PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name: }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Alice}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZcb{}) SET p.score = 99}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Set multiple properties at once}
\PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) SET p.active = true, p.last\PYGZus{}seen = timestamp()}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Set relationship properties}
\PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (a:Person)\PYGZhy{}[r:KNOWS]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(b:Person) SET r.since = 2020}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{REMOVE — Delete Properties}
\label{\detokenize{getting_started:remove-delete-properties}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Remove a property from all matching nodes}
\PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) WHERE p.temp\PYGZus{}flag IS NOT NULL REMOVE p.temp\PYGZus{}flag}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{DELETE — Remove Nodes}
\label{\detokenize{getting_started:delete-remove-nodes}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Delete nodes that match the pattern}
\PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name: }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Dave}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZcb{}) DELETE p}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\section{FOREACH — Iterate Over a List}
\label{\detokenize{getting_started:foreach-iterate-over-a-list}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{FOREACH}} applies a write clause (SET, CREATE, MERGE, DELETE) to each
element of a list.  It is the standard Cypher idiom for batch mutations:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Mark every person in a list as active}
\PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH (p:Person)}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    WITH collect(p) AS people}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    FOREACH (person IN people | SET person.active = true)}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    \PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Create a chain of nodes from a list of values}
\PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    FOREACH (name IN [\PYGZsq{}Alice\PYGZsq{}, \PYGZsq{}Bob\PYGZsq{}, \PYGZsq{}Carol\PYGZsq{}] |}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{        MERGE (p:Person \PYGZob{}name: name\PYGZcb{})}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    )}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    \PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\section{Functional Expressions}
\label{\detokenize{getting_started:functional-expressions}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
PyCypher supports the full suite of Cypher functional expressions for
in\sphinxhyphen{}query list manipulation and subquery predicates.  All are evaluated in
a \sphinxstylestrong{single vectorised pass} over the frame — no per\sphinxhyphen{}row Python overhead.


\subsection{List Comprehensions}
\label{\detokenize{getting_started:list-comprehensions}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[}variable IN list WHERE condition | map\_expression{]}}} — filter and/or
transform a list inline.  The WHERE clause and map expression are optional:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Filter — keep only high scores}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN [s IN p.scores WHERE s \PYGZgt{} 50] AS high\PYGZus{}scores}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Transform — double every score}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN [s IN p.scores | s * 2] AS doubled}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Filter + transform in one step}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN [s IN p.scores WHERE s \PYGZgt{} 50 | s * 2] AS high\PYGZus{}doubled}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Pattern Comprehensions}
\label{\detokenize{getting_started:pattern-comprehensions}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[}(anchor)\sphinxhyphen{}{[}:REL{]}\sphinxhyphen{}>(target) WHERE condition | map\_expression{]}}} — collect
matched graph neighbours into a per\sphinxhyphen{}row list:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Names of all people each person knows}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN p.name AS person, }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{       [(p)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(f) | f.name] AS friends}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Older friends only}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN p.name AS person, }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{       [(p)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(f:Person) WHERE f.age \PYGZgt{} p.age | f.name] AS older\PYGZus{}friends}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Quantifier Predicates}
\label{\detokenize{getting_started:quantifier-predicates}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Test whether a condition holds for some, all, none, or exactly one element:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} any() — at least one element satisfies the predicate}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) WHERE any(s IN p.scores WHERE s \PYGZgt{} 90) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN p.name AS name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} all() — every element satisfies the predicate (vacuously true for empty)}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) WHERE all(s IN p.scores WHERE s \PYGZgt{}= 0) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN p.name AS name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} none() — no element satisfies the predicate}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) WHERE none(s IN p.scores WHERE s \PYGZlt{} 0) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN p.name AS name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} single() — exactly one element satisfies the predicate}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) WHERE single(s IN p.scores WHERE s \PYGZgt{} 95) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN p.name AS name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{EXISTS Subqueries}
\label{\detokenize{getting_started:exists-subqueries}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{EXISTS \{ MATCH ... WHERE ... \}}} tests whether an inner graph pattern
produces at least one result.  The inner query is \sphinxstylestrong{batched}: it executes
once for all outer rows, not once per row:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} People who know at least one other person}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) WHERE EXISTS }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ MATCH (p)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(q) \PYGZcb{} }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN p.name AS name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} NOT EXISTS — people who know nobody}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) WHERE NOT EXISTS }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ MATCH (p)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(q) \PYGZcb{} }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN p.name AS name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Filter the subquery match with WHERE}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{WHERE EXISTS }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ MATCH (p)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(q:Person) WHERE q.age \PYGZgt{} 30 \PYGZcb{} }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN p.name AS name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{REDUCE}
\label{\detokenize{getting_started:reduce}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{reduce(accumulator = initial, variable IN list | step\_expression)}} —
fold a list into a scalar value.  The accumulator carries the running
result; \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{step\_expression}} receives both the accumulator and the current
element:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Sum of a list of scores}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN reduce(total = 0, s IN p.scores | total + s) AS score\PYGZus{}sum}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Maximum element}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN reduce(m = \PYGZhy{}1, s IN p.scores | CASE WHEN s \PYGZgt{} m THEN s ELSE m END) AS max\PYGZus{}score}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\section{Scalar Functions}
\label{\detokenize{getting_started:scalar-functions}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Over 130 scalar functions are available in any expression context.  All
functions are null\sphinxhyphen{}safe: passing \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{null}} returns \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{null}} unless documented
otherwise.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Math and trigonometric functions (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{abs}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{sin}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{log}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pow}}, etc.)
are evaluated using numpy C\sphinxhyphen{}level array operations — no per\sphinxhyphen{}row Python
overhead.  String predicate functions (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{startsWith}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{endsWith}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{contains}}) use the pandas \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{.str}} Cython accessor.  These two categories
are safe to use in WHERE clauses on large frames without performance concern.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} String manipulation}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) RETURN toUpper(p.name) AS upper\PYGZus{}name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Null\PYGZhy{}safe default via coalesce}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN coalesce(p.nickname, p.name) AS display\PYGZus{}name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Type predicate — filter rows where a property has the expected type}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (r:Record) WHERE isString(r.code) AND isInteger(r.count) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN r.code, r.count}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Temporal — parse and decompose a duration}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN duration(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{P1Y6M}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{).months AS months}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} → 6}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Rounding with explicit mode (Neo4j 5.x 3\PYGZhy{}arg form)}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Modes: HALF\PYGZus{}UP (default, away from zero), HALF\PYGZus{}DOWN, HALF\PYGZus{}EVEN (banker\PYGZsq{}s),}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{}        CEILING, FLOOR, UP (away from zero), DOWN (truncation)}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) RETURN round(p.score, 2, }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{HALF\PYGZus{}EVEN}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{) AS rounded}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} round(2.5, 0, \PYGZsq{}HALF\PYGZus{}EVEN\PYGZsq{}) → 2.0 (nearest even)}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} round(2.5, 0, \PYGZsq{}HALF\PYGZus{}UP\PYGZsq{})   → 3.0 (away from zero)}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} round(2.5, 0, \PYGZsq{}CEILING\PYGZsq{})   → 3.0 (toward +∞)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Bitwise operations — ETL flag/bitmask processing (Neo4j 5.x)}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (e:Event) WHERE bitAnd(e.flags, 4) \PYGZgt{} 0 }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN e.name AS name, bitOr(e.flags, 1) AS flags\PYGZus{}with\PYGZus{}bit0}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} bitAnd / bitOr / bitXor / bitNot / bitShiftLeft / bitShiftRight}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} All accept integer args, return null when any arg is null.}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Function categories} (131 functions registered as of this writing):

Discover all registered functions at runtime with
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.star.Star.available_functions}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{available\_functions()}}}}}:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{available\PYGZus{}functions}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} [\PYGZsq{}abs\PYGZsq{}, \PYGZsq{}acos\PYGZsq{}, \PYGZsq{}asin\PYGZsq{}, ..., \PYGZsq{}valuetype\PYGZsq{}, \PYGZsq{}values\PYGZsq{}]}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Auto\sphinxhyphen{}generated Column Names}
\label{\detokenize{getting_started:auto-generated-column-names}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
When a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN}} item has no explicit \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{AS}} alias, pycypher generates a
display name from the expression — matching Neo4j’s behaviour:


\begin{savenotes}\sphinxattablestart
\sphinxthistablewithglobalstyle
\centering
\begin{tabular}[t]{\X{50}{100}\X{50}{100}}
\sphinxtoprule
\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
RETURN expression
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Result column name
\\
\sphinxmidrule
\sphinxtableatstartofbodyhook
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN p.name}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{name}}  \sphinxstyleemphasis{(property name, unqualified)}
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN p}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{p}}  \sphinxstyleemphasis{(variable name)}
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN toUpper(p.name)}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{toUpper(name)}}
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN abs(p.age)}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{abs(age)}}
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN p.age + 1}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{age + 1}}
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN 42}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{42}}
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN 'hello'}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{hello}}
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN true}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{true}}
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN null}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{null}}
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN p.age > 25}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{age > 25}}  \sphinxstyleemphasis{(comparison)}
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN p.name STARTS WITH 'A'}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{name STARTS WITH A}}
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN p.name IS NULL}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{name IS NULL}}
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN NOT p.active}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{NOT active}}
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN p.age > 18 AND p.active}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{age > 18 AND active}}
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN \sphinxhyphen{}p.age}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\sphinxhyphen{}age}}  \sphinxstyleemphasis{(unary negation)}
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN \$limit}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\$limit}}  \sphinxstyleemphasis{(query parameter)}
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN count(*)}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{count(*)}}
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN xs{[}0{]}}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{xs{[}0{]}}}  \sphinxstyleemphasis{(index lookup)}
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN xs{[}1..3{]}}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{xs{[}1..3{]}}}  \sphinxstyleemphasis{(slicing)}
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN {[}x IN xs \textbackslash{}| x * 2{]}}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[}x IN xs \textbackslash{}| x * 2{]}}}  \sphinxstyleemphasis{(list comprehension)}
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN CASE WHEN … END}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{case}}
\\
\sphinxbottomrule
\end{tabular}
\sphinxtableafterendhook\par
\sphinxattableend\end{savenotes}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Using \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{AS}} always overrides the auto\sphinxhyphen{}generated name:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Two aliasless columns — each gets its own display name}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) RETURN toUpper(p.name), p.age + 1}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{result}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{columns}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{tolist}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} [\PYGZsq{}toUpper(name)\PYGZsq{}, \PYGZsq{}age + 1\PYGZsq{}]}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\section{Running Tests}
\label{\detokenize{getting_started:running-tests}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
uv\PYG{+w}{ }run\PYG{+w}{ }pytest\PYG{+w}{             }\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} full suite}
uv\PYG{+w}{ }run\PYG{+w}{ }pytest\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}n\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+m}{4}\PYG{+w}{        }\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} parallel (faster)}
uv\PYG{+w}{ }run\PYG{+w}{ }pytest\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}k\PYG{+w}{ }Person\PYG{+w}{   }\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} filter by keyword}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}
