\subsection{The 60\sphinxhyphen{}Second Mental Model}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/cypher_for_sql_users:the-60-second-mental-model}}

\begin{savenotes}\sphinxattablestart
\sphinxthistablewithglobalstyle
\centering
\begin{tabulary}{\linewidth}[t]{TTT}
\sphinxtoprule
\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
SQL Concept
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Cypher Equivalent
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Key Difference
\\
\sphinxmidrule
\sphinxtableatstartofbodyhook
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SELECT}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Same role — choose output columns
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{FROM table}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MATCH (n:Label)}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Nodes replace tables
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WHERE}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WHERE}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Same syntax for property filters
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{JOIN ... ON}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{()\sphinxhyphen{}{[}:REL{]}\sphinxhyphen{}>()}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Relationships replace JOINs
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{LEFT JOIN}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{OPTIONAL MATCH}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Preserves rows with no match
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{GROUP BY}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleemphasis{(implicit)}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Non\sphinxhyphen{}aggregated RETURN columns group
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ORDER BY}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ORDER BY}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Identical
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{LIMIT}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{LIMIT}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Identical
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{INSERT INTO}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{CREATE}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Creates nodes or relationships
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{UPDATE}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SET}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Sets properties on matched nodes
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DELETE FROM}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DELETE}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Removes matched nodes
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
subquery
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WITH}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Pipes results between query stages
\\
\sphinxbottomrule
\end{tabulary}
\sphinxtableafterendhook\par
\sphinxattableend\end{savenotes}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The biggest conceptual shift: \sphinxstylestrong{in SQL you join tables; in Cypher you
traverse relationships.}


\subsection{Side\sphinxhyphen{}by\sphinxhyphen{}Side Examples}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/cypher_for_sql_users:side-by-side-examples}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
All examples below use this dataset:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{import}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pandas}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{as}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pd}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{Star}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{ingestion}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}

\PYG{n}{people} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}ID\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{3}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{4}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Alice}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Bob}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Carol}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Dave}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{age}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{30}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{25}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{35}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{28}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{dept}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{eng}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{sales}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{eng}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{sales}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{n}{products} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}ID\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{10}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{20}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{30}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{title}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Widget}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Gadget}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Gizmo}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{price}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mf}{9.99}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mf}{49.99}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mf}{14.99}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{n}{purchases} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}ID\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{100}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{101}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{102}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{103}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}SOURCE\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{3}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}TARGET\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{10}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{20}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{30}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{10}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{n}{context} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{from\PYGZus{}dict}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n}{people}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Product}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n}{products}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{BOUGHT}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n}{purchases}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{star} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{SELECT … FROM … WHERE}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/cypher_for_sql_users:select-from-where}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{SQL:}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k}{SELECT}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{age}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{FROM}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{Person}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{WHERE}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{age}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{o}{\PYGZgt{}}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+m+mi}{28}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Cypher:}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) WHERE p.age \PYGZgt{} 28 RETURN p.name AS name, p.age AS age}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
 name  age
Alice   30
Carol   35
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Key difference: In Cypher, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{p}} is a \sphinxstyleemphasis{variable} bound to each \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Person}}
node.  You access properties with dot notation: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{p.age}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{p.name}}.


\subsubsection{JOIN (Inner Join via Relationship)}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/cypher_for_sql_users:join-inner-join-via-relationship}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{SQL:}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k}{SELECT}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{p}\PYG{p}{.}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{pr}\PYG{p}{.}\PYG{n}{title}
\PYG{k}{FROM}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{Person}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{p}
\PYG{k}{JOIN}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{purchases}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{ON}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{purchases}\PYG{p}{.}\PYG{n}{person\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{p}\PYG{p}{.}\PYG{n}{id}
\PYG{k}{JOIN}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{Product}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{pr}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{ON}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{pr}\PYG{p}{.}\PYG{n}{id}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{purchases}\PYG{p}{.}\PYG{n}{product\PYGZus{}id}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Cypher:}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person)\PYGZhy{}[:BOUGHT]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(pr:Product) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN p.name AS buyer, pr.title AS product}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
buyer product
Alice  Widget
Alice   Gizmo
  Bob  Gadget
Carol  Widget
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Key difference: No \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ON}} clause needed — the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\sphinxhyphen{}{[}:BOUGHT{]}\sphinxhyphen{}>}} pattern
encodes the join condition.  The arrow \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\sphinxhyphen{}>}} indicates direction.


\subsubsection{LEFT JOIN (OPTIONAL MATCH)}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/cypher_for_sql_users:left-join-optional-match}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{SQL:}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k}{SELECT}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{p}\PYG{p}{.}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{pr}\PYG{p}{.}\PYG{n}{title}
\PYG{k}{FROM}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{Person}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{p}
\PYG{k}{LEFT}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{JOIN}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{purchases}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{ON}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{purchases}\PYG{p}{.}\PYG{n}{person\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{p}\PYG{p}{.}\PYG{n}{id}
\PYG{k}{LEFT}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{JOIN}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{Product}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{pr}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{ON}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{pr}\PYG{p}{.}\PYG{n}{id}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{purchases}\PYG{p}{.}\PYG{n}{product\PYGZus{}id}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Cypher:}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{OPTIONAL MATCH (p)\PYGZhy{}[:BOUGHT]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(pr:Product) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN p.name AS person, pr.title AS product}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
person product
 Alice  Widget
 Alice   Gizmo
   Bob  Gadget
 Carol  Widget
  Dave    None
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Key difference: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{OPTIONAL MATCH}} preserves all \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Person}} rows.
Dave appears with \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}} because he has no purchases.


\subsubsection{GROUP BY with Aggregation}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/cypher_for_sql_users:group-by-with-aggregation}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{SQL:}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k}{SELECT}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{dept}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{COUNT}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{o}{*}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{AS}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{headcount}
\PYG{k}{FROM}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{Person}
\PYG{k}{GROUP}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{BY}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{dept}
\PYG{k}{ORDER}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{BY}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{dept}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Cypher:}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN p.dept AS department, count(p) AS headcount }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ORDER BY department}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
department  headcount
       eng          2
     sales          2
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Key difference: No \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{GROUP BY}} clause.  Cypher groups implicitly by
all non\sphinxhyphen{}aggregated columns in RETURN.  \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{count(p)}} counts nodes, not
rows.


\subsubsection{Subqueries (WITH Clause)}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/cypher_for_sql_users:subqueries-with-clause}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{SQL:}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k}{SELECT}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{FROM}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{k}{SELECT}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{age}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{FROM}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{Person}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{WHERE}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{age}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{o}{\PYGZgt{}}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+m+mi}{25}
\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{sub}
\PYG{k}{WHERE}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{age}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{o}{\PYGZlt{}}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+m+mi}{35}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Cypher:}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{WHERE p.age \PYGZgt{} 25 }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{WITH p.name AS name, p.age AS age }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{WHERE age \PYGZlt{} 35 }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
name
Alice
 Dave
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Key difference: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WITH}} pipes results between query stages, like a
subquery.  Each \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WITH}} creates a new scope — only columns listed in
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WITH}} are available downstream.


\subsubsection{EXISTS (Subquery Predicate)}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/cypher_for_sql_users:exists-subquery-predicate}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{SQL:}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k}{SELECT}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{FROM}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{Person}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{p}
\PYG{k}{WHERE}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{EXISTS}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{k}{SELECT}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{FROM}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{purchases}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{WHERE}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{person\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{p}\PYG{p}{.}\PYG{n}{id}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Cypher:}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{WHERE EXISTS }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ MATCH (p)\PYGZhy{}[:BOUGHT]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}() \PYGZcb{} }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN p.name AS name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
name
Alice
  Bob
Carol
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{COLLECT (No SQL Equivalent)}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/cypher_for_sql_users:collect-no-sql-equivalent}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Cypher can aggregate values into lists — something SQL cannot do natively:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person)\PYGZhy{}[:BOUGHT]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(pr:Product) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN p.name AS buyer, collect(pr.title) AS products}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
buyer        products
Alice [Widget, Gizmo]
  Bob        [Gadget]
Carol        [Widget]
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Common SQL Patterns Translated}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/cypher_for_sql_users:common-sql-patterns-translated}}

\subsubsection{DISTINCT}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/cypher_for_sql_users:distinct}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{SQL:} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SELECT DISTINCT dept FROM Person}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Cypher:}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k}{MATCH}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{p}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{n}{Person}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{RETURN}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{DISTINCT}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{p}\PYG{p}{.}\PYG{n}{dept}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{AS}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{dept}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{COUNT with Filter}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/cypher_for_sql_users:count-with-filter}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{SQL:} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Person WHERE age > 30}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Cypher:}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k}{MATCH}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{p}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{n}{Person}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{WHERE}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{p}\PYG{p}{.}\PYG{n}{age}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{p}{\PYGZgt{}}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+m}{30}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{RETURN}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{count}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{p}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{AS}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{n}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{LIKE (String Matching)}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/cypher_for_sql_users:like-string-matching}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{SQL:} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SELECT name FROM Person WHERE name LIKE 'A\%'}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Cypher:}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k}{MATCH}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{p}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{n}{Person}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{WHERE}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{p}\PYG{p}{.}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{STARTS}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{WITH}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s}{\PYGZsq{}A\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{RETURN}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{p}\PYG{p}{.}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{AS}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{name}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Other string predicates: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ENDS WITH}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{CONTAINS}}.


\subsubsection{IN List}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/cypher_for_sql_users:in-list}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{SQL:} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SELECT name FROM Person WHERE dept IN ('eng', 'sales')}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Cypher:}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k}{MATCH}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{p}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{n}{Person}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{WHERE}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{p}\PYG{p}{.}\PYG{n}{dept}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{IN}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{o}{[}\PYG{l+s}{\PYGZsq{}eng\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s}{\PYGZsq{}sales\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{o}{]}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{RETURN}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{p}\PYG{p}{.}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{AS}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{name}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Note: Cypher uses square brackets \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[} {]}}} for lists, not parentheses.


\subsubsection{COALESCE / NULL Handling}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/cypher_for_sql_users:coalesce-null-handling}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{SQL:} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SELECT COALESCE(nickname, name) FROM Person}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Cypher:}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k}{MATCH}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{p}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{n}{Person}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{RETURN}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{coalesce}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{p}\PYG{p}{.}\PYG{n}{nickname}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{p}\PYG{p}{.}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{AS}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{display\PYGZus{}name}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
NULL handling: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{IS NULL}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{IS NOT NULL}} work identically to SQL.


\subsubsection{CASE Expressions}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/cypher_for_sql_users:case-expressions}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{SQL:} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SELECT CASE WHEN age > 30 THEN 'senior' ELSE 'junior' END FROM Person}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Cypher:}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k}{MATCH}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{p}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{n}{Person}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{RETURN}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{CASE}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{WHEN}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{p}\PYG{p}{.}\PYG{n}{age}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{p}{\PYGZgt{}}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+m}{30}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{THEN}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s}{\PYGZsq{}senior\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{ELSE}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s}{\PYGZsq{}junior\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{END}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{AS}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{level}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{INSERT / UPDATE / DELETE}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/cypher_for_sql_users:insert-update-delete}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{SQL:}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k}{INSERT}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{INTO}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{Person}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{age}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{VALUES}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}Eve\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+m+mi}{29}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{;}
\PYG{k}{UPDATE}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{Person}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{SET}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{age}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+m+mi}{31}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{WHERE}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}Alice\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{;}
\PYG{k}{DELETE}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{FROM}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{Person}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{WHERE}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}Dave\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{;}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Cypher:}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k}{CREATE}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{e}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{n}{Person}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s}{\PYGZsq{}Eve\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{age}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+m}{29}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{k}{MATCH}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{p}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{n}{Person}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s}{\PYGZsq{}Alice\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{SET}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{p}\PYG{p}{.}\PYG{n}{age}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{p}{=}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+m}{31}

\PYG{k}{MATCH}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{p}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{n}{Person}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s}{\PYGZsq{}Dave\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{DELETE}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n}{p}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{When to Use Graph Patterns vs SQL Thinking}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/cypher_for_sql_users:when-to-use-graph-patterns-vs-sql-thinking}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Graph patterns work better when:}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
You’re traversing relationships between entities (social networks,
supply chains, dependency graphs)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Join depth is variable or unknown (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MATCH path = (a)\sphinxhyphen{}{[}:KNOWS*1..5{]}\sphinxhyphen{}>(b)}})

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
You want to express “find all connected entities” naturally

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{SQL thinking works fine when:}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
You’re filtering a single entity type (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MATCH (p:Person) WHERE ...}})

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
You’re doing simple aggregation (counts, sums, averages)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
You have a fixed, known join structure

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{PyCypher handles both well} — the Cypher syntax is expressive for graph
traversal while remaining readable for simple table\sphinxhyphen{}like queries.


\subsection{Key Syntax Differences Cheat Sheet}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/cypher_for_sql_users:key-syntax-differences-cheat-sheet}}

\begin{savenotes}\sphinxattablestart
\sphinxthistablewithglobalstyle
\centering
\begin{tabular}[t]{\X{35}{100}\X{35}{100}\X{30}{100}}
\sphinxtoprule
\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
SQL
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Cypher
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Note
\\
\sphinxmidrule
\sphinxtableatstartofbodyhook
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{table.column}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{variable.property}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Dot notation for both
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{'string'}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{'string'}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Same quoting
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{NULL}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{null}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Lowercase in Cypher
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{TRUE / FALSE}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{true / false}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Lowercase in Cypher
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{(1, 2, 3)}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[}1, 2, 3{]}}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Square brackets for lists
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{AS alias}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{AS alias}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Same syntax
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{AND / OR / NOT}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{AND / OR / NOT}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Same syntax
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{<> / !=}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{<>}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Cypher uses \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{<>}} only
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BETWEEN a AND b}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{>= a AND <= b}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
No BETWEEN keyword
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{LIKE '\%text\%'}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{CONTAINS 'text'}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Named predicates instead
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{OFFSET n}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SKIP n}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Different keyword
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{COUNT(*)}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{count(*)}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Lowercase functions
\\
\sphinxbottomrule
\end{tabular}
\sphinxtableafterendhook\par
\sphinxattableend\end{savenotes}

\sphinxstepscope


\section{Basic Query Execution}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/basic_query_parsing:basic-query-execution}}\label{\detokenize{tutorials/basic_query_parsing::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This tutorial shows how to run Cypher queries against your data using PyCypher.


\subsection{Learning Objectives}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/basic_query_parsing:learning-objectives}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Load data and build a query context

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Execute MATCH / WHERE / RETURN queries

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Filter, sort, and aggregate results

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Inspect the parsed AST when needed

\end{itemize}


\subsection{Setup}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/basic_query_parsing:setup}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{import}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pandas}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{as}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pd}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{ingestion}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{Star}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Load data from a CSV (or pass a DataFrame directly)}
\PYG{n}{context} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}entity}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{data/people.csv}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{build}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{star} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
If your data is already in memory as a DataFrame, the fastest path is
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{from\_dict()}}:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{people} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}ID\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{3}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:}   \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Alice}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Bob}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Carol}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{age}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:}    \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{30}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{25}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{35}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{context} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{from\PYGZus{}dict}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n}{people}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{star} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Running Queries}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/basic_query_parsing:running-queries}}

\subsubsection{Simple MATCH}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/basic_query_parsing:simple-match}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Returns a pandas DataFrame}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) RETURN p.name AS name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{result}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{}     name}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} 0  Alice}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} 1    Bob}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} 2  Carol}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Filtering with WHERE}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/basic_query_parsing:filtering-with-where}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) WHERE p.age \PYGZgt{} 25 RETURN p.name AS name, p.age AS age}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{result}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{}     name  age}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} 0  Alice   30}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} 1  Carol   35}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Sorting and Limiting}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/basic_query_parsing:sorting-and-limiting}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Top 2 oldest people}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) RETURN p.name AS name ORDER BY p.age DESC LIMIT 2}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Aggregation}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/basic_query_parsing:aggregation}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) RETURN count(p) AS total, avg(p.age) AS avg\PYGZus{}age}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Relationship Patterns}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/basic_query_parsing:relationship-patterns}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{context} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}entity}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{data/people.csv}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}entity}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Product}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{data/products.csv}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}relationship}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{BOUGHT}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{data/purchases.csv}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
                      \PYG{n}{source\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{user\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{target\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{product\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{build}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{star} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH (buyer:Person)\PYGZhy{}[:BOUGHT]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(item:Product)}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    WHERE buyer.age \PYGZgt{}= 30}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    RETURN buyer.name AS buyer, item.name AS product}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    ORDER BY buyer.name}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    \PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Discovering Available Functions}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/basic_query_parsing:discovering-available-functions}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.star.Star.available_functions}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{available\_functions()}}}}} to see all registered
scalar functions at any time:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{available\PYGZus{}functions}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} [\PYGZsq{}abs\PYGZsq{}, \PYGZsq{}acos\PYGZsq{}, \PYGZsq{}asin\PYGZsq{}, ..., \PYGZsq{}toupper\PYGZsq{}, \PYGZsq{}trim\PYGZsq{}]}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Use them in any expression context}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) RETURN toUpper(p.name) AS upper\PYGZus{}name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Advanced: Inspecting the Parsed AST}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/basic_query_parsing:advanced-inspecting-the-parsed-ast}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
For users who need to programmatically inspect or introspect parsed query
structure, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{from\_cypher()}} returns a
fully\sphinxhyphen{}typed Pydantic AST:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{ast\PYGZus{}models}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{ASTConverter}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{Match}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{Return}

\PYG{n}{query} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) WHERE p.age \PYGZgt{} 30 RETURN p.name AS name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{n}{ast} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ASTConverter}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{from\PYGZus{}cypher}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{query}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} ast is a Query object; clauses is the flat list of parsed clauses}
\PYG{k}{for} \PYG{n}{clause} \PYG{o+ow}{in} \PYG{n}{ast}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{clauses}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n+nb}{type}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{clause}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n+nv+vm}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}name\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{clause}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Check the first clause is a MATCH}
\PYG{n}{match\PYGZus{}clause} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ast}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{clauses}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{0}\PYG{p}{]}
\PYG{k}{assert} \PYG{n+nb}{isinstance}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{match\PYGZus{}clause}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{Match}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{WHERE predicate:}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{match\PYGZus{}clause}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{where}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Check the last clause is a RETURN}
\PYG{n}{return\PYGZus{}clause} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ast}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{clauses}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{]}
\PYG{k}{assert} \PYG{n+nb}{isinstance}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{return\PYGZus{}clause}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{Return}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN items:}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{return\PYGZus{}clause}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{items}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Error Handling}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/basic_query_parsing:error-handling}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k}{try}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) RETURN p.salary AS s}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{except} \PYG{n+ne}{KeyError} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n}{e}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Property \PYGZsq{}salary\PYGZsq{} not found on entity type \PYGZsq{}Person\PYGZsq{}}
    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Property error: }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{e}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{k}{try}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (x:Unknown) RETURN x.name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{except} \PYG{n+ne}{ValueError} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n}{e}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Entity type \PYGZsq{}Unknown\PYGZsq{} is not registered}
    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Entity error: }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{e}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\label{\detokenize{tutorials/basic_query_parsing:next-steps}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{user_guide/query_processing::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Query Processing}}}} — execution model deep\sphinxhyphen{}dive

\section{Graph Modeling}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/graph_modeling:graph-modeling}}\label{\detokenize{tutorials/graph_modeling::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Learn how to design your tabular data as a graph for PyCypher — choosing
entity types, modeling relationships, handling properties, and avoiding
common pitfalls.

\begin{sphinxcontents}
\sphinxstylecontentstitle{In this tutorial}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{tutorials/graph_modeling:id1}}{\hyperref[\detokenize{tutorials/graph_modeling:prerequisites}]{\sphinxcrossref{Prerequisites}}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{tutorials/graph_modeling:id2}}{\hyperref[\detokenize{tutorials/graph_modeling:why-graph-modeling-matters}]{\sphinxcrossref{Why Graph Modeling Matters}}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{tutorials/graph_modeling:id3}}{\hyperref[\detokenize{tutorials/graph_modeling:the-core-abstraction}]{\sphinxcrossref{The Core Abstraction}}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
