\subsection{Prerequisites}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/graph_modeling:prerequisites}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
PyCypher installed (see {\hyperref[\detokenize{getting_started::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Getting Started}}}})

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Completed {\hyperref[\detokenize{tutorials/basic_query_parsing::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Basic Query Execution}}}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Familiarity with pandas DataFrames

\end{itemize}


\subsection{Why Graph Modeling Matters}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/graph_modeling:why-graph-modeling-matters}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
PyCypher executes Cypher queries against DataFrames, not a graph database.
This means \sphinxstylestrong{you} decide how to carve your tabular data into entities and
relationships.  Good modeling choices make queries natural and fast; poor
choices force awkward workarounds and slow cross\sphinxhyphen{}joins.


\subsection{The Core Abstraction}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/graph_modeling:the-core-abstraction}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
PyCypher has two primitives:

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Entities} — things with an identity and properties (nodes in graph terms):

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{import}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pandas}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{as}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pd}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{ingestion}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}

\PYG{n}{people} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}ID\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{3}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Alice}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Bob}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Carol}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{age}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{30}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{25}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{35}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Relationships} — directed connections between entities:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{knows} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}SOURCE\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}TARGET\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{3}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{since}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2020}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2021}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_ID\_\_}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_SOURCE\_\_}}, and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_TARGET\_\_}} columns are the
structural glue.  Everything else is a property.


\subsection{Designing Entities}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/graph_modeling:designing-entities}}

\subsubsection{One Table per Concept}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/graph_modeling:one-table-per-concept}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Each real\sphinxhyphen{}world concept becomes a separate entity type.  Don’t merge
unrelated data into a single table:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} GOOD: separate entity types}
\PYG{n}{context} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}entity}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{people\PYGZus{}df}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}entity}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Company}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{companies\PYGZus{}df}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}entity}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Product}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{products\PYGZus{}df}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{build}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} BAD: one mega\PYGZhy{}table with a \PYGZdq{}type\PYGZdq{} column}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} This forces every query to filter by type manually}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Choosing IDs}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/graph_modeling:choosing-ids}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_ID\_\_}} column uniquely identifies each row within its entity type.
IDs can be integers or strings — choose whatever your source data provides:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Integer IDs (from a database primary key)}
\PYG{n}{people} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}ID\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{3}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Alice}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Bob}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Carol}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} String IDs (from a natural key or UUID)}
\PYG{n}{people} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}ID\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{alice\PYGZhy{}01}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{bob\PYGZhy{}02}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{carol\PYGZhy{}03}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Alice}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Bob}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Carol}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Using an existing column as the ID}
\PYG{n}{raw} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{user\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{3}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Alice}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Bob}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Carol}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{context} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}entity}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{raw}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{id\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{user\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{build}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{warning}{Warning:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
IDs must be unique within each entity type.  Duplicate IDs produce
unexpected cross\sphinxhyphen{}join results during MATCH.
\end{sphinxadmonition}


\subsubsection{Properties}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/graph_modeling:properties}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Every non\sphinxhyphen{}ID column becomes a property accessible via \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{n.property\_name}}
in Cypher:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{people} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}ID\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{3}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Alice}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Bob}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Carol}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{age}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{30}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{25}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{35}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{active}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{k+kc}{True}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{k+kc}{True}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{k+kc}{False}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{scores}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{85}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{92}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{70}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{95}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{88}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{60}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} list properties work too}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Property types map directly from pandas dtypes: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{int64}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{float64}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}} (strings), \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{bool}}, and Python lists.


\subsection{Designing Relationships}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/graph_modeling:designing-relationships}}

\subsubsection{Relationship Direction}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/graph_modeling:relationship-direction}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Relationships in PyCypher are \sphinxstylestrong{directed}: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_SOURCE\_\_}} → \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_TARGET\_\_}}.
Model the direction that reflects the real\sphinxhyphen{}world semantics:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Person \PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{} Person (social link)}
\PYG{n}{knows} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}SOURCE\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}   \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} who initiates}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}TARGET\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{3}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}   \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} who is known}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Person \PYGZhy{}[:WORKS\PYGZus{}AT]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{} Company (employment)}
\PYG{n}{works\PYGZus{}at} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}SOURCE\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}   \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} employee}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}TARGET\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{10}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{20}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} company}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
In queries, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{(a)\sphinxhyphen{}{[}:KNOWS{]}\sphinxhyphen{}>(b)}} follows the direction.  Use
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{(a)<\sphinxhyphen{}{[}:KNOWS{]}\sphinxhyphen{}(b)}} or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{(a)\sphinxhyphen{}{[}:KNOWS{]}\sphinxhyphen{}(b)}} for reverse or undirected.


\subsubsection{Relationship Properties}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/graph_modeling:relationship-properties}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Add columns beyond \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_SOURCE\_\_}} and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_TARGET\_\_}} for relationship
properties:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{works\PYGZus{}at} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}SOURCE\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{3}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}TARGET\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{10}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{10}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{20}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{role}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Engineer}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Manager}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Analyst}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{start\PYGZus{}year}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2018}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2015}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2020}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Query them by binding the relationship to a variable:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person)\PYGZhy{}[r:WORKS\PYGZus{}AT]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(c:Company) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN p.name, r.role, c.name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Multiple Relationship Types}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/graph_modeling:multiple-relationship-types}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Register each relationship type separately:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{context} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}entity}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{people}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}entity}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Company}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{companies}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}entity}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Product}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{products}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}relationship}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{KNOWS}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{knows\PYGZus{}df}\PYG{p}{,}
                      \PYG{n}{source\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{person\PYGZus{}a}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{target\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{person\PYGZus{}b}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}relationship}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{WORKS\PYGZus{}AT}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{works\PYGZus{}at\PYGZus{}df}\PYG{p}{,}
                      \PYG{n}{source\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{employee\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{target\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{company\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}relationship}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{BOUGHT}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{purchases\PYGZus{}df}\PYG{p}{,}
                      \PYG{n}{source\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{buyer\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{target\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{product\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{build}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Common Modeling Patterns}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/graph_modeling:common-modeling-patterns}}

\subsubsection{Hub\sphinxhyphen{}and\sphinxhyphen{}Spoke (Star Schema)}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/graph_modeling:hub-and-spoke-star-schema}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A central entity connected to many satellite entities.  Natural for
customer\sphinxhyphen{}centric or product\sphinxhyphen{}centric analytics:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
Person ──WORKS\PYGZus{}AT──\PYGZgt{} Company
  │
  ├──BOUGHT──\PYGZgt{} Product
  │
  └──LIVES\PYGZus{}IN──\PYGZgt{} City
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} \PYGZdq{}Who bought products from their own city?\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH (p:Person)\PYGZhy{}[:BOUGHT]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(prod:Product),}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{          (p)\PYGZhy{}[:LIVES\PYGZus{}IN]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(c:City),}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{          (prod)\PYGZhy{}[:SOLD\PYGZus{}IN]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(c)}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    RETURN p.name, prod.title, c.name}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    \PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Chain (Sequential Events)}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/graph_modeling:chain-sequential-events}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Events linked in sequence — log analysis, process workflows:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
Event1 ──NEXT──\PYGZgt{} Event2 ──NEXT──\PYGZgt{} Event3
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{events} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}ID\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{e1}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{e2}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{e3}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{e4}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{action}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{login}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{view\PYGZus{}page}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{add\PYGZus{}cart}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{checkout}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{timestamp}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{2024\PYGZhy{}01\PYGZhy{}01 10:00}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{2024\PYGZhy{}01\PYGZhy{}01 10:05}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
                   \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{2024\PYGZhy{}01\PYGZhy{}01 10:12}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{2024\PYGZhy{}01\PYGZhy{}01 10:15}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{next\PYGZus{}event} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}SOURCE\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{e1}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{e2}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{e3}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}TARGET\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{e2}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{e3}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{e4}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Find 3\PYGZhy{}step funnels}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH (a:Event)\PYGZhy{}[:NEXT]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(b:Event)\PYGZhy{}[:NEXT]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(c:Event)}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    RETURN a.action, b.action, c.action}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    \PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Bipartite Graph}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/graph_modeling:bipartite-graph}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Two entity types connected only through relationships (no intra\sphinxhyphen{}type edges).
Common for recommendation systems:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
User ──RATED──\PYGZgt{} Movie
User ──RATED──\PYGZgt{} Movie
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Users who rated the same movie as Alice}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH (alice:User \PYGZob{}name: \PYGZsq{}Alice\PYGZsq{}\PYGZcb{})\PYGZhy{}[:RATED]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(m:Movie)\PYGZlt{}\PYGZhy{}[:RATED]\PYGZhy{}(other:User)}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    WHERE other.name \PYGZlt{}\PYGZgt{} \PYGZsq{}Alice\PYGZsq{}}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    RETURN DISTINCT other.name AS similar\PYGZus{}user, m.title AS shared\PYGZus{}movie}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    \PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Try It Yourself}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/graph_modeling:try-it-yourself}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Exercise 1}: Model a university dataset with Students, Courses, and
Professors.  Students enroll in courses (ENROLLED\_IN) and professors
teach courses (TEACHES).

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Your DataFrames here:}
\PYG{n}{students} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}ID\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{year}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{courses} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}ID\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{title}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{credits}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{professors} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}ID\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{dept}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{enrolled\PYGZus{}in} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}SOURCE\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}TARGET\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{teaches} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}SOURCE\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}TARGET\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Query: \PYGZdq{}Which professors teach courses that Alice is enrolled in?\PYGZdq{}}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Exercise 2}: You have a CSV of email messages with \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{sender}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{recipient}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{subject}}, and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{timestamp}} columns.  How would you
model this as entities and relationships?


\subsection{Common Pitfalls}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/graph_modeling:common-pitfalls}}\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Forgetting ``\_\_ID\_\_``} — Without a unique ID column, entity rows
cannot be joined to relationships.  Use \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{id\_col}} in ContextBuilder
to rename an existing column.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Mismatched ID types} — If \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_SOURCE\_\_}} contains strings but
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_ID\_\_}} contains integers, relationships won’t match.  Ensure
consistent types.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Denormalized relationships} — Putting relationship data as columns
on an entity table (e.g. \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{person.company\_name}}) prevents graph
traversal.  Extract it into a separate relationship table.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Undirected data forced into one direction} — For symmetric
relationships (e.g. “is friends with”), either store both directions
or use undirected match syntax \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{(a)\sphinxhyphen{}{[}:FRIENDS{]}\sphinxhyphen{}(b)}}.

\end{enumerate}


\subsection{Next Steps}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/graph_modeling:next-steps}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{tutorials/basic_query_parsing::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Basic Query Execution}}}} — execute queries against your modeled data

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{tutorials/pattern_matching::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Pattern Matching}}}} — advanced traversal techniques

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{tutorials/data_etl_pipeline::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Data ETL Pipeline}}}} — load data from files and databases

\end{itemize}

\sphinxstepscope


\section{Pattern Matching}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/pattern_matching:pattern-matching}}\label{\detokenize{tutorials/pattern_matching::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Advanced techniques for matching graph patterns in Cypher queries.


\subsection{Learning Objectives}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/pattern_matching:learning-objectives}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Match relationships between nodes with typed and untyped patterns

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use label predicates to filter by entity type at query time

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Traverse variable\sphinxhyphen{}length paths and find shortest paths

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use OPTIONAL MATCH for left\sphinxhyphen{}join semantics

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Combine multiple MATCH clauses for cross\sphinxhyphen{}product queries

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Collect graph neighbours with pattern comprehensions

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Test list conditions with quantifier predicates and EXISTS subqueries

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Fold lists with REDUCE expressions

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Transform lists inline with list comprehensions

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Pass external values safely with query parameters

\end{itemize}


\subsection{Prerequisites}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/pattern_matching:prerequisites}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This tutorial assumes you have completed {\hyperref[\detokenize{tutorials/basic_query_parsing::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Basic Query Execution}}}} and
have a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Star}} instance ready.  All examples below use this setup:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{import}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pandas}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{as}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pd}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{Star}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{ingestion}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}

\PYG{n}{ctx} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{from\PYGZus{}dict}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}ID\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:}   \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{p1}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{p2}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{p3}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{p4}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:}     \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Alice}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Bob}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Carol}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Dave}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{age}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:}      \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{30}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{25}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{35}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{28}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{scores}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:}   \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{85}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{92}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{70}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{55}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{95}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{88}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{60}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{40}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Product}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}ID\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:}  \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{x1}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{x2}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{title}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:}   \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Widget}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Gadget}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{price}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:}   \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mf}{9.99}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mf}{49.99}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{KNOWS}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}SOURCE\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{p1}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{p2}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{p3}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}TARGET\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{p2}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{p3}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{p4}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{since}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:}      \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2020}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2021}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2019}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{BOUGHT}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}SOURCE\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{p1}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{p3}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}TARGET\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{x1}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{x2}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{star} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{ctx}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Relationship Patterns}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/pattern_matching:relationship-patterns}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The simplest graph query matches a directed edge between two nodes:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (a:Person)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(b:Person) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN a.name AS knower, b.name AS known}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Alice→Bob, Bob→Carol, Carol→Dave}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The relationship type is specified inside square brackets (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[}:KNOWS{]}}}).
You can also bind the relationship to a variable to access its properties:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (a:Person)\PYGZhy{}[r:KNOWS]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(b:Person) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN a.name AS from, b.name AS to, r.since AS since}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Inline property filters work on both nodes and relationships:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Only KNOWS edges from 2020 or later}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (a:Person)\PYGZhy{}[r:KNOWS }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}since: 2020\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(b:Person) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN a.name AS from, b.name AS to}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Inline node property filter}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (a:Person }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name: }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Alice}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZcb{})\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(b:Person) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN b.name AS friend}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Label Predicates in WHERE}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/pattern_matching:label-predicates-in-where}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
When you use an unlabeled node pattern (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MATCH (n)}}), PyCypher scans
all registered entity types.  \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WHERE n:Label}} filters to a specific type
at query time:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Filter to only Person nodes from a heterogeneous scan}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (n) WHERE n:Person RETURN n.name AS name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Negation — exclude a specific type}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (n) WHERE NOT n:Product RETURN n.name AS name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Combine label predicate with property filter}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (n) WHERE n:Person AND n.age \PYGZgt{} 30 RETURN n.name AS name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Label predicates compose with AND / OR / NOT like any other WHERE
predicate.  The compound form \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{n:Label1:Label2}} applies AND semantics —
the node must have both labels.


\subsection{Variable\sphinxhyphen{}Length Paths}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/pattern_matching:variable-length-paths}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Append \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{*min..max}} to a relationship pattern to match paths of variable
hop depth.  PyCypher executes these as a BFS traversal:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Any number of KNOWS hops (1 or more, capped internally)}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (a:Person)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS*]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(b:Person) RETURN a.name, b.name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Exactly 2–3 hops}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (a:Person)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS*2..3]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(b:Person) RETURN a.name, b.name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Up to 4 hops — find indirect connections}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (a:Person)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS*1..4]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(b:Person) RETURN a.name, b.name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{tip}{Tip:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Always prefer bounded hop limits (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{*1..3}}) over unbounded (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{*}}) on
large graphs.  Unbounded paths explore every reachable edge, and
execution time is proportional to the number of reachable edges.
\end{sphinxadmonition}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Use \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{length()}} on a named path variable to get the hop count:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH p = (a:Person)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS*1..4]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(b:Person) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN a.name, b.name, length(p) AS hops}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{shortestPath and allShortestPaths}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/pattern_matching:shortestpath-and-allshortestpaths}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{shortestPath()}} finds the minimum\sphinxhyphen{}hop route between two nodes.
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{allShortestPaths()}} returns every path tied for the minimum length:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Shortest route from Alice to Dave}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH (a:Person \PYGZob{}name: \PYGZsq{}Alice\PYGZsq{}\PYGZcb{}), (b:Person \PYGZob{}name: \PYGZsq{}Dave\PYGZsq{}\PYGZcb{})}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH p = shortestPath((a)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS*]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(b))}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    RETURN a.name AS start, b.name AS end, length(p) AS hops}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    \PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} One row: start=\PYGZsq{}Alice\PYGZsq{}, end=\PYGZsq{}Dave\PYGZsq{}, hops depends on graph structure}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} All shortest paths (every path at minimum hop count)}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH (a:Person \PYGZob{}name: \PYGZsq{}Alice\PYGZsq{}\PYGZcb{}), (b:Person \PYGZob{}name: \PYGZsq{}Dave\PYGZsq{}\PYGZcb{})}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH p = allShortestPaths((a)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS*]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(b))}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    RETURN a.name AS start, b.name AS end, length(p) AS hops}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    \PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
If no path exists between the two nodes, the result is empty (not an error).


\subsection{OPTIONAL MATCH — Left\sphinxhyphen{}Join Semantics}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/pattern_matching:optional-match-left-join-semantics}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{OPTIONAL MATCH}} preserves rows from the preceding \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MATCH}} even when
the optional pattern has no match.  Unmatched variables are bound to
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{null}} — just like a SQL LEFT JOIN:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Everyone is returned; product is null for those who bought nothing}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH (p:Person)}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    OPTIONAL MATCH (p)\PYGZhy{}[:BOUGHT]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(item:Product)}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    WITH p.name AS person, item.title AS product}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    RETURN person, product}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    \PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Find people who have NOT bought anything}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH (p:Person)}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    OPTIONAL MATCH (p)\PYGZhy{}[:BOUGHT]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(item:Product)}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    WITH p.name AS person, item.title AS product}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    WHERE product IS NULL}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    RETURN person}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    \PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Null\PYGZhy{}safe display with coalesce}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH (p:Person)}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    OPTIONAL MATCH (p)\PYGZhy{}[:BOUGHT]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(item:Product)}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    WITH p.name AS person, coalesce(item.title, \PYGZsq{}nothing\PYGZsq{}) AS product}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    RETURN person, product}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    \PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Cross\sphinxhyphen{}Product MATCH}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/pattern_matching:cross-product-match}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
When two MATCH patterns share no common variables, PyCypher produces the
Cartesian product of both scans (equivalent to SQL CROSS JOIN):

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} All (person, product) pairs}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person), (pr:Product) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN p.name AS person, pr.title AS product}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Self cross\PYGZhy{}join: all ordered (A, B) pairs where A != B}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person), (q:Person) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{WHERE p.name \PYGZlt{}\PYGZgt{} q.name }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN p.name AS pname, q.name AS qname}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Pattern Comprehensions}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/pattern_matching:pattern-comprehensions}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Pattern comprehensions collect graph neighbours into a per\sphinxhyphen{}row list
without a separate MATCH clause.  The syntax embeds a pattern inside
square brackets:

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[}(anchor)\sphinxhyphen{}{[}:REL{]}\sphinxhyphen{}>(target) WHERE condition | map\_expression{]}}}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Names of all people each person knows}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN p.name AS person, }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{       [(p)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(f) | f.name] AS friends}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Only older friends (filter with WHERE inside the comprehension)}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN p.name AS person, }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{       [(p)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(f:Person) WHERE f.age \PYGZgt{} p.age | f.name] AS older\PYGZus{}friends}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Pattern comprehensions are evaluated via a single \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.merge}} across all
anchor rows — they do not loop per row.


\subsection{EXISTS Subqueries}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/pattern_matching:exists-subqueries}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{EXISTS \{ MATCH ... WHERE ... \}}} tests whether an inner pattern produces
at least one result.  The subquery executes once for the entire outer
frame (batched), not once per row:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} People who know at least one other person}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) WHERE EXISTS }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ MATCH (p)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(q) \PYGZcb{} }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN p.name AS name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} NOT EXISTS — people who know nobody}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) WHERE NOT EXISTS }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ MATCH (p)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(q) \PYGZcb{} }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN p.name AS name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Filter the subquery match with WHERE}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{WHERE EXISTS }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ MATCH (p)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(q:Person) WHERE q.age \PYGZgt{} 30 \PYGZcb{} }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN p.name AS name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Quantifier Predicates}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/pattern_matching:quantifier-predicates}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Test whether a condition holds for some, all, none, or exactly one element
of a list:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} any() — at least one score above 90}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) WHERE any(s IN p.scores WHERE s \PYGZgt{} 90) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN p.name AS name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} all() — every score is non\PYGZhy{}negative (vacuously true for empty lists)}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) WHERE all(s IN p.scores WHERE s \PYGZgt{}= 0) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN p.name AS name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} none() — no score below 50}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) WHERE none(s IN p.scores WHERE s \PYGZlt{} 50) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN p.name AS name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} single() — exactly one score above 90}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) WHERE single(s IN p.scores WHERE s \PYGZgt{} 90) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN p.name AS name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{REDUCE Expressions}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/pattern_matching:reduce-expressions}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{reduce(accumulator = initial, variable IN list | step)}} folds a list into
a single value.  The accumulator carries the running result through each
element:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Sum of all scores for each person}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN p.name AS name, }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{       reduce(total = 0, s IN p.scores | total + s) AS score\PYGZus{}sum}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Alice: 177, Bob: 125, Carol: 243, Dave: 40}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Count elements that meet a condition (scores above 80)}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN p.name AS name, }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{       reduce(cnt = 0, s IN p.scores | }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{              CASE WHEN s \PYGZgt{} 80 THEN cnt + 1 ELSE cnt END) AS high\PYGZus{}scores}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Build a running maximum}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN p.name AS name, }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{       reduce(m = \PYGZhy{}1, s IN p.scores | }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{              CASE WHEN s \PYGZgt{} m THEN s ELSE m END) AS max\PYGZus{}score}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
REDUCE is the Cypher equivalent of Python’s \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{functools.reduce()}} — use it
when aggregation functions like \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{sum()}} or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{max()}} don’t cover your
use case.


\subsection{List Comprehensions}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/pattern_matching:list-comprehensions}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
List comprehensions transform each element of a list inline:

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[}variable IN list | map\_expression{]}}}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Double every score}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN p.name AS name, }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{       [s IN p.scores | s * 2] AS doubled}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Add a WHERE filter to keep only high scores}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN p.name AS name, }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{       [s IN p.scores WHERE s \PYGZgt{}= 80 | s] AS high\PYGZus{}scores}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Transform to strings}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN p.name AS name, }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{       [s IN p.scores | toString(s)] AS score\PYGZus{}strings}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
List comprehensions differ from \sphinxstyleemphasis{pattern} comprehensions (above) — they
operate on an existing list property rather than traversing graph edges.


\subsection{Query Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/pattern_matching:query-parameters}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\$name}} syntax to safely pass external values into queries without
string interpolation:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Parameterized lookup — safe from injection}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) WHERE p.name = \PYGZdl{}target RETURN p.age AS age}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{parameters}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{target}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Alice}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Multiple parameters}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) WHERE p.age \PYGZgt{}= \PYGZdl{}min\PYGZus{}age AND p.age \PYGZlt{}= \PYGZdl{}max\PYGZus{}age }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN p.name AS name, p.age AS age}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{parameters}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{min\PYGZus{}age}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{l+m+mi}{25}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{max\PYGZus{}age}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{l+m+mi}{32}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{warning}{Warning:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Never interpolate user input directly into query strings.  Always use
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{parameters=\{\}}} — see {\hyperref[\detokenize{tutorials/integration_guide::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Integration Guide}}}} for safety patterns.
\end{sphinxadmonition}


\subsection{Performance Tips}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/pattern_matching:performance-tips}}\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Push WHERE early.} Place selective filters immediately after MATCH
to reduce the number of rows before joins and projections.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Bound your paths.} Use \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{*1..3}} instead of \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{*}} to prevent
unbounded BFS traversals on large graphs.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Use inline property filters.} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MATCH (p:Person \{name: 'Alice'\})}}
filters during the scan phase, before any joins occur.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Prefer pattern comprehensions} over separate MATCH + collect() when
you need a per\sphinxhyphen{}row list of neighbours — they express the intent more
concisely and execute in a single merge pass.

\end{enumerate}

\end{itemize}

\section{Query Validation}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/query_validation:query-validation}}\label{\detokenize{tutorials/query_validation::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Learn how to validate openCypher queries before execution using PyCypher’s
two\sphinxhyphen{}level validation system: \sphinxstylestrong{syntax validation} (grammar parsing) and
\sphinxstylestrong{semantic validation} (variable scoping, aggregation rules, etc.).

\sphinxAtStartPar

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{tutorials/query_validation:id11}}{\hyperref[\detokenize{tutorials/query_validation:ast-level-validation}]{\sphinxcrossref{AST\sphinxhyphen{}Level Validation}}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{tutorials/query_validation:id12}}{\hyperref[\detokenize{tutorials/query_validation:runtime-exception-handling}]{\sphinxcrossref{Runtime Exception Handling}}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{tutorials/query_validation:id13}}{\hyperref[\detokenize{tutorials/query_validation:putting-it-together}]{\sphinxcrossref{Putting It Together}}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{tutorials/query_validation:id14}}{\hyperref[\detokenize{tutorials/query_validation:next-steps}]{\sphinxcrossref{Next Steps}}}

\end{itemize}
\end{sphinxcontents}


\subsection{Prerequisites}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/query_validation:prerequisites}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
PyCypher installed (see {\hyperref[\detokenize{getting_started::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Getting Started}}}})

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Familiarity with Cypher query syntax

\end{itemize}


\subsection{Overview}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/query_validation:overview}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
PyCypher provides two independent validation layers:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Syntax validation} — checks whether a query string is grammatically valid
Cypher via {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.grammar_parser.GrammarParser.validate}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{validate()}}}}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Semantic validation} — checks whether a syntactically valid query makes
\sphinxstyleemphasis{logical} sense (no undefined variables, correct aggregation usage, etc.)
via {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.semantic_validator.SemanticValidator}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SemanticValidator}}}}}.

\end{enumerate}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Both can be used \sphinxstyleemphasis{before} executing a query, allowing you to surface errors
early — especially useful in interactive tools or multi\sphinxhyphen{}tenant systems.


\subsection{Syntax Validation}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/query_validation:syntax-validation}}

\subsubsection{Quick Check}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/query_validation:quick-check}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.grammar_parser.GrammarParser.validate}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{validate()}}}}} method returns
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{True}} for valid Cypher and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{False}} for syntax errors without raising:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{grammar\PYGZus{}parser}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{GrammarParser}

\PYG{n}{parser} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{GrammarParser}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{n}{parser}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{validate}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (n:Person) RETURN n.name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} True}
\PYG{n}{parser}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{validate}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{METCH (n) RETRN n}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}               \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} False}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Catching Parse Errors}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/query_validation:catching-parse-errors}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
For richer diagnostics, parse the query directly and catch Lark exceptions:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{lark}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{exceptions}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{UnexpectedInput}

\PYG{k}{try}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n}{tree} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{parser}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{parse}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (n:Person RETURN n}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{except} \PYG{n}{UnexpectedInput} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n}{e}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Syntax error at line }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{e}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{line}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{, column }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{e}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{column}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{  }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{e}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Lark raises specific subclasses — \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{UnexpectedToken}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{UnexpectedCharacters}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{UnexpectedEOF}} — that you can catch individually for more targeted handling.


\subsection{Semantic Validation}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/query_validation:semantic-validation}}

\subsubsection{Basic Usage}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/query_validation:basic-usage}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.semantic_validator.SemanticValidator}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SemanticValidator}}}}} walks a parsed
Lark tree and reports issues:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{grammar\PYGZus{}parser}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{GrammarParser}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{semantic\PYGZus{}validator}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{SemanticValidator}

\PYG{n}{parser} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{GrammarParser}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{validator} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{SemanticValidator}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{n}{tree} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{parser}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{parse}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (n:Person) RETURN m}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{errors} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{validator}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{validate}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{tree}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{k}{for} \PYG{n}{error} \PYG{o+ow}{in} \PYG{n}{errors}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{error}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} ERROR: Variable \PYGZsq{}m\PYGZsq{} is used but not defined}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Error Severity Levels}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/query_validation:error-severity-levels}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Each {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.semantic_validator.ValidationError}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ValidationError}}}}} carries a severity:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{semantic\PYGZus{}validator}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{ErrorSeverity}

\PYG{k}{for} \PYG{n}{error} \PYG{o+ow}{in} \PYG{n}{errors}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{k}{if} \PYG{n}{error}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{severity} \PYG{o}{==} \PYG{n}{ErrorSeverity}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{ERROR}\PYG{p}{:}
        \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{BLOCK: }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{error}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{message}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{k}{elif} \PYG{n}{error}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{severity} \PYG{o}{==} \PYG{n}{ErrorSeverity}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{WARNING}\PYG{p}{:}
        \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{WARN:  }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{error}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{message}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{k}{elif} \PYG{n}{error}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{severity} \PYG{o}{==} \PYG{n}{ErrorSeverity}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{INFO}\PYG{p}{:}
        \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{INFO:  }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{error}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{message}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{ERROR} — the query will fail or produce wrong results

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{WARNING} — the query will work but may have issues

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{INFO} — suggestions for improvement

\end{itemize}


\subsubsection{What Gets Validated}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/query_validation:what-gets-validated}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The semantic validator checks:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Undefined variables} — using a variable that was never bound in a MATCH
or WITH clause

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Variable scope} — variables must be visible in the clause where they
are referenced

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Aggregation rules} — mixing aggregated and non\sphinxhyphen{}aggregated expressions
in RETURN without grouping

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Function signatures} — known function names and arity

\end{itemize}


\subsubsection{Convenience Wrapper}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/query_validation:convenience-wrapper}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
For the simplest use case, import the top\sphinxhyphen{}level \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{validate\_query()}}
helper:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{validate\PYGZus{}query}

\PYG{n}{errors} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{validate\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (n:Person) RETURN m}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{for} \PYG{n}{error} \PYG{o+ow}{in} \PYG{n}{errors}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{error}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{severity}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{value}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{: }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{error}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{message}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{AST\sphinxhyphen{}Level Validation}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/query_validation:ast-level-validation}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Beyond the Lark\sphinxhyphen{}tree semantic validator, the typed AST layer
({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.ast_models}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.ast\_models}}}}}) provides a {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.ValidationResult}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ValidationResult}}}}}
framework.  This is used internally but is also available for custom checks:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{ast\PYGZus{}models}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{ValidationResult}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{ValidationSeverity}

\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ValidationResult}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{result}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}error}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Missing RETURN clause}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{result}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}warning}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Unused variable }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{x}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{suggestion}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Remove or reference it}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{k}{if} \PYG{n}{result}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{is\PYGZus{}valid}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{No errors}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{else}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{k}{for} \PYG{n}{issue} \PYG{o+ow}{in} \PYG{n}{result}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{errors}\PYG{p}{:}
        \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{issue}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ValidationResult}} object provides filtered access:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{result.errors}} — error\sphinxhyphen{}level issues only

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{result.warnings}} — warning\sphinxhyphen{}level issues only

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{result.infos}} — informational issues only

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{result.is\_valid}} — \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{True}} if no errors (warnings are acceptable)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{result.has\_errors}} / \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{result.has\_warnings}} — boolean checks

\end{itemize}


\subsection{Runtime Exception Handling}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/query_validation:runtime-exception-handling}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
When validation is skipped or insufficient, PyCypher raises specific
exceptions at execution time.  These are designed to be actionable:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{VariableNotFoundError}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{UnsupportedFunctionError}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{GraphTypeNotFoundError}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{k}{try}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (n:Person) RETURN m.name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{except} \PYG{n}{VariableNotFoundError} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n}{e}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} e.variable\PYGZus{}name: the undefined variable}
    \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} e.available\PYGZus{}variables: what IS defined}
    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Unknown variable }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{e}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{variable\PYGZus{}name}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{  Available: }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{e}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{available\PYGZus{}variables}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{k}{try}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (n:Person) RETURN noSuchFunc(n)}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{except} \PYG{n}{UnsupportedFunctionError} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n}{e}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} e.function\PYGZus{}name: what was called}
    \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} e.supported\PYGZus{}functions: list of valid alternatives}
    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{No function }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{e}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{function\PYGZus{}name}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{k}{try}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (g:Ghost) RETURN g}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{except} \PYG{n}{GraphTypeNotFoundError} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n}{e}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} e.type\PYGZus{}name: the unknown entity label}
    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Entity type }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{e}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{type\PYGZus{}name}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ not in context}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
See the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher}} package docstring for the complete exception hierarchy.


\subsection{Putting It Together}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/query_validation:putting-it-together}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A robust validation pipeline might look like this:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{lark}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{exceptions}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{UnexpectedInput}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{grammar\PYGZus{}parser}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{GrammarParser}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{semantic\PYGZus{}validator}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{SemanticValidator}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{ErrorSeverity}

\PYG{k}{def}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nf}{validate\PYGZus{}before\PYGZus{}run}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{query}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n+nb}{str}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{o}{\PYGZgt{}} \PYG{n+nb}{list}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{n+nb}{str}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}Return a list of human\PYGZhy{}readable error messages, or [] if valid.\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{n}{parser} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{GrammarParser}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{n}{issues} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{p}{]}

    \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Step 1: syntax check}
    \PYG{k}{try}\PYG{p}{:}
        \PYG{n}{tree} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{parser}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{parse}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{query}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{k}{except} \PYG{n}{UnexpectedInput} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n}{e}\PYG{p}{:}
        \PYG{k}{return} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Syntax error at }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{e}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{line}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{:}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{e}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{column}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{: }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{e}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}

    \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Step 2: semantic check}
    \PYG{n}{validator} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{SemanticValidator}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{k}{for} \PYG{n}{error} \PYG{o+ow}{in} \PYG{n}{validator}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{validate}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{tree}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{:}
        \PYG{k}{if} \PYG{n}{error}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{severity} \PYG{o}{==} \PYG{n}{ErrorSeverity}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{ERROR}\PYG{p}{:}
            \PYG{n}{issues}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{append}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{error}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{message}\PYG{p}{)}

    \PYG{k}{return} \PYG{n}{issues}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Next Steps}

{\hyperref[\detokenize{tutorials/ast_manipulation::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{AST Manipulation}}}} — inspect and build AST nodes programmatically

{\hyperref[\detokenize{user_guide/query_processing::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Query Processing}}}} — detailed pipeline reference

\sphinxstepscope


\section{AST Manipulation}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/ast_manipulation:ast-manipulation}}\label{\detokenize{tutorials/ast_manipulation::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Learn how to create, modify, and traverse AST nodes programmatically using
PyCypher’s Pydantic\sphinxhyphen{}based model layer.

\begin{sphinxcontents}
\sphinxstylecontentstitle{In this tutorial}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
