\subsection{Prerequisites}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/troubleshooting:prerequisites}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
PyCypher installed (see {\hyperref[\detokenize{getting_started::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Getting Started}}}})

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
A working \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Star}} instance (see {\hyperref[\detokenize{tutorials/basic_query_parsing::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Basic Query Execution}}}})

\end{itemize}


\subsection{Diagnostic Tools}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/troubleshooting:diagnostic-tools}}

\subsubsection{Enable Debug Logging}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/troubleshooting:enable-debug-logging}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The single most useful diagnostic step:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{import}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{logging}
\PYG{n}{logging}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{getLogger}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{pycypher}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{setLevel}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{logging}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DEBUG}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
This prints parse timing, clause\sphinxhyphen{}by\sphinxhyphen{}clause execution, row counts, and
memory usage for every query.


\subsubsection{Validate Before Executing}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/troubleshooting:validate-before-executing}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Catch errors early without running the full query:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{validate\PYGZus{}query}

\PYG{n}{errors} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{validate\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (n:Person) RETURN m}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{for} \PYG{n}{e} \PYG{o+ow}{in} \PYG{n}{errors}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{e}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{severity}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{value}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{: }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{e}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{message}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} ERROR: Variable \PYGZsq{}m\PYGZsq{} is used but not defined}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Check Available Functions}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/troubleshooting:check-available-functions}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{available\PYGZus{}functions}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} [\PYGZsq{}abs\PYGZsq{}, \PYGZsq{}acos\PYGZsq{}, \PYGZsq{}asin\PYGZsq{}, ..., \PYGZsq{}valuetype\PYGZsq{}, \PYGZsq{}values\PYGZsq{}]}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Inspect the Parsed AST}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/troubleshooting:inspect-the-parsed-ast}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{ast\PYGZus{}models}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{ASTConverter}

\PYG{n}{ast} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ASTConverter}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{from\PYGZus{}cypher}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) RETURN p.name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{for} \PYG{n}{clause} \PYG{o+ow}{in} \PYG{n}{ast}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{clauses}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n+nb}{type}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{clause}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n+nv+vm}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}name\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{clause}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Query Metrics}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/troubleshooting:query-metrics}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{shared}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{metrics}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{QUERY\PYGZus{}METRICS}

\PYG{n}{stats} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{QUERY\PYGZus{}METRICS}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{snapshot}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Queries: }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{stats}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{total\PYGZus{}queries}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{, Errors: }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{stats}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{error\PYGZus{}rate}\PYG{l+s+si}{:}\PYG{l+s+s2}{.1\PYGZpc{}}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{p50: }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{stats}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{timing\PYGZus{}p50\PYGZus{}ms}\PYG{l+s+si}{:}\PYG{l+s+s2}{.0f}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ms, p99: }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{stats}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{timing\PYGZus{}p99\PYGZus{}ms}\PYG{l+s+si}{:}\PYG{l+s+s2}{.0f}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ms}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Parse Errors}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/troubleshooting:parse-errors}}

\subsubsection{“Syntax error” or UnexpectedInput}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/troubleshooting:syntax-error-or-unexpectedinput}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Symptom}: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{lark.exceptions.UnexpectedInput}} when parsing.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Common causes}:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Missing closing parenthesis}:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
MATCH (p:Person WHERE p.age \PYGZgt{} 30 RETURN p.name
\PYGZsh{}                 \PYGZca{} missing closing )
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Missing relationship direction arrow}:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
MATCH (a)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS]\PYGZhy{}(b)    \PYGZsh{} undirected — OK
MATCH (a)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS](b)     \PYGZsh{} missing arrow — ERROR
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Using SQL keywords instead of Cypher}:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYGZsh{} SQL style (wrong)
SELECT p.name FROM Person p WHERE p.age \PYGZgt{} 30

\PYGZsh{} Cypher style (correct)
MATCH (p:Person) WHERE p.age \PYGZgt{} 30 RETURN p.name
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Unquoted strings in WHERE}:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
WHERE p.name = Alice     \PYGZsh{} ERROR — unquoted
WHERE p.name = \PYGZsq{}Alice\PYGZsq{}   \PYGZsh{} correct
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\end{enumerate}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Fix}: Use \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{GrammarParser.validate()}} for a quick syntax check:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{grammar\PYGZus{}parser}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{GrammarParser}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{GrammarParser}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{validate}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) RETURN p}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} True/False}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Missing or Empty Results}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/troubleshooting:missing-or-empty-results}}

\subsubsection{Query returns empty DataFrame}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/troubleshooting:query-returns-empty-dataframe}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Checklist}:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Entity type registered?} Check your ContextBuilder call:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} If your data uses \PYGZdq{}User\PYGZdq{} but your query says \PYGZdq{}Person\PYGZdq{}:}
\PYG{n}{MATCH} \PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{p}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{n}{Person}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} returns nothing}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Fix: use the same label}
\PYG{n}{MATCH} \PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{u}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{n}{User}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{IDs match between entities and relationships?}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Entity IDs are integers}
\PYG{n}{people} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}ID\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{3}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} But relationship sources are strings — no matches!}
\PYG{n}{knows} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}SOURCE\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{1}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{2}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}TARGET\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{2}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{3}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Fix: ensure consistent types}
\PYG{n}{knows}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}SOURCE\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{knows}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}SOURCE\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{astype}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n+nb}{int}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{knows}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}TARGET\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{knows}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}TARGET\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{astype}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n+nb}{int}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Relationship direction wrong?}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Data: Alice \PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{} Bob (source=Alice, target=Bob)}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Query: who does Bob know?}
\PYG{n}{MATCH} \PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{b}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{n}{Person} \PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{Bob}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{n}{KNOWS}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{o}{\PYGZgt{}}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{other}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Returns nothing because Bob is the TARGET, not SOURCE}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Fix: reverse the direction}
\PYG{n}{MATCH} \PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{other}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{n}{KNOWS}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{o}{\PYGZgt{}}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{b}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{n}{Person} \PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{Bob}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{WHERE filter too restrictive?} Remove the WHERE clause temporarily
to see if the MATCH alone produces results.

\end{enumerate}


\subsubsection{Property returns NULL unexpectedly}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/troubleshooting:property-returns-null-unexpectedly}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Cause}: Property name mismatch (case\sphinxhyphen{}sensitive).

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} DataFrame has \PYGZdq{}Name\PYGZdq{} but query uses \PYGZdq{}name\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{n}{MATCH} \PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{p}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{n}{Person}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{n}{RETURN} \PYG{n}{p}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{name}   \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} NULL — wrong case}
\PYG{n}{MATCH} \PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{p}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{n}{Person}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{n}{RETURN} \PYG{n}{p}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{Name}   \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} correct}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Fix}: Check your DataFrame column names:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{people\PYGZus{}df}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{columns}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{tolist}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} [\PYGZsq{}\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}ID\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}\PYGZsq{}, \PYGZsq{}Name\PYGZsq{}, \PYGZsq{}age\PYGZsq{}]  ← note capital N}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Unexpected extra rows}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/troubleshooting:unexpected-extra-rows}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Cause}: Usually a cross\sphinxhyphen{}join from unconnected MATCH patterns:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} This produces |Person| × |Product| rows (Cartesian product)}
\PYG{n}{MATCH} \PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{p}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{n}{Person}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{pr}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{n}{Product}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{n}{RETURN} \PYG{n}{p}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{pr}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{title}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Fix: connect them with a relationship}
\PYG{n}{MATCH} \PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{p}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{n}{Person}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{n}{BOUGHT}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{o}{\PYGZgt{}}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{pr}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{n}{Product}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{n}{RETURN} \PYG{n}{p}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{pr}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{title}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Performance Issues}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/troubleshooting:performance-issues}}

\subsubsection{Query is slow}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/troubleshooting:query-is-slow}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Step 1}: Enable debug logging to identify the slow clause.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Step 2}: Check for these common causes:


\begin{savenotes}\sphinxattablestart
\sphinxthistablewithglobalstyle
\centering
\begin{tabular}[t]{\X{40}{100}\X{60}{100}}
\sphinxtoprule
\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Pattern
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Fix
\\
\sphinxmidrule
\sphinxtableatstartofbodyhook
\sphinxAtStartPar
Unbounded path \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\sphinxhyphen{}{[}*{]}\sphinxhyphen{}>}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Add bounds: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\sphinxhyphen{}{[}*1..3{]}\sphinxhyphen{}>}}
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
Cross\sphinxhyphen{}join \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MATCH (a), (b)}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use relationship: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{(a)\sphinxhyphen{}{[}:REL{]}\sphinxhyphen{}>(b)}}
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
Wide RETURN with many properties
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Select only needed properties
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
Missing WHERE on large scan
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Add early filter: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WHERE p.active}}
\\
\sphinxbottomrule
\end{tabular}
\sphinxtableafterendhook\par
\sphinxattableend\end{savenotes}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Step 3}: Use the query profiler for detailed timing:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{query\PYGZus{}profiler}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{QueryProfiler}

\PYG{n}{profiler} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{QueryProfiler}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{star}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{report} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{profiler}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{profile}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS*]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(q) RETURN p.name, q.name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{report}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Hotspot: }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{report}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{hotspot}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Suggestions: }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{report}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{recommendations}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Step 4}: Set a timeout to prevent runaway queries:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{query}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{timeout\PYGZus{}seconds}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{5.0}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
See {\hyperref[\detokenize{user_guide/performance_tuning::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Performance Tuning}}}} for comprehensive tuning guidance.


\subsubsection{Memory usage is high}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/troubleshooting:memory-usage-is-high}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Cause}: Large intermediate results from cross\sphinxhyphen{}joins or unbounded paths.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Fix}:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Set a memory budget}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{query}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{memory\PYGZus{}budget\PYGZus{}bytes}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{500} \PYG{o}{*} \PYG{l+m+mi}{1024} \PYG{o}{*} \PYG{l+m+mi}{1024}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Or limit cross\PYGZhy{}join size globally}
\PYG{k+kn}{import}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{os}
\PYG{n}{os}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{environ}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}MAX\PYGZus{}CROSS\PYGZus{}JOIN\PYGZus{}ROWS}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{1000000}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Exception Reference}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/troubleshooting:exception-reference}}

\begin{savenotes}\sphinxattablestart
\sphinxthistablewithglobalstyle
\centering
\begin{tabular}[t]{\X{35}{100}\X{65}{100}}
\sphinxtoprule
\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Exception
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Meaning and fix
\\
\sphinxmidrule
\sphinxtableatstartofbodyhook
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{VariableNotFoundError}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Variable used but never bound in MATCH/WITH.
Check spelling and scope.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{UnsupportedFunctionError}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Function name not recognized.  Use
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{star.available\_functions()}} to see valid names.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{GraphTypeNotFoundError}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Entity label not registered in the context.
Check your ContextBuilder calls.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{QueryTimeoutError}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Query exceeded its time budget.  Simplify the query
or increase the timeout.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{QueryMemoryBudgetError}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Estimated memory exceeds the budget.  Add filters
or increase the budget.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{UnexpectedInput}} (Lark)
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Cypher syntax error.  Check parentheses, quotes,
and keyword spelling.
\\
\sphinxbottomrule
\end{tabular}
\sphinxtableafterendhook\par
\sphinxattableend\end{savenotes}


\subsection{Try It Yourself}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/troubleshooting:try-it-yourself}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Exercise}: The following query returns no results.  Find and fix the bug:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{people} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}ID\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{3}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Alice}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Bob}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Carol}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{knows} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}SOURCE\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{1}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{2}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}TARGET\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{2}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{3}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{context} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{from\PYGZus{}dict}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n}{people}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{KNOWS}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n}{knows}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{star} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (a:Person)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(b:Person) RETURN a.name, b.name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{result}\PYG{p}{)}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Empty!}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Next Steps}

\item {}

{\hyperref[\detokenize{tutorials/query_validation::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Query Validation}}}} — pre\sphinxhyphen{}execution validation

\section{Learning Pathway}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/index:learning-pathway}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The tutorials are organized into three tiers.  Start at your level and
work forward:

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Beginner} — Your first graph queries
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\setcounter{enumi}{-1}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{tutorials/cypher_for_sql_users::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Cypher for SQL Users}}}} — \sphinxstyleemphasis{Know SQL but not Cypher?}  Side\sphinxhyphen{}by\sphinxhyphen{}side
translation guide covering SELECT→RETURN, JOIN→relationships, GROUP BY,
subqueries, and more.  Read in 10 minutes.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{tutorials/basic_query_parsing::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Basic Query Execution}}}} — Load data, execute MATCH/WHERE/RETURN, sort
and aggregate results.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{tutorials/graph_modeling::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Graph Modeling}}}} — Design your tabular data as entities and
relationships.  Modeling patterns, ID strategies, common pitfalls.

\end{enumerate}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Intermediate} — Real\sphinxhyphen{}world graph analysis
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\setcounter{enumi}{2}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{tutorials/pattern_matching::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Pattern Matching}}}} — Variable\sphinxhyphen{}length paths, shortestPath, OPTIONAL
MATCH, pattern comprehensions, quantifier predicates, EXISTS subqueries.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{tutorials/query_validation::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Query Validation}}}} — Pre\sphinxhyphen{}execution syntax and semantic validation.
Error severity levels, custom validation pipelines.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{tutorials/ast_manipulation::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{AST Manipulation}}}} — Programmatic AST inspection, traversal, and
construction using Pydantic models.

\end{enumerate}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Advanced} — Production systems
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\setcounter{enumi}{5}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{tutorials/data_etl_pipeline::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Data ETL Pipeline}}}} — ContextBuilder, YAML pipelines, the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{nmetl}}
CLI, data sources, and output sinks.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{tutorials/integration_guide::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Integration Guide}}}} — Embed PyCypher in web APIs, batch jobs, and
notebooks.  Query parameters, error handling, context refresh patterns.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{tutorials/production_deployment::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Production Deployment Patterns}}}} — Backend selection, rate limiting, audit
logging, timeouts, and caching for production workloads.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{tutorials/troubleshooting::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Troubleshooting Queries}}}} — Diagnose parse errors, missing results,
performance problems, and unexpected output.

\end{enumerate}

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{tip}{Tip:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Each tutorial builds on earlier ones.  The beginner tutorials establish
the vocabulary and patterns that intermediate and advanced tutorials
assume.
\end{sphinxadmonition}


\section{Prerequisites}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/index:prerequisites}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Python 3.14 or higher (free\sphinxhyphen{}threaded build recommended)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
PyCypher packages installed (see {\hyperref[\detokenize{getting_started::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Getting Started}}}})

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Basic familiarity with pandas DataFrames

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Each tutorial includes:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Clear learning objectives

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Complete, runnable code examples

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
“Try it yourself” exercises

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Common pitfalls and solutions

\end{itemize}


\section{Related Resources}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/index:related-resources}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{getting_started::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Getting Started}}}} — Installation and quick\sphinxhyphen{}start examples

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{user_guide/index::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{User Guide}}}} — In\sphinxhyphen{}depth reference guides

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar

\item {}

\end{itemize}


\subsection{Example Scripts}
\label{\detokenize{tutorials/index:example-scripts}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{examples/}} directory contains runnable scripts that complement these
tutorials.  Run any script with \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{uv run python examples/<script>.py}}:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{quickstart.py}} — Minimal end\sphinxhyphen{}to\sphinxhyphen{}end query (pairs with {\hyperref[\detokenize{tutorials/basic_query_parsing::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Basic Query Execution}}}})

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{advanced\_grammar\_examples.py}} — Complex Cypher patterns including
variable\sphinxhyphen{}length paths, comprehensions, and REDUCE (pairs with {\hyperref[\detokenize{tutorials/pattern_matching::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Pattern Matching}}}})

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{functions\_in\_where.py}} — Using scalar functions inside WHERE predicates

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{scalar\_functions\_in\_with.py}} — Transforming data with functions in WITH clauses

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ast\_conversion\_example.py}} — Programmatic AST inspection and traversal
(pairs with {\hyperref[\detokenize{tutorials/ast_manipulation::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{AST Manipulation}}}})

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{multi\_query\_composition.py}} — Multi\sphinxhyphen{}query ETL pipelines with cross\sphinxhyphen{}query
dependencies (pairs with {\hyperref[\detokenize{tutorials/data_etl_pipeline::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Data ETL Pipeline}}}})

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{backend\_selection.py}} — Choosing between pandas, DuckDB, and Polars
backends (pairs with {\hyperref[\detokenize{tutorials/production_deployment::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Production Deployment Patterns}}}})

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{production\_patterns.py}} — Rate limiting, audit logging, timeouts, and
caching for production workloads (pairs with {\hyperref[\detokenize{tutorials/production_deployment::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Production Deployment Patterns}}}})

\end{itemize}

\sphinxstepscope


\chapter{API Reference}
\label{\detokenize{api/index:api-reference}}\label{\detokenize{api/index::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Complete API reference for all PyCypher packages.  Each module’s public
classes, functions, and constants are documented with type signatures,
parameter descriptions, return values, and usage examples extracted from
source docstrings.

\sphinxstepscope


\section{PyCypher API}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher-api}}\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The PyCypher package provides comprehensive openCypher query parsing, AST
processing, and BindingFrame\sphinxhyphen{}based query execution against in\sphinxhyphen{}memory
DataFrames.


\subsection{Quick Start}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:quick-start}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{import}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pandas}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{as}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pd}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{Star}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Build a graph context from DataFrames}
\PYG{n}{people} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}ID\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{3}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Alice}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Bob}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Carol}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{age}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{30}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{25}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{35}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{context} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}entity}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{people}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{build}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Execute a Cypher query}
\PYG{n}{star} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) WHERE p.age \PYGZgt{} 28 RETURN p.name, p.age ORDER BY p.age}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{result}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Pre\PYGZhy{}execution validation}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{validate\PYGZus{}query}

\PYG{n}{errors} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{validate\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (n:Person) RETURN m}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{for} \PYG{n}{error} \PYG{o+ow}{in} \PYG{n}{errors}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{error}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{severity}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{value}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{: }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{error}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{message}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Backend selection (auto, pandas, duckdb, polars)}
\PYG{n}{context} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}entity}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{people}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{build}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{backend}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{auto}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{backend\PYGZus{}name}\PYG{p}{)}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} \PYGZdq{}pandas\PYGZdq{}, \PYGZdq{}duckdb\PYGZdq{}, or \PYGZdq{}polars\PYGZdq{}}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Core Modules}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:core-modules}}

\subsubsection{AST Models}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:ast-models}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Pydantic\sphinxhyphen{}based AST node definitions for the openCypher grammar.  Every
parsed query is represented as a tree of these immutable model classes.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.ast\_models@\spxentry{pycypher.ast\_models}}\index{pycypher.ast\_models@\spxentry{pycypher.ast\_models}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.ast_models}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Pydantic models for openCypher AST nodes.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This package provides strongly\sphinxhyphen{}typed Pydantic models for the Abstract Syntax Tree
generated by the grammar parser. It includes utilities for converting dict\sphinxhyphen{}based
ASTs to typed models, traversing, modifying, and pretty\sphinxhyphen{}printing the AST.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Important Notes:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
All variable references are represented as Variable instances (not strings)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
This includes variables in patterns (NodePattern, RelationshipPattern),
comprehensions (ListComprehension, PatternComprehension), quantifiers,
and all other binding contexts

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The ASTConverter automatically wraps string variable names in Variable
instances during conversion

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Usage:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{grammar\PYGZus{}parser}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{GrammarParser}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{ast\PYGZus{}models}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{ASTConverter}

\PYG{n}{parser} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{GrammarParser}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{parse\PYGZus{}tree} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{parser}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{parse}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (n:Person) RETURN n}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{raw\PYGZus{}ast} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{parser}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{transformer}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{transform}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{parse\PYGZus{}tree}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{n}{converter} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ASTConverter}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{typed\PYGZus{}ast} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{converter}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{convert}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{raw\PYGZus{}ast}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Traverse the AST}
\PYG{k}{for} \PYG{n}{node} \PYG{o+ow}{in} \PYG{n}{typed\PYGZus{}ast}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{traverse}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Node type: }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{node}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n+nv+vm}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}class\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n+nv+vm}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}name\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Pretty print}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{typed\PYGZus{}ast}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{pretty}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Access variable names}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{ast\PYGZus{}models}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{NodePattern}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{Variable}
\PYG{n}{node} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{NodePattern}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{variable}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{Variable}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{n}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{labels}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{)}

\begin{itemize}

\index{cypher\_validate() (pycypher.ast\_models.ASTNode method)@\spxentry{cypher\_validate()}\spxextra{pycypher.ast\_models.ASTNode method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.ASTNode.cypher_validate}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{cypher\_validate}}}
{}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Validate this AST node and return any issues found.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Runs a suite of validators to check for:
\sphinxhyphen{} Undefined variable references
\sphinxhyphen{} Unused variables
\sphinxhyphen{} Missing labels (performance issues)
\sphinxhyphen{} Unreachable conditions
\sphinxhyphen{} Type mismatches
\sphinxhyphen{} And other common query anti\sphinxhyphen{}patterns
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
ValidationResult containing all issues found

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.ValidationResult}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleemphasis{ValidationResult}}}}

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Example}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{query}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{cypher\PYGZus{}validate}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k}{if} \PYG{o+ow}{not} \PYG{n}{result}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{is\PYGZus{}valid}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{result}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\end{fulllineitems}

the query execution plan.

\index{random\_hash() (in module pycypher.ast\_models)@\spxentry{random\_hash()}\spxextra{in module pycypher.ast\_models}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.random_hash}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.ast\_models.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{random\_hash}}}
{}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Generate a cryptographically secure random hash string for column naming.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Creates a unique identifier using cryptographically secure random bytes
and SHA256 hashing. Used to generate collision\sphinxhyphen{}resistant column names
during algebraic operations.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A 64\sphinxhyphen{}character hexadecimal hash string (SHA256 digest).

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}

\end{description}\end{quote}
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Security:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Uses secrets.token\_bytes() for cryptographically secure randomness

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Uses SHA256 instead of broken MD5 hash function

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
32 bytes of entropy provide 2\textasciicircum{}256 possible values

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\end{fulllineitems}

FOREACH clause – iterative list mutation.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Iterates over every element of \sphinxstyleemphasis{list\_expression}, binds it to \sphinxstyleemphasis{variable},
and executes the inner \sphinxstyleemphasis{clauses} (SET, CREATE, MERGE, DELETE, REMOVE) for
each element.  This is a side\sphinxhyphen{}effect\sphinxhyphen{}only construct; \sphinxstyleemphasis{variable} does not
escape into the outer query scope.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

MATCH clause – binds graph patterns to the binding table.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Searches the graph for subgraphs matching \sphinxstyleemphasis{pattern} and adds one row per
match. When \sphinxstyleemphasis{optional} is True (OPTIONAL MATCH), rows with no match are
retained with NULL\sphinxhyphen{}filled columns. An optional \sphinxstyleemphasis{where} expression filters
matches before they enter the binding table.

\end{description}\end{quote}

MERGE clause – creates a pattern only if it does not already exist.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Performs an upsert: if \sphinxstyleemphasis{pattern} matches existing data, the match is bound;
otherwise the pattern is created. Optional \sphinxstyleemphasis{on\_create} and \sphinxstyleemphasis{on\_match} SET
actions run conditionally depending on whether a new entity was created.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Contains the ordered list of clauses that make up the query pipeline
(e.g., MATCH \sphinxhyphen{}> WHERE \sphinxhyphen{}> WITH \sphinxhyphen{}> RETURN). Clauses are executed sequentially,
with each clause consuming and producing a binding table.

RETURN clause – specifies the query output.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Projects expressions from the binding table into the result set, analogous
to SQL SELECT. Supports DISTINCT deduplication, ORDER BY sorting, and
SKIP/LIMIT pagination.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{all\_flags}} is a parallel list of booleans – \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{all\_flags{[}i{]}}} is
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{True}} when the join between \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{statements{[}i{]}}} and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{statements{[}i+1{]}}}
uses \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{UNION ALL}} (no deduplication).

\sphinxAtStartPar
WITH clause – intermediate projection and scope boundary.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Acts as a pipeline stage between clauses, projecting selected expressions
into the downstream binding table. Supports the same modifiers as RETURN
(DISTINCT, ORDER BY, SKIP, LIMIT) plus an optional WHERE filter. Variables
not projected by WITH are not visible to subsequent clauses.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
CASE expression – conditional value selection.

\sphinxAtStartPar
When \sphinxstyleemphasis{expression} is set (simple form), each WHEN condition is compared to it
for equality. When \sphinxstyleemphasis{expression} is None (searched form), each WHEN condition
is evaluated as a boolean. The first matching WHEN returns its result;
\sphinxstyleemphasis{else\_expr} provides a fallback (NULL if absent).
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

\subsubsection*{Example}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} [x IN [1,2,3] WHERE x \PYGZgt{} 1 | x * 2]}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{comp} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ListComprehension}\PYG{p}{(}

\subsubsection*{Example}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} person\PYGZob{}.name, .age, adult: person.age \PYGZgt{}= 18\PYGZcb{}}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{proj} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{MapProjection}\PYG{p}{(}

\subsubsection*{Example}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} [p = (a)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(b) WHERE b.age \PYGZgt{} 30 | b.name]}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{comp} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{PatternComprehension}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{variable}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{Variable}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{p}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{pattern}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{Pattern}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]

\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{all\PYGZus{}expr} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Quantifier}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{quantifier}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ALL}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}

\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{list\PYGZus{}expr}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{ListLiteral}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{value}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{l+m+mi}{3}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{where}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{Comparison}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{operator}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZgt{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{left}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{Variable}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{x}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{right}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{IntegerLiteral}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{value}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{0}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }\PYG{p}{)}

\subsubsection*{Example}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} REDUCE(sum = 0, x IN [1,2,3] | sum + x)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{reduce\PYGZus{}expr} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Reduce}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{accumulator}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{Variable}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{sum}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{initial}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{IntegerLiteral}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{value}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{0}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{variable}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{Variable}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{x}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{list\PYGZus{}expr}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{ListLiteral}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{value}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{l+m+mi}{3}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{map\PYGZus{}expr}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{Arithmetic}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{operator}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{+}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{left}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{Variable}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{sum}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{right}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{Variable}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{x}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Variable reference in Cypher queries.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Represents a variable name used in expressions, patterns, and bindings.
Variables are used throughout the AST to reference nodes, relationships,
paths, and other values.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}
\index{name (pycypher.ast\_models.Variable attribute)@\spxentry{name}\spxextra{pycypher.ast\_models.Variable attribute}}

The variable name (e.g., ‘n’, ‘person’, ‘rel’)
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}


\begin{sphinxadmonition}{note}{Note:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
As of the latest refactoring, all variable references in the AST
(including in patterns, comprehensions, and other binding contexts)
are represented as Variable instances, not plain strings. This ensures
consistency throughout the AST structure.
\end{sphinxadmonition}

\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{typed\PYGZus{}ast} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{convert\PYGZus{}ast}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{raw\PYGZus{}ast}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{typed\PYGZus{}ast}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{pretty}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}

\index{extract\_referenced\_variables() (in module pycypher.ast\_models)@\spxentry{extract\_referenced\_variables()}\spxextra{in module pycypher.ast\_models}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.extract_referenced_variables}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.ast\_models.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{extract\_referenced\_variables}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{node}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Extract all variable names referenced in an AST expression.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Uses the AST’s built\sphinxhyphen{}in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{find\_all(Variable)}} visitor to collect variable
names.  This traversal is complete (handles all AST node types) and
depth\sphinxhyphen{}limited (raises \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SecurityError}} if nesting exceeds the
configured maximum).

\sphinxAtStartPar
For \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PropertyLookup}} nodes (e.g. \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{p.name}}), extracts the base variable
name (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{p}}).

\sphinxAtStartPar
This is the \sphinxstylestrong{canonical} variable extraction function – all call sites
that need variable names from expression ASTs should use this function
rather than implementing ad\sphinxhyphen{}hoc traversal.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{node}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.ASTNode}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ASTNode}}}}}) – An AST node (typically a WHERE predicate or expression).

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Set of variable name strings.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#set}{set}{[}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}{]}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Validate an AST for common issues and anti\sphinxhyphen{}patterns.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This function performs comprehensive validation including:
\sphinxhyphen{} Undefined variable detection
\sphinxhyphen{} Unreachable code detection
\sphinxhyphen{} Performance anti\sphinxhyphen{}patterns
\sphinxhyphen{} Type consistency checks

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{node}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.ASTNode}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ASTNode}}}}}) – Root AST node to validate

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{strict}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}}) – If True, treat warnings as errors

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
ValidationResult containing all issues found

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.ValidationResult}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleemphasis{ValidationResult}}}}

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Example}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{validate\PYGZus{}ast}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{typed\PYGZus{}ast}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k}{if} \PYG{o+ow}{not} \PYG{n}{result}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{is\PYGZus{}valid}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{result}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{k}{for} \PYG{n}{error} \PYG{o+ow}{in} \PYG{n}{result}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{errors}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }        \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Error: }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{error}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{message}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\end{fulllineitems}



\subsubsection{Grammar Parser}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:grammar-parser}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Lark\sphinxhyphen{}based Cypher parser with LRU caching of parsed grammars and AST trees.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.grammar\_parser@\spxentry{pycypher.grammar\_parser}}\index{pycypher.grammar\_parser@\spxentry{pycypher.grammar\_parser}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.grammar_parser}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
openCypher Grammar Parser using Lark.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This module implements a parser for the openCypher query language based on the
grammar specification in grammar.bnf. It uses the Lark parsing library to parse
Cypher queries into abstract syntax trees (ASTs).

\sphinxAtStartPar
The parser uses a CompositeTransformer architecture with specialized transformers
(LiteralTransformer, ExpressionTransformer, PatternTransformer, StatementTransformer)
that follow the Single Responsibility Principle, replacing the previous monolithic
CypherASTTransformer design.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This is an alternative implementation to cypher\_parser.py, providing a more
comprehensive grammar coverage directly based on the BNF specification.
\subsubsection*{Example}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{grammar\PYGZus{}parser}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{GrammarParser}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{parser} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{GrammarParser}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{query} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (n:Person }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name: }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Alice}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZcb{}) RETURN n}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{tree} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{parser}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{parse}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{query}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{tree}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{pretty}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{ast} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{parser}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{parse\PYGZus{}to\PYGZus{}ast}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{query}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Parse a Cypher query into an AST.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Results are cached by query string so that repeated parsing of the
same query (common in ETL pipelines) returns instantly.

\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar

Dict

{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.exceptions.CypherSyntaxError}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{CypherSyntaxError}}}}} – If the query has syntax errors.

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{validate() (pycypher.grammar\_parser.GrammarParser method)@\spxentry{validate()}\spxextra{pycypher.grammar\_parser.GrammarParser method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.grammar_parser.GrammarParser.validate}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{validate}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{query}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Validate a Cypher query without returning the parse tree.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Returns \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{True}} when \sphinxstyleemphasis{query} is syntactically valid Cypher.
Returns \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{False}} only for genuine Lark parse errors
(\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{UnexpectedInput}} and its subclasses:
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{UnexpectedToken}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{UnexpectedCharacters}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{UnexpectedEOF}}).

\sphinxAtStartPar
Any \sphinxstyleemphasis{other} exception (e.g. a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{VisitError}}
wrapping an internal transformer bug, or a plain
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#AttributeError}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{AttributeError}}}) is \sphinxstylestrong{not} caught here and propagates to the
caller.  This ensures that internal bugs surface immediately rather
than being silently masked as “invalid query” responses.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{query}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – The Cypher query string to validate.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
True if the query is syntactically valid, False if it
contains a genuine parse error.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{bool}

\sphinxlineitem{Raises}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{lark.exceptions.VisitError}} – If the grammar transformer raises
    an internal error while processing the parse tree.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#Exception}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Exception}}} – Any other non\sphinxhyphen{}parse exception raised during parsing.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}

\index{get\_default\_parser() (in module pycypher.grammar\_parser)@\spxentry{get\_default\_parser()}\spxextra{in module pycypher.grammar\_parser}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.grammar_parser.get_default_parser}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.grammar\_parser.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{get\_default\_parser}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{keyword-only-separator}{\DUrole{o}{\sphinxstyleabbreviation{*}}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{debug}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{False}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Return a cached \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{GrammarParser}} instance.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The Lark grammar compilation step inside \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{GrammarParser.\_\_init\_\_}} is
expensive (\textasciitilde{}0.175 s per call).  This factory builds the parser exactly
once per unique \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{debug}} flag value and returns the same object on every
subsequent call, eliminating per\sphinxhyphen{}query re\sphinxhyphen{}instantiation overhead.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{debug}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}}) – Passed through to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{GrammarParser.\_\_init\_\_}}.  Separate
instances are cached for \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{debug=True}} and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{debug=False}}.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A fully\sphinxhyphen{}initialised, reusable parser instance.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.grammar_parser.GrammarParser}]{\sphinxcrossref{GrammarParser}}}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}



\subsubsection{Grammar Transformers}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:grammar-transformers}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Visitor classes that convert Lark parse trees into PyCypher AST models.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.grammar\_transformers@\spxentry{pycypher.grammar\_transformers}}\index{pycypher.grammar\_transformers@\spxentry{pycypher.grammar\_transformers}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.grammar_transformers}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Specialized AST transformers for openCypher grammar parsing.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This module contains focused transformer classes that replace the monolithic
CypherASTTransformer with specialized, single\sphinxhyphen{}responsibility transformers.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Each transformer inherits from a grammar\sphinxhyphen{}rule mixin that provides the
production implementations of all transformer methods for its domain:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
LiteralTransformer: Handles literal values (strings, numbers, booleans, lists, maps)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
ExpressionTransformer: Handles expressions (arithmetic, logical, comparisons, predicates)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
FunctionTransformer: Handles function calls, CASE, comprehensions, reduce, quantifiers

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
PatternTransformer: Handles graph patterns (nodes, relationships, paths)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
StatementTransformer: Handles Cypher statements and clauses (MATCH, RETURN, etc.)

\end{itemize}

string literals (with escape\sphinxhyphen{}sequence processing), boolean literals,
null, lists, maps, parameters, and variable names.

\index{CompositeTransformer (class in pycypher.grammar\_transformers)@\spxentry{CompositeTransformer}\spxextra{class in pycypher.grammar\_transformers}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.grammar_transformers.CompositeTransformer}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.grammar\_transformers.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{CompositeTransformer}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Transformer}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Composite transformer that delegates to specialized transformers.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class maintains the same interface as the original monolithic
CypherASTTransformer but delegates method calls to focused, specialized
transformer instances. Each delegate inherits all production methods
from its corresponding grammar\sphinxhyphen{}rule mixin, so no fallback is needed.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Method resolution is cached on the instance after first lookup to avoid
repeated iteration through delegates. This also eliminates per\sphinxhyphen{}call
logging that caused Rich \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{OSError: bad file descriptor}} during
multi\sphinxhyphen{}threaded AST cache warmup, since Rich’s \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Console}} is not
thread\sphinxhyphen{}safe.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\subsubsection{Grammar Rule Mixins}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:grammar-rule-mixins}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Modular mixin classes that compose the grammar transformer.  Each mixin
handles a specific grammar rule group (expressions, clauses, patterns, etc.).
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.grammar\_rule\_mixins@\spxentry{pycypher.grammar\_rule\_mixins}}\index{pycypher.grammar\_rule\_mixins@\spxentry{pycypher.grammar\_rule\_mixins}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.grammar_rule_mixins}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Mixin classes for CypherASTTransformer rule groups.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Splits the monolithic 178\sphinxhyphen{}method CypherASTTransformer into focused,
composable mixin classes.  Each mixin groups methods for a related set
of grammar rules.  \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{CypherASTTransformer}} inherits from all mixins,
preserving Lark’s method\sphinxhyphen{}name\sphinxhyphen{}based visitor pattern via Python MRO.


\paragraph{Architecture}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:architecture}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
LiteralRulesMixin          — number, string, boolean, null, list, map, parameter
ExpressionRulesMixin       — operators, boolean/comparison/arithmetic/string/null expressions
FunctionRulesMixin         — function invocation, CASE, list/pattern comprehension, reduce, quantifiers, map projection
PatternRulesMixin          — node/relationship patterns, labels, properties, path lengths
ClauseRulesMixin           — MATCH, RETURN, WITH, SET, DELETE, CREATE, MERGE, UNION, ORDER BY, etc.
┆
CypherASTTransformer(ClauseRulesMixin, PatternRulesMixin, FunctionRulesMixin, ExpressionRulesMixin, LiteralRulesMixin, Transformer)
    └── grammar string + GrammarParser (grammar\PYGZus{}parser.py)
\end{sphinxVerbatim}
\index{ClauseRulesMixin (class in pycypher.grammar\_rule\_mixins)@\spxentry{ClauseRulesMixin}\spxextra{class in pycypher.grammar\_rule\_mixins}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.grammar_rule_mixins.ClauseRulesMixin}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.grammar\_rule\_mixins.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{ClauseRulesMixin}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Grammar rule methods for Cypher clauses and statement structure.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Handles:
\sphinxhyphen{} Top\sphinxhyphen{}level query structure (cypher\_query, statement\_list, query\_statement)
\sphinxhyphen{} UNION operations
\sphinxhyphen{} Read clauses (MATCH, OPTIONAL MATCH, UNWIND)
\sphinxhyphen{} Write clauses (CREATE, MERGE, DELETE, SET, REMOVE, FOREACH)
\sphinxhyphen{} CALL/YIELD for procedure invocation
\sphinxhyphen{} RETURN and WITH clauses (items, aliases, DISTINCT)
\sphinxhyphen{} WHERE clause
\sphinxhyphen{} ORDER BY, SKIP, LIMIT modifiers

\index{ExpressionRulesMixin (class in pycypher.grammar\_rule\_mixins)@\spxentry{ExpressionRulesMixin}\spxextra{class in pycypher.grammar\_rule\_mixins}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.grammar_rule_mixins.ExpressionRulesMixin}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.grammar\_rule\_mixins.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{ExpressionRulesMixin}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Grammar rule methods for expressions, operators, and predicates.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Handles:
\sphinxhyphen{} Operator keywords (add\_op, mult\_op, pow\_op, unary\_op)
\sphinxhyphen{} Boolean expressions (or, xor, and, not)
\sphinxhyphen{} Comparison expressions
\sphinxhyphen{} Arithmetic expressions (add, mult, power, unary)
\sphinxhyphen{} String predicate expressions (STARTS WITH, ENDS WITH, CONTAINS, IN, =\textasciitilde{})
\sphinxhyphen{} Null predicate expressions (IS NULL, IS NOT NULL)
\sphinxhyphen{} Label predicate expressions
\sphinxhyphen{} Postfix expressions (property lookup, index, slicing)
\sphinxhyphen{} Special expressions (count(*), EXISTS, inline pattern predicates)

\sphinxAtStartPar
Note: Uses \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Token}} from lark for string\_predicate\_op processing.
\index{add\_expression() (pycypher.grammar\_rule\_mixins.ExpressionRulesMixin method)@\spxentry{add\_expression()}\spxextra{pycypher.grammar\_rule\_mixins.ExpressionRulesMixin method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.grammar_rule_mixins.ExpressionRulesMixin.add_expression}}

{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{add\_expression}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{args}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Transform addition and subtraction arithmetic expressions.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Addition and subtraction have equal precedence and associate left\sphinxhyphen{}to\sphinxhyphen{}right.
This method builds a left\sphinxhyphen{}associative tree of Arithmetic nodes.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{args}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{[}}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Any}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{]}}}) – Alternating operands and operators {[}left, op1, right1, op2, right2, …{]}.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Single operand unchanged, or nested dict nodes with type “Arithmetic”
containing operator (“+” or “\sphinxhyphen{}“), left operand, and right operand.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleemphasis{Any}} | \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{dict}{[}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}, \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleemphasis{Any}}{]}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Transform AND boolean expression with short\sphinxhyphen{}circuit evaluation.

\sphinxAtStartPar
AND has higher precedence than OR/XOR. Multiple ANDs are collected into
a single node for easier optimization and execution planning.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{args}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{[}}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Any}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{]}}}) – One or more NOT expression operands.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Single operand, or dict with type “And” containing all operands.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleemphasis{Any}} | \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{dict}{[}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}, \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleemphasis{Any}}{]}

\index{comparison\_expression() (pycypher.grammar\_rule\_mixins.ExpressionRulesMixin method)@\spxentry{comparison\_expression()}\spxextra{pycypher.grammar\_rule\_mixins.ExpressionRulesMixin method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.grammar_rule_mixins.ExpressionRulesMixin.comparison_expression}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{comparison\_expression}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{args}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Transform comparison expression with operators like =, <>, <, >, <=, >=.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Comparison expressions allow comparing values for equality or ordering.
Multiple comparisons can be chained (e.g., a < b < c), which is transformed
into nested comparison nodes. This structure is necessary for type checking
and ensuring all operands are comparable.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Single operands pass through to avoid unnecessary wrapping. This is necessary
for efficient AST traversal and avoiding deep nesting for simple expressions.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{args}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{[}}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Any}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{]}}}) – Alternating operands and operators {[}left, op1, right1, op2, right2, …{]}.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Single operand unchanged, or nested dict nodes with type “Comparison”
containing operator, left operand, and right operand for each comparison.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleemphasis{Any}} | \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{dict}{[}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}, \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleemphasis{Any}}{]}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Extract the content of an EXISTS subquery.

\sphinxAtStartPar
EXISTS content can be either:
1. A simple pattern with optional WHERE clause (implicit match)
2. A full query with MATCH/UNWIND/WITH clauses and optional RETURN
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{args}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{[}}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Any}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{]}}}) – Parsed subquery content (pattern or query clauses).

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The subquery content unchanged, or a wrapped ExistsSubquery dict
when the content is a full subquery with multiple clauses.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleemphasis{Any}} | None

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{inline\_pattern\_predicate() (pycypher.grammar\_rule\_mixins.ExpressionRulesMixin method)@\spxentry{inline\_pattern\_predicate()}\spxextra{pycypher.grammar\_rule\_mixins.ExpressionRulesMixin method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.grammar_rule_mixins.ExpressionRulesMixin.inline_pattern_predicate}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{inline\_pattern\_predicate}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{args}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Transform an inline pattern predicate into an EXISTS expression.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{(a)\sphinxhyphen{}{[}:R{]}\sphinxhyphen{}>(b)}} in a WHERE clause is shorthand for
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{EXISTS \{ (a)\sphinxhyphen{}{[}:R{]}\sphinxhyphen{}>(b) \}}}.  This transformer converts the parsed
alternating node/relationship args into the same \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Exists}} dict that
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{exists\_expression}} produces when given a simple pattern body.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Earley with \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ambiguity="explicit"}} can produce \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_ambig}} Tree nodes
when an anonymous node \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{()}} matches multiple grammar paths.  We
resolve each such node by transforming the first valid alternative so
that the result is always a plain dict.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{args}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{[}}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Any}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{]}}}) – Alternating node\_pattern and relationship\_pattern dicts (or
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_ambig}} Trees for anonymous nodes).  At least three elements
(node, rel, node) are guaranteed by the grammar \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{+}}.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Dict with \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{type="Exists"}} whose \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{content}} is a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Pattern}}
wrapping a single \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PathPattern}} \sphinxhyphen{}> \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PatternElement}}.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{dict}{[}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}, \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleemphasis{Any}}{]}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{label\_predicate\_expression() (pycypher.grammar\_rule\_mixins.ExpressionRulesMixin method)@\spxentry{label\_predicate\_expression()}\spxextra{pycypher.grammar\_rule\_mixins.ExpressionRulesMixin method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.grammar_rule_mixins.ExpressionRulesMixin.label_predicate_expression}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{label\_predicate\_expression}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{args}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Transform label predicate expression (n:Label or n:Label1:Label2).

\sphinxAtStartPar
A label predicate checks whether the node bound to a variable has a
specific label.  \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{n:Person}} is true when \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{n}} is a Person node.
Multiple colon\sphinxhyphen{}separated labels (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{n:Person:Employee}}) mean the node
must have ALL listed labels (AND semantics).

\sphinxAtStartPar
When no labels follow the expression, this rule is transparent (passes
through unchanged).
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{args}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{[}}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Any}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{]}}}) – {[}expression{]} or {[}expression, label\_name1, label\_name2, …{]}.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Expression unchanged if no labels, or dict with type
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"LabelPredicate"}} containing \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{operand}} and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{labels}}.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleemphasis{Any}} | \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{dict}{[}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}, \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleemphasis{Any}}{]}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

Transform multiplication, division, and modulo arithmetic expressions.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Multiplication, division, and modulo have equal precedence, higher than
addition/subtraction, and associate left\sphinxhyphen{}to\sphinxhyphen{}right.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{args}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{[}}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Any}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{]}}}) – Alternating operands and operators {[}left, op1, right1, op2, right2, …{]}.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Single operand unchanged, or nested dict nodes with type “Arithmetic”
containing operator (“*”, “/”, or “\%”), left operand, and right operand.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleemphasis{Any}} | \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{dict}{[}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}, \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleemphasis{Any}}{]}

\end{description}\end{quote}
