\index{not\_expression() (pycypher.grammar\_rule\_mixins.ExpressionRulesMixin method)@\spxentry{not\_expression()}\spxextra{pycypher.grammar\_rule\_mixins.ExpressionRulesMixin method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.grammar_rule_mixins.ExpressionRulesMixin.not_expression}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{not\_expression}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{args}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Transform NOT expression with proper boolean negation handling.

\sphinxAtStartPar
NOT is a unary boolean operator that negates its operand. Multiple NOTs
can be chained (e.g., NOT NOT x), so we count them and apply modulo 2
logic: odd count = negate, even count = no\sphinxhyphen{}op (double negation cancels).

\sphinxAtStartPar
The NOT\_KEYWORD terminal has higher priority than IDENTIFIER in the grammar,
ensuring “NOT” is parsed as a keyword rather than a variable name. These
terminals are passed as Lark Token objects, so we filter them separately
from the expression being negated.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This careful handling is necessary because:
1. NOT can be ambiguous with variable names in some contexts
2. Terminal priorities must be explicit to avoid parse errors
3. Multiple negations need semantic simplification
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{args}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{[}}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Any}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{]}}}) – Mix of NOT\_KEYWORD Token objects and the comparison expression.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The expression unchanged (even NOTs), or wrapped in Not node (odd NOTs).

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleemphasis{Any}} | \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{dict}{[}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}, \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleemphasis{Any}}{]}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{null\_predicate\_expression() (pycypher.grammar\_rule\_mixins.ExpressionRulesMixin method)@\spxentry{null\_predicate\_expression()}\spxextra{pycypher.grammar\_rule\_mixins.ExpressionRulesMixin method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.grammar_rule_mixins.ExpressionRulesMixin.null_predicate_expression}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{null\_predicate\_expression}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{args}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Transform IS NULL or IS NOT NULL predicate expressions.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Null predicates check whether an expression evaluates to NULL, which is
necessary because NULL cannot be compared with = or <> in SQL/Cypher
semantics (NULL = NULL is false, not true). IS NULL and IS NOT NULL
are the only correct ways to test for null values.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This method wraps the expression in a NullCheck node only when a null
operator is present. Otherwise, it passes through the expression unchanged
to avoid unnecessary wrapper nodes in the AST.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{args}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{[}}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Any}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{]}}}) – {[}expression{]} or {[}expression, null\_check\_operator{]}.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Expression unchanged if no null check, or dict with type “NullCheck”
containing the operator (“IS NULL” or “IS NOT NULL”) and operand.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleemphasis{Any}} | \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{dict}{[}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}, \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleemphasis{Any}}{]}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

{}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Transform OR boolean expression with short\sphinxhyphen{}circuit evaluation.

\sphinxAtStartPar
OR has lowest precedence among boolean operators. Multiple OR operations
are collected into a single node for easier optimization. Single operands
pass through to avoid unnecessary wrapping.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{args}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{[}}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Any}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{]}}}) – One or more XOR expression operands.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Single operand, or dict with type “Or” containing all operands.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleemphasis{Any}} | \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{dict}{[}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}, \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleemphasis{Any}}{]}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{string\_predicate\_expression() (pycypher.grammar\_rule\_mixins.ExpressionRulesMixin method)@\spxentry{string\_predicate\_expression()}\spxextra{pycypher.grammar\_rule\_mixins.ExpressionRulesMixin method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.grammar_rule_mixins.ExpressionRulesMixin.string_predicate_expression}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{string\_predicate\_expression}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{args}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Transform string predicate expressions (STARTS WITH, ENDS WITH, CONTAINS, =\textasciitilde{}, IN).

\sphinxAtStartPar
String predicates provide specialized string matching operations that are more
efficient and expressive than using regular expressions for common patterns.
The IN operator tests set membership. These operations are necessary for
text search, filtering, and pattern matching in graph queries.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Multiple string predicates can be chained, creating nested nodes for
complex string filtering logic. Single operands pass through to avoid
unnecessary AST depth.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{args}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{[}}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Any}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{]}}}) – Alternating operands and operators {[}left, op1, right1, op2, right2, …{]}.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Single operand unchanged, or nested dict nodes with type “StringPredicate”
containing operator, left operand, and right operand for each predicate.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleemphasis{Any}} | \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{dict}{[}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}, \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleemphasis{Any}}{]}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{string\_predicate\_op() (pycypher.grammar\_rule\_mixins.ExpressionRulesMixin method)@\spxentry{string\_predicate\_op()}\spxextra{pycypher.grammar\_rule\_mixins.ExpressionRulesMixin method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.grammar_rule_mixins.ExpressionRulesMixin.string_predicate_op}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{string\_predicate\_op}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{args}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Extract and normalize string predicate operator keywords.

\sphinxAtStartPar
String predicate operators can be multi\sphinxhyphen{}word keywords (STARTS WITH, ENDS WITH)
or single tokens (CONTAINS, IN, =\textasciitilde{}). This method joins multi\sphinxhyphen{}word operators
with spaces and uppercases them for consistent AST representation.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Normalization to uppercase is necessary because Cypher is case\sphinxhyphen{}insensitive
for keywords, and consistent casing enables reliable pattern matching and
operator dispatch during query execution.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{args}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{[}}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Any}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{]}}}) – One or more keyword tokens forming the operator.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Space\sphinxhyphen{}separated, uppercased operator string (e.g., “STARTS WITH”).

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Transform XOR (exclusive OR) boolean expression.

\sphinxAtStartPar
XOR returns true only if operands differ. Multiple XORs are collected
for easier analysis. This is less common than AND/OR but necessary for
complete boolean logic support.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{args}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{[}}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Any}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{]}}}) – One or more AND expression operands.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleemphasis{Any}} | \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{dict}{[}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}, \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleemphasis{Any}}{]}

\index{FunctionRulesMixin (class in pycypher.grammar\_rule\_mixins)@\spxentry{FunctionRulesMixin}\spxextra{class in pycypher.grammar\_rule\_mixins}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.grammar_rule_mixins.FunctionRulesMixin}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.grammar\_rule\_mixins.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{FunctionRulesMixin}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Grammar rule methods for function invocations and complex expressions.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Handles:
\sphinxhyphen{} Function invocation (built\sphinxhyphen{}in, user\sphinxhyphen{}defined, namespaced)
\sphinxhyphen{} Function arguments and DISTINCT modifier
\sphinxhyphen{} CASE expressions (simple and searched forms)
\sphinxhyphen{} List comprehension (variable IN list WHERE cond | projection)
\sphinxhyphen{} Pattern comprehension (graph pattern matching into lists)
\sphinxhyphen{} REDUCE expression (fold/reduce over lists)
\sphinxhyphen{} Quantifier expressions (ALL, ANY, SINGLE, NONE)
\sphinxhyphen{} Map projection (property selection and transformation)

\index{LiteralRulesMixin (class in pycypher.grammar\_rule\_mixins)@\spxentry{LiteralRulesMixin}\spxextra{class in pycypher.grammar\_rule\_mixins}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.grammar_rule_mixins.LiteralRulesMixin}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.grammar\_rule\_mixins.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{LiteralRulesMixin}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Grammar rule methods for literal values, parameters, and variable names.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Handles:
\sphinxhyphen{} Number literals (signed, unsigned, hex, octal, float, Inf, NaN)
\sphinxhyphen{} String literals (escape sequences)
\sphinxhyphen{} Boolean literals (TRUE / FALSE)
\sphinxhyphen{} NULL literal
\sphinxhyphen{} List literals and list elements
\sphinxhyphen{} Map literals, entries, and individual entries
\sphinxhyphen{} Parameter references (\$name, \$0)
\sphinxhyphen{} Variable name normalization (backtick stripping)

\index{PatternRulesMixin (class in pycypher.grammar\_rule\_mixins)@\spxentry{PatternRulesMixin}\spxextra{class in pycypher.grammar\_rule\_mixins}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.grammar_rule_mixins.PatternRulesMixin}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.grammar\_rule\_mixins.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{PatternRulesMixin}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Grammar rule methods for graph pattern matching constructs.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Handles:
\sphinxhyphen{} Pattern and path pattern construction
\sphinxhyphen{} Pattern elements and shortest path
\sphinxhyphen{} Node patterns (labels, properties, inline WHERE)
\sphinxhyphen{} Relationship patterns (direction, types, variable\sphinxhyphen{}length paths)
\sphinxhyphen{} Label expressions (AND, OR, NOT on labels)
\sphinxhyphen{} Property maps and property key\sphinxhyphen{}value pairs
\sphinxhyphen{} Path length ranges for variable\sphinxhyphen{}length relationships

\subsubsection{AST Converter}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:ast-converter}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Converts dictionary\sphinxhyphen{}based Lark parse trees into typed Pydantic AST models.
Includes the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{from\_cypher()}} classmethod for one\sphinxhyphen{}step parse\sphinxhyphen{}and\sphinxhyphen{}convert.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.ast\_converter@\spxentry{pycypher.ast\_converter}}\index{pycypher.ast\_converter@\spxentry{pycypher.ast\_converter}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.ast_converter}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Converts dictionary\sphinxhyphen{}based AST to typed Pydantic models.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Extracted from \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ast\_models.py}} to separate the \textasciitilde{}60 conversion methods from the
76 Pydantic model definitions, reducing cognitive load and enabling independent
testing of the converter logic.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The public entry point is {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_converter.ASTConverter.from_cypher}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ASTConverter.from\_cypher()}}}}} which parses a Cypher
query string and returns a typed {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.ASTNode}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ASTNode}}}}}.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Usage:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{ast\PYGZus{}converter}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{ASTConverter}

\PYG{n}{ast} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ASTConverter}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{from\PYGZus{}cypher}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (n:Person) RETURN n.name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}
\index{ASTConverter (class in pycypher.ast\_converter)@\spxentry{ASTConverter}\spxextra{class in pycypher.ast\_converter}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_converter.ASTConverter}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.ast\_converter.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{ASTConverter}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Converts dictionary\sphinxhyphen{}based AST to Pydantic models.
\index{from\_cypher() (pycypher.ast\_converter.ASTConverter class method)@\spxentry{from\_cypher()}\spxextra{pycypher.ast\_converter.ASTConverter class method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_converter.ASTConverter.from_cypher}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{classmethod}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{from\_cypher}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{cypher}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Parse Cypher query and convert to typed AST.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Delegates to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_parse\_cypher\_cached()}}, which caches the result by
query string so the expensive Earley parse is performed at most once per
unique query in the lifetime of the process.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{cypher}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Cypher query string.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Typed \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ASTNode}}.  The same object is returned for identical
\sphinxstyleemphasis{cypher} strings (the result is shared — do not mutate it).

\sphinxlineitem{Raises}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}} – If AST conversion fails (grammar/model mismatch).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{lark.exceptions.UnexpectedInput}} – If \sphinxstyleemphasis{cypher} is syntactically invalid.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.ASTNode}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleemphasis{ASTNode}}}}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\subsubsection{AST Rewriter}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:ast-rewriter}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Programmatic AST transformations: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WITH *}} expansion, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN}} stripping,
and multi\sphinxhyphen{}query merging for pipeline composition.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.ast\_rewriter@\spxentry{pycypher.ast\_rewriter}}\index{pycypher.ast\_rewriter@\spxentry{pycypher.ast\_rewriter}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.ast_rewriter}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
AST Rewriting Engine for multi\sphinxhyphen{}query composition.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Provides utilities for creating, cloning, merging, and serializing Cypher
ASTs.  The core operations are:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{create\_with\_star()} — creates a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WITH *}} clause AST node

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{strip\_return()} — removes RETURN clauses from a Query AST (immutable)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{merge\_queries()} — combines multiple Query ASTs into one with \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WITH *}}
separators and intermediate RETURN stripping

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{to\_cypher()} — serializes a Query AST back to a valid Cypher string

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
All operations are \sphinxstylestrong{immutable} — input ASTs are never mutated.
\index{ASTRewriter (class in pycypher.ast\_rewriter)@\spxentry{ASTRewriter}\spxextra{class in pycypher.ast\_rewriter}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_rewriter.ASTRewriter}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.ast\_rewriter.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{ASTRewriter}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Rewrite, merge, and serialize Cypher ASTs.

\sphinxAtStartPar
All methods are stateless and produce new AST objects without
mutating the originals.
\index{create\_with\_star() (pycypher.ast\_rewriter.ASTRewriter method)@\spxentry{create\_with\_star()}\spxextra{pycypher.ast\_rewriter.ASTRewriter method}}

The parser represents \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WITH *}} as a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{With}} node with an
empty \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{items}} list.

\index{strip\_return() (pycypher.ast\_rewriter.ASTRewriter method)@\spxentry{strip\_return()}\spxextra{pycypher.ast\_rewriter.ASTRewriter method}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Return a copy of \sphinxstyleemphasis{query} with all RETURN clauses removed.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The original AST is \sphinxstylestrong{not} mutated.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{query}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.Query}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Query}}}}}) – The Query AST to strip.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A new \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Query}} with RETURN clauses removed.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.Query}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleemphasis{Query}}}}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{merge\_queries() (pycypher.ast\_rewriter.ASTRewriter method)@\spxentry{merge\_queries()}\spxextra{pycypher.ast\_rewriter.ASTRewriter method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_rewriter.ASTRewriter.merge_queries}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{merge\_queries}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{queries}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Merge multiple Query ASTs into a single Query.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Between each pair of queries a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WITH *}} clause is inserted so
that variables from earlier queries are visible to later ones.
Intermediate RETURN clauses are stripped (only the final query’s
RETURN is preserved).
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{queries}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{[}}}{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.Query}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Query}}}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{]}}}) – Ordered list of Query ASTs to merge.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A single \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Query}} combining all input clauses.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.Query}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleemphasis{Query}}}}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\subsection{Data Model}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:data-model}}

\subsubsection{Relational Models}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:relational-models}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Entity/relationship data containers, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Context}}, and the relational algebra
operator hierarchy.  \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Context}} is the primary data holder — it stores entity
and relationship mappings and manages the backend engine lifecycle.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.relational\_models@\spxentry{pycypher.relational\_models}}\index{pycypher.relational\_models@\spxentry{pycypher.relational\_models}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.relational_models}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Data containers and context for PyCypher query execution.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This module defines the data containers (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{EntityTable}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RelationshipTable}})
and the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Context}} object that together form the runtime environment for Cypher
query execution via the BindingFrame execution path in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{star.py}}.


\paragraph{Data containers}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:data-containers}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{EntityTable}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Holds all entity IDs and attributes for a single entity type.
Source data is a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.DataFrame}} or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pyarrow.Table}} with an
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_ID\_\_}} column plus attribute columns.  \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{to\_pandas(context)}}
returns the DataFrame with all columns prefixed by the entity type
(e.g. \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Person\_\_name}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Person\_\_\_\_ID\_\_}}).

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RelationshipTable}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Holds all relationship IDs and endpoint references for a single
relationship type.  Source data must have \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_ID\_\_}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_SOURCE\_\_}},
and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_TARGET\_\_}} columns.  \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{to\_pandas(context)}} applies the same
type\sphinxhyphen{}prefix convention (e.g. \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{KNOWS\_\_\_\_SOURCE\_\_}}).

\end{description}


\paragraph{Context and atomicity}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:context-and-atomicity}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Context}} holds the entity and relationship mappings that a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Star}}
instance queries against.  It also implements query\sphinxhyphen{}scoped shadow writes:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{begin\_query()}} — opens a transaction by initialising an empty shadow
layer.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
SET\sphinxhyphen{}clause mutations write to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{context.\_shadow{[}entity\_type{]}}} (a copy of
the canonical DataFrame) rather than directly to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{source\_obj}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{commit\_query()}} — promotes shadow DataFrames to canonical \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{source\_obj}}
on each entity table.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{rollback\_query()}} — discards the shadow, leaving \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{source\_obj}}
unchanged.

\end{enumerate}

\sphinxAtStartPar
This guarantees that a failed query never leaves the context in a
partially\sphinxhyphen{}mutated state.


\paragraph{Key constants}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:key-constants}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ID\_COLUMN}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Name of the identity column inside source DataFrames (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"\_\_ID\_\_"}}).

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RELATIONSHIP\_SOURCE\_COLUMN}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Source\sphinxhyphen{}node ID column in relationship DataFrames (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"\_\_SOURCE\_\_"}}).

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RELATIONSHIP\_TARGET\_COLUMN}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Target\sphinxhyphen{}node ID column in relationship DataFrames (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"\_\_TARGET\_\_"}}).

\end{description}
\index{Context (class in pycypher.relational\_models)@\spxentry{Context}\spxextra{class in pycypher.relational\_models}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.relational_models.Context}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.relational\_models.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{Context}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{*}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{backend=None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{entity\_mapping=EntityMapping(mapping=\{\})}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{relationship\_mapping=RelationshipMapping(mapping=\{\})}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{cypher\_functions=<factory>}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://pydantic.dev/docs/validation/latest/api/pydantic/base\_model/\#pydantic.BaseModel}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BaseModel}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Context for translation operations.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The optional \sphinxstyleemphasis{backend} parameter selects the DataFrame computation engine.
Pass \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"pandas"}} (default), \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"duckdb"}}, or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"auto"}} to choose a
backend.  \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"auto"}} inspects the total entity count and selects the most
efficient engine automatically.  You can also pass a pre\sphinxhyphen{}constructed
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.backend_engine.BackendEngine}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BackendEngine}}}}} instance directly.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Examples:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Default — identical to current behaviour}
\PYG{n}{Context}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{entity\PYGZus{}mapping}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{relationship\PYGZus{}mapping}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Explicit DuckDB backend for analytical workloads}
\PYG{n}{Context}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{backend}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{duckdb}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Auto\PYGZhy{}select based on data size}
\PYG{n}{Context}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{backend}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{auto}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

{}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Bracket a single query execution with an isolated per\sphinxhyphen{}Context scope.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Used by \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Star.execute\_query()}} to wrap the entire call so
concurrent calls sharing this \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Context}} don’t race on
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_parameters}}/\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_shadow}}/\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_shadow\_rels}}/\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_query\_deadline}}/etc.

{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{keyword-only-separator}{\DUrole{o}{\sphinxstyleabbreviation{*}}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{backend}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{o}{**}\DUrole{n}{data}}}

\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Initialise the query context with entity/relationship data and a backend engine.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{backend}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Any}}}) – Backend engine or hint string (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"pandas"}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"duckdb"}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"polars"}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"auto"}}).  Defaults to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PandasBackend}}
when \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}} or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"pandas"}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{**data}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Any}}}) – Pydantic field values — typically \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{entity\_mapping}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{relationship\_mapping}}, and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{functions}}.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{note}{Note:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The backend is dispatched to during query execution via the
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}}}}} and
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.mutation_engine.MutationEngine}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MutationEngine}}}}} paths.  See
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.backend_engine}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.backend\_engine}}}}} for the engine implementations.
\end{sphinxadmonition}

\end{fulllineitems}

        \PYG{n}{relationships}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{WORKS\PYGZus{}AT}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{l+m+mi}{3}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{custom\PYGZus{}functions}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{)}

\index{set\_deadline() (pycypher.relational\_models.Context method)@\spxentry{set\_deadline()}\spxextra{pycypher.relational\_models.Context method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.relational_models.Context.set_deadline}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{set\_deadline}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{timeout\_seconds}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Arm the per\sphinxhyphen{}query timeout clock.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Call this \sphinxstylestrong{before} {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.relational_models.Context.begin_query}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{begin\_query()}}}}} so that the deadline is in
effect for the entire query transaction.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar

\sphinxAtStartPar
Promote shadow DataFrames to the canonical entity and relationship tables.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Handles both updates to existing tables \sphinxstyleemphasis{and} newly created tables
(e.g. from a CREATE clause referencing a label not yet in the mapping).

\sphinxAtStartPar
The method ensures shadow state is \sphinxstyleemphasis{always} cleared, even if an
exception occurs mid\sphinxhyphen{}commit (e.g. constructing an \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{EntityTable}}
for a new label fails).  This prevents stale shadow data from
leaking into subsequent queries.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

speculative mutation paths.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Source of truth for all IDs and attributes for a specific entity type.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{to\_pandas}} prefixes every column with the entity type, e.g. a source
DataFrame with columns \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[}"\_\_ID\_\_", "name", "age"{]}}} for entity type
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"Person"}} is returned as \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[}"Person\_\_\_\_ID\_\_", "Person\_\_name",
"Person\_\_age"{]}}}.  This prevents column\sphinxhyphen{}name collisions when multiple
entity types are joined together.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.relational_models.EntityTable.from_dataframe}}

{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Construct an \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{EntityTable}} from a pandas DataFrame.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This is the recommended factory for the common case where you already
have a pandas DataFrame.  Column names and attribute maps are inferred
automatically, eliminating the need to specify them manually.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{entity\_type}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – The entity label (e.g. \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"Person"}}).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{df}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{pd.DataFrame}}) – Source pandas DataFrame.  Must contain a column named
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_ID\_\_}} unless \sphinxstyleemphasis{id\_col} is provided.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{id\_col}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – If the identity column in \sphinxstyleemphasis{df} is not named \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_ID\_\_}},
pass its current name here — it will be renamed automatically.
Raises \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}} if the column is not found.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A new {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.relational_models.EntityTable}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{EntityTable}}}}} ready for use in a
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.relational_models.Context}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Context}}}}}.

\sphinxlineitem{Raises}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}} – If \sphinxstyleemphasis{id\_col} is specified but not found, or if
    \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_ID\_\_}} is absent and \sphinxstyleemphasis{id\_col} is not provided.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.relational_models.EntityTable}]{\sphinxcrossref{EntityTable}}}

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Examples}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Minimal usage when \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_ID\_\_}} is already present:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{import}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pandas}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{as}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pd}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{EntityTable}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{ID\PYGZus{}COLUMN}

\PYG{n}{df} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}ID\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Alice}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Bob}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{table} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{EntityTable}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{from\PYGZus{}dataframe}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{df}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Custom identity column:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{df} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{person\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Alice}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Bob}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{table} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{EntityTable}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{from\PYGZus{}dataframe}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{df}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{id\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{person\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\index{to\_pandas() (pycypher.relational\_models.EntityTable method)@\spxentry{to\_pandas()}\spxextra{pycypher.relational\_models.EntityTable method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.relational_models.EntityTable.to_pandas}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{to\_pandas}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{context}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Materialise this entity table as a pandas DataFrame with prefixed columns.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Converts the underlying source (Arrow table or DataFrame) to pandas and
prefixes every column with {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:id43}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{entity\_type}}}}} to prevent naming collisions
in downstream joins (e.g. \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Person\_\_\_\_ID\_\_}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Person\_\_name}}).
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{context}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.relational_models.Context}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Context}}}}}) – Current execution context (unused but required by interface).

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.DataFrame}} with type\sphinxhyphen{}prefixed column names.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
pd.DataFrame

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{to\_spark() (pycypher.relational\_models.EntityTable method)@\spxentry{to\_spark()}\spxextra{pycypher.relational\_models.EntityTable method}}

\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{to\_spark}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{context}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Convert EntityTable to a PySpark DataFrame.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Delegates to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_to\_spark\_with\_prefix()}}
using {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:id43}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{entity\_type}}}}} as the column prefix.  Handles both native PySpark
DataFrames (column rename only) and non\sphinxhyphen{}Spark sources (pandas conversion).
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{context}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.relational_models.Context}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Context}}}}}) – PyCypher Context

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
PySpark DataFrame with columns prefixed by \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\{entity\_type\}\_\_}}.

\sphinxlineitem{Raises}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ImportError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ImportError}}} – If PySpark is not installed.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleemphasis{Any}}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Source of truth for all IDs and attributes for a specific relationship type.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{to\_pandas}} prefixes every column with the relationship type, e.g. a
source DataFrame with columns \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[}"\_\_ID\_\_", "\_\_SOURCE\_\_", "\_\_TARGET\_\_"{]}}}
for relationship type \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"KNOWS"}} is returned as
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[}"KNOWS\_\_\_\_ID\_\_", "KNOWS\_\_\_\_SOURCE\_\_", "KNOWS\_\_\_\_TARGET\_\_"{]}}}.

\end{description}\end{quote}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.relational_models.RelationshipTable.from_dataframe}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{classmethod}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{from\_dataframe}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{relationship\_type}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{df}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{keyword-only-separator}{\DUrole{o}{\sphinxstyleabbreviation{*}}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{id\_col}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{source\_col}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{target\_col}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Construct a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RelationshipTable}} from a pandas DataFrame.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This is the recommended factory for the common case where you already
have a pandas DataFrame describing relationships.  Column names and
attribute maps are inferred automatically, eliminating the need to
specify them manually.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{relationship\_type}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – The relationship label (e.g. \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"KNOWS"}}).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{df}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{pd.DataFrame}}) – Source pandas DataFrame.  Must contain columns named
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_ID\_\_}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_SOURCE\_\_}}, and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_TARGET\_\_}} unless the
corresponding \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{*\_col}} parameters are provided.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{id\_col}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – If the identity column in \sphinxstyleemphasis{df} is not named \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_ID\_\_}},
pass its current name here — it will be renamed automatically.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{source\_col}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – If the source\sphinxhyphen{}node column is not named
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_SOURCE\_\_}}, pass its current name here.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{target\_col}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – If the target\sphinxhyphen{}node column is not named
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_TARGET\_\_}}, pass its current name here.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A new {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.relational_models.RelationshipTable}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RelationshipTable}}}}} ready for use in a
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.relational_models.Context}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Context}}}}}.

\sphinxlineitem{Raises}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}} – If a required column (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_ID\_\_}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_SOURCE\_\_}},
    \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_TARGET\_\_}}) is missing and the corresponding \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{*\_col}}
    parameter was not provided, or if a specified \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{*\_col}} is
    not found in the DataFrame.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.relational_models.RelationshipTable}]{\sphinxcrossref{RelationshipTable}}}

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Examples}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Minimal usage when canonical columns are already present:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{import}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pandas}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{as}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pd}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{RelationshipTable}

\PYG{n}{df} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}ID\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}SOURCE\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{10}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{20}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}TARGET\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{30}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{40}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{since}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2020}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2021}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{table} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{RelationshipTable}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{from\PYGZus{}dataframe}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{KNOWS}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{df}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Custom column names:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{df} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{rel\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{from\PYGZus{}node}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{10}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{20}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{to\PYGZus{}node}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{30}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{40}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{table} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{RelationshipTable}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{from\PYGZus{}dataframe}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{KNOWS}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{df}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{id\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{rel\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{source\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{from\PYGZus{}node}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{target\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{to\PYGZus{}node}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\end{fulllineitems}

\subsubsection{Constants}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:constants}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Shared constants (column names, sentinel values) used across modules.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.constants@\spxentry{pycypher.constants}}\index{pycypher.constants@\spxentry{pycypher.constants}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.constants}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Shared constants and small utilities used across pycypher modules.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Extracting these from \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{relational\_models}} and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{binding\_evaluator}}
breaks two circular\sphinxhyphen{}import cycles:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{relational\_models}} ↔ \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{backend\_engine}} (shared \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ID\_COLUMN}} constant)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{binding\_evaluator}} ↔ \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{scalar\_function\_evaluator}} (shared
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_normalize\_func\_args}} helper)

\end{enumerate}

\subsection{Query Execution}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:query-execution}}

\subsubsection{Star (Query Orchestrator)}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:star-query-orchestrator}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The main entry point for query execution.  \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Star}} accepts a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Context}},
parses Cypher queries, and returns results as pandas DataFrames.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.star@\spxentry{pycypher.star}}\index{pycypher.star@\spxentry{pycypher.star}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.star}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Translation layer from Cypher AST to BindingFrame execution.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The main entry point for users is the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Star}} class, which accepts a
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Context}} (containing \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{EntityTable}} and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RelationshipTable}} objects),
parses a Cypher query string, and executes it against the registered
DataFrames via the BindingFrame execution path.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Star}} is a thin \sphinxstylestrong{facade} that coordinates specialized components:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.clause_executor.ClauseExecutor}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ClauseExecutor}}}}} — clause dispatch and execution

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.query_analyzer.QueryAnalyzer}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{QueryAnalyzer}}}}} — pre\sphinxhyphen{}execution planning and optimization

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{QueryExplainer}} — EXPLAIN\sphinxhyphen{}style text plans

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.pattern_matcher.PatternMatcher}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PatternMatcher}}}}} — MATCH clause translation

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.mutation_engine.MutationEngine}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MutationEngine}}}}} — CREATE/SET/DELETE execution

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.projection_planner.ProjectionPlanner}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ProjectionPlanner}}}}} — RETURN/WITH evaluation

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.frame_joiner.FrameJoiner}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{FrameJoiner}}}}} — frame merging and joining

\end{itemize}
\index{ResultCache (class in pycypher.star)@\spxentry{ResultCache}\spxextra{class in pycypher.star}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.star.ResultCache}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.star.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{ResultCache}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{max\_size\_bytes}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{104857600}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{ttl\_seconds}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{0.0}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{lock\_timeout\_seconds}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
LRU cache for query results with size\sphinxhyphen{}bounded eviction and TTL support.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Keys are derived from the normalised query string and parameters.
Values are copied DataFrames so mutations to the returned frame do not
corrupt the cached entry.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The cache is automatically invalidated when the underlying \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Context}}
commits a mutation (SET / CREATE / DELETE / MERGE / REMOVE).

\sphinxAtStartPar
Thread\sphinxhyphen{}safety:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Mutating operations (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{get}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{put}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{invalidate}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{clear}})
acquire an exclusive write lock.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Read\sphinxhyphen{}only operations (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{stats}}) acquire a shared read lock, allowing
concurrent stats collection without blocking cache operations.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
All lock acquisitions use adaptive timeouts (scaled by operation
complexity) to prevent deadlocks.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The write\sphinxhyphen{}lock owner thread is tracked for deadlock diagnostics.

\end{itemize}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}
\index{\_\_init\_\_() (pycypher.star.ResultCache method)@\spxentry{\_\_init\_\_()}\spxextra{pycypher.star.ResultCache method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.star.ResultCache.__init__}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_init\_\_}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{max\_size\_bytes}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{104857600}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{ttl\_seconds}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{0.0}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{lock\_timeout\_seconds}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Initialize the result cache.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{max\_size\_bytes}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Maximum total memory for cached DataFrames.
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{0}} disables caching entirely.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ttl\_seconds}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Time\sphinxhyphen{}to\sphinxhyphen{}live per entry in seconds.
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{0}} means entries never expire by time (only by LRU eviction
or mutation\sphinxhyphen{}based invalidation).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{lock\_timeout\_seconds}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – Base timeout for lock acquisition in seconds.
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}} uses the class default (5 s).  The actual timeout is
scaled by a per\sphinxhyphen{}operation multiplier (e.g. \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{put}} gets 2x
the base timeout).  Set to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{0}} for non\sphinxhyphen{}blocking semantics.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{invalidate() (pycypher.star.ResultCache method)@\spxentry{invalidate()}\spxextra{pycypher.star.ResultCache method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.star.ResultCache.invalidate}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{invalidate}}}
{}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bump the generation counter, lazily invalidating all entries.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Called when a mutation is committed to the Context.  Existing entries
are not deleted immediately — they are evicted on the next \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{get()}}
that detects the stale generation, or during LRU eviction.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

Store a query result in the cache.

\sphinxAtStartPar
If the lock cannot be acquired within the adaptive timeout, the
result is silently not cached (query execution is unaffected).
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

\index{Star (class in pycypher.star)@\spxentry{Star}\spxextra{class in pycypher.star}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.star.Star}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.star.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{Star}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{context}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{keyword-only-separator}{\DUrole{o}{\sphinxstyleabbreviation{*}}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{result\_cache\_max\_mb}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{result\_cache\_ttl\_seconds}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Main entry point for PyCypher query execution.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Accepts a {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.relational_models.Context}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Context}}}}} (containing
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{EntityTable}} and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RelationshipTable}} objects), parses a Cypher query
string, and executes it against the registered DataFrames via the
BindingFrame execution path.


\paragraph{Query lifecycle}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:query-lifecycle}}\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Parse} — Cypher string → AST via {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.grammar_parser.GrammarParser}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{GrammarParser}}}}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Plan} — AST is analysed for complexity, memory budget, and timeout.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Execute} — clauses are processed sequentially, building up a
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}}}}} through MATCH, WHERE,
WITH, and RETURN stages.  Mutations (CREATE/SET/DELETE) are staged
in a shadow layer and committed on success.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Return} — the final BindingFrame is projected to a pandas DataFrame.

\end{enumerate}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Example:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{import}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pandas}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{as}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pd}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{Star}

\PYG{n}{people} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}ID\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{3}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Alice}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Bob}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Carol}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{age}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{30}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{25}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{35}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{star} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}entity}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{people}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{build}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) WHERE p.age \PYGZgt{} 28 RETURN p.name ORDER BY p.name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Internally, Star delegates to specialized engines:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.pattern_matcher.PatternMatcher}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PatternMatcher}}}}} — MATCH clause translation

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.path_expander.PathExpander}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PathExpander}}}}} — variable\sphinxhyphen{}length path BFS

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.mutation_engine.MutationEngine}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MutationEngine}}}}} — CREATE/SET/DELETE execution

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.clause_executor.ClauseExecutor}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ClauseExecutor}}}}} — clause dispatch and execution loop

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.query_analyzer.QueryAnalyzer}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{QueryAnalyzer}}}}} — pre\sphinxhyphen{}execution planning

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{QueryExplainer}} — EXPLAIN text plans

\end{itemize}

{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Stream an \sphinxstyleemphasis{eligible} query’s result straight to \sphinxstyleemphasis{uri} (out\sphinxhyphen{}of\sphinxhyphen{}core).

\sphinxAtStartPar
When the opt\sphinxhyphen{}in relation engine is enabled and \sphinxstyleemphasis{query} is in the
eligible subset, this builds a lazy DuckDB relation and streams it to
the sink via \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{COPY … TO}} — no pandas materialisation — and returns
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{True}}.  Otherwise nothing is written and it returns \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{False}} so the
caller can fall back to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{execute\_query}} + a DataFrame writer.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{query}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Any}}}) – Cypher query string or parsed AST.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{uri}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Destination URI (bare path / \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{file://}}; cloud is rejected).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{fmt}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Any}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – Optional explicit output format; inferred from the extension
when \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}}.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{True}} if the query was streamed, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{False}} if not eligible.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{bool}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\subsubsection{Clause Executor}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:clause-executor}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Clause\sphinxhyphen{}by\sphinxhyphen{}clause execution engine for the BindingFrame IR.  Handles
clause\sphinxhyphen{}type dispatch (MATCH, WITH, RETURN, SET, DELETE, CREATE, etc.),
UNWIND processing, WHERE filter application, and dead column elimination.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.clause\_executor@\spxentry{pycypher.clause\_executor}}\index{pycypher.clause\_executor@\spxentry{pycypher.clause\_executor}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.clause_executor}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Clause\sphinxhyphen{}by\sphinxhyphen{}clause execution engine for the BindingFrame IR.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Extracted from {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.star}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.star}}}}} to separate clause dispatch and
execution logic from the main orchestration facade.  The
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.clause_executor.ClauseExecutor}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ClauseExecutor}}}}} handles:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Clause\sphinxhyphen{}type dispatch (MATCH, WITH, RETURN, SET, DELETE, CREATE, etc.)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
MATCH clause handling (regular, OPTIONAL, merge)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
UNWIND clause processing with seed\sphinxhyphen{}frame management

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
WHERE filter application

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Dead column elimination

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The main clause execution loop

\end{itemize}
\index{ClauseExecutor (class in pycypher.clause\_executor)@\spxentry{ClauseExecutor}\spxextra{class in pycypher.clause\_executor}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Execute Cypher clauses against the BindingFrame IR.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Encapsulates clause dispatch, MATCH handling, UNWIND processing,
WHERE filtering, and the main execution loop.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{pattern\_matcher}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.pattern_matcher.PatternMatcher}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{PatternMatcher}}}}}) – PatternMatcher for MATCH operations.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{mutations}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.mutation_engine.MutationEngine}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{MutationEngine}}}}}) – MutationEngine for CREATE/SET/DELETE operations.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{frame\_joiner}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.frame_joiner.FrameJoiner}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{FrameJoiner}}}}}) – FrameJoiner for frame merging.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{projection\_planner}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.projection_planner.ProjectionPlanner}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ProjectionPlanner}}}}}) – ProjectionPlanner for RETURN/WITH clauses.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{query\_analyzer}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.query_analyzer.QueryAnalyzer}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{QueryAnalyzer}}}}}) – QueryAnalyzer for pre\sphinxhyphen{}execution planning.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Apply a WHERE predicate to \sphinxstyleemphasis{result\_frame}, with optional fallback.

\sphinxAtStartPar
First tries evaluating \sphinxstyleemphasis{where\_expr} against \sphinxstyleemphasis{result\_frame}.  If that
raises \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}} or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{KeyError}}, falls back to evaluating against
\sphinxstyleemphasis{fallback\_frame} and applying the boolean mask positionally.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{where\_expr}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Any}}}) – AST expression node for the WHERE predicate.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{result\_frame}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}}}}}) – The frame to filter.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{fallback\_frame}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}}}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – Optional pre\sphinxhyphen{}projection frame for variable lookup.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A new filtered \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}}.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleemphasis{BindingFrame}}}}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\subsubsection{Pattern Matcher}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pattern-matcher}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Handles MATCH clause pattern evaluation — node matching, relationship
traversal, and variable\sphinxhyphen{}length path expansion.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.pattern\_matcher@\spxentry{pycypher.pattern\_matcher}}\index{pycypher.pattern\_matcher@\spxentry{pycypher.pattern\_matcher}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.pattern_matcher}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Pattern matching engine for translating Cypher MATCH patterns to BindingFrames.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Extracted from {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.star.Star}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Star}}}}} to reduce the god object.
The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PatternMatcher}} handles all pattern\sphinxhyphen{}related operations:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Node scanning} — scan a NodePattern into a BindingFrame.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Pattern path traversal} — translate a PatternPath into a BindingFrame
via scans, joins, and BFS expansion.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{MATCH clause translation} — combine multiple pattern paths and apply
WHERE predicates.

\end{enumerate}


\paragraph{Architecture}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:id54}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
PatternMatcher
├── node\PYGZus{}pattern\PYGZus{}to\PYGZus{}binding\PYGZus{}frame()  — single node scan
├── pattern\PYGZus{}path\PYGZus{}to\PYGZus{}binding\PYGZus{}frame()  — full path traversal
└── match\PYGZus{}to\PYGZus{}binding\PYGZus{}frame()  — MATCH clause coordinator
    └── delegates to PathExpander for variable\PYGZhy{}length paths
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Translates Cypher MATCH patterns into BindingFrame instances.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Delegates BFS\sphinxhyphen{}based operations to {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.path_expander.PathExpander}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PathExpander}}}}}.


\paragraph{Algorithm overview}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:algorithm-overview}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
For a Cypher pattern like \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MATCH (a:Person)\sphinxhyphen{}{[}:KNOWS{]}\sphinxhyphen{}>(b:Person)}}:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Node scan} — \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{node\_pattern\_to\_binding\_frame()}} scans each node
pattern into a single\sphinxhyphen{}column BindingFrame (e.g. column \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"a"}} with
all Person IDs).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Relationship join} — for each relationship in the path, the
relationship table is joined on source/target IDs to connect adjacent
node frames.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Multi\sphinxhyphen{}path merge} — when a MATCH has multiple comma\sphinxhyphen{}separated
patterns, each is translated independently and then joined on shared
variable names.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Predicate pushdown} — WHERE predicates referencing only one path
are pushed down \sphinxstyleemphasis{before} the cross\sphinxhyphen{}path join to reduce row counts.

\end{enumerate}

\sphinxAtStartPar
For variable\sphinxhyphen{}length paths (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[}*1..3{]}}}), step 2 delegates to
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.path_expander.PathExpander}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PathExpander}}}}} for BFS expansion.

\end{description}\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Predicate pushdown}: when the MATCH has multiple pattern paths and
a WHERE clause, the predicate is analysed to determine if it only
references variables from a single path.  If so, the filter is applied
to that path’s frame \sphinxstyleemphasis{before} the join, reducing the join’s input
size.  Cross\sphinxhyphen{}path predicates are applied after the join as before.
