\subsubsection{Path Expander}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:path-expander}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
BFS\sphinxhyphen{}based path expansion for variable\sphinxhyphen{}length relationship patterns
(e.g. \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{(a)\sphinxhyphen{}{[}:KNOWS*1..3{]}\sphinxhyphen{}>(b)}}).
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.path\_expander@\spxentry{pycypher.path\_expander}}\index{pycypher.path\_expander@\spxentry{pycypher.path\_expander}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.path_expander}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
BFS\sphinxhyphen{}based path expansion for variable\sphinxhyphen{}length and shortest\sphinxhyphen{}path patterns.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Extracted from {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.star.Star}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Star}}}}} to reduce the god object.
The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PathExpander}} handles all BFS\sphinxhyphen{}related operations:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Variable\sphinxhyphen{}length paths} — \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[}*m..n{]}}} hop\sphinxhyphen{}bounded BFS expansion.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Shortest path} — \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{shortestPath}} and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{allShortestPaths}} BFS.

\end{enumerate}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The expander operates on {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}}}}}
instances and returns new frames with expanded path results.


\paragraph{Architecture}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:id55}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
PathExpander
├── expand\PYGZus{}variable\PYGZus{}length\PYGZus{}path()  — BFS with hop\PYGZhy{}bounded frontier
└── shortest\PYGZus{}path\PYGZus{}to\PYGZus{}binding\PYGZus{}frame()  — BFS + min\PYGZhy{}hop filter
\end{sphinxVerbatim}
\index{PathExpander (class in pycypher.path\_expander)@\spxentry{PathExpander}\spxextra{class in pycypher.path\_expander}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.path_expander.PathExpander}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.path\_expander.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{PathExpander}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{context}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
BFS\sphinxhyphen{}based path expansion for variable\sphinxhyphen{}length and shortest\sphinxhyphen{}path patterns.


\paragraph{Algorithm overview}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:id56}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
For a pattern like \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{(a)\sphinxhyphen{}{[}:KNOWS*1..3{]}\sphinxhyphen{}>(b)}}:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Seed} — the starting BindingFrame provides initial node IDs in
column \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{a}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Frontier expansion} — at each hop, the frontier is joined with
the relationship table to discover next\sphinxhyphen{}hop nodes.  The frontier is
deduplicated per (start, current\sphinxhyphen{}tip) pair to avoid exponential
blowup in cyclic graphs.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Hop collection} — rows at each hop in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[}min\_hops, max\_hops{]}}}
are collected into the result.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Safety limits} — unbounded paths (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[}*{]}}}) are capped at
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_MAX\_UNBOUNDED\_PATH\_HOPS}} (20) hops.  The frontier size
is capped at \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_MAX\_FRONTIER\_ROWS}} (1M rows) and total
accumulated results at \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_MAX\_BFS\_TOTAL\_ROWS}} (5M rows);
exceeding either raises \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SecurityError}}.

\end{enumerate}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Complexity: O(V + E * max\_hops) per seed row, where V = nodes and
E = edges of the traversed type.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{param context}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The execution context with entity/relationship tables.

\end{description}\end{quote}

\index{expand\_variable\_length\_path() (pycypher.path\_expander.PathExpander method)@\spxentry{expand\_variable\_length\_path()}\spxextra{pycypher.path\_expander.PathExpander method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.path_expander.PathExpander.expand_variable_length_path}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{expand\_variable\_length\_path}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{start\_frame}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{start\_var}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{rel\_type}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{direction}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{end\_var}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{end\_type}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{min\_hops}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{max\_hops}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{anon\_counter}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{path\_length\_col}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{row\_limit}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
BFS hop\sphinxhyphen{}by\sphinxhyphen{}hop expansion of a variable\sphinxhyphen{}length relationship pattern.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Builds a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}} containing one row per reachable
(start, end) pair at each hop count in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[}min\_hops, max\_hops{]}}}.
The frontier is deduplicated at each hop to avoid exponential blowup.

\sphinxAtStartPar
When \sphinxstyleemphasis{row\_limit} is provided, expansion stops early once enough rows
have been collected.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{start\_frame}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}}}}}) – BindingFrame whose \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{start\_var}} column seeds the BFS.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{start\_var}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Column in \sphinxstyleemphasis{start\_frame} that holds the starting node IDs.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{rel\_type}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Relationship type to traverse.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{direction}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.RelationshipDirection}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{RelationshipDirection}}}}}) – Traversal direction.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{end\_var}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Output column name for the reached endpoint IDs.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{end\_type}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – Entity type string for \sphinxstyleemphasis{end\_var} in the type registry.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{min\_hops}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Minimum hop count to include in results.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{max\_hops}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – Maximum hop count; \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}} means unbounded.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{anon\_counter}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{[}}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{]}}}) – Mutable counter for synthetic variable names.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{path\_length\_col}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – If given, adds an integer column recording hop count.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{row\_limit}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – If given, stop BFS once this many result rows accumulated.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}} with columns from \sphinxstyleemphasis{start\_frame} plus
\sphinxstyleemphasis{end\_var} (and optionally \sphinxstyleemphasis{path\_length\_col}).

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame}]{\sphinxcrossref{BindingFrame}}}

\end{description}\end{quote}

{}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Execute a shortestPath / allShortestPaths pattern via BFS.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Runs BFS from the start node, then filters to the minimum\sphinxhyphen{}hop rows per
(start, end) pair.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{path}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.PatternPath}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{PatternPath}}}}}) – PatternPath with \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{shortest\_path\_mode}} set.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{anon\_counter}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{[}}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{]}}}) – Mutable counter for synthetic variable names.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{context\_frame}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}}}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – Optional preceding BindingFrame for variable binding.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{node\_scanner}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Any}}) – Callable that scans a NodePattern into a BindingFrame.
Signature: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{(node, anon\_counter, context\_frame) \sphinxhyphen{}> BindingFrame}}.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A BindingFrame with the shortest\sphinxhyphen{}path result rows.

{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame}]{\sphinxcrossref{BindingFrame}}}

\subsubsection{Expression Renderer}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:expression-renderer}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Converts AST expression nodes into human\sphinxhyphen{}readable column names for
RETURN/WITH clause output.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.expression\_renderer@\spxentry{pycypher.expression\_renderer}}\index{pycypher.expression\_renderer@\spxentry{pycypher.expression\_renderer}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.expression_renderer}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Visitor\sphinxhyphen{}pattern expression renderer for human\sphinxhyphen{}readable display text.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Extracted from \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_expr\_display\_text}} to reduce
cyclomatic complexity in the Star orchestrator.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ExpressionRenderer}} translates Cypher AST expression nodes into
short human\sphinxhyphen{}readable strings, used for auto\sphinxhyphen{}naming RETURN columns when
no explicit \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{AS}} alias is present.


\paragraph{Architecture}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:id57}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Uses a dispatch dictionary mapping AST node types to focused handler
methods, replacing the previous 30+ \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{isinstance}} chain (CC \textasciitilde{}54) with
individual methods (CC \textasciitilde{}3 each).

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
ExpressionRenderer
├── render()  — public dispatch entry point
├── \PYGZus{}render\PYGZus{}variable()
├── \PYGZus{}render\PYGZus{}property\PYGZus{}lookup()
├── \PYGZus{}render\PYGZus{}function\PYGZus{}invocation()
├── \PYGZus{}render\PYGZus{}arithmetic()
├── \PYGZus{}render\PYGZus{}comparison()
├── ...
└── (one handler per AST node type)
\end{sphinxVerbatim}
\index{ExpressionRenderer (class in pycypher.expression\_renderer)@\spxentry{ExpressionRenderer}\spxextra{class in pycypher.expression\_renderer}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.expression_renderer.ExpressionRenderer}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.expression\_renderer.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{ExpressionRenderer}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar

\sphinxAtStartPar
Render Cypher AST expression nodes as human\sphinxhyphen{}readable text.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Thread\sphinxhyphen{}safe and stateless — a single instance can be shared across
queries.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Usage:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{renderer} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ExpressionRenderer}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{text} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{renderer}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{render}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{some\PYGZus{}ast\PYGZus{}expr}\PYG{p}{)}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} e.g. \PYGZdq{}n.name\PYGZdq{} or \PYGZdq{}count(n)\PYGZdq{}}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

\subsection{BindingFrame Execution}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:bindingframe-execution}}

\subsubsection{BindingFrame}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:bindingframe}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The core execution abstraction — a BindingFrame is a named, typed DataFrame
that tracks variable bindings through pattern matching and clause evaluation.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.binding\_frame@\spxentry{pycypher.binding\_frame}}\index{pycypher.binding\_frame@\spxentry{pycypher.binding\_frame}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.binding_frame}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
BindingFrame — the core IR abstraction for the refactored query engine.

\sphinxAtStartPar
A BindingFrame is a DataFrame whose columns are named after Cypher variables
(plain strings such as \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"p"}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"q"}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"r"}}).  Each row is one possible
assignment of entity IDs to those variables.  Attributes are \sphinxstylestrong{never} stored in
a BindingFrame; they are fetched on demand from the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Context}} via
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{get\_property()}}.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This eliminates three sources of fragility in the legacy pipeline:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The opaque 32\sphinxhyphen{}hex HASH\_ID column names produced by \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Projection}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PREFIXED\_ENTITY}} column names (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Person\_\_name}}) that bleed into
operator logic via \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_ensure\_full\_entity\_data}} and similar hacks.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{variable\_map}} / \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{variable\_type\_map}} metadata that must be threaded
through every \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Relation}} subclass to recover which column belongs to which
Cypher variable.

\end{enumerate}

\sphinxAtStartPar
With BindingFrame the column \sphinxstyleemphasis{is} the variable — no lookup table required.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Usage:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{bf} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{BindingFrame}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{bindings}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{p}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{3}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{q}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{4}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{5}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{6}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{type\PYGZus{}registry}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{p}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{q}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{context}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{ctx}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{names} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{bf}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}property}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{p}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}   \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} pd.Series of person names}
\PYG{n}{filtered} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{bf}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{filter}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{names} \PYG{o}{==} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Alice}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} BindingFrame with one row}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}
\index{PATH\_HOP\_COLUMN\_PREFIX (in module pycypher.binding\_frame)@\spxentry{PATH\_HOP\_COLUMN\_PREFIX}\spxextra{in module pycypher.binding\_frame}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.PATH_HOP_COLUMN_PREFIX}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.binding\_frame.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{PATH\_HOP\_COLUMN\_PREFIX}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }'\_path\_hop\_'}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Prefix for path\sphinxhyphen{}hop\sphinxhyphen{}count columns produced by variable\sphinxhyphen{}length path expansion.
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{star.py}} writes \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{f"\{PATH\_HOP\_COLUMN\_PREFIX\}\{path\_var\}"}} and
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{binding\_evaluator.py}} reads it when evaluating \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{length(path\_var)}}.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{BindingFrame (class in pycypher.binding\_frame)@\spxentry{BindingFrame}\spxextra{class in pycypher.binding\_frame}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.binding\_frame.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{bindings}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{type\_registry}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{context}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{\_property\_cache=<factory>}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
A table of variable bindings where every column is a Cypher variable name.

\sphinxAtStartPar
A BindingFrame is the core intermediate representation (IR) for the
query engine.  Each column is named after a Cypher variable (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"p"}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"q"}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"r"}}), and each row represents one candidate assignment of
entity/relationship IDs to those variables.  \sphinxstylestrong{Attributes are never
stored here} — they are fetched on\sphinxhyphen{}demand from the Context via
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame.get_property}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{get\_property()}}}}}.


\paragraph{Key operations}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:key-operations}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame.get_property}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{get\_property()}}}}} — fetch attribute values via ID\sphinxhyphen{}keyed join

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame.filter}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{filter()}}}}} — apply a boolean mask (WHERE clause)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame.join}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{join()}}}}} — inner join on shared variable names (multi\sphinxhyphen{}MATCH)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame.cross_join}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{cross\_join()}}}}} — Cartesian product (disjoint patterns)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame.mutate}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{mutate()}}}}} — write property values back through shadow layer

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Example:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} After MATCH (p:Person)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(q:Person), the BindingFrame}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} might look like:}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{}   bindings = DataFrame(\PYGZob{}\PYGZdq{}p\PYGZdq{}: [1, 1, 2], \PYGZdq{}q\PYGZdq{}: [2, 3, 3]\PYGZcb{})}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{}   type\PYGZus{}registry = \PYGZob{}\PYGZdq{}p\PYGZdq{}: \PYGZdq{}Person\PYGZdq{}, \PYGZdq{}q\PYGZdq{}: \PYGZdq{}Person\PYGZdq{}\PYGZcb{}}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{}}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} To evaluate WHERE p.age \PYGZgt{} 25:}
\PYG{n}{ages} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{bf}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}property}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{p}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{age}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}   \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Series: [30, 30, 25]}
\PYG{n}{filtered} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{bf}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{filter}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{ages} \PYG{o}{\PYGZgt{}} \PYG{l+m+mi}{25}\PYG{p}{)}      \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} keeps rows 0 and 1}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\index{type\_registry (pycypher.binding\_frame.BindingFrame attribute)@\spxentry{type\_registry}\spxextra{pycypher.binding\_frame.BindingFrame attribute}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Fetch entity attribute values for all rows in this frame.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Looks up the entity IDs in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{bindings{[}var\_name{]}}}, then performs an
ID\sphinxhyphen{}keyed lookup against the entity table stored in the context.  The
result is a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.Series}} aligned with \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{self.bindings}} (same index,
same length).
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{var\_name}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – The Cypher variable name (e.g. \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"p"}}).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{prop\_name}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – The entity attribute name (e.g. \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"name"}}).

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.Series}} of property values, one per binding row.

\sphinxlineitem{Raises}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#KeyError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{KeyError}}} – If \sphinxstyleemphasis{var\_name} is not in the type registry.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}} – If \sphinxstyleemphasis{var\_name} is not a column in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{bindings}}.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/reference/api/pandas.Series.html\#pandas.Series}{\sphinxstyleemphasis{Series}}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{note}{Note:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
If \sphinxstyleemphasis{prop\_name} does not exist as a column in the entity table, a
Series of \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.NA}} values is returned (per Cypher null semantics)
rather than raising an exception.
\end{sphinxadmonition}

\end{fulllineitems}

{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{var\_name}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{prop\_names}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Fetch multiple properties for \sphinxstyleemphasis{var\_name} in a single indexed join.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This is a performance optimization over calling {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame.get_property}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{get\_property()}}}}}
multiple times for the same variable.  Instead of N separate
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Series.map()}} calls, the IDs are joined against the entity table
once and all requested columns are extracted simultaneously.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Falls back to per\sphinxhyphen{}property {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame.get_property}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{get\_property()}}}}} for multi\sphinxhyphen{}type
variables or when the entity type cannot be resolved.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{var\_name}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – The Cypher variable name (e.g. \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"p"}}).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{prop\_names}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{[}}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{]}}}) – List of property names to fetch.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A dict mapping each property name to a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.Series}} of values.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{dict}{[}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}, \sphinxhref{https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/reference/api/pandas.Series.html\#pandas.Series}{\sphinxstyleemphasis{Series}}{]}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Inner\sphinxhyphen{}join two BindingFrames on a pair of columns.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Used to connect a node scan to a relationship scan (or two scans of
different entity types that share a structural constraint).

\sphinxAtStartPar
After the join the redundant \sphinxstyleemphasis{right\_col} is dropped when it differs
from \sphinxstyleemphasis{left\_col}, leaving a single unified column for the shared ID.

\sphinxAtStartPar

\end{itemize}

{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.exceptions.VariableNotFoundError}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{VariableNotFoundError}}}}} – If either join column is absent from its frame.

{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame}]{\sphinxcrossref{BindingFrame}}}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\index{project() (pycypher.binding\_frame.BindingFrame method)@\spxentry{project()}\spxextra{pycypher.binding\_frame.BindingFrame method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame.project}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{project}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{computed}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Build an output DataFrame from pre\sphinxhyphen{}evaluated column Series.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This is the final step before returning results to the caller.  Each
key in \sphinxstyleemphasis{computed} becomes a column in the output DataFrame.

\sphinxAtStartPar
In Phase 1 the caller is responsible for evaluating expressions into
Series first (e.g. using \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{get\_property}}).  Phase 4 will introduce
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingExpressionEvaluator}} which accepts \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Expression}} objects
directly.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{computed}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{dict}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{[}}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxhref{https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/reference/api/pandas.Series.html\#pandas.Series}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Series}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{]}}}) – Mapping from output alias to a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.Series}} aligned
with \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{self.bindings}}.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A plain \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.DataFrame}} with one column per alias.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/reference/api/pandas.DataFrame.html\#pandas.DataFrame}{\sphinxstyleemphasis{DataFrame}}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Write a new or updated property back to the entity table in context.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Aligns \sphinxstyleemphasis{values} by entity ID so that only the rows present in this
BindingFrame are updated; rows not present are left unchanged.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This mutates \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{context.entity\_mapping{[}type{]}.source\_obj}} in place,
which is the same object that \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{get\_property}} reads from.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{var\_name}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – The Cypher variable whose entity table is updated.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{prop\_name}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – The attribute name to set or overwrite.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{values}} (\sphinxhref{https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/reference/api/pandas.Series.html\#pandas.Series}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Series}}}) – A \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.Series}} of new values aligned with \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{self.bindings}}.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Raises}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.exceptions.VariableNotFoundError}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{VariableNotFoundError}}}}} – If \sphinxstyleemphasis{var\_name} is not in the frame.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.exceptions.GraphTypeNotFoundError}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{GraphTypeNotFoundError}}}}} – If the variable’s type is not in any mapping.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{mutate\_batch() (pycypher.binding\_frame.BindingFrame method)@\spxentry{mutate\_batch()}\spxextra{pycypher.binding\_frame.BindingFrame method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame.mutate_batch}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{mutate\_batch}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{var\_name}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{properties}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Write multiple properties back to the entity table in a single pass.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This is a batch optimisation of {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame.mutate}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{mutate()}}}}} for the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SET p = \{…\}}}
map\sphinxhyphen{}literal expansion pattern.  Instead of calling {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame.mutate}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{mutate()}}}}} once
per key (each of which builds a separate dict and scans the ID column),
this method builds a single updates DataFrame with all properties and
applies them via a single \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{merge}} + column assignment.

\sphinxAtStartPar
For \sphinxstyleemphasis{k} properties and \sphinxstyleemphasis{n} rows, {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame.mutate}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{mutate()}}}}} called \sphinxstyleemphasis{k} times is
O(n * k) with \sphinxstyleemphasis{k} dict constructions and \sphinxstyleemphasis{k} ID column scans.
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{mutate\_batch}} reduces this to O(n + k) — one merge pass plus per\sphinxhyphen{}column
assignment from the merged result.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{var\_name}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – The Cypher variable whose entity table is updated.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{properties}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{dict}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{[}}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxhref{https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/reference/api/pandas.Series.html\#pandas.Series}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Series}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{]}}}) – Mapping from property name to a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.Series}} of new
values, each aligned with \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{self.bindings}}.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Raises}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.exceptions.VariableNotFoundError}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{VariableNotFoundError}}}}} – If \sphinxstyleemphasis{var\_name} is not in the frame.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.exceptions.GraphTypeNotFoundError}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{GraphTypeNotFoundError}}}}} – If the variable’s type is not in any mapping.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\subsubsection{Evaluator Protocol}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:evaluator-protocol}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Protocol interface for expression evaluation that breaks circular imports.
Defines the minimal contract sub\sphinxhyphen{}evaluators need from the top\sphinxhyphen{}level
expression evaluator.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.evaluator\_protocol@\spxentry{pycypher.evaluator\_protocol}}\index{pycypher.evaluator\_protocol@\spxentry{pycypher.evaluator\_protocol}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.evaluator_protocol}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Protocol interface for expression evaluation — breaks circular imports.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ExpressionEvaluatorProtocol}} defines the minimal contract that
sub\sphinxhyphen{}evaluators (collection, exists, etc.) need from the top\sphinxhyphen{}level
expression evaluator.  By depending on this protocol instead of the
concrete {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_evaluator.BindingExpressionEvaluator}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingExpressionEvaluator}}}}},
circular import chains are eliminated.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Usage in sub\sphinxhyphen{}evaluators:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{evaluator\PYGZus{}protocol}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{ExpressionEvaluatorProtocol}

\PYG{k}{def}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nf}{some\PYGZus{}method}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n+nb+bp}{self}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{evaluator}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n}{ExpressionEvaluatorProtocol}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{o}{\PYGZgt{}} \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{evaluator}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{evaluate}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{expr}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}
\index{ExpressionEvaluatorProtocol (class in pycypher.evaluator\_protocol)@\spxentry{ExpressionEvaluatorProtocol}\spxextra{class in pycypher.evaluator\_protocol}}

\begin{fulllineitems}

{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{o}{*}\DUrole{n}{args}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{o}{**}\DUrole{n}{kwargs}}}
{}

Minimal interface for recursive expression evaluation.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Any evaluator that provides \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{evaluate()}} and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{frame}} satisfies
this protocol, including \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingExpressionEvaluator}}.

\subsubsection{BindingExpression Evaluator}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:bindingexpression-evaluator}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Vectorised expression evaluator that dispatches AST expression nodes to
specialised evaluator modules.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.binding\_evaluator@\spxentry{pycypher.binding\_evaluator}}\index{pycypher.binding\_evaluator@\spxentry{pycypher.binding\_evaluator}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.binding_evaluator}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
BindingExpressionEvaluator — expression evaluator for the BindingFrame IR.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Evaluates Cypher AST expressions against a {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}}}}},
returning a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.Series}} of per\sphinxhyphen{}row results.


\paragraph{Design}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:design}}\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{No ``Relation`` dependency} — property lookups go through
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame.get_property}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{get\_property()}}}}} instead of joining a
prefixed entity DataFrame.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Clean return type} — returns a single \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.Series}} (not a tuple).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{``CaseExpression`` support} — handled via a vectorised \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.Series.where()}}
reduction (both searched \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{CASE WHEN}} and simple \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{CASE expr WHEN}} forms).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Aggregation\sphinxhyphen{}aware} — \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{evaluate\_aggregation()}} returns a scalar for use in
WITH and RETURN clause grouping.  Supported aggregation functions:
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{collect}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{count}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{sum}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{avg}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{min}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{max}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{stdev}} (sample, ddof=1), \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{stdevp}} (population, ddof=0),
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{percentileCont(expr, p)}} (linear interpolation), and
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{percentileDisc(expr, p)}} (lower/discrete interpolation).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Null\sphinxhyphen{}safe boolean logic} — AND, OR, NOT, XOR are delegated to
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BooleanExpressionEvaluator}} which uses Kleene three\sphinxhyphen{}valued logic.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Modular architecture} — arithmetic operations are delegated to
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ArithmeticExpressionEvaluator}}, boolean logic to
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BooleanExpressionEvaluator}}, aggregations to
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{AggregationExpressionEvaluator}}, collections to
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{CollectionExpressionEvaluator}}, and scalar functions to
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ScalarFunctionEvaluator}}.

\end{enumerate}
\index{BindingExpressionEvaluator (class in pycypher.binding\_evaluator)@\spxentry{BindingExpressionEvaluator}\spxextra{class in pycypher.binding\_evaluator}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_evaluator.BindingExpressionEvaluator}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.binding\_evaluator.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingExpressionEvaluator}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{frame}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Evaluates Cypher AST expressions against a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}}.

\sphinxAtStartPar
All property lookups delegate to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame.get\_property()}}, which
performs an ID\sphinxhyphen{}keyed join against the entity table stored in the context.
No DataFrame column prefixes are ever used.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Supported expression types:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PropertyLookup}} (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{p.name}})

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Variable}} references (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{p}}) — returns the ID column

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Literal values (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{IntegerLiteral}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{FloatLiteral}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{StringLiteral}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BooleanLiteral}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{NullLiteral}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ListLiteral}})

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Arithmetic}} operations (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{+}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\sphinxhyphen{}}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{*}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{/}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\%}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\textasciicircum{}}})

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Unary}} operations (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{+}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\sphinxhyphen{}}})

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Comparison}} predicates (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{=}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{<>}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{<}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{>}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{<=}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{>=}})

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{NullCheck}} predicates (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{IS NULL}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{IS NOT NULL}})

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{StringPredicate}} (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{STARTS WITH}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ENDS WITH}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{CONTAINS}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{=\textasciitilde{}}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{IN}})

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Boolean logic (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{And}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Or}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Not}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Xor}})

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{FunctionInvocation}} (dispatched through \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ScalarFunctionRegistry}})

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{CaseExpression}} (simple and searched CASE/WHEN/ELSE)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Graph introspection: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{labels(n)}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{type(r)}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{keys(n)}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{properties(n)}}
(pre\sphinxhyphen{}evaluated intercepts that read the type registry before evaluating args)

\end{itemize}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

\index{\_\_init\_\_() (pycypher.binding\_evaluator.BindingExpressionEvaluator method)@\spxentry{\_\_init\_\_()}\spxextra{pycypher.binding\_evaluator.BindingExpressionEvaluator method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_evaluator.BindingExpressionEvaluator.__init__}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_init\_\_}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{frame}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Initialise the evaluator.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Sub\sphinxhyphen{}evaluators are constructed lazily on first access via \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{@property}}
descriptors.  This avoids the overhead of importing and instantiating
all 5 sub\sphinxhyphen{}evaluator modules for every \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingExpressionEvaluator}}
construction — most queries only touch 1–2 of them.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar

Evaluate \sphinxstyleemphasis{expression} and return a per\sphinxhyphen{}row result Series.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The returned Series has the same length as \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{self.frame}} and is
indexed \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{0 … N\sphinxhyphen{}1}}.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.Series}} of per\sphinxhyphen{}row values.

\sphinxlineitem{Raises}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#NotImplementedError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{NotImplementedError}}} – For expression types not yet handled.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}} – For semantic errors (unknown variable, missing property).

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
FrameSeries

\end{description}\end{quote}

\index{evaluate\_aggregation() (pycypher.binding\_evaluator.BindingExpressionEvaluator method)@\spxentry{evaluate\_aggregation()}\spxextra{pycypher.binding\_evaluator.BindingExpressionEvaluator method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_evaluator.BindingExpressionEvaluator.evaluate_aggregation}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{evaluate\_aggregation}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{agg\_expression}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Evaluate an aggregation expression and return a scalar value.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Architecture Loop 280 \sphinxhyphen{} Phase 3: Delegates to AggregationExpressionEvaluator.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Supports \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{collect}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{count}} / \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{COUNT(*)}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{sum}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{avg}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{min}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{max}}.  Also handles {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.Arithmetic}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Arithmetic}}}}}
nodes whose branches contain aggregations (e.g. \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{count(*) + 1}} or
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{sum(p.salary) * 2}}).
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{agg\_expression}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.Expression}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Expression}}}}}) – A {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.FunctionInvocation}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{FunctionInvocation}}}}},
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.CountStar}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{CountStar}}}}}, or
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.Arithmetic}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Arithmetic}}}}} node.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A scalar aggregated value.

\sphinxlineitem{Raises}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}} – For unsupported aggregation functions or missing arguments.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Any

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{evaluate\_aggregation\_grouped() (pycypher.binding\_evaluator.BindingExpressionEvaluator method)@\spxentry{evaluate\_aggregation\_grouped()}\spxextra{pycypher.binding\_evaluator.BindingExpressionEvaluator method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_evaluator.BindingExpressionEvaluator.evaluate_aggregation_grouped}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{evaluate\_aggregation\_grouped}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{agg\_expression}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{group\_df}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{group\_key\_aliases}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Vectorised grouped aggregation — one scalar per group.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Evaluates \sphinxstyleemphasis{agg\_expression}’s inner argument \sphinxstylestrong{once} over the entire
frame, then uses \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.Series.groupby().agg()}} to produce one
aggregated value per group.  This replaces the previous pattern of
creating a new \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}} and
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_evaluator.BindingExpressionEvaluator}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingExpressionEvaluator}}}}} per group, cutting O(N\_groups)
object allocations and O(N\_groups × frame\_size) mask operations down
to a single vectorised pandas pass.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{agg\_expression}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.Expression}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Expression}}}}}) – A {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.FunctionInvocation}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{FunctionInvocation}}}}},
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.CountStar}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{CountStar}}}}}, or
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.Arithmetic}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Arithmetic}}}}} aggregation node.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{group\_df}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{pd.DataFrame}}) – DataFrame of evaluated GROUP BY key columns (one row per
frame row, columns named by \sphinxstyleemphasis{group\_key\_aliases}).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{group\_key\_aliases}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{[}}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{]}}}) – Ordered list of GROUP BY column names in
\sphinxstyleemphasis{group\_df}.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.Series}} with one value per unique group (in
first\sphinxhyphen{}seen order, matching the order produced by
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{group\_df.groupby(..., sort=False)}}), or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}} when the
expression cannot be vectorised (e.g. Arithmetic wrapping
aggregations) — the caller should fall back to per\sphinxhyphen{}group
evaluation in that case.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
pd.Series | None

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}



\subsubsection{Arithmetic Evaluator}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:arithmetic-evaluator}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Handles \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{+}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\sphinxhyphen{}}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{*}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{/}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\%}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\textasciicircum{}}} with null propagation and
type coercion.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.arithmetic\_evaluator@\spxentry{pycypher.arithmetic\_evaluator}}\index{pycypher.arithmetic\_evaluator@\spxentry{pycypher.arithmetic\_evaluator}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.arithmetic_evaluator}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
ArithmeticExpressionEvaluator \sphinxhyphen{} Specialized evaluator for arithmetic and comparison operations.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This module provides focused evaluation of arithmetic operations extracted from the
BindingExpressionEvaluator god object. This is Phase 1 of the systematic refactoring
to decompose the 2904\sphinxhyphen{}line evaluator into specialized, maintainable components.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Architecture Loop 277 \sphinxhyphen{} Phase 1 Implementation
\index{ArithmeticExpressionEvaluator (class in pycypher.arithmetic\_evaluator)@\spxentry{ArithmeticExpressionEvaluator}\spxextra{class in pycypher.arithmetic\_evaluator}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.arithmetic_evaluator.ArithmeticExpressionEvaluator}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.arithmetic\_evaluator.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{ArithmeticExpressionEvaluator}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{frame}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{keyword-only-separator}{\DUrole{o}{\sphinxstyleabbreviation{*}}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{evaluator\_factory}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Specialized evaluator for arithmetic and comparison expressions.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class handles all arithmetic operations (+, \sphinxhyphen{}, *, /, \%, \textasciicircum{}),
comparison operations (=, <>, <, >, <=, >=), and unary operations (+x, \sphinxhyphen{}x)
that were previously part of the BindingExpressionEvaluator god object.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The evaluator maintains identical semantics to the original implementation
while providing a focused, testable interface for arithmetic operations.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

Evaluate a binary arithmetic expression.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Handles arithmetic operations (+, \sphinxhyphen{}, *, /, \%, \textasciicircum{}) with proper null handling,
temporal arithmetic support, and Cypher\sphinxhyphen{}compliant semantics.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar

\sphinxAtStartPar

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{right\_expr}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Any}}) – Right operand expression

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{expression\_evaluator}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.evaluator_protocol.ExpressionEvaluatorProtocol}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ExpressionEvaluatorProtocol}}}}}) – Main expression evaluator for recursive evaluation

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Series with arithmetic results

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.exceptions.UnsupportedOperatorError}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{UnsupportedOperatorError}}}}} – If operator is not supported

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#TypeError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{TypeError}}} – If operands have incompatible types

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar

\end{description}\end{quote}

\subsubsection{Boolean Evaluator}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:boolean-evaluator}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Handles \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{AND}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{OR}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{NOT}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{XOR}} with Cypher’s ternary null logic.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.boolean\_evaluator@\spxentry{pycypher.boolean\_evaluator}}\index{pycypher.boolean\_evaluator@\spxentry{pycypher.boolean\_evaluator}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.boolean_evaluator}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
BooleanExpressionEvaluator \sphinxhyphen{} Specialized evaluator for boolean logic operations.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This module provides focused evaluation of boolean operations extracted from the
BindingExpressionEvaluator god object. This is Phase 2 of the systematic refactoring
to decompose the 2904\sphinxhyphen{}line evaluator into specialized, maintainable components.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Architecture Loop 277 \sphinxhyphen{} Phase 2 Implementation
\index{kleene\_and() (in module pycypher.boolean\_evaluator)@\spxentry{kleene\_and()}\spxextra{in module pycypher.boolean\_evaluator}}

{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Kleene three\sphinxhyphen{}valued AND: null AND false = false; null AND true = null.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Vectorised via numpy — no Python\sphinxhyphen{}level loop.  \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{isna()}} catches all null
sentinels (None, np.nan, pd.NA), making this more correct than the previous
implementation which only checked \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}} and NaN floats.

\end{description}\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Specialized evaluator for boolean logic expressions.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class handles all boolean operations (AND, OR, NOT, XOR) with proper
Kleene three\sphinxhyphen{}valued logic semantics that were previously part of the
BindingExpressionEvaluator god object.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The evaluator maintains identical semantics to the original implementation
while providing a focused, testable interface for boolean operations.

\end{description}\end{quote}

{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Evaluate Cypher NOT expr — three\sphinxhyphen{}valued boolean negation.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Three\sphinxhyphen{}valued logic: NOT true → false, NOT false → true,
NOT null → null.  The WHERE clause’s fillna(False) converts
the null to false for filtering, correctly excluding null rows.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{operand\_expr}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.Expression}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Expression}}}}}) – Expression to negate

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{expression\_evaluator}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.evaluator_protocol.ExpressionEvaluatorProtocol}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ExpressionEvaluatorProtocol}}}}}) – Main expression evaluator for recursive evaluation

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Series with boolean NOT results using Kleene logic

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
FrameSeries

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

{}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Evaluate a multi\sphinxhyphen{}operand boolean fold using the boolean fold ops table.

\sphinxAtStartPar
All operands are null\sphinxhyphen{}coerced to False before the binary operation
so that missing values do not propagate through filter predicates.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{key}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – One of “and”, “or”, or “xor”

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{operands}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{[}}}{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.Expression}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Expression}}}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{]}}}) – List of Cypher AST expression nodes

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{expression\_evaluator}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.evaluator_protocol.ExpressionEvaluatorProtocol}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ExpressionEvaluatorProtocol}}}}}) – Main expression evaluator for recursive evaluation

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
A boolean Series of per\sphinxhyphen{}row results

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}} – If key is not a supported boolean operation

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
FrameSeries

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}



\subsubsection{Comparison Evaluator}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:comparison-evaluator}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Handles \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{=}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{<>}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{<}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{>}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{<=}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{>=}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{IS NULL}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{IS NOT NULL}}, unary operators, and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{CASE}} expressions.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.comparison\_evaluator@\spxentry{pycypher.comparison\_evaluator}}\index{pycypher.comparison\_evaluator@\spxentry{pycypher.comparison\_evaluator}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.comparison_evaluator}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
ComparisonEvaluator — comparison, null\sphinxhyphen{}check, unary, and CASE expression evaluation.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Extracted from {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.binding_evaluator}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.binding\_evaluator}}}}} to isolate the “dispatch table
+ null propagation” family of operations into a focused, independently testable
module.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Handles:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Binary comparison predicates (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{=}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{<>}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{<}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{>}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{<=}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{>=}})

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Null\sphinxhyphen{}check predicates (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{IS NULL}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{IS NOT NULL}})

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Unary arithmetic operators (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{+}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\sphinxhyphen{}}})

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
CASE expressions (searched and simple forms)

\end{itemize}
\index{ComparisonEvaluator (class in pycypher.comparison\_evaluator)@\spxentry{ComparisonEvaluator}\spxextra{class in pycypher.comparison\_evaluator}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.comparison_evaluator.ComparisonEvaluator}}
\pysigstartsignatures

{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{frame}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{keyword-only-separator}{\DUrole{o}{\sphinxstyleabbreviation{*}}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{evaluator\_factory}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Evaluates comparison, null\sphinxhyphen{}check, unary, and CASE expressions.

\sphinxAtStartPar
All methods accept an \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{evaluator}} parameter — the parent
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_evaluator.BindingExpressionEvaluator}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingExpressionEvaluator}}}}} — so that
sub\sphinxhyphen{}expressions can be recursively evaluated without circular imports.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

\index{evaluate\_case() (pycypher.comparison\_evaluator.ComparisonEvaluator method)@\spxentry{evaluate\_case()}\spxextra{pycypher.comparison\_evaluator.ComparisonEvaluator method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.comparison_evaluator.ComparisonEvaluator.evaluate_case}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{evaluate\_case}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{case\_expr}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{when\_clauses}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{else\_expr}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{evaluator}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Evaluate a CASE expression vectorially using \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.Series.where}}.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Supports both \sphinxstyleemphasis{searched} CASE (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{CASE WHEN cond THEN val …}}) and
\sphinxstyleemphasis{simple} CASE (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{CASE expr WHEN match THEN val …}}).

\sphinxAtStartPar
The algorithm iterates WHEN clauses in \sphinxstylestrong{reverse} and repeatedly
applies:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{then\PYGZus{}series}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{where}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{condition\PYGZus{}series}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{result}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
so that the \sphinxstyleemphasis{first} matching WHEN clause wins.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{case\_expr}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.Expression}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Expression}}}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – The \sphinxstyleemphasis{simple} CASE discriminant expression, or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}}
for a \sphinxstyleemphasis{searched} CASE.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{when\_clauses}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{[}}}{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.WhenClause}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{WhenClause}}}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{]}}}) – Ordered list of {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.WhenClause}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WhenClause}}}}}
nodes.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{else\_expr}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.Expression}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Expression}}}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – Optional ELSE expression; produces \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}} when absent.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{evaluator}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.evaluator_protocol.ExpressionEvaluatorProtocol}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ExpressionEvaluatorProtocol}}}}}) – Parent expression evaluator for recursive evaluation.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.Series}} of per\sphinxhyphen{}row CASE results.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
FrameSeries

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}



\subsubsection{String Predicate Evaluator}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:string-predicate-evaluator}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Handles \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{STARTS WITH}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ENDS WITH}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{CONTAINS}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{IN}}, and
regex matching with null propagation.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.string\_predicate\_evaluator@\spxentry{pycypher.string\_predicate\_evaluator}}\index{pycypher.string\_predicate\_evaluator@\spxentry{pycypher.string\_predicate\_evaluator}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.string_predicate_evaluator}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
StringPredicateEvaluator — string and membership predicate evaluation.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Extracted from {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.binding_evaluator}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.binding\_evaluator}}}}} to isolate the string
predicate family (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{STARTS WITH}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ENDS WITH}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{CONTAINS}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{=\textasciitilde{}}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{IN}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{NOT IN}}) into a focused, independently testable module.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Includes ReDoS protection for the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{=\textasciitilde{}}} regex operator.
\index{StringPredicateEvaluator (class in pycypher.string\_predicate\_evaluator)@\spxentry{StringPredicateEvaluator}\spxextra{class in pycypher.string\_predicate\_evaluator}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.string_predicate_evaluator.StringPredicateEvaluator}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.string\_predicate\_evaluator.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{StringPredicateEvaluator}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{keyword-only-separator}{\DUrole{o}{\sphinxstyleabbreviation{*}}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{evaluator\_factory}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Evaluates string and membership predicates.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Supported operators:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"STARTS WITH"}} — vectorised \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{str.startswith}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"ENDS WITH"}} — vectorised \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{str.endswith}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"CONTAINS"}} — vectorised \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{str.contains}} (literal, not regex)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"=\textasciitilde{}"}} — full\sphinxhyphen{}match regex via \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{str.fullmatch}} with ReDoS protection

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"IN"}} — membership test against a list (three\sphinxhyphen{}valued logic)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"NOT IN"}} — inverse membership test (three\sphinxhyphen{}valued logic)

\end{itemize}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

evaluator over a derived frame. Unused today — accepted for

\index{evaluate\_string\_predicate() (pycypher.string\_predicate\_evaluator.StringPredicateEvaluator method)@\spxentry{evaluate\_string\_predicate()}\spxextra{pycypher.string\_predicate\_evaluator.StringPredicateEvaluator method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.string_predicate_evaluator.StringPredicateEvaluator.evaluate_string_predicate}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{evaluate\_string\_predicate}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{op}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{left\_expr}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{right\_expr}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{evaluator}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Evaluate a string or membership predicate.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The right\sphinxhyphen{}hand operand is evaluated and its first element is used as
the scalar pattern/set for all rows — Cypher string predicates always
compare against a constant pattern.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{op}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – The predicate operator string.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{left\_expr}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.Expression}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Expression}}}}}) – The expression producing the string or value to test.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{right\_expr}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.Expression}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Expression}}}}}) – The expression producing the pattern/list to test against.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{evaluator}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.evaluator_protocol.ExpressionEvaluatorProtocol}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ExpressionEvaluatorProtocol}}}}}) – Parent expression evaluator for recursive evaluation.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A boolean \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.Series}} of per\sphinxhyphen{}row results.

\sphinxlineitem{Raises}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#TypeError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{TypeError}}} – If \sphinxstyleemphasis{left\_expr} evaluates to a non\sphinxhyphen{}string, non\sphinxhyphen{}null Series
    and \sphinxstyleemphasis{op} is a string accessor operator.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.exceptions.UnsupportedOperatorError}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{UnsupportedOperatorError}}}}} – If \sphinxstyleemphasis{op} is not a recognised string predicate
    operator.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
FrameSeries

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}



\subsubsection{EXISTS Evaluator}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:exists-evaluator}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Handles \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{EXISTS \{ MATCH ... \}}} subquery evaluation and pattern
comprehensions with batched execution.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.exists\_evaluator@\spxentry{pycypher.exists\_evaluator}}\index{pycypher.exists\_evaluator@\spxentry{pycypher.exists\_evaluator}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.exists_evaluator}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
ExistsEvaluator — EXISTS predicate and pattern comprehension evaluation.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Extracted from {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.binding_evaluator}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.binding\_evaluator}}}}} to isolate the EXISTS and
pattern comprehension logic (the two largest inline method families) into a
focused, independently testable module.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Handles:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{EXISTS \{ (a)\sphinxhyphen{}{[}:TYPE{]}\sphinxhyphen{}>(b) \}}} — single\sphinxhyphen{}hop pattern existence check

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{EXISTS \{ MATCH ... WHERE ... \}}} — full subquery existence check

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[}(a)\sphinxhyphen{}{[}:TYPE{]}\sphinxhyphen{}>(b) WHERE pred | expr{]}}} — pattern comprehensions

\end{itemize}
\index{ExistsEvaluator (class in pycypher.exists\_evaluator)@\spxentry{ExistsEvaluator}\spxextra{class in pycypher.exists\_evaluator}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.exists_evaluator.ExistsEvaluator}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.exists\_evaluator.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{ExistsEvaluator}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{frame}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Evaluates EXISTS predicates and pattern comprehensions.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Follows the established evaluator pattern: takes a
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}}}}} at init time and receives
the parent evaluator as a callback parameter on each public method.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar

inner pattern has at least one match anchored on the row’s bound
variables, and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{False}} otherwise.

{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.Query}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Query}}}}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{evaluator}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.evaluator_protocol.ExpressionEvaluatorProtocol}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ExpressionEvaluatorProtocol}}}}}) – The parent \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingExpressionEvaluator}} for
recursive expression evaluation.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
A \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.Series}} of \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{bool}} values, one per row.

\sphinxAtStartPar
FrameSeries

\pysigstartsignatures
