{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{evaluate\_pattern\_comprehension}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{pc}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{evaluator}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Evaluate a Cypher pattern comprehension row by row.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Supports single\sphinxhyphen{}hop directed patterns:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[}(src)\sphinxhyphen{}{[}:TYPE{]}\sphinxhyphen{}>(tgt) | map\_expr{]}}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[}(src)\sphinxhyphen{}{[}:TYPE{]}\sphinxhyphen{}>(tgt) WHERE pred | map\_expr{]}}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[}(src)<\sphinxhyphen{}{[}:TYPE{]}\sphinxhyphen{}(tgt) | map\_expr{]}}}  (incoming direction)

\end{itemize}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{pc}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.PatternComprehension}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{PatternComprehension}}}}}) – The {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.PatternComprehension}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PatternComprehension}}}}} AST node.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{evaluator}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.evaluator_protocol.ExpressionEvaluatorProtocol}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ExpressionEvaluatorProtocol}}}}}) – The parent \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingExpressionEvaluator}} for
recursive expression evaluation.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.Series}} of lists, one per row in the current BindingFrame.

\sphinxlineitem{Raises}
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.exceptions.PatternComprehensionError}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{PatternComprehensionError}}}}} – If the pattern is not a single\sphinxhyphen{}hop path.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
FrameSeries

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}



\subsubsection{Aggregation Evaluator}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:aggregation-evaluator}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Handles \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{count()}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{sum()}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{avg()}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{collect()}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{stDev()}}, etc.
with grouped and full\sphinxhyphen{}table aggregation.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.aggregation\_evaluator@\spxentry{pycypher.aggregation\_evaluator}}\index{pycypher.aggregation\_evaluator@\spxentry{pycypher.aggregation\_evaluator}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.aggregation_evaluator}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
AggregationExpressionEvaluator \sphinxhyphen{} Specialized evaluator for aggregation operations.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This module provides focused evaluation of aggregation operations extracted from the
BindingExpressionEvaluator god object. This is Phase 3 of the systematic refactoring
to decompose the 2904\sphinxhyphen{}line evaluator into specialized, maintainable components.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Architecture Loop 280 \sphinxhyphen{} Phase 3 Implementation

\sphinxAtStartPar
Specialized evaluator for aggregation expressions.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class handles all aggregation operations (SUM, AVG, MIN, MAX, COUNT,
percentiles, standard deviation) that were previously part of the
BindingExpressionEvaluator god object.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The evaluator maintains identical semantics to the original implementation
while providing a focused, testable interface for aggregation operations.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

\subsubsection{Aggregation Planner}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:aggregation-planner}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Detects aggregation expressions in RETURN/WITH items and plans
grouped vs full\sphinxhyphen{}table aggregation dispatch.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.aggregation\_planner@\spxentry{pycypher.aggregation\_planner}}\index{pycypher.aggregation\_planner@\spxentry{pycypher.aggregation\_planner}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.aggregation_planner}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
AggregationPlanner — aggregation detection and grouped evaluation.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Extracted from {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.star}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.star}}}}} to isolate the cohesive aggregation
planning family (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_contains\_aggregation}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_aggregate\_items}}) into
a focused, independently testable module.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Handles:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Recursive aggregation detection in expression trees

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Three aggregation modes: no\sphinxhyphen{}agg, full\sphinxhyphen{}table, grouped

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Dual\sphinxhyphen{}purpose min/max function disambiguation

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Vectorised grouped aggregation with per\sphinxhyphen{}group fallback

\end{itemize}
\index{AggregationPlanner (class in pycypher.aggregation\_planner)@\spxentry{AggregationPlanner}\spxextra{class in pycypher.aggregation\_planner}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.aggregation_planner.AggregationPlanner}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.aggregation\_planner.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{AggregationPlanner}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Detects aggregations in expressions and evaluates projection items.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Stateless — all state is passed via method parameters.
\index{contains\_aggregation() (pycypher.aggregation\_planner.AggregationPlanner method)@\spxentry{contains\_aggregation()}\spxextra{pycypher.aggregation\_planner.AggregationPlanner method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.aggregation_planner.AggregationPlanner.contains_aggregation}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{contains\_aggregation}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{expression}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Check recursively whether \sphinxstyleemphasis{expression} contains any aggregate function.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Walks the full expression tree so that aggregations nested inside
arithmetic or logical operators are detected correctly — e.g.
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{count(p.name) + 1}} or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{sum(p.salary) * 2}}.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{expression}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Any}}}) – AST expression node to inspect.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
True if the expression or any descendant is an aggregation function.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{bool}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{aggregate\_items() (pycypher.aggregation\_planner.AggregationPlanner method)@\spxentry{aggregate\_items()}\spxextra{pycypher.aggregation\_planner.AggregationPlanner method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.aggregation_planner.AggregationPlanner.aggregate_items}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{aggregate\_items}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{items}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{frame}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Evaluate projection items (with optional aggregation) against a BindingFrame.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Alias inference must be complete on all items before calling this method.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Handles three modes:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{No aggregation} — vectorized expression evaluation, one column per item.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Full\sphinxhyphen{}table aggregation} — reduces all rows to a single aggregated row.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Grouped aggregation} — groups by non\sphinxhyphen{}aggregation keys, computes
aggregations per group.

\end{itemize}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{items}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{[}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Any}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{]}}}) – List of \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ReturnItem}} / \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WithItem}} nodes with \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{.alias}} set.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{frame}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}}}}}) – Input {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}}}}}.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A plain \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.DataFrame}}.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
pd.DataFrame

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}



\subsubsection{Collection Evaluator}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:collection-evaluator}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Handles list/map operations: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[}idx{]}}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{keys()}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{range()}}, list
comprehensions, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{REDUCE}}, and quantifier predicates (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{all()}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{any()}}).
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.collection\_evaluator@\spxentry{pycypher.collection\_evaluator}}\index{pycypher.collection\_evaluator@\spxentry{pycypher.collection\_evaluator}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.collection_evaluator}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Collection Expression Evaluator for PyCypher.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Architecture Loop 283 \sphinxhyphen{} Collection operations extracted from BindingExpressionEvaluator
god object into focused, testable, and maintainable CollectionExpressionEvaluator.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This module contains all collection\sphinxhyphen{}related expression evaluation functionality:
\sphinxhyphen{} List comprehensions
\sphinxhyphen{} Quantifiers (ANY/ALL/NONE)
\sphinxhyphen{} REDUCE expressions
\sphinxhyphen{} Pattern comprehensions
\sphinxhyphen{} Slicing operations
\sphinxhyphen{} Property lookup
\sphinxhyphen{} Map literals and projections

\sphinxAtStartPar
The evaluator follows the established delegation pattern from previous god object
extractions (AggregationExpressionEvaluator, ArithmeticExpressionEvaluator).
\index{CollectionExpressionEvaluator (class in pycypher.collection\_evaluator)@\spxentry{CollectionExpressionEvaluator}\spxextra{class in pycypher.collection\_evaluator}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Evaluates collection\sphinxhyphen{}related expressions.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class handles complex collection operations that were previously
embedded within the BindingExpressionEvaluator god object. By extracting
these operations, we achieve better separation of concerns and improved
maintainability.

\end{description}\end{quote}

\index{eval\_property\_lookup() (pycypher.collection\_evaluator.CollectionExpressionEvaluator method)@\spxentry{eval\_property\_lookup()}\spxextra{pycypher.collection\_evaluator.CollectionExpressionEvaluator method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.collection_evaluator.CollectionExpressionEvaluator.eval_property_lookup}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{eval\_property\_lookup}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{var\_expr}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{prop\_name}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{expression\_evaluator}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Evaluate a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PropertyLookup}} expression (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{expr.prop}}).

\sphinxAtStartPar
Handles two cases:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Variable target} — delegates to {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame.get_property}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{get\_property()}}}}}
which resolves the property from the entity/relationship tables in the
current context.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Any other expression target} — evaluates \sphinxstyleemphasis{var\_expr} to obtain a
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.Series}} of values.  Elements that are \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{dict}} (e.g. produced by
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.MapLiteral}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MapLiteral}}}}} or
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.MapProjection}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MapProjection}}}}}) have \sphinxstyleemphasis{prop\_name} extracted
via \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{dict.get}}; non\sphinxhyphen{}dict elements yield \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}} (null).

\end{itemize}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{var\_expr}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.Expression}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Expression}}}}}) – The expression to the left of the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{.}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{prop\_name}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – The property key to look up.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{expression\_evaluator}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.evaluator_protocol.ExpressionEvaluatorProtocol}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ExpressionEvaluatorProtocol}}}}}) – Main expression evaluator for recursive evaluation.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.Series}} of extracted property values, one per frame row.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
FrameSeries

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{eval\_slicing() (pycypher.collection\_evaluator.CollectionExpressionEvaluator method)@\spxentry{eval\_slicing()}\spxextra{pycypher.collection\_evaluator.CollectionExpressionEvaluator method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.collection_evaluator.CollectionExpressionEvaluator.eval_slicing}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{eval\_slicing}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{coll\_expr}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{start\_expr}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{end\_expr}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{expression\_evaluator}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Evaluate \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{coll{[}from..to{]}}} slicing.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Both bounds are optional and inclusive/exclusive per Cypher semantics
(\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{list{[}a..b{]}}} returns elements at indices \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{a}} up to but not
including \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{b}}, identical to Python \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{seq{[}a:b{]}}}).  Either bound
being \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}} means open\sphinxhyphen{}ended.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Works for lists, tuples, and strings.  Out\sphinxhyphen{}of\sphinxhyphen{}bounds indices are
handled silently by Python’s slice semantics.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{coll\_expr}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.Expression}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Expression}}}}}) – Expression that evaluates to list or string to slice.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{start\_expr}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.Expression}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Expression}}}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – Start index expression (can be None).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{end\_expr}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.Expression}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Expression}}}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – End index expression (can be None).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{expression\_evaluator}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.evaluator_protocol.ExpressionEvaluatorProtocol}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ExpressionEvaluatorProtocol}}}}}) – Main expression evaluator for recursive evaluation.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.Series}} of sliced values.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
FrameSeries

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{eval\_list\_comprehension() (pycypher.collection\_evaluator.CollectionExpressionEvaluator method)@\spxentry{eval\_list\_comprehension()}\spxextra{pycypher.collection\_evaluator.CollectionExpressionEvaluator method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.collection_evaluator.CollectionExpressionEvaluator.eval_list_comprehension}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{eval\_list\_comprehension}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{lc}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{expression\_evaluator}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Evaluate a Cypher list comprehension in a single vectorised pass.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Implements \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[}var IN list\_expr WHERE where\_expr | map\_expr{]}}}.

\sphinxAtStartPar
All (row\_idx, element) pairs are exploded into a single flat
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}}}}} so that the WHERE
predicate and map expression are each evaluated \sphinxstylestrong{once} across all
elements rather than once per element.  Per\sphinxhyphen{}row lists are reconstructed
from the surviving (row\_idx, mapped\_value) pairs.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{lc}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.ListComprehension}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ListComprehension}}}}}) – The ListComprehension AST node.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{expression\_evaluator}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.evaluator_protocol.ExpressionEvaluatorProtocol}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ExpressionEvaluatorProtocol}}}}}) – Main expression evaluator for recursive evaluation.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.Series}} of lists, one per row in the current BindingFrame.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
FrameSeries

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Evaluate quantifier expressions (ANY/ALL/NONE).

\sphinxAtStartPar

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
FrameSeries

\index{eval\_quantifier\_vectorized() (pycypher.collection\_evaluator.CollectionExpressionEvaluator method)@\spxentry{eval\_quantifier\_vectorized()}\spxextra{pycypher.collection\_evaluator.CollectionExpressionEvaluator method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.collection_evaluator.CollectionExpressionEvaluator.eval_quantifier_vectorized}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{eval\_quantifier\_vectorized}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{q}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{expression\_evaluator}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Vectorized quantifier evaluation using explode\sphinxhyphen{}evaluate\sphinxhyphen{}group strategy.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Performance Loop 290: Replaces O(rows × elements) evaluation pattern
with single exploded frame evaluation, reducing 10,000 → 1 BindingFrame allocation.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Strategy:
1. Explode all (row, element) pairs into one flat frame
2. Evaluate WHERE condition once on flattened frame
3. Group results back by original row and apply quantifier logic
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{q}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.Quantifier}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Quantifier}}}}}) – Quantifier AST node.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{expression\_evaluator}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.evaluator_protocol.ExpressionEvaluatorProtocol}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ExpressionEvaluatorProtocol}}}}}) – Main expression evaluator for recursive evaluation.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.Series}} of boolean results.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
FrameSeries

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{eval\_map\_literal() (pycypher.collection\_evaluator.CollectionExpressionEvaluator method)@\spxentry{eval\_map\_literal()}\spxextra{pycypher.collection\_evaluator.CollectionExpressionEvaluator method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.collection_evaluator.CollectionExpressionEvaluator.eval_map_literal}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{eval\_map\_literal}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{map\_literal}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{expression\_evaluator}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Evaluate \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\{key: expr, ...\}}} map literal.

\sphinxAtStartPar
If the entries (converted expression forms) are populated, each expression
is evaluated per\sphinxhyphen{}row. Otherwise the raw value dict (primitives only) is
returned as\sphinxhyphen{}is.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Accepts the actual {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.MapLiteral}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MapLiteral}}}}} AST node
directly — \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{entries}} is a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{dict{[}str, Expression{]}}} and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{value}}
is a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{dict{[}str, Any{]}}}.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{map\_literal}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.MapLiteral}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{MapLiteral}}}}}) – The {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.MapLiteral}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MapLiteral}}}}} AST node.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{expression\_evaluator}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.evaluator_protocol.ExpressionEvaluatorProtocol}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ExpressionEvaluatorProtocol}}}}}) – Main expression evaluator for recursive evaluation.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\index{eval\_map\_projection() (pycypher.collection\_evaluator.CollectionExpressionEvaluator method)@\spxentry{eval\_map\_projection()}\spxextra{pycypher.collection\_evaluator.CollectionExpressionEvaluator method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.collection_evaluator.CollectionExpressionEvaluator.eval_map_projection}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{eval\_map\_projection}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{mp}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{expression\_evaluator}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Evaluate \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{n\{.prop, key: expr, .*\}}} map projection.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Returns a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.Series}} of dicts — one per row — where each dict
contains the projected keys and their values.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Three element forms are supported:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MapElement(property="name")}} (no expression) — copies the named
property from the source variable.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MapElement(property="k", expression=expr)}} — evaluates \sphinxstyleemphasis{expr} and
stores the result under key \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"k"}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MapElement(all\_properties=True)}} — includes every non\sphinxhyphen{}ID property
of the source variable (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{.*}} wildcard).

\end{itemize}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{mp}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.MapProjection}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{MapProjection}}}}}) – MapProjection AST node.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{expression\_evaluator}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.evaluator_protocol.ExpressionEvaluatorProtocol}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ExpressionEvaluatorProtocol}}}}}) – Main expression evaluator for recursive evaluation.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.Series}} of projected map objects.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
FrameSeries

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}



\subsubsection{Scalar Functions}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:scalar-functions}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Built\sphinxhyphen{}in Cypher scalar function registry (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{toString()}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{toInteger()}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{trim()}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{abs()}}, math, string, temporal, and type\sphinxhyphen{}predicate functions).
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.scalar\_functions@\spxentry{pycypher.scalar\_functions}}\index{pycypher.scalar\_functions@\spxentry{pycypher.scalar\_functions}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.scalar_functions}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Scalar function registry and implementations for Cypher expressions.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Scalar functions operate on entire pandas Series at once (vectorized across
rows), as opposed to aggregation functions which reduce multiple rows to one
value.  Functions never call Python per\sphinxhyphen{}row loops where a vectorised
alternative exists.


\paragraph{Vectorisation strategy by category}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:vectorisation-strategy-by-category}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Math} (abs, ceil, floor, sign, sqrt, cbrt, log, log2, log10, exp, pow,
hypot, fmod): numpy C\sphinxhyphen{}level operations via a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_make\_math1\_np}} /
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_make\_trig1\_np}} factory.  One numpy array call per Series.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Trigonometric} (sin, cos, tan, asin, acos, atan, atan2, sinh, cosh,
tanh, cot, haversin, degrees, radians): same numpy factory.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{String predicates} (startsWith, endsWith, contains): pandas \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{.str}}
Cython accessor; \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{contains}} always uses \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{regex=False}} for literal
matching.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{String transforms} (toUpper, toLower, trim, ltrim, rtrim, etc.):
pandas \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{.str}} accessor.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Type conversion} (toInteger, toFloat): \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.to\_numeric}} + numpy
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{np.fix}}; no per\sphinxhyphen{}row Python.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
All remaining categories use \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.Series.apply()}} because their logic is
inherently element\sphinxhyphen{}wise (e.g. list operations, temporal parsing, encoding).
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{round()}} uses Python’s \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{decimal}} module (no numpy equivalent).  It
supports an optional third \sphinxstyleemphasis{mode} argument selecting one of seven Neo4j 5.x
rounding modes: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{HALF\_UP}} (default, ties away from zero), \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{HALF\_DOWN}}
(ties toward zero), \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{HALF\_EVEN}} (banker’s rounding), \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{CEILING}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{FLOOR}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{UP}} (always away from zero), \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DOWN}} (truncation).

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
This module provides:
\sphinxhyphen{} ScalarFunctionRegistry: Singleton registry for managing scalar functions
\sphinxhyphen{} Built\sphinxhyphen{}in function implementations:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{String}: toUpper, toLower, upper, lower, trim, ltrim, rtrim,
substring, size, left, right, replace, split, reverse, length, isEmpty

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Extended string}: lpad, rpad, repeat, btrim, indexOf, charAt, char, charCodeAt, normalize,
toStringOrNull, startsWith, endsWith, contains, byteSize, join

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Type conversion}: toString, toInteger, toFloat, toBoolean,
toBooleanOrNull, toIntegerOrNull, toFloatOrNull

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Math}: abs, ceil, floor, round, sign, sqrt, cbrt, log, exp, log10, pow,
sinh, cosh, tanh, log2, hypot, fmod, gcd, lcm

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Bitwise}: bitAnd, bitOr, bitXor, bitNot, bitShiftLeft, bitShiftRight

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Trigonometric}: sin, cos, tan, asin, acos, atan, atan2, cot, haversin

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Angle conversion}: degrees, radians

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Constants and random}: pi, e, rand

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{List}: head, last, tail, range, sort, flatten, toStringList, toIntegerList, toFloatList, toBooleanList, toList, min, max

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Map}: keys, values, properties

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Temporal (parse)}: date, datetime, localdatetime, duration

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Temporal (truncate)}: date.truncate, datetime.truncate, localdatetime.truncate

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Temporal (now)}: timestamp, localtime, localdate

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Type introspection}: valueType

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Type predicates}: isString, isInteger, isFloat, isBoolean, isList, isMap

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Hash \& encoding}: md5, sha1, sha256, encodeBase64, decodeBase64

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Utility}: coalesce, id, elementId, nullIf, isNaN, isInfinite, isFinite, infinity, randomUUID, exists

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Function aliases}: now (timestamp), len (length), str (toString),
int (toInteger), float (toFloat), bool (toBoolean)

\end{itemize}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Plugin architecture for registering custom functions

\end{itemize}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k+kn}{import}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pandas}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{as}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pd}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{scalar\PYGZus{}functions}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{ScalarFunctionRegistry}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{registry} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ScalarFunctionRegistry}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}instance}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{input\PYGZus{}series} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{Series}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{hello}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{world}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{registry}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{toUpper}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{n}{input\PYGZus{}series}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{result}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{tolist}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{[\PYGZsq{}HELLO\PYGZsq{}, \PYGZsq{}WORLD\PYGZsq{}]}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\index{ScalarFunctionRegistry (class in pycypher.scalar\_functions)@\spxentry{ScalarFunctionRegistry}\spxextra{class in pycypher.scalar\_functions}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.scalar_functions.ScalarFunctionRegistry}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.scalar\_functions.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{ScalarFunctionRegistry}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Singleton registry for scalar functions.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This registry manages all scalar functions available in Cypher expressions.
Functions are registered at module import time and can be executed by name.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Architecture:
\sphinxhyphen{} Singleton pattern ensures single source of truth
\sphinxhyphen{} Function names are case\sphinxhyphen{}insensitive for Cypher compatibility
\sphinxhyphen{} All functions operate on pandas Series (vectorized operations)
\sphinxhyphen{} Returns Series with same length as inputs

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{WHERE clause usage}: all registered functions are safe for use in WHERE
clause predicates (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MATCH ... WHERE f(p.prop) = value}}).  They operate
row\sphinxhyphen{}by\sphinxhyphen{}row and are evaluated by \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ExpressionEvaluator.\_evaluate\_scalar\_function}}
which joins against the entity table on\sphinxhyphen{}demand to fetch property values.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{NOT permitted in WHERE}: aggregation functions (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{count}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{sum}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{avg}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{min}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{max}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{collect}}) are intentionally absent from this registry.
The query translator in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{star.py}} guards against aggregations in WHERE predicates
and raises a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}} with an actionable message before evaluation begins.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Null safety contract}: every registered function must return \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{NaN}}/\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}}
for null inputs rather than raising.  All built\sphinxhyphen{}in functions satisfy this contract.
Custom functions registered via \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{register\_function}} should follow the same rule.
\subsubsection*{Example}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{registry} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ScalarFunctionRegistry}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}instance}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{registry}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{register\PYGZus{}function}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{name}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{double}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n+nb}{callable}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k}{lambda} \PYG{n}{s}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n}{s} \PYG{o}{*} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{min\PYGZus{}args}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{max\PYGZus{}args}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{description}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Double the value}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{example}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{double(3) → 6}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{registry}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{double}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{Series}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{3}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{max\PYGZus{}args}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,}

\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{example}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{toUpper(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{hello}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{) → }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{HELLO}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }\PYG{p}{)}

\subsubsection{Scalar Function Evaluator}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:scalar-function-evaluator}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Dispatches scalar function calls from AST nodes to the registered
implementations.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.scalar\_function\_evaluator@\spxentry{pycypher.scalar\_function\_evaluator}}\index{pycypher.scalar\_function\_evaluator@\spxentry{pycypher.scalar\_function\_evaluator}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.scalar_function_evaluator}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Scalar Function Evaluator for Cypher Scalar and Graph Introspection Functions.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This module provides the ScalarFunctionEvaluator class, extracted from BindingExpressionEvaluator
as part of Architecture Loop Phase 5. It handles evaluation of scalar functions including
graph introspection functions that require pre\sphinxhyphen{}argument evaluation.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Architecture Loop Phase 5 Note:
This extraction follows the proven delegation pattern established in previous loops,
maintaining 100\% backward compatibility while achieving clear separation of concerns.
\index{ScalarFunctionEvaluator (class in pycypher.scalar\_function\_evaluator)@\spxentry{ScalarFunctionEvaluator}\spxextra{class in pycypher.scalar\_function\_evaluator}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.scalar_function_evaluator.ScalarFunctionEvaluator}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.scalar\_function\_evaluator.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{ScalarFunctionEvaluator}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{frame}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{keyword-only-separator}{\DUrole{o}{\sphinxstyleabbreviation{*}}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{evaluator\_factory}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Evaluates Cypher scalar functions and graph introspection functions.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class handles the evaluation of scalar function calls, with special handling
for graph introspection functions that need to access variable metadata before
argument evaluation. Functions handled include:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxstylestrong{Graph Introspection Functions} (pre\sphinxhyphen{}evaluation):}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
labels(n) → returns entity type label list

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
type(r) → returns relationship type string

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
keys(n) → returns property column names (excluding internal columns)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
properties(n) → returns property dict (with shadow layer support)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
startNode(r) → returns relationship source node ID

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
endNode(r) → returns relationship target node ID

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxstylestrong{Path Length Function}:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
length(path\_var) → returns hop count from variable\sphinxhyphen{}length path

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Registry Delegation}: All other scalar functions are delegated to
ScalarFunctionRegistry for processing.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Aggregation Prevention}: Prevents aggregation functions from being
used in scalar expression contexts.

\end{itemize}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}
\index{\_\_init\_\_() (pycypher.scalar\_function\_evaluator.ScalarFunctionEvaluator method)@\spxentry{\_\_init\_\_()}\spxextra{pycypher.scalar\_function\_evaluator.ScalarFunctionEvaluator method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.scalar_function_evaluator.ScalarFunctionEvaluator.__init__}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_init\_\_}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{frame}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{keyword-only-separator}{\DUrole{o}{\sphinxstyleabbreviation{*}}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{evaluator\_factory}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Initialize scalar function evaluator with binding frame context.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{frame}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}}}}}) – The BindingFrame containing variable bindings, type registry,
and context for function evaluation.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{evaluator\_factory}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.evaluator_protocol.ExpressionEvaluatorFactory}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ExpressionEvaluatorFactory}}}}}) – Factory for constructing a fresh expression
evaluator over a derived frame.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{evaluate\_scalar\_function() (pycypher.scalar\_function\_evaluator.ScalarFunctionEvaluator method)@\spxentry{evaluate\_scalar\_function()}\spxextra{pycypher.scalar\_function\_evaluator.ScalarFunctionEvaluator method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.scalar_function_evaluator.ScalarFunctionEvaluator.evaluate_scalar_function}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{evaluate\_scalar\_function}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{func\_name}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{func\_args}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{expression\_evaluator}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Main entry point for scalar function evaluation.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Handles the following function families before delegating to the
ScalarFunctionRegistry:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{``length(path\_var)``} — returns the hop\sphinxhyphen{}count column stored by
the variable\sphinxhyphen{}length\sphinxhyphen{}path expander (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_path\_hops\_<var>}} column).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{``labels(n)``} — returns \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[}{[}"Person"{]}, …{]}}} using the
type registry; does not touch the entity table.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{``type(r)``} — returns the relationship type string per row.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{``keys(n)``} — returns the list of user\sphinxhyphen{}visible property column
names for the entity or relationship type, excluding internal
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_ID\_\_}}/\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_SOURCE\_\_}}/\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_TARGET\_\_}} columns.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{``properties(n)``} — returns a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{dict}} of all user\sphinxhyphen{}visible
properties per row, reading from the shadow layer if available.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{``startNode(r)`` / ``endNode(r)``} — returns the source or
target node ID of each relationship row.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
If the function name matches a known aggregation (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{count}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{sum}},
etc.) it raises \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}} to prevent accidental use in scalar
contexts (e.g. \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WHERE count(*) > 3}} is invalid Cypher).

\sphinxAtStartPar
All other functions are passed to the
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.scalar_functions.ScalarFunctionRegistry}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ScalarFunctionRegistry}}}}} for
dispatch (built\sphinxhyphen{}in scalar functions such as \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{toInteger}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{toLower}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{substring}}, etc., plus any user\sphinxhyphen{}registered functions).
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{func\_name}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – The Cypher function name (case\sphinxhyphen{}insensitive matching is
applied internally).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{func\_args}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Any}}) – Raw argument value from the AST — either a list of
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.Expression}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Expression}}}}} nodes or a single
expression; normalised to a list by
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_normalize\_func\_args()}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{expression\_evaluator}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.evaluator_protocol.ExpressionEvaluatorProtocol}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ExpressionEvaluatorProtocol}}}}}) – Reference to the parent BindingExpressionEvaluator
for recursive expression evaluation.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.Series}} of per\sphinxhyphen{}row results.

\sphinxlineitem{Raises}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}} – If \sphinxstyleemphasis{func\_name} is an aggregation function used in a
    scalar context, or if the function is unknown to the registry.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
FrameSeries

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}



\subsection{Execution Engine}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:execution-engine}}

\subsubsection{Configuration}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:configuration}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Centralized runtime configuration: query timeouts, cross\sphinxhyphen{}join limits,
result cache sizes, and BFS hop caps.  All settings are driven by
environment variables.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.config@\spxentry{pycypher.config}}\index{pycypher.config@\spxentry{pycypher.config}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.config}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Centralized configuration for pycypher runtime behaviour.

\sphinxAtStartPar
All environment\sphinxhyphen{}variable\sphinxhyphen{}driven configuration is read here at import time
and exposed as module\sphinxhyphen{}level constants.  This avoids scattered \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{os.environ}}
reads and provides a single place to document, validate, and override
runtime settings.


\paragraph{Environment Variables}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:environment-variables}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_QUERY\_TIMEOUT\_S}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Default wall\sphinxhyphen{}clock budget for a single query (seconds).
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}} (default) means no timeout.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_MAX\_CROSS\_JOIN\_ROWS}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Hard ceiling on cross\sphinxhyphen{}join result size (rows).  Prevents accidental
Cartesian explosions.  Default: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{10\_000\_000}}.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_RESULT\_CACHE\_MAX\_MB}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Maximum in\sphinxhyphen{}memory size for the query result cache (megabytes).
Default: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{100}}.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_RESULT\_CACHE\_TTL\_S}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Time\sphinxhyphen{}to\sphinxhyphen{}live for cached query results (seconds).  \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{0}} (default)
means entries never expire (only evicted by size pressure).

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_MAX\_UNBOUNDED\_PATH\_HOPS}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Hard cap on BFS hops for unbounded variable\sphinxhyphen{}length paths (e.g.
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[}*{]}}}).  Default: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{20}}.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_AST\_CACHE\_MAX}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Maximum number of parsed ASTs cached per \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{GrammarParser}} instance.
LRU eviction when full.  Default: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{1024}}.  \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{0}} disables caching.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_MAX\_QUERY\_SIZE\_BYTES}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Hard ceiling on raw query string size (bytes).  Rejects queries larger
than this before parsing.  Default: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{1\_048\_576}} (1 MiB).

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_MAX\_QUERY\_NESTING\_DEPTH}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Maximum nesting depth for AST traversal (e.g. deeply nested WHERE
clauses).  Prevents stack exhaustion on adversarial inputs.
Default: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{200}}.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_MAX\_COLLECTION\_SIZE}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Hard ceiling on generated collection sizes — \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{range()}} lists,
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{repeat()}}/\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{lpad()}}/\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{rpad()}} output lengths, and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{UNWIND}}
expansion.  Prevents memory exhaustion from adversarial inputs.
Default: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{1\_000\_000}} (1 M elements/characters).

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_AUDIT\_LOG}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Enable structured query audit logging.  Set to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{1}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{true}}, or
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{yes}} to emit one JSON record per query to the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.audit}}
logger.  Off by default.  Records include query ID, timestamp,
elapsed time, row count, and parameter names (never values).

\end{description}
\subsubsection*{Examples}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Set a 30\sphinxhyphen{}second query timeout and reduce the cross\sphinxhyphen{}join ceiling:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{export} \PYG{n}{PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}QUERY\PYGZus{}TIMEOUT\PYGZus{}S}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{30}
\PYG{n}{export} \PYG{n}{PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}MAX\PYGZus{}CROSS\PYGZus{}JOIN\PYGZus{}ROWS}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{100000}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Disable AST caching (useful for grammar development):

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{export} \PYG{n}{PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}AST\PYGZus{}CACHE\PYGZus{}MAX}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{0}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Increase the result cache to 500 MB with a 5\sphinxhyphen{}minute TTL:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{export} \PYG{n}{PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}RESULT\PYGZus{}CACHE\PYGZus{}MAX\PYGZus{}MB}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{500}
\PYG{n}{export} \PYG{n}{PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}RESULT\PYGZus{}CACHE\PYGZus{}TTL\PYGZus{}S}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{300}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Inspect active configuration at runtime:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{nmetl} \PYG{n}{config} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{n}{show}\PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{n}{effective}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}
\index{AST\_CACHE\_MAX\_ENTRIES (in module pycypher.config)@\spxentry{AST\_CACHE\_MAX\_ENTRIES}\spxextra{in module pycypher.config}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.config.AST_CACHE_MAX_ENTRIES}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.config.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{AST\_CACHE\_MAX\_ENTRIES}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{int}}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }1024}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Maximum number of parsed ASTs to cache per GrammarParser instance.

\sphinxAtStartPar
When the cache reaches this size, the least\sphinxhyphen{}recently\sphinxhyphen{}used entry is evicted.
Set to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{0}} to disable AST caching entirely.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{COMPLEXITY\_WARN\_THRESHOLD (in module pycypher.config)@\spxentry{COMPLEXITY\_WARN\_THRESHOLD}\spxextra{in module pycypher.config}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.config.COMPLEXITY_WARN_THRESHOLD}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.config.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{COMPLEXITY\_WARN\_THRESHOLD}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{int}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{|}\DUrole{w}{ }\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html\#None}{None}}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }None}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Soft threshold for query complexity warnings.  Queries scoring above this
emit a warning but still execute.  \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}} (default, env \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{0}}) disables
warnings.  Set lower than \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MAX\_COMPLEXITY\_SCORE}} to get early alerts.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{apply\_preset() (in module pycypher.config)@\spxentry{apply\_preset()}\spxextra{in module pycypher.config}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.config.apply_preset}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.config.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{apply\_preset}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{name}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Apply a named configuration preset by updating module globals.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Available presets:
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"development"}} (default)}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Safe for learning — no timeouts, generous limits, no rate limiting.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"production"}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Defensive limits for user\sphinxhyphen{}facing queries — 30 s timeout,
100 K cross\sphinxhyphen{}join ceiling, complexity gate at 100, rate limiting.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"high\_performance"}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Trusted environment — 2 min timeout, 10 M cross\sphinxhyphen{}join ceiling,
500 MB cache, no complexity gate, no rate limiting.

\end{description}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Example:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{config}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{apply\PYGZus{}preset}
\PYG{n}{apply\PYGZus{}preset}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{production}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Raises}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}} – If \sphinxstyleemphasis{name} is not a recognised preset.

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\begin{fulllineitems}

\subsubsection{Backend Engine}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:backend-engine}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Protocol\sphinxhyphen{}based abstraction layer for pluggable DataFrame computation
backends.  Includes \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PandasBackend}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DuckDBBackend}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PolarsBackend}},
health checks, and a circuit breaker for graceful failover.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.backend\_engine@\spxentry{pycypher.backend\_engine}}\index{pycypher.backend\_engine@\spxentry{pycypher.backend\_engine}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.backend_engine}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Backend abstraction layer for pluggable DataFrame engines.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Provides a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BackendEngine}} protocol that allows the query engine to work
with different DataFrame backends (pandas, Dask, DuckDB, Polars) without
coupling to any specific implementation.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The protocol surface is derived from an audit of the actual pandas API calls
in the PyCypher execution path (688 import references, 918 API calls across
18 source files).  Rather than abstracting all 918 calls, the protocol
captures the \textasciitilde{}15 primitive operations that \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{star.py}},
and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MutationEngine}} depend on.


\paragraph{Architecture}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:id67}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
BackendEngine (Protocol)
├── PandasBackend     — current default, zero\PYGZhy{}cost wrapper
├── DuckDBBackend     — analytical queries, SQL\PYGZhy{}based operations
├── PolarsBackend     — Arrow\PYGZhy{}native, lazy\PYGZhy{}evaluated scaling
└── (future) DaskBackend
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Concrete implementations live in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.backends}}:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.backends.pandas\_backend}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.backends.duckdb\_backend}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.backends.polars\_backend}}

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Usage:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{engine} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{select\PYGZus{}backend}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{hint}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{auto}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{estimated\PYGZus{}rows}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{500\PYGZus{}000}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{ids} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{engine}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{scan\PYGZus{}entity}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{source\PYGZus{}obj}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{filtered} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{engine}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{filter}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{ids}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{mask}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{joined} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{engine}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{join}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{left}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{right}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{on}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}ID\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{engine}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{to\PYGZus{}pandas}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{joined}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\paragraph{Integration Status}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:integration-status}}
\begin{sphinxadmonition}{note}{Note:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Partially integrated into the execution path.}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BackendEngine}} protocol is wired into the core execution path via
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}} delegation.  When \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{context.backend}} is available, the
following operations route through the backend:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame.join()}} — inner joins with strategy hints

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame.left\_join()}} — left outer joins (OPTIONAL MATCH)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame.cross\_join()}} — Cartesian products

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame.filter()}} — boolean mask filtering

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame.rename()}} — column renaming

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.pattern_matcher}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pattern\_matcher}}}}} — concat and distinct for multi\sphinxhyphen{}type
scans and variable\sphinxhyphen{}length path expansion

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Selecting \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{backend="duckdb"}} at \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Context}} construction now routes
these operations through the DuckDB engine.
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.mutation_engine}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{mutation\_engine}}}}} — CREATE (concat), DELETE/DETACH DELETE
(filter) route through backend when available

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Not yet delegated (future work):
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Property resolution in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame.get\_property()}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Aggregation operations in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{AggregationEvaluator}}

\end{itemize}
\end{sphinxadmonition}
\index{BackendEngine (class in pycypher.backend\_engine)@\spxentry{BackendEngine}\spxextra{class in pycypher.backend\_engine}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.backend_engine.BackendEngine}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.backend\_engine.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{BackendEngine}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{o}{*}\DUrole{n}{args}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{o}{**}\DUrole{n}{kwargs}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Protocol}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Protocol}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Protocol for pluggable DataFrame computation backends.

\sphinxAtStartPar
All methods operate on “frames” — the backend\sphinxhyphen{}specific DataFrame type.
The pandas backend uses \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.DataFrame}}; other backends use their native
types and convert to pandas only at materialisation boundaries.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The protocol covers five categories of operation, mapped from the actual
usage patterns in the PyCypher execution path:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Scan} — \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{scan\_entity}} loads entity IDs from source data.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Transform} — \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{filter}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{join}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{rename}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{concat}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{distinct}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{assign\_column}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{drop\_columns}} reshape frames.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Aggregate} — \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{aggregate}} performs grouped or full\sphinxhyphen{}table aggregation.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Order} — \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{sort}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{limit}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{skip}} control output ordering.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Materialise} — \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{to\_pandas}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{row\_count}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{is\_empty}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{memory\_estimate\_bytes}} convert or inspect frames.

\end{enumerate}

\index{CircuitBreaker (class in pycypher.backend\_engine)@\spxentry{CircuitBreaker}\spxextra{class in pycypher.backend\_engine}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.backend_engine.CircuitBreaker}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.backend\_engine.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{CircuitBreaker}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{failure\_threshold}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{3}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{recovery\_timeout}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{60.0}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Per\sphinxhyphen{}backend circuit breaker with configurable thresholds.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Tracks consecutive failures for a backend.  When failures exceed
\sphinxstyleemphasis{failure\_threshold}, the circuit opens and the backend is bypassed for
\sphinxstyleemphasis{recovery\_timeout} seconds.  After the timeout, one probe request is
allowed; if it succeeds the circuit closes, otherwise it reopens.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{failure\_threshold}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Consecutive failures before opening the circuit.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{recovery\_timeout}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Seconds to wait before probing a failed backend.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}
\index{\_\_init\_\_() (pycypher.backend\_engine.CircuitBreaker method)@\spxentry{\_\_init\_\_()}\spxextra{pycypher.backend\_engine.CircuitBreaker method}}

{}

circuit open.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{recovery\_timeout}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Seconds before attempting a probe after open.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\index{DuckDBBackend (class in pycypher.backend\_engine)@\spxentry{DuckDBBackend}\spxextra{class in pycypher.backend\_engine}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.backend_engine.DuckDBBackend}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.backend\_engine.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{DuckDBBackend}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{keyword-only-separator}{\DUrole{o}{\sphinxstyleabbreviation{*}}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{memory\_limit}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{temp\_directory}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{max\_temp\_directory\_size}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{preserve\_insertion\_order}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
DuckDB\sphinxhyphen{}based backend for analytical workloads.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Uses DuckDB’s in\sphinxhyphen{}process OLAP engine for efficient columnar operations.
Particularly effective for:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Large aggregations (GROUP BY with many groups)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Join optimisation (DuckDB’s query planner handles strategy selection)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Sort/limit composition (DuckDB fuses ORDER BY + LIMIT efficiently)

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{sort()}} method returns a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DuckDBLazyFrame}} so that a subsequent
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{limit()}} can compose ORDER BY + LIMIT into a single DuckDB query.
All other operations accept \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DuckDBLazyFrame}} inputs transparently
and return \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.DataFrame}} for full backward compatibility.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

\index{\_\_init\_\_() (pycypher.backend\_engine.DuckDBBackend method)@\spxentry{\_\_init\_\_()}\spxextra{pycypher.backend\_engine.DuckDBBackend method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.backend_engine.DuckDBBackend.__init__}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_init\_\_}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{keyword-only-separator}{\DUrole{o}{\sphinxstyleabbreviation{*}}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{memory\_limit}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{temp\_directory}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{max\_temp\_directory\_size}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{preserve\_insertion\_order}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Create a new DuckDB backend with an in\sphinxhyphen{}memory connection.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The connection is created immediately and held for the lifetime of
the backend.  Use as a context manager or call {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.backend_engine.DuckDBBackend.close}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{close()}}}}} to
release the underlying DuckDB resources.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Spill / out\sphinxhyphen{}of\sphinxhyphen{}core settings are \sphinxstylestrong{opt\sphinxhyphen{}in}: any argument left as
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}} falls back to its \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_DUCKDB\_*}} environment variable
(see \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_spill\_config()}}), and if that is also unset the DuckDB
default is used unchanged.  Settings are passed through DuckDB’s
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{connect(config=...)}} dict rather than interpolated into SQL, so
operator\sphinxhyphen{}supplied values cannot inject SQL.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{memory\_limit}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – Soft RAM budget before DuckDB spills to disk
(e.g. \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"4GB"}}).  Env: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_DUCKDB\_MEMORY\_LIMIT}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{temp\_directory}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – Directory for spilled intermediates.
Env: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_DUCKDB\_TEMP\_DIRECTORY}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{max\_temp\_directory\_size}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – Cap on spill directory size
(e.g. \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"50GB"}}).
Env: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_DUCKDB\_MAX\_TEMP\_DIRECTORY\_SIZE}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{preserve\_insertion\_order}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – When \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{False}}, DuckDB may reorder rows
to reduce memory on large results.  Left unset by default so
ordering semantics are unchanged.
Env: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_DUCKDB\_PRESERVE\_INSERTION\_ORDER}}.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Explicitly close the DuckDB connection.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Safe to call multiple times — subsequent calls are no\sphinxhyphen{}ops.  Uses
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{getattr}} so it is also safe when \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_init\_\_}} raised before
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_conn}} was assigned (e.g. invalid spill config), which \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_del\_\_}}
would otherwise turn into an \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{AttributeError}} at GC time.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Sort via DuckDB — returns lazy for sort+limit fusion.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Returns a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DuckDBLazyFrame}} so that a subsequent \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{limit()}}
can compose ORDER BY + LIMIT into a single DuckDB query instead
of materialising the full sort result first.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DuckDBLazyFrame}} auto\sphinxhyphen{}materialises when accessed via pandas
methods, so callers that don’t chain \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{limit()}} still get correct
results transparently.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Transparent wrapper that records per\sphinxhyphen{}operation timing and counts.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Delegates all \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BackendEngine}} operations to an inner backend while
recording timing, operation counts, and emitting DEBUG\sphinxhyphen{}level log
messages for each operation.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

\index{PandasBackend (class in pycypher.backend\_engine)@\spxentry{PandasBackend}\spxextra{class in pycypher.backend\_engine}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.backend_engine.PandasBackend}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.backend\_engine.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{PandasBackend}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Pandas\sphinxhyphen{}based backend — wraps existing pandas operations.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This is the default backend and provides identical behaviour to the
current codebase.  It serves as:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The reference implementation for the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BackendEngine}} protocol.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The fallback when other backends are unavailable or inappropriate.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
A zero\sphinxhyphen{}cost abstraction — no overhead compared to raw pandas calls.

\end{enumerate}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{join}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{left}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{right}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{on}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{how}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'inner'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{strategy}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'auto'}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Merge via pandas with optional strategy routing.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Strategy effects on PandasBackend:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{'auto'}} / \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{'hash'}}: default \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.merge}} (uses hash internally).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{'broadcast'}}: swap left/right so the smaller side is the build
table — reduces hash table memory for asymmetric joins.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{'merge'}}: sort both sides on the join key first, then merge.
Optimal when inputs are already sorted.

\end{itemize}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

Return an independent copy so the caller can mutate freely.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The copy is O(1) under pandas 3.0+ CoW until the caller actually
mutates the data.  Required because \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{inplace=True}} operations
bypass CoW and mutate the original.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}

\index{PolarsBackend (class in pycypher.backend\_engine)@\spxentry{PolarsBackend}\spxextra{class in pycypher.backend\_engine}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.backend_engine.PolarsBackend}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.backend\_engine.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{PolarsBackend}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Polars\sphinxhyphen{}based backend for single\sphinxhyphen{}machine scaling (1GB–50GB).

\sphinxAtStartPar
Uses Polars’ Apache Arrow\sphinxhyphen{}native columnar format with lazy evaluation
and query optimisation.  Key advantages over pandas:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Arrow\sphinxhyphen{}native}: Zero\sphinxhyphen{}copy interop with Arrow, Parquet, IPC.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Lazy evaluation}: Builds a query plan, optimises, then executes.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Multi\sphinxhyphen{}threaded}: Automatic parallelism across CPU cores.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Memory\sphinxhyphen{}efficient}: Columnar + lazy means only materialised data
is held in memory.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The backend accepts pandas DataFrames at API boundaries (to match the
protocol) and converts internally.  For maximum performance, future
work will accept Polars LazyFrames directly from data sources.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

\index{\_\_init\_\_() (pycypher.backend\_engine.SparkBackend method)@\spxentry{\_\_init\_\_()}\spxextra{pycypher.backend\_engine.SparkBackend method}}
