\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_init\_\_}}}
{}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Create or attach to a SparkSession.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Uses \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{getOrCreate}}; if no session was active beforehand this backend
is considered the owner and will \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{stop()}} the session on
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.backend_engine.SparkBackend.close}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{close()}}}}}.  If a session already existed (e.g. a test fixture or a
shared driver) the backend does not own it and {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.backend_engine.SparkBackend.close}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{close()}}}}} is a
no\sphinxhyphen{}op for the session.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

boundary\sphinxhyphen{}materialised pandas operation.

\end{description}\end{quote}

\index{close() (pycypher.backend\_engine.SparkBackend method)@\spxentry{close()}\spxextra{pycypher.backend\_engine.SparkBackend method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.backend_engine.SparkBackend.close}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{close}}}
{}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Stop the SparkSession only if this backend created it.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Idempotent and safe to call multiple times.  A backend that reused an
existing session never stops it — this keeps the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{close()}} call in
the pipeline run path from tearing down a shared session.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

cannot be expressed against an unordered Spark DataFrame.  This mirrors

Join two frames in Spark, returned as pandas.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleemphasis{strategy} is accepted for protocol compatibility but ignored — Spark’s
Catalyst optimiser selects the join algorithm.  For non\sphinxhyphen{}cross joins the
key column(s) in \sphinxstyleemphasis{on} are coalesced to a single output column (Spark
semantics), matching \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DuckDBBackend.join}}.

\end{description}\end{quote}

engine has already applied any ORDER BY before LIMIT.

{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Run a lightweight health probe against \sphinxstyleemphasis{backend}.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Creates a tiny DataFrame, performs a scan + filter + join cycle, and
verifies the result.  Returns \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{True}} if the backend is operational,
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{False}} on any exception.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{backend}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.backend_engine.BackendEngine}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{BackendEngine}}}}}) – The backend instance to probe.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{True}} if all probe operations succeed with correct results.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{bool}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{keyword-only-separator}{\DUrole{o}{\sphinxstyleabbreviation{*}}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{hint}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'auto'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{estimated\_rows}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{0}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{run\_health\_check}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{False}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{instrument}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{False}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Select the optimal backend engine for the workload.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{hint}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Backend preference — \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{'auto'}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{'pandas'}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{'duckdb'}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{'polars'}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{estimated\_rows}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Estimated total rows to process.  Used by \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{'auto'}}
to decide between backends.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{run\_health\_check}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}}) – If \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{True}}, run a lightweight health probe on the
selected backend before returning it.  On failure the circuit
breaker is tripped and the next backend in the fallback chain
is tried.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{instrument}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}}) – If \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{True}}, wrap the selected backend with
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.backend_engine.InstrumentedBackend}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{InstrumentedBackend}}}}} for timing and count recording.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BackendEngine}} instance.

\sphinxlineitem{Raises}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#RuntimeError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{RuntimeError}}} – If all backends fail health checks (only possible when
    \sphinxstyleemphasis{run\_health\_check} is \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{True}}).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}} – If \sphinxstyleemphasis{hint} is not a recognised backend name.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.backend_engine.BackendEngine}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleemphasis{BackendEngine}}}}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The auto\sphinxhyphen{}selection heuristic:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
< 100K rows → PandasBackend (low overhead, no startup cost)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
>= 100K rows → fallback chain with circuit breaker awareness

\end{itemize}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{select\_backend\_for\_query() (in module pycypher.backend\_engine)@\spxentry{select\_backend\_for\_query()}\spxextra{in module pycypher.backend\_engine}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.backend_engine.select_backend_for_query}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.backend\_engine.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{select\_backend\_for\_query}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{keyword-only-separator}{\DUrole{o}{\sphinxstyleabbreviation{*}}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{current\_backend}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{optimization\_hints}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{estimated\_rows}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{0}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{instrument}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{False}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Re\sphinxhyphen{}evaluate backend selection using post\sphinxhyphen{}optimization query hints.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Called after the query optimizer produces cardinality estimates and
join strategy hints.  Returns a replacement backend if the hints
suggest the current backend is suboptimal, or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}} if no change
is needed.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{current\_backend}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.backend_engine.BackendEngine}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{BackendEngine}}}}}) – The backend currently in use.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{optimization\_hints}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{dict}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{[}}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{object}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{]}}}) – Merged hints from \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{OptimizationPlan}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{estimated\_rows}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Row estimate from the query planner (overrides
the Context\sphinxhyphen{}level entity count if available).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{instrument}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}}) – Wrap the replacement with {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.backend_engine.InstrumentedBackend}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{InstrumentedBackend}}}}}.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A replacement {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.backend_engine.BackendEngine}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BackendEngine}}}}}, or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}} if the current
backend is already optimal.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.backend_engine.BackendEngine}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleemphasis{BackendEngine}}}} | None

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{validate\_identifier() (in module pycypher.backend\_engine)@\spxentry{validate\_identifier()}\spxextra{in module pycypher.backend\_engine}}

\pysigstartsignatures

{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.backend\_engine.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{validate\_identifier}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{name}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Validate that \sphinxstyleemphasis{name} is a safe SQL identifier.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Column and table names interpolated into DuckDB SQL must not contain
characters that could alter query semantics.  This is a defence\sphinxhyphen{}in\sphinxhyphen{}depth
measure on top of DuckDB’s \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"quoted identifier"}} syntax.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{name}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – The identifier to validate.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleemphasis{name} unchanged if valid.

\sphinxlineitem{Raises}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}} – If \sphinxstyleemphasis{name} contains characters outside \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[}A\sphinxhyphen{}Za\sphinxhyphen{}z0\sphinxhyphen{}9\_{]}}}
    or does not start with a letter or underscore.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}



\subsubsection{Query Planner}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:query-planner}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Handles memory estimation, operation reordering, and execution plan
optimization.  Integrates with the backend engine for adaptive join
strategy selection.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.query\_planner@\spxentry{pycypher.query\_planner}}\index{pycypher.query\_planner@\spxentry{pycypher.query\_planner}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.query_planner}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Intelligent query planner for optimised join and aggregation strategies.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The query planner analyses the structure of a query (join cardinalities,
filter selectivity, available indices) and selects optimal execution
strategies.  Think of it as the DHF stack coordinator in \sphinxstyleemphasis{Altered Carbon} —
routing consciousness data through the most efficient neural pathway based
on bandwidth, latency, and sleeve compatibility.


\paragraph{Architecture}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:id68}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
QueryPlanner              — low\PYGZhy{}level strategy selection (join/agg)
QueryPlanAnalyzer         — AST\PYGZhy{}aware analysis (cardinality, memory, pushdown)
├── JoinStrategy (enum: HASH, BROADCAST, MERGE, NESTED\PYGZus{}LOOP)
├── JoinPlan (dataclass: left, right, strategy, estimated\PYGZus{}cost)
├── QueryPlan (dataclass: ordered list of JoinPlans + agg strategy)
└── AnalysisResult (dataclass: per\PYGZhy{}clause cardinality, memory, pushdown)
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The planner is invoked \sphinxstylestrong{before} execution to produce a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{QueryPlan}}.
The executor (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{star.py}}) then follows the plan rather than using a
fixed join order.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Usage:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Low\PYGZhy{}level strategy selection}
\PYG{n}{planner} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{QueryPlanner}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{plan} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{planner}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{plan\PYGZus{}join}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{left\PYGZus{}size}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1\PYGZus{}000\PYGZus{}000}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{right\PYGZus{}size}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{100}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} plan.strategy == JoinStrategy.BROADCAST}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} AST\PYGZhy{}aware analysis}
\PYG{n}{analyzer} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{QueryPlanAnalyzer}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{query\PYGZus{}ast}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{context}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{analyzer}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{analyze}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} result.clause\PYGZus{}cardinalities, result.estimated\PYGZus{}peak\PYGZus{}bytes, etc.}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\index{SORT\_AGG (pycypher.query\_planner.AggStrategy attribute)@\spxentry{SORT\_AGG}\spxextra{pycypher.query\_planner.AggStrategy attribute}}

\pysigline

\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Sort\sphinxhyphen{}based aggregation — sort then scan.
Best when data is already sorted on group keys.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{STREAMING\_AGG (pycypher.query\_planner.AggStrategy attribute)@\spxentry{STREAMING\_AGG}\spxextra{pycypher.query\_planner.AggStrategy attribute}}

\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Streaming aggregation — process chunks, merge partial results.
Best for very large datasets that don’t fit in memory.

\end{fulllineitems}

Available join algorithms, ordered by memory overhead.
\index{HASH (pycypher.query\_planner.JoinStrategy attribute)@\spxentry{HASH}\spxextra{pycypher.query\_planner.JoinStrategy attribute}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.query_planner.JoinStrategy.HASH}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{HASH}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }'hash'}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Hash join — build hash table on the smaller side, probe with the larger.
Best for general\sphinxhyphen{}purpose equi\sphinxhyphen{}joins.  O(N + M) time, O(min(N,M)) space.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{BROADCAST (pycypher.query\_planner.JoinStrategy attribute)@\spxentry{BROADCAST}\spxextra{pycypher.query\_planner.JoinStrategy attribute}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.query_planner.JoinStrategy.BROADCAST}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{BROADCAST}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }'broadcast'}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Broadcast join — replicate the small table to every partition.
Best when one side is orders of magnitude smaller (< 10K rows).
O(N) time, O(small) space.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{MERGE (pycypher.query\_planner.JoinStrategy attribute)@\spxentry{MERGE}\spxextra{pycypher.query\_planner.JoinStrategy attribute}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.query_planner.JoinStrategy.MERGE}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{MERGE}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }'merge'}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Merge join — both sides pre\sphinxhyphen{}sorted on the join key.
Best when data is already sorted (e.g. from ORDER BY or index).
O(N + M) time, O(1) space.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{NESTED\_LOOP (pycypher.query\_planner.JoinStrategy attribute)@\spxentry{NESTED\_LOOP}\spxextra{pycypher.query\_planner.JoinStrategy attribute}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.query_planner.JoinStrategy.NESTED_LOOP}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{NESTED\_LOOP}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }'nested\_loop'}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Nested loop — brute\sphinxhyphen{}force.  Only for very small datasets or
non\sphinxhyphen{}equi joins.  O(N × M) time.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{LEAPFROG (pycypher.query\_planner.JoinStrategy attribute)@\spxentry{LEAPFROG}\spxextra{pycypher.query\_planner.JoinStrategy attribute}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.query_planner.JoinStrategy.LEAPFROG}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{LEAPFROG}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }'leapfrog'}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
LeapfrogTriejoin — worst\sphinxhyphen{}case optimal multi\sphinxhyphen{}way join.
Best for 3+ relations sharing a common join variable (e.g. triangle
queries).  O(N\textasciicircum{}\{w/2\}) vs O(N\textasciicircum{}\{w\sphinxhyphen{}1\}) for iterated binary joins.

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Walks a Cypher AST and Context to produce cardinality, memory, and
join strategy analysis.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This is the Phase 2.1 integration point — called from
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Star.\_execute\_query\_binding\_frame\_inner()}} before execution begins
to produce an \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{AnalysisResult}} that guides strategy selection.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{query}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.Query}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Query}}}}}) – Parsed Cypher query AST.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{context}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.relational_models.Context}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Context}}}}}) – The execution context with entity/relationship tables.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\index{estimate\_predicate\_selectivity() (pycypher.query\_planner.QueryPlanAnalyzer method)@\spxentry{estimate\_predicate\_selectivity()}\spxextra{pycypher.query\_planner.QueryPlanAnalyzer method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.query_planner.QueryPlanAnalyzer.estimate_predicate_selectivity}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{estimate\_predicate\_selectivity}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{predicate}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Estimate the selectivity of a WHERE predicate using column
statistics when available, falling back to heuristic defaults.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Supported predicate patterns:
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{p.age > 30}} → range selectivity from column stats
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{p.name = 'Alice'}} → equality selectivity (1/NDV)
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{p.age > 30 AND p.dept = 'Eng'}} → product of selectivities
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{p.age > 30 OR p.dept = 'Eng'}} → capped union
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{NOT pred}} → 1 \sphinxhyphen{} selectivity
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\subsubsection{Query Analyzer}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:query-analyzer}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Pre\sphinxhyphen{}execution query analysis, planning, and MATCH reordering.  Performs
lazy computation graph planning, rule\sphinxhyphen{}based optimization, cardinality\sphinxhyphen{}based
MATCH clause reordering, LIMIT pushdown, and memory budget enforcement.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.query\_analyzer@\spxentry{pycypher.query\_analyzer}}\index{pycypher.query\_analyzer@\spxentry{pycypher.query\_analyzer}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.query_analyzer}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Pre\sphinxhyphen{}execution query analysis, planning, and MATCH reordering.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Extracted from {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.star}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.star}}}}} to separate query analysis concerns
from the main execution orchestration.  The {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.query_analyzer.QueryAnalyzer}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{QueryAnalyzer}}}}} performs:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Lazy computation graph planning (memory estimation, structural analysis)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Rule\sphinxhyphen{}based query optimization

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Cardinality\sphinxhyphen{}based MATCH clause reordering

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
LIMIT pushdown hint extraction

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Memory budget enforcement

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Cardinality feedback recording

\end{itemize}
\index{QueryAnalyzer (class in pycypher.query\_analyzer)@\spxentry{QueryAnalyzer}\spxextra{class in pycypher.query\_analyzer}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.query_analyzer.QueryAnalyzer}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.query\_analyzer.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{QueryAnalyzer}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{context}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{cardinality\_feedback}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{frame\_joiner}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{agg\_planner}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Pre\sphinxhyphen{}execution query analysis and optimization.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Encapsulates all planning logic that runs before clause\sphinxhyphen{}by\sphinxhyphen{}clause
execution: computation graph building, optimizer rules, cardinality
estimation, memory budget checks, and MATCH reordering.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{context}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.relational_models.Context}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Context}}}}}) – The PyCypher \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Context}} for entity/relationship stats.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{cardinality\_feedback}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.cardinality_estimator.CardinalityFeedbackStore}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{CardinalityFeedbackStore}}}}}) – Feedback store for correcting heuristic estimates.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{frame\_joiner}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.frame_joiner.FrameJoiner}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{FrameJoiner}}}}}) – FrameJoiner for passing pre\sphinxhyphen{}computed join plans.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{agg\_planner}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Any}}) – AggregationPlanner for aggregation detection in LIMIT pushdown.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\index{extract\_limit\_hint() (pycypher.query\_analyzer.QueryAnalyzer method)@\spxentry{extract\_limit\_hint()}\spxextra{pycypher.query\_analyzer.QueryAnalyzer method}}

{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{extract\_limit\_hint}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{query}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Extract a LIMIT value for pushdown if the query pattern is safe.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Returns the integer LIMIT value when the query has the simple pattern
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MATCH ... RETURN ... LIMIT N}} (no aggregation, no DISTINCT, no
ORDER BY, no SKIP, no WITH).  Returns \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}} when pushdown is unsafe.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{query}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Any}}}) – Parsed {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.Query}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Query}}}}} AST node.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Integer limit for pushdown, or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}} if pushdown is unsafe.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{int} | None

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{analyze\_and\_plan() (pycypher.query\_analyzer.QueryAnalyzer method)@\spxentry{analyze\_and\_plan()}\spxextra{pycypher.query\_analyzer.QueryAnalyzer method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.query_analyzer.QueryAnalyzer.analyze_and_plan}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{analyze\_and\_plan}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{query}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Run query planning, memory budget enforcement, and LIMIT pushdown.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Performs all pre\sphinxhyphen{}execution analysis:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Builds a lazy computation graph for memory estimates.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Runs the rule\sphinxhyphen{}based \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{QueryOptimizer}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Runs \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{QueryPlanAnalyzer}} for cardinality and join strategies.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Enforces the memory budget.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Extracts a LIMIT pushdown hint when safe.

\end{itemize}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{query}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Any}}}) – A parsed {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.Query}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Query}}}}} AST node.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
An optional row\sphinxhyphen{}limit hint for MATCH pushdown, or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}}.

\sphinxlineitem{Raises}
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.exceptions.QueryMemoryBudgetError}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{QueryMemoryBudgetError}}}}} – If an explicit memory budget is exceeded.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{int} | None

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{apply\_match\_reordering() (pycypher.query\_analyzer.QueryAnalyzer method)@\spxentry{apply\_match\_reordering()}\spxextra{pycypher.query\_analyzer.QueryAnalyzer method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.query_analyzer.QueryAnalyzer.apply_match_reordering}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{apply\_match\_reordering}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{query}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Reorder consecutive MATCH clauses by estimated cardinality.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Processes MATCH clauses smallest\sphinxhyphen{}first to minimize intermediate
result sizes and reduce cross\sphinxhyphen{}join explosion risk.  Only reorders
\sphinxstyleemphasis{consecutive} MATCH runs — never moves a MATCH past a WITH, RETURN,
SET, or other clause boundary.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Mutates \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{query.clauses}} in place.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}



\subsubsection{Cardinality Estimator}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:cardinality-estimator}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Column and table statistics for cardinality estimation.  Provides
per\sphinxhyphen{}column NDV, null fraction, histograms, and self\sphinxhyphen{}correcting feedback
via actual\sphinxhyphen{}vs\sphinxhyphen{}estimated ratios.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.cardinality\_estimator@\spxentry{pycypher.cardinality\_estimator}}\index{pycypher.cardinality\_estimator@\spxentry{pycypher.cardinality\_estimator}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.cardinality_estimator}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Column and table statistics for cardinality estimation.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Provides reusable statistical primitives that can be consumed by the query
planner, query plan analyzer, or any other component that needs selectivity
estimates.  Extracted from \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{query\_planner.py}} to reduce module coupling
and enable independent testing.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Key classes:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.cardinality_estimator.ColumnStatistics}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ColumnStatistics}}}}} — per\sphinxhyphen{}column NDV, null fraction, histograms.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.cardinality_estimator.TableStatistics}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{TableStatistics}}}}} — lazily computed column statistics for a table.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.cardinality_estimator.CardinalityFeedbackStore}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{CardinalityFeedbackStore}}}}} — accumulates actual\sphinxhyphen{}vs\sphinxhyphen{}estimated
ratios for self\sphinxhyphen{}correcting estimates.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Usage:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{stats} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{TableStatistics}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{df}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{col} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{stats}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{column\PYGZus{}stats}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{age}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{if} \PYG{n}{col} \PYG{o+ow}{is} \PYG{o+ow}{not} \PYG{k+kc}{None}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n}{sel} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{col}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{equality\PYGZus{}selectivity}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} 1/NDV adjusted for nulls}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}
\index{CardinalityFeedbackStore (class in pycypher.cardinality\_estimator)@\spxentry{CardinalityFeedbackStore}\spxextra{class in pycypher.cardinality\_estimator}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.cardinality_estimator.CardinalityFeedbackStore}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.cardinality\_estimator.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{CardinalityFeedbackStore}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Accumulates actual vs estimated cardinality ratios per entity type.

\sphinxAtStartPar
After each query execution, call {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.cardinality_estimator.CardinalityFeedbackStore.record}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{record()}}}}} with the entity types
involved and the (estimated, actual) row counts.  Before a future
estimate, call {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.cardinality_estimator.CardinalityFeedbackStore.correction_factor}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{correction\_factor()}}}}} to get a multiplicative
adjustment derived from historical accuracy.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Thread\sphinxhyphen{}safe via a simple lock.  History is bounded to the most recent
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_MAX\_HISTORY}} observations per entity type.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

Return a multiplicative correction for \sphinxstyleemphasis{entity\_type}.

If the estimator consistently overestimates by 2x, this returns
\textasciitilde{}0.5 so the caller can multiply the heuristic estimate by it.
Returns 1.0 when no history is available.

\index{range\_selectivity() (pycypher.cardinality\_estimator.ColumnStatistics method)@\spxentry{range\_selectivity()}\spxextra{pycypher.cardinality\_estimator.ColumnStatistics method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.cardinality_estimator.ColumnStatistics.range_selectivity}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{range\_selectivity}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{low}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{high}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Selectivity for range predicates (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{col > low}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{col < high}}).

\sphinxAtStartPar
When a histogram is available, estimates selectivity by summing the
fraction of rows in bins that overlap the query range.  Falls back
to a uniform distribution assumption when no histogram is present.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}

\subsubsection{Scan Operators}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:scan-operators}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Scan and filter operators for the BindingFrame execution path.  Handles
entity/relationship table scanning, predicate pushdown, and dtype coercion.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.scan\_operators@\spxentry{pycypher.scan\_operators}}\index{pycypher.scan\_operators@\spxentry{pycypher.scan\_operators}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.scan_operators}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Scan and filter operators for the BindingFrame execution path.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Extracted from {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.binding_frame}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.binding\_frame}}}}} to separate scan\sphinxhyphen{}level
concerns (entity/relationship table scanning, predicate pushdown,
dtype coercion) from the core BindingFrame data container.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Classes:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
EntityScan — produces a BindingFrame of entity IDs for a given type.
RelationshipScan — produces a BindingFrame of relationship IDs.
BindingFilter — filters a BindingFrame by a boolean AST predicate.

\sphinxlineitem{Helpers:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\_coerce\_pushdown\_ids — coerce pushdown IDs to match target column dtype.
\_coerce\_pushdown\_series — coerce a pushdown Series to match target dtype.

\end{description}
\index{EntityScan (class in pycypher.scan\_operators)@\spxentry{EntityScan}\spxextra{class in pycypher.scan\_operators}}

The resulting frame has a single column named \sphinxstyleemphasis{var\_name} containing every
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_ID\_\_}} value from the entity table.  Attributes are \sphinxstylestrong{not} included —
they are fetched on demand via \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame.get\_property()}}.

Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Produces a BindingFrame of all relationship IDs for a given type.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The resulting frame has \sphinxstylestrong{three} columns:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{rel\_var}} — the relationship’s own \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_ID\_\_}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_src\_\{rel\_var\}}} — the source\sphinxhyphen{}node \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_SOURCE\_\_}} ID.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_tgt\_\{rel\_var\}}} — the target\sphinxhyphen{}node \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_TARGET\_\_}} ID.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The source and target columns are structural join keys consumed by the
pattern translator (Phase 5); they are \sphinxstylestrong{not} user\sphinxhyphen{}visible Cypher
variables and are therefore absent from the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{type\_registry}}.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Return a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}} containing relationship IDs for this type.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Supports \sphinxstylestrong{predicate pushdown}: when \sphinxstyleemphasis{source\_ids} or \sphinxstyleemphasis{target\_ids}
are provided, only relationships whose \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_SOURCE\_\_}} / \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_TARGET\_\_}}
column values appear in the given ID set are materialised.  This
avoids loading the full relationship table when the query pattern
already constrains one endpoint.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{context}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Any}}) – The query {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.relational_models.Context}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Context}}}}}.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{source\_ids}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{FrameSeries}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – If provided, only materialise relationships whose
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_SOURCE\_\_}} is in this set.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{target\_ids}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{FrameSeries}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – If provided, only materialise relationships whose
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_TARGET\_\_}} is in this set.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}} with columns \sphinxstyleemphasis{rel\_var}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_src\_\{rel\_var\}}},
and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_tgt\_\{rel\_var\}}}.

\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame}]{\sphinxcrossref{BindingFrame}}}

Filters a BindingFrame by evaluating a boolean AST expression.

\sphinxAtStartPar

Unlike \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{FilterRows}}, it never touches prefixed column names — it
delegates directly to {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_evaluator.BindingExpressionEvaluator}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingExpressionEvaluator}}}}}.

\subsubsection{Query Complexity}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:query-complexity}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Scores a parsed Cypher AST for resource\sphinxhyphen{}consumption risk: clause count,
join count, variable\sphinxhyphen{}length paths, aggregation depth, and cross\sphinxhyphen{}product
potential.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.query\_complexity@\spxentry{pycypher.query\_complexity}}\index{pycypher.query\_complexity@\spxentry{pycypher.query\_complexity}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.query_complexity}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Query complexity scoring for resource protection.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Analyzes a parsed Cypher AST to compute a complexity score based on:
\sphinxhyphen{} Number of clauses
\sphinxhyphen{} Number of MATCH patterns and joins
\sphinxhyphen{} Variable\sphinxhyphen{}length path expansion (unbounded is expensive)
\sphinxhyphen{} Aggregation and DISTINCT operations
\sphinxhyphen{} Nesting depth (FOREACH, subqueries)
\sphinxhyphen{} Cross\sphinxhyphen{}product potential (multiple unrelated MATCH clauses)

\sphinxAtStartPar
The score can be compared against a configurable limit to reject or warn
about queries that are likely to consume excessive resources.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Usage:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{query\PYGZus{}complexity}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{score\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{QueryComplexityError}

\PYG{n}{score} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{score\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{parsed\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} score.total == 15, score.breakdown == \PYGZob{}\PYGZdq{}clauses\PYGZdq{}: 3, \PYGZdq{}joins\PYGZdq{}: 4, ...\PYGZcb{}}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Or with a hard limit:}
\PYG{n}{score} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{score\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{parsed\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{max\PYGZus{}score}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{50}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Raises QueryComplexityError if score exceeds limit}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}
\index{DEFAULT\_WEIGHTS (in module pycypher.query\_complexity)@\spxentry{DEFAULT\_WEIGHTS}\spxextra{in module pycypher.query\_complexity}}

\subsubsection{Query Optimizer}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:query-optimizer}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Rule\sphinxhyphen{}based query optimization pipeline: filter pushdown, limit pushdown,
join reordering, and predicate simplification.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.query\_optimizer@\spxentry{pycypher.query\_optimizer}}\index{pycypher.query\_optimizer@\spxentry{pycypher.query\_optimizer}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.query_optimizer}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Rule\sphinxhyphen{}based query optimizer with pluggable optimization passes.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Provides a framework for applying optimization rules to parsed Cypher ASTs
before execution.  Each rule inspects the AST, estimates cost savings, and
optionally transforms the execution hints.  Think of it as the Bene Gesserit
breeding programme — each optimization rule is a carefully designed genetic
modification applied to the query’s execution DNA.


\paragraph{Architecture}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:id95}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
QueryOptimizer
├── OptimizationRule (abstract)     — single optimization pass
│   ├── FilterPushdownRule          — move WHERE closer to scans
│   ├── LimitPushdownRule           — early termination for simple queries
│   ├── PredicateSimplificationRule — simplify boolean expressions
│   └── JoinReorderingRule          — minimize intermediate result sizes
├── OptimizationResult              — outcome of a single rule
├── OptimizationPlan                — aggregated plan with all applied rules
└── OptimizeStage                   — Pipeline stage adapter
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Usage:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Standalone}
\PYG{n}{optimizer} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{QueryOptimizer}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{default}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{plan} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{optimizer}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{optimize}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{query\PYGZus{}ast}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{context}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{plan}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{explain}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} As Pipeline stage}
\PYG{n}{pipeline} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Pipeline}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{default}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{pipeline}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{insert\PYGZus{}after}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{validate}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{OptimizeStage}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Extending:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k}{class}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nc}{MyRule}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{OptimizationRule}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n}{name} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{my\PYGZus{}rule}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{k}{def}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nf}{analyze}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n+nb+bp}{self}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{ast}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{context}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{:}
        \PYG{k}{return} \PYG{n}{OptimizationResult}\PYG{p}{(}
            \PYG{n}{rule\PYGZus{}name}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n+nb+bp}{self}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{p}{,}
            \PYG{n}{applied}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k+kc}{True}\PYG{p}{,}
            \PYG{n}{description}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Applied my optimization}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
            \PYG{n}{estimated\PYGZus{}speedup}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{1.5}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{n}{optimizer} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{QueryOptimizer}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{n}{MyRule}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}
\index{OptimizationResult (class in pycypher.query\_optimizer)@\spxentry{OptimizationResult}\spxextra{class in pycypher.query\_optimizer}}

\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures

\index{FilterPushdownRule (class in pycypher.query\_optimizer)@\spxentry{FilterPushdownRule}\spxextra{class in pycypher.query\_optimizer}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.query_optimizer.FilterPushdownRule}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.query\_optimizer.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{FilterPushdownRule}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.query_optimizer.OptimizationRule}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{OptimizationRule}}}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Move WHERE filters closer to data source scans.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Detects patterns where a WHERE clause can be applied during pattern
matching rather than as a post\sphinxhyphen{}filter, reducing intermediate result
sizes.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Applies when:
\sphinxhyphen{} MATCH has a WHERE clause referencing only variables from that MATCH
\sphinxhyphen{} WHERE predicates are simple comparisons on indexed properties

\sphinxAtStartPar
Detect opportunities to use graph\sphinxhyphen{}native index scans.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Identifies MATCH patterns with relationship traversals that can benefit
from adjacency index lookups (O(degree) instead of O(E) table scans)
and inline property filters that can use property value indexes
(O(1) instead of O(N) scans).

\subsubsection{Query Validator}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:query-validator}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Combined validation: RETURN clause checks, clause ordering, and
structural rule enforcement.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.query\_validator@\spxentry{pycypher.query\_validator}}\index{pycypher.query\_validator@\spxentry{pycypher.query\_validator}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.query_validator}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Combined query validation for multi\sphinxhyphen{}query composition.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Validates combined Cypher query ASTs for structural integrity:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Clause ordering} — RETURN must be the final clause if present

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{RETURN uniqueness} — at most one RETURN clause allowed

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Structural consistency} — clauses follow valid Cypher sequencing rules

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
All validation errors are collected (not fail\sphinxhyphen{}fast) so the user receives
a complete diagnostic picture.
\index{ValidationResult (class in pycypher.query\_validator)@\spxentry{ValidationResult}\spxextra{class in pycypher.query\_validator}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.query_validator.ValidationResult}}

{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{is\_valid=True}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{errors=<factory>}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures

\sphinxAtStartPar

\end{description}\end{quote}

\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures

\subsubsection{Query Formatter}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:query-formatter}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Cypher query formatting and linting with configurable style rules.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.query\_formatter@\spxentry{pycypher.query\_formatter}}\index{pycypher.query\_formatter@\spxentry{pycypher.query\_formatter}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.query_formatter}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Cypher query formatter and linter.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Normalizes Cypher query formatting with configurable style rules:
uppercase keywords, consistent indentation, clause\sphinxhyphen{}per\sphinxhyphen{}line layout.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Designed for use in:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
CLI: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{nmetl format\sphinxhyphen{}query "MATCH ..."}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Pre\sphinxhyphen{}commit hooks: validate query formatting in CI

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Editor integrations: format\sphinxhyphen{}on\sphinxhyphen{}save via LSP

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Programmatic use: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{format\_query(text)}}

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Usage:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{query\PYGZus{}formatter}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{format\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{lint\PYGZus{}query}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Format a query}
\PYG{n}{formatted} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{format\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{match (n:Person) where n.age \PYGZgt{} 30 return n.name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} MATCH (n:Person)}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} WHERE n.age \PYGZgt{} 30}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} RETURN n.name}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Lint a query (returns list of issues)}
\PYG{n}{issues} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{lint\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{match (n) return n}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} [LintIssue(line=1, message=\PYGZdq{}Keyword \PYGZsq{}match\PYGZsq{} should be uppercase: MATCH\PYGZdq{})]}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Environment variables:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_FORMAT\_UPPERCASE}} — uppercase keywords (default: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{1}})

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_FORMAT\_INDENT}} — indentation width (default: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{2}})

\end{itemize}
\index{format\_query() (in module pycypher.query\_formatter)@\spxentry{format\_query()}\spxextra{in module pycypher.query\_formatter}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.query_formatter.format_query}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.query\_formatter.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{format\_query}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{query}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{keyword-only-separator}{\DUrole{o}{\sphinxstyleabbreviation{*}}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{uppercase}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{True}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{indent}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{2}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Format a Cypher query string.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Applies consistent formatting:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
One clause per line (MATCH, WHERE, RETURN, etc.)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Uppercase keywords

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Normalized whitespace

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Consistent indentation for sub\sphinxhyphen{}clauses

\end{itemize}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{query}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – The Cypher query string to format.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{uppercase}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}}) – Whether to uppercase keywords.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{indent}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Indentation width for sub\sphinxhyphen{}clauses.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The formatted query string.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Lint a Cypher query for formatting issues.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Checks:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Keywords should be uppercase

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
No trailing whitespace

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Consistent use of single or double quotes

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Parse errors (if parser is available)

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{query}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – The Cypher query to lint.

\subsubsection{Query Profiler}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:query-profiler}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Traces individual query execution to identify hot spots, track memory
allocation, and generate optimization recommendations.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.query\_profiler@\spxentry{pycypher.query\_profiler}}\index{pycypher.query\_profiler@\spxentry{pycypher.query\_profiler}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.query_profiler}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Query profiling and bottleneck analysis tools.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Traces individual query execution to identify hot spots, track memory
allocation, and generate optimization recommendations.  Built on top
of the per\sphinxhyphen{}clause timing infrastructure in {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/shared:module-shared.metrics}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{shared.metrics}}}}}.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Usage:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{query\PYGZus{}profiler}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{QueryProfiler}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{star}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{Star}

\PYG{n}{profiler} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{QueryProfiler}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{report} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{profiler}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{profile}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) RETURN p.name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{report}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{report}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{hotspot}\PYG{p}{)}          \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Slowest clause type}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{report}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{recommendations}\PYG{p}{)}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Optimization suggestions}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}
\index{ProfileReport (class in pycypher.query\_profiler)@\spxentry{ProfileReport}\spxextra{class in pycypher.query\_profiler}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.query_profiler.ProfileReport}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.query\_profiler.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{ProfileReport}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{query}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{total\_time\_ms}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{parse\_time\_ms}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{plan\_time\_ms}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clause\_timings}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{row\_count}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{hotspot}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{recommendations}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{memory\_delta\_mb=0.0}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{backend\_timings=<factory>}}}

{}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Return a combined diagnostic summary of clause and backend timings.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Aggregates all profile reports in history into a single summary with:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{query\_count}}: Number of profiled queries.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{clause\_timings}}: Aggregated per\sphinxhyphen{}clause total milliseconds.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{backend\_timings}}: Latest backend operation timing summary.

\end{itemize}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Dict with combined clause\sphinxhyphen{}level and operation\sphinxhyphen{}level metrics.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{dict}{[}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}, \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleemphasis{Any}}{]}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\subsubsection{Input Validator}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:input-validator}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Pre\sphinxhyphen{}parse input sanitization: query length limits, ID checks, content
safety, and parseability verification.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.input\_validator@\spxentry{pycypher.input\_validator}}\index{pycypher.input\_validator@\spxentry{pycypher.input\_validator}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.input_validator}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Input validation for multi\sphinxhyphen{}query composition.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Validates the list of \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{(query\_id, cypher\_string)}} pairs before they
enter the composition pipeline.  Checks performed:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Query ID uniqueness} — duplicate IDs are rejected

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Non\sphinxhyphen{}empty content} — empty or whitespace\sphinxhyphen{}only Cypher strings are rejected

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Non\sphinxhyphen{}empty query IDs} — blank query IDs are rejected

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Parseability} — each Cypher string must be parseable by \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ASTConverter}}

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
All errors are collected (not fail\sphinxhyphen{}fast) for complete diagnostics.

\subsubsection{Frame Joiner}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:frame-joiner}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
BindingFrame join and merge operations: coerce\sphinxhyphen{}join, multi\sphinxhyphen{}MATCH frame
merging, and OPTIONAL MATCH left\sphinxhyphen{}join semantics.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.frame\_joiner@\spxentry{pycypher.frame\_joiner}}\index{pycypher.frame\_joiner@\spxentry{pycypher.frame\_joiner}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.frame_joiner}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
FrameJoiner — frame merging and optional\sphinxhyphen{}match join orchestration.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Extracted from {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.star}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.star}}}}} to isolate the cohesive join
orchestration family (optional match, frame merging, coerce\sphinxhyphen{}join) into
a focused, independently testable module.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Handles:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
OPTIONAL MATCH left\sphinxhyphen{}join semantics with null\sphinxhyphen{}column injection on failure

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Multi\sphinxhyphen{}MATCH frame merging via keyed or cross joins

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Join strategy selection (shared variable → inner join, else cross join)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Seed frame creation for clause\sphinxhyphen{}first queries

\end{itemize}
\index{FrameJoiner (class in pycypher.frame\_joiner)@\spxentry{FrameJoiner}\spxextra{class in pycypher.frame\_joiner}}

\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.frame_joiner.FrameJoiner.coerce_join}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{coerce\_join}}}

{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Join two BindingFrame objects.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Selects the join strategy based on shared variables:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Shared variable}: keyed inner\sphinxhyphen{}join on the first shared column name.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{No shared variable}: Cartesian cross\sphinxhyphen{}join.

\end{itemize}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{frame\_a}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}}}}}) – The left {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}}}}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{frame\_b}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}}}}}) – The right {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}}}}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{join\_plan}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.query_planner.JoinPlan}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{JoinPlan}}}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – Optional pre\sphinxhyphen{}computed \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{JoinPlan}} from
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{QueryPlanAnalyzer}} to avoid redundant planning.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A merged {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}}}}}.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame}]{\sphinxcrossref{BindingFrame}}}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Join multiple frames using LeapfrogTriejoin when applicable.

\sphinxAtStartPar
When 3+ frames share a common variable, uses the worst\sphinxhyphen{}case optimal
LeapfrogTriejoin algorithm. Otherwise falls back to iterated pairwise
joins.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{frames}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{[}}}{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}}}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{]}}}) – Two or more BindingFrames to join.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A single merged BindingFrame.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame}]{\sphinxcrossref{BindingFrame}}}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Merge a new MATCH frame into the existing execution frame.

\sphinxAtStartPar
When a query contains multiple MATCH clauses, each new result frame
must be combined with the accumulated frame via either a keyed join
(shared variable) or a cross\sphinxhyphen{}join (no shared variables).

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Filter pushdown}: WHERE conjuncts that reference only variables
from \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{match\_frame}} are applied \sphinxstyleemphasis{before} the join to reduce
intermediate result sizes.  Remaining predicates (those referencing
variables from both frames) are applied after the join.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{current\_frame}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}}}}}) – The accumulated BindingFrame from preceding clauses.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{match\_frame}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}}}}}) – The new frame produced by the current MATCH clause.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{where\_clause}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.Expression}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Expression}}}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – Optional WHERE predicate node from the MATCH clause.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The merged {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}}}}}.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame}]{\sphinxcrossref{BindingFrame}}}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\subsubsection{LeapfrogTriejoin}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:leapfrogtriejoin}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Worst\sphinxhyphen{}case optimal multi\sphinxhyphen{}way join algorithm (Veldhuizen 2014).  Activated
automatically for 3+ frame joins on a shared variable, achieving O(N\textasciicircum{}\{w/2\})
complexity vs O(N\textasciicircum{}\{w\sphinxhyphen{}1\}) for iterated binary joins.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.leapfrog\_triejoin@\spxentry{pycypher.leapfrog\_triejoin}}\index{pycypher.leapfrog\_triejoin@\spxentry{pycypher.leapfrog\_triejoin}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.leapfrog_triejoin}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
LeapfrogTriejoin — worst\sphinxhyphen{}case optimal join algorithm for multi\sphinxhyphen{}way joins.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Implements the LeapfrogTriejoin algorithm from Veldhuizen 2014 for efficient
multi\sphinxhyphen{}way intersection joins. This is particularly effective for cyclic query
patterns (e.g., triangle queries) where pairwise binary joins produce large
intermediate results.


\paragraph{Architecture}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:id123}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The algorithm operates on \sphinxstylestrong{sorted iterators} over relation columns. For a
multi\sphinxhyphen{}way join on a shared variable, instead of building pairwise hash tables,
all relations are intersected simultaneously via a “leapfrog” scan:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Sort each relation’s join column.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Maintain a cursor per relation, always pointing at the current value.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Advance cursors in round\sphinxhyphen{}robin order, “leaping” past values that cannot
appear in the intersection.

\end{enumerate}

\sphinxAtStartPar
This yields worst\sphinxhyphen{}case optimal time complexity O(N\textasciicircum{}\{w/2\}) for w\sphinxhyphen{}way joins
on relations of size N, versus O(N\textasciicircum{}\{w\sphinxhyphen{}1\}) for iterated binary joins.


\paragraph{Integration}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:integration}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Called from {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.frame_joiner.FrameJoiner}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{FrameJoiner}}}}} when 3+ frames
share a common join variable.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Falls back to pairwise binary joins when the leapfrog strategy is not
applicable (no shared variable across all frames, or only 2 frames).

\end{itemize}
\subsubsection*{References}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Veldhuizen, T. L. (2014). “Leapfrog Triejoin: A Simple, Worst\sphinxhyphen{}Case
Optimal Join Algorithm.” ICDT 2014.

\end{itemize}

\index{leapfrog\_triejoin() (in module pycypher.leapfrog\_triejoin)@\spxentry{leapfrog\_triejoin()}\spxextra{in module pycypher.leapfrog\_triejoin}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.leapfrog_triejoin.leapfrog_triejoin}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.leapfrog\_triejoin.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{leapfrog\_triejoin}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{frames}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{join\_var}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Perform a multi\sphinxhyphen{}way LeapfrogTriejoin on BindingFrames sharing \sphinxstyleemphasis{join\_var}.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This is the main entry point. It:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Sorts each frame’s join column.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Runs the leapfrog intersection to find common keys.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Assembles the result by cross\sphinxhyphen{}producing matching rows per key.

\end{enumerate}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Falls back gracefully for edge cases (empty frames, single frame).
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{frames}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{[}}}{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}}}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{]}}}) – Two or more BindingFrames that all contain column \sphinxstyleemphasis{join\_var}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{join\_var}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – The shared variable name to join on.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A new BindingFrame with the intersection result.

\sphinxlineitem{Raises}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}} – If fewer than 2 frames are provided or \sphinxstyleemphasis{join\_var} is
    missing from any frame.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame}]{\sphinxcrossref{BindingFrame}}}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\subsubsection{Graph Index}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:graph-index}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Graph\sphinxhyphen{}native index structures for accelerating pattern matching: adjacency
indexes, property value indexes, and label\sphinxhyphen{}partitioned indexes.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.graph\_index@\spxentry{pycypher.graph\_index}}\index{pycypher.graph\_index@\spxentry{pycypher.graph\_index}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.graph_index}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Graph\sphinxhyphen{}native index structures for accelerating pattern matching.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Provides adjacency indexes, property value indexes, and label\sphinxhyphen{}partitioned
indexes that transform pattern matching from O(E) full table scans to
O(degree) neighbor lookups.


\paragraph{Architecture}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:id124}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
GraphIndexManager
├── AdjacencyIndex         — per\PYGZhy{}relationship\PYGZhy{}type adjacency lists
│   ├── outgoing[src\PYGZus{}id]   → list of (rel\PYGZus{}id, tgt\PYGZus{}id)
│   └── incoming[tgt\PYGZus{}id]   → list of (rel\PYGZus{}id, src\PYGZus{}id)
├── PropertyValueIndex     — per\PYGZhy{}(entity\PYGZus{}type, property) hash index
│   └── value\PYGZus{}to\PYGZus{}ids[val]  → set of entity IDs
└── EntityLabelIndex       — per\PYGZhy{}label sorted ID arrays for fast membership
    └── ids: np.ndarray    — sorted entity IDs for O(log n) lookup
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Indexes are built lazily on first access and invalidated when the Context
commits mutations (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{commit\_query()}} increments \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_data\_epoch}}).

\sphinxAtStartPar
Usage:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Indexes are managed through the Context:}
\PYG{n}{ctx} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Context}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{entity\PYGZus{}mapping}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{relationship\PYGZus{}mapping}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{manager} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ctx}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{index\PYGZus{}manager}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} lazily created}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Neighbor lookup — O(degree) instead of O(E)}
\PYG{n}{neighbors} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{manager}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}neighbors}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{KNOWS}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{source\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{42}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{direction}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{outgoing}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}

Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Adjacency list index for a single relationship type.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Stores both outgoing (source → targets) and incoming (target → sources)
adjacency lists for O(degree) neighbor lookups instead of O(E) table scans.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

\index{VectorizedPropertyStore (class in pycypher.graph\_index)@\spxentry{VectorizedPropertyStore}\spxextra{class in pycypher.graph\_index}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.graph_index.VectorizedPropertyStore}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.graph\_index.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{VectorizedPropertyStore}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{entity\_type}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{sorted\_ids}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{property\_arrays}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Pre\sphinxhyphen{}sorted entity ID array with aligned property columns for O(n log n) bulk lookups.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Instead of hash\sphinxhyphen{}based \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.Series.map()}} (O(n) amortised but with high constant
factor due to Python\sphinxhyphen{}level hashing) or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.DataFrame.reindex()}} (creates intermediate
DataFrames), this store uses \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{np.searchsorted}} on a pre\sphinxhyphen{}sorted ID array to resolve
entity IDs to row positions, then fancy\sphinxhyphen{}indexes directly into numpy property arrays.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Build cost: O(N log N) for the sort (one\sphinxhyphen{}time, cached).
Lookup cost: O(k log N) for k query IDs against N stored entities.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Fetch multiple properties in a single searchsorted pass.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The binary search is done once and reused for all properties,
making this significantly faster than N separate fetch() calls.

\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{query\_ids}} (\sphinxhref{https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html\#numpy.ndarray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ndarray}}}) – Array of entity IDs to look up.

\end{itemize}

{}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Pre\sphinxhyphen{}build VectorizedPropertyStores for all entity and relationship types.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Call this at query start to ensure the fast O(k log N) path in
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame.get\_property()}} is always available, avoiding fallback
to the slower hash\sphinxhyphen{}based \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.Series.map()}} path.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Build cost is O(N log N) per entity type (one\sphinxhyphen{}time sort), amortised
across all subsequent property lookups in the query.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}
