\subsubsection{Projection Planner}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:projection-planner}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Plans RETURN and WITH clause projections: alias inference, qualification,
and modifier application (ORDER BY, LIMIT, SKIP, DISTINCT).
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.projection\_planner@\spxentry{pycypher.projection\_planner}}\index{pycypher.projection\_planner@\spxentry{pycypher.projection\_planner}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.projection_planner}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
ProjectionPlanner — RETURN/WITH clause evaluation and projection modifiers.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Extracted from {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.star}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.star}}}}} to isolate the cohesive projection
family (alias inference, disambiguation, RETURN evaluation, WITH evaluation,
DISTINCT/ORDER BY/SKIP/LIMIT modifiers) into a focused, independently
testable module.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Handles:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Alias inference from expression AST nodes (Variable, PropertyLookup, etc.)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Alias disambiguation for colliding inferred names

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
RETURN clause evaluation (projection + aggregation + modifiers)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
WITH clause evaluation (projection + aggregation + WHERE + modifiers)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
DISTINCT, ORDER BY (ASC/DESC with NULLS FIRST/LAST), SKIP, LIMIT

\end{itemize}
\index{ProjectionPlanner (class in pycypher.projection\_planner)@\spxentry{ProjectionPlanner}\spxextra{class in pycypher.projection\_planner}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.projection_planner.ProjectionPlanner}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.projection\_planner.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{ProjectionPlanner}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{agg\_planner}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{renderer}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{where\_fn}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Evaluates RETURN and WITH clauses, applying projection modifiers.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Stateless aside from injected dependencies — all mutable state is
passed via method parameters.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{agg\_planner}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.aggregation_planner.AggregationPlanner}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{AggregationPlanner}}}}}) – {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.aggregation_planner.AggregationPlanner}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{AggregationPlanner}}}}}
for aggregation detection and grouped evaluation.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{renderer}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.expression_renderer.ExpressionRenderer}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ExpressionRenderer}}}}}) – {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.expression_renderer.ExpressionRenderer}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ExpressionRenderer}}}}}
for generating human\sphinxhyphen{}readable expression text.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{where\_fn}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Callable}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{[}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{...}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}}}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{]}}}) – Callback to apply a WHERE filter to a frame.  Signature:
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{(where\_expr, result\_frame, fallback\_frame=None) \sphinxhyphen{}> BindingFrame}}.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\index{infer\_alias() (pycypher.projection\_planner.ProjectionPlanner method)@\spxentry{infer\_alias()}\spxextra{pycypher.projection\_planner.ProjectionPlanner method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.projection_planner.ProjectionPlanner.infer_alias}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{infer\_alias}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{expression}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Infer a display alias from an expression when none is given.

\sphinxAtStartPar
For \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Variable}} returns the variable name, for \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PropertyLookup}}
returns the property name (unqualified), and for all other expression
types delegates to the expression renderer which generates a
human\sphinxhyphen{}readable text representation.  Returns \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}} only for
expression types that have no sensible short representation.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{expression}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Any}}}) – AST expression node to inspect.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A string alias, or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}} if no representation is available.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str} | None

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{apply\_projection\_modifiers() (pycypher.projection\_planner.ProjectionPlanner method)@\spxentry{apply\_projection\_modifiers()}\spxextra{pycypher.projection\_planner.ProjectionPlanner method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.projection_planner.ProjectionPlanner.apply_projection_modifiers}}

\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{apply\_projection\_modifiers}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{df}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clause}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{frame}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Apply DISTINCT, ORDER BY, SKIP, and LIMIT to a projected DataFrame.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Called at the end of both {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.projection_planner.ProjectionPlanner.return_from_frame}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{return\_from\_frame()}}}}} and
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.projection_planner.ProjectionPlanner.with_to_binding_frame}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{with\_to\_binding\_frame()}}}}} so that both RETURN and WITH honour the
full set of projection modifiers defined in the AST.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{df}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{pd.DataFrame}}) – The fully\sphinxhyphen{}projected result DataFrame (aliases as columns).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clause}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Any}}) – AST \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Return}} or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{With}} node carrying the modifiers.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{frame}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}}}}}) – The pre\sphinxhyphen{}projection {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}}}}}
used to evaluate ORDER BY expressions that reference original
entity variables.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A new \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.DataFrame}} with modifiers applied.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
pd.DataFrame

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{return\_from\_frame() (pycypher.projection\_planner.ProjectionPlanner method)@\spxentry{return\_from\_frame()}\spxextra{pycypher.projection\_planner.ProjectionPlanner method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.projection_planner.ProjectionPlanner.return_from_frame}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{return\_from\_frame}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{return\_clause}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{frame}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Evaluate a RETURN clause against a BindingFrame and return results.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Handles simple expression projection and aggregation (full\sphinxhyphen{}table and
grouped).  Delegates to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{AggregationPlanner}}.  Applies DISTINCT,
ORDER BY, SKIP, and LIMIT after projection via
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.projection_planner.ProjectionPlanner.apply_projection_modifiers}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{apply\_projection\_modifiers()}}}}}.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{return\_clause}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Any}}) – AST {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.Return}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Return}}}}} node.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{frame}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}}}}}) – Current {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}}}}}.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A plain \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.DataFrame}} with columns matching RETURN aliases.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
pd.DataFrame

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{with\_to\_binding\_frame() (pycypher.projection\_planner.ProjectionPlanner method)@\spxentry{with\_to\_binding\_frame()}\spxextra{pycypher.projection\_planner.ProjectionPlanner method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.projection_planner.ProjectionPlanner.with_to_binding_frame}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{with\_to\_binding\_frame}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{with\_clause}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{frame}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Translate a WITH clause into a new BindingFrame.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Supports three modes:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Simple projection} (no aggregations): evaluate each expression and
build a new frame where the columns are the aliases.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Full aggregation} (all items are aggregations): reduce all rows to
a single aggregated row.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Grouped aggregation} (mixed items): group by non\sphinxhyphen{}aggregation keys,
then compute aggregations per group.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The resulting frame has \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{type\_registry = \{\}}} since the columns are
computed values rather than entity IDs.  \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Variable}} lookups in
subsequent WHERE / RETURN clauses will fall through to plain column
access in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{bindings}}.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar

\subsubsection{Variable Manager}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:variable-manager}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Variable scoping, conflict detection, and safe renaming for multi\sphinxhyphen{}query
composition.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.variable\_manager@\spxentry{pycypher.variable\_manager}}\index{pycypher.variable\_manager@\spxentry{pycypher.variable\_manager}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.variable_manager}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Variable namespace management for Cypher query composition.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Provides conflict detection, systematic renaming, and binding preservation
when composing multiple Cypher queries that may share variable namespaces.


\paragraph{Architecture}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:id135}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
VariableManager
├── collect\PYGZus{}variables()      — gather all defined variable names from AST
├── detect\PYGZus{}conflicts()       — find overlapping names between two ASTs
├── generate\PYGZus{}unique\PYGZus{}name()   — produce collision\PYGZhy{}free variable names
└── rename\PYGZus{}variables()       — deep\PYGZhy{}copy AST with variable names replaced
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The rename operation is \sphinxstyleemphasis{immutable} — it returns a new AST tree and never
mutates the original.  This is critical for composability: the caller can
rename one branch of a UNION without affecting the other.
\index{VariableManager (class in pycypher.variable\_manager)@\spxentry{VariableManager}\spxextra{class in pycypher.variable\_manager}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.variable_manager.VariableManager}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.variable\_manager.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{VariableManager}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Manage variable namespaces across Cypher query ASTs.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Provides utilities for detecting variable conflicts between queries,
generating unique variable names, and rewriting AST trees with
renamed variables while preserving structural binding relationships.

if the candidate already exists in \sphinxstyleemphasis{existing}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.variable_manager.VariableManager.rename_variables}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{rename\_variables}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{node}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{rename\_map}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Return a new AST with variables renamed per the mapping.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Deep\sphinxhyphen{}copies the AST tree, replacing every \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Variable.name}} that
appears as a key in \sphinxstyleemphasis{rename\_map} with the corresponding value.
Variables not in the map are left unchanged.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The original AST is \sphinxstylestrong{never mutated}.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{node}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.ASTNode}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ASTNode}}}}}) – Root AST node to rewrite.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{rename\_map}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{dict}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{[}}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{]}}}) – Mapping from old variable name → new variable name.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A new AST tree with renamed variables.

\sphinxlineitem{Raises}
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.exceptions.SecurityError}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{SecurityError}}}}} – If the AST exceeds the maximum nesting depth.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.ASTNode}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleemphasis{ASTNode}}}}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}



\subsubsection{Pipeline}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pipeline}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Stage\sphinxhyphen{}based query execution pipeline with parse, validate, plan, and
execute stages.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.pipeline@\spxentry{pycypher.pipeline}}\index{pycypher.pipeline@\spxentry{pycypher.pipeline}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.pipeline}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Query execution pipeline abstraction.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Formalises the implicit execution stages in {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.star.Star}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Star}}}}} into
a composable, extensible pipeline architecture.  Think of it as the Holtzman
engine of the query processor — each stage is a precisely calibrated fold in
space\sphinxhyphen{}time that transforms the query from raw text to final results.


\paragraph{Architecture}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:id136}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
Pipeline
├── Stage (abstract)           — one step in the execution flow
│   ├── ParseStage             — string → AST
│   ├── ValidateStage          — semantic checks + complexity scoring
│   ├── PlanStage              — query analysis + memory estimation
│   ├── ExecuteStage           — clause\PYGZhy{}by\PYGZhy{}clause evaluation
│   └── FormatStage            — projection modifiers + result shaping
├── PipelineContext            — shared state flowing through stages
└── PipelineResult             — final output with metadata
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Each stage receives a {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.pipeline.PipelineContext}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PipelineContext}}}}}, transforms it, and passes it
to the next stage.  Stages may short\sphinxhyphen{}circuit (e.g. cache hit) by setting
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{context.result}} early.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Usage:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Default pipeline with all built\PYGZhy{}in stages}
\PYG{n}{pipeline} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Pipeline}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{default}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Custom pipeline with extra validation}
\PYG{n}{pipeline} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Pipeline}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{[}
    \PYG{n}{ParseStage}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{my\PYGZus{}custom\PYGZus{}lint\PYGZus{}stage}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{ValidateStage}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{PlanStage}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{ExecuteStage}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{FormatStage}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pipeline}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{run}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{query}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (n) RETURN n}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{star}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Extending the pipeline:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k}{class}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nc}{MyCustomStage}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{Stage}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n}{name} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{custom\PYGZus{}lint}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}

    \PYG{k}{def}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nf}{execute}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n+nb+bp}{self}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{ctx}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n}{PipelineContext}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{o}{\PYGZgt{}} \PYG{n}{PipelineContext}\PYG{p}{:}
        \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Inspect ctx.ast, modify ctx.metadata, etc.}
        \PYG{k}{return} \PYG{n}{ctx}

\PYG{n}{pipeline} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Pipeline}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{default}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{pipeline}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{insert\PYGZus{}after}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{parse}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{MyCustomStage}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}
\index{Pipeline (class in pycypher.pipeline)@\spxentry{Pipeline}\spxextra{class in pycypher.pipeline}}

Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Mutable state that flows through pipeline stages.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Each stage reads from and writes to this context.  The context
accumulates parsed AST, plan analysis, execution results, timing
metrics, and any custom metadata injected by user stages.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

\index{ExecuteStage (class in pycypher.pipeline)@\spxentry{ExecuteStage}\spxextra{class in pycypher.pipeline}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.pipeline.ExecuteStage}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.pipeline.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{ExecuteStage}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.pipeline.Stage}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Stage}}}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Execute the parsed query against the Star engine.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Dispatches to the appropriate Star internal execution method based
on the AST type (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Query}} vs \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{UnionQuery}}).  This stage calls
the inner execution methods directly rather than delegating back to
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Star.execute\_query()}}, which allows the pipeline to be used
\sphinxstyleemphasis{from within} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{execute\_query()}} without circular delegation.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Populates \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ctx.result}} with the output DataFrame and stores the
parsed AST and mutation flag in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ctx.metadata}} for downstream use.

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline

\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.pipeline.Stage}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Stage}}}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Run semantic validation and complexity scoring.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Checks query complexity against \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ctx.max\_complexity\_score}} if set.
Also runs scoring when \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{COMPLEXITY\_WARN\_THRESHOLD}} is configured,
even without a hard limit, so that warnings can be emitted.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Populates \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ctx.metadata{[}"complexity\_score"{]}}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ctx.metadata{[}"complexity\_details"{]}}}, and
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ctx.metadata{[}"complexity\_warnings"{]}}} when scoring runs.

\sphinxAtStartPar
When \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{max\_complexity\_score}} is provided, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{score\_query()}} will
raise \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{QueryComplexityError}} if the score exceeds the limit.

\subsubsection{Lazy Evaluation}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:lazy-evaluation}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Defers computation until results are needed, enabling efficient processing
of large datasets and variable\sphinxhyphen{}length paths.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.lazy\_eval@\spxentry{pycypher.lazy\_eval}}\index{pycypher.lazy\_eval@\spxentry{pycypher.lazy\_eval}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.lazy_eval}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Lazy evaluation engine for deferred query execution.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Builds a computation graph of operations that are only executed when
results are materialised.  This enables:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Operation fusion} — combine compatible sequential operations
(e.g. filter→filter becomes a single filter with combined predicate).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Predicate pushdown} — move filters as early as possible in the graph.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Dead column elimination} — drop columns that are never referenced
downstream.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Memory estimation} — estimate peak memory before execution to select
optimal backend/strategy.

\end{enumerate}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The computation graph is a DAG where nodes are operations and edges
represent data flow.  Think of it as the DHF stack’s neural mapping —
consciousness (data) flows through optimised pathways before being
materialised in the target sleeve (output DataFrame).


\paragraph{Architecture}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:id149}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
LazyFrame  (user\PYGZhy{}facing handle)
  └── ComputationGraph (internal DAG)
        ├── ScanNode (leaf: entity/relationship scan)
        ├── FilterNode (predicate application)
        ├── JoinNode (two\PYGZhy{}input merge)
        ├── ProjectNode (column selection)
        ├── AggNode (aggregation)
        ├── SortNode (ordering)
        └── LimitNode (row truncation)
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Usage:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
List of node IDs where each node appears after all its inputs.
Empty list if the graph has no nodes.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{list}{[}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{int}{]}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{fuse\_filters() (in module pycypher.lazy\_eval)@\spxentry{fuse\_filters()}\spxextra{in module pycypher.lazy\_eval}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.lazy_eval.fuse_filters}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.lazy\_eval.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{fuse\_filters}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{graph}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Fuse consecutive filter nodes into a single filter.

\sphinxAtStartPar
When two filters are chained (F2 ← F1 ← source), they can be
combined into a single filter with an AND\sphinxhyphen{}combined predicate.
This reduces DataFrame materialisation from 2× to 1×.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{graph}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.lazy_eval.ComputationGraph}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ComputationGraph}}}}}) – The computation graph to optimise.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A new graph with consecutive filters fused.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.lazy_eval.ComputationGraph}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleemphasis{ComputationGraph}}}}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{push\_filters\_down() (in module pycypher.lazy\_eval)@\spxentry{push\_filters\_down()}\spxextra{in module pycypher.lazy\_eval}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.lazy_eval.push_filters_down}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.lazy\_eval.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{push\_filters\_down}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{graph}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Push filter operations below joins where possible.

\sphinxAtStartPar
If a filter only references columns from one side of a join,
it can be applied before the join — reducing the join’s input size.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{graph}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.lazy_eval.ComputationGraph}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ComputationGraph}}}}}) – The computation graph to optimise.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A new graph with filters pushed down past joins.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.lazy_eval.ComputationGraph}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleemphasis{ComputationGraph}}}}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Estimate peak memory usage for executing the computation graph.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Walks the graph in topological order and estimates the peak
concurrent memory — like calculating the maximum neural bandwidth
required for a multi\sphinxhyphen{}sleeve consciousness transfer.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{graph}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.lazy_eval.ComputationGraph}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ComputationGraph}}}}}) – The computation graph to analyse.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{avg\_row\_bytes}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Average bytes per row for estimation.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Estimated peak memory in bytes.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{int}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\index{build\_computation\_graph() (in module pycypher.lazy\_eval)@\spxentry{build\_computation\_graph()}\spxextra{in module pycypher.lazy\_eval}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.lazy_eval.build_computation_graph}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.lazy\_eval.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{build\_computation\_graph}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{query}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Translate a parsed Cypher Query AST into a ComputationGraph.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This bridges the gap between the AST (produced by the grammar parser)
and the lazy evaluation engine.  The resulting graph can be optimised
with {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.lazy_eval.fuse_filters}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{fuse\_filters()}}}}}, {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.lazy_eval.push_filters_down}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{push\_filters\_down()}}}}}, and analysed
with {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.lazy_eval.estimate_memory}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{estimate\_memory()}}}}} before execution.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The translation is \sphinxstyleemphasis{structural} — it mirrors the clause sequence in the
AST rather than simulating full execution semantics.  This is sufficient
for the optimisation passes to detect filter fusion and pushdown
opportunities.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{query}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Any}}}) – A parsed {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.Query}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Query}}}}} AST node.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.lazy_eval.ComputationGraph}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ComputationGraph}}}}} representing the query’s operations.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.lazy_eval.ComputationGraph}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleemphasis{ComputationGraph}}}}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{compute\_live\_columns() (in module pycypher.lazy\_eval)@\spxentry{compute\_live\_columns()}\spxextra{in module pycypher.lazy\_eval}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.lazy_eval.compute_live_columns}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.lazy\_eval.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_live\_columns}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clauses}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute the set of live (needed) columns after each clause.

\sphinxAtStartPar
For each clause index \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{i}}, returns the set of variable names that are
referenced by \sphinxstyleemphasis{any} clause at index \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{i+1}} or later.  A \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}} entry
means “keep all columns” (conservative fallback for mutation clauses
where dropping columns could lose data).

\sphinxAtStartPar
The result list has the same length as \sphinxstyleemphasis{clauses}.  Entry \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{i}} is the
set of columns that must be \sphinxstylestrong{retained} in the frame produced by clause
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{i}} — any column not in this set can be safely dropped.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clauses}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{[}}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Any}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{]}}}) – The ordered list of AST clause nodes from a parsed query.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A list parallel to \sphinxstyleemphasis{clauses} where each element is either a
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{set{[}str{]}}} of live variable names or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}} (keep\sphinxhyphen{}all).

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{list}{[}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#frozenset}{frozenset}{[}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}{]} | None{]}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}



\subsubsection{Result Cache}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:result-cache}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
LRU query result cache with size\sphinxhyphen{}bounded eviction and TTL support.
Uses \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{OrderedDict}} for O(1) LRU operations, readers\sphinxhyphen{}writer lock for
concurrent access, and generation\sphinxhyphen{}based invalidation on mutation commits.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.result\_cache@\spxentry{pycypher.result\_cache}}\index{pycypher.result\_cache@\spxentry{pycypher.result\_cache}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.result_cache}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
LRU query result cache with size\sphinxhyphen{}bounded eviction and TTL support.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Extracted from \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{star.py}} to provide a focused, single\sphinxhyphen{}responsibility module
for query result caching.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The cache uses an \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{OrderedDict}} for O(1) LRU operations, a readers\sphinxhyphen{}writer
lock for concurrent stats reads, adaptive timeouts based on operation
complexity, deadlock detection with owner\sphinxhyphen{}thread tracking, and generation\sphinxhyphen{}based
invalidation triggered by mutation commits.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Usage:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{cache} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ResultCache}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{max\PYGZus{}size\PYGZus{}bytes}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{100} \PYG{o}{*} \PYG{l+m+mi}{1024} \PYG{o}{*} \PYG{l+m+mi}{1024}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{ttl\PYGZus{}seconds}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{300}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{cache}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{put}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (n) RETURN n}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{k+kc}{None}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{result\PYGZus{}df}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{cached} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{cache}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{get}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (n) RETURN n}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{k+kc}{None}\PYG{p}{)}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} returns copy or None}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}
\index{ReadWriteLock (class in pycypher.result\_cache)@\spxentry{ReadWriteLock}\spxextra{class in pycypher.result\_cache}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.result_cache.ReadWriteLock}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.result\_cache.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{ReadWriteLock}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
A readers\sphinxhyphen{}writer lock with timeout support and owner tracking.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Multiple threads can hold the read lock concurrently. The write lock
is exclusive — no readers or other writers may hold a lock while the
write lock is held.

\sphinxAtStartPar
All lock acquisitions use timeout\sphinxhyphen{}bounded waits to prevent deadlocks.
The current write\sphinxhyphen{}lock owner thread is tracked for diagnostics.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

\index{ResultCache (class in pycypher.result\_cache)@\spxentry{ResultCache}\spxextra{class in pycypher.result\_cache}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.result_cache.ResultCache}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.result\_cache.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{ResultCache}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{max\_size\_bytes}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{104857600}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{ttl\_seconds}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{0.0}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{lock\_timeout\_seconds}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
LRU cache for query results with size\sphinxhyphen{}bounded eviction and TTL support.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Keys are derived from the normalised query string and parameters.
Values are copied DataFrames so mutations to the returned frame do not
corrupt the cached entry.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The cache is automatically invalidated when the underlying \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Context}}
commits a mutation (SET / CREATE / DELETE / MERGE / REMOVE).

\sphinxAtStartPar
Thread\sphinxhyphen{}safety:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Mutating operations (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{get}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{put}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{invalidate}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{clear}})
acquire an exclusive write lock.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Read\sphinxhyphen{}only operations (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{stats}}) acquire a shared read lock, allowing
concurrent stats collection without blocking cache operations.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
All lock acquisitions use adaptive timeouts (scaled by operation
complexity) to prevent deadlocks.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The write\sphinxhyphen{}lock owner thread is tracked for deadlock diagnostics.

\end{itemize}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}
\index{\_\_init\_\_() (pycypher.result\_cache.ResultCache method)@\spxentry{\_\_init\_\_()}\spxextra{pycypher.result\_cache.ResultCache method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.result_cache.ResultCache.__init__}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_init\_\_}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{max\_size\_bytes}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{104857600}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{ttl\_seconds}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{0.0}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{lock\_timeout\_seconds}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Initialize the result cache.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{max\_size\_bytes}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Maximum total memory for cached DataFrames.
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{0}} disables caching entirely.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ttl\_seconds}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Time\sphinxhyphen{}to\sphinxhyphen{}live per entry in seconds.
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{0}} means entries never expire by time (only by LRU eviction
or mutation\sphinxhyphen{}based invalidation).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{lock\_timeout\_seconds}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – Base timeout for lock acquisition in seconds.
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}} uses the class default (5 s).  The actual timeout is
scaled by a per\sphinxhyphen{}operation multiplier (e.g. \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{put}} gets 2x
the base timeout).  Set to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{0}} for non\sphinxhyphen{}blocking semantics.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Bump the generation counter, lazily invalidating all entries.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Called when a mutation is committed to the Context.  Existing entries
are not deleted immediately — they are evicted on the next \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{get()}}
that detects the stale generation, or during LRU eviction.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\subsubsection{Timeout Handler}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:timeout-handler}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Timeout management for query execution.  Provides a context manager that
arms both cooperative and hard (SIGALRM) timeouts with reliable cleanup.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.timeout\_handler@\spxentry{pycypher.timeout\_handler}}\index{pycypher.timeout\_handler@\spxentry{pycypher.timeout\_handler}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.timeout_handler}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Timeout management for query execution.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Provides a context\sphinxhyphen{}manager that arms both cooperative and hard (SIGALRM)
timeouts and cleans up reliably in the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{finally}} block.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Usage:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{handler} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{TimeoutHandler}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{timeout\PYGZus{}seconds}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{5.0}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{query\PYGZus{}str}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH ...}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{with} \PYG{n}{handler}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} execute query — cooperative check\PYGZus{}timeout() between clauses}
    \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} SIGALRM fires if stuck in C extension beyond deadline}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}
\index{TimeoutHandler (class in pycypher.timeout\_handler)@\spxentry{TimeoutHandler}\spxextra{class in pycypher.timeout\_handler}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.timeout_handler.TimeoutHandler}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.timeout\_handler.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{TimeoutHandler}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{context}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{keyword-only-separator}{\DUrole{o}{\sphinxstyleabbreviation{*}}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{timeout\_seconds}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{query\_str}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{''}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{start\_time}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Arm and disarm query timeouts with cooperative + SIGALRM protection.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The handler is a context manager.  On \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_enter\_\_}} it:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Sets the cooperative deadline on the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Context}} (checked between clauses).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Installs a SIGALRM handler on Unix main\sphinxhyphen{}thread (catches stuck C extensions).

\end{enumerate}

\sphinxAtStartPar
On \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_exit\_\_}} it:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Cancels the SIGALRM.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Restores the previous signal handler.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Clears the cooperative deadline on the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Context}}.

\end{enumerate}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Thread\sphinxhyphen{}safety: SIGALRM is only armed when running on the main thread.
On non\sphinxhyphen{}main threads or non\sphinxhyphen{}Unix platforms, only the cooperative deadline
is used.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{context}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Any}}) – The {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.relational_models.Context}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Context}}}}} to set/clear
the cooperative deadline on.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{timeout\_seconds}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – Wall\sphinxhyphen{}clock timeout in seconds.  \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}} means no timeout.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{query\_str}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Query text for inclusion in timeout error messages.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{start\_time}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – Reference time (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{time.perf\_counter()}}) for elapsed calculation.
Defaults to the time of \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_enter\_\_}}.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\subsubsection{Mutation Engine}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:mutation-engine}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Handles write operations: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{CREATE}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SET}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DELETE}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{REMOVE}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MERGE}}, and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{FOREACH}} clauses with shadow\sphinxhyphen{}layer transaction semantics.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.mutation\_engine@\spxentry{pycypher.mutation\_engine}}\index{pycypher.mutation\_engine@\spxentry{pycypher.mutation\_engine}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.mutation_engine}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Mutation engine — all write\sphinxhyphen{}path operations for Cypher query execution.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Extracted from the Star god\sphinxhyphen{}object to provide a focused, single\sphinxhyphen{}responsibility
module for CREATE, SET, DELETE, DETACH DELETE, MERGE, FOREACH, and REMOVE
clause execution.

\sphinxAtStartPar
All mutations are staged in the per\sphinxhyphen{}query shadow layer on the
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.relational_models.Context}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Context}}}}} and only committed when the
enclosing query succeeds.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Usage:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{engine} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{MutationEngine}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{ctx}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{engine}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{process\PYGZus{}create}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{clause}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{current\PYGZus{}frame}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{engine}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{set\PYGZus{}properties}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{clause}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{frame}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{engine}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{process\PYGZus{}delete}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{clause}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{frame}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}
\index{MutationEngine (class in pycypher.mutation\_engine)@\spxentry{MutationEngine}\spxextra{class in pycypher.mutation\_engine}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.mutation_engine.MutationEngine}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.mutation\_engine.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{MutationEngine}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{context}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Encapsulates all write\sphinxhyphen{}path operations for Cypher query execution.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Each method corresponds to a mutation clause type (SET, CREATE, DELETE,
MERGE, FOREACH, REMOVE).  All mutations go through the shadow\sphinxhyphen{}write
layer on the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Context}}, ensuring atomicity at the query level.


\paragraph{State transition model}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:state-transition-model}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Mutations follow a two\sphinxhyphen{}phase pattern:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Stage} — each mutation method writes to the Context’s shadow
layer (a copy\sphinxhyphen{}on\sphinxhyphen{}write overlay).  The original DataFrames are
never modified in\sphinxhyphen{}place during execution.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Commit} — after all clauses execute successfully, the shadow
layer is merged into the live entity/relationship tables.  On
failure, the shadow layer is discarded (rollback semantics).

\end{enumerate}

\sphinxAtStartPar
This ensures that a query like
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MATCH (a) SET a.x = 1 SET a.y = a.x + 1}} sees consistent state
within the same query, and a failing query leaves the context unchanged.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Supported clauses:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{CREATE}} — {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.mutation_engine.MutationEngine.process_create}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{process\_create()}}}}} — insert new nodes/relationships

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SET}} — {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.mutation_engine.MutationEngine.set_properties}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{set\_properties()}}}}} — update properties on matched entities

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DELETE}} / \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DETACH DELETE}} — {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.mutation_engine.MutationEngine.process_delete}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{process\_delete()}}}}} — remove entities

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MERGE}} — {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.mutation_engine.MutationEngine.process_merge}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{process\_merge()}}}}} — match\sphinxhyphen{}or\sphinxhyphen{}create with ON CREATE/MATCH SET

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{FOREACH}} — {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.mutation_engine.MutationEngine.process_foreach}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{process\_foreach()}}}}} — iterate and mutate

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{REMOVE}} — handled via SET with null values

\end{itemize}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{param context}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.relational_models.Context}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Context}}}}} holding
entity and relationship mappings.

\end{description}\end{quote}

mappings. Mutations are applied through the shadow\sphinxhyphen{}write layer.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Apply a SET clause against a BindingFrame (mutates context in place).

\sphinxAtStartPar
Only {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.SetPropertyItem}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SetPropertyItem}}}}} is supported.  Each
item evaluates its value expression via
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_evaluator.BindingExpressionEvaluator}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingExpressionEvaluator}}}}} and
calls {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame.mutate}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{mutate()}}}}} to write
back to the entity table.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{set\_clause}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.Set}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Set}}}}}) – AST {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ast_models.Set}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Set}}}}} node.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{frame}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}}}}}) – Current {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.binding_frame.BindingFrame}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}}}}}.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\subsection{Multi\sphinxhyphen{}Query Composition}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:multi-query-composition}}

\subsubsection{Multi\sphinxhyphen{}Query Analyzer}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:multi-query-analyzer}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Builds a dependency graph from multiple Cypher queries by analyzing
entity/relationship types produced and consumed by each query.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.multi\_query\_analyzer@\spxentry{pycypher.multi\_query\_analyzer}}\index{pycypher.multi\_query\_analyzer@\spxentry{pycypher.multi\_query\_analyzer}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.multi_query_analyzer}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Multi\sphinxhyphen{}query dependency analysis — extracts produces/consumes from ASTs.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Provides the core dependency analysis components for multi\sphinxhyphen{}query composition:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.multi_query_analyzer.QueryNode}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{QueryNode}}}}} — represents a single query with produces/consumes metadata

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.multi_query_analyzer.DependencyGraph}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DependencyGraph}}}}} — container with topological sort capability

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.multi_query_analyzer.QueryDependencyAnalyzer}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{QueryDependencyAnalyzer}}}}} — extracts entity/relationship types from ASTs

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
These components are used by \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.multi\_query\_rewriter}} to determine
execution order when combining multiple Cypher queries.
\index{QueryNode (class in pycypher.multi\_query\_analyzer)@\spxentry{QueryNode}\spxextra{class in pycypher.multi\_query\_analyzer}}

{}

\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Return nodes in dependency\sphinxhyphen{}respecting execution order.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Uses Kahn’s algorithm: repeatedly selects nodes whose dependencies
have all been processed.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar

\pysigstartsignatures

{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.multi\_query\_analyzer.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{QueryDependencyAnalyzer}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Analyzes query ASTs to build a dependency graph.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Parses each Cypher query, extracts the entity/relationship types it
creates (produces) and matches (consumes), then infers inter\sphinxhyphen{}query
dependencies from the produces/consumes overlap.

\subsubsection{Multi\sphinxhyphen{}Query Executor}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:multi-query-executor}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Orchestrates execution of multi\sphinxhyphen{}query pipelines with dependency\sphinxhyphen{}aware
ordering and shared context propagation.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.multi\_query\_executor@\spxentry{pycypher.multi\_query\_executor}}\index{pycypher.multi\_query\_executor@\spxentry{pycypher.multi\_query\_executor}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.multi_query_executor}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Multi\sphinxhyphen{}query executor — the user\sphinxhyphen{}facing API for query composition.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Orchestrates all multi\sphinxhyphen{}query components into a single entry point:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Input validation} — validates query pairs before processing

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Dependency analysis} — determines execution order from AST analysis

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Query combination} — merges queries with \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WITH *}} and RETURN stripping

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Execution} — runs the combined query via \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Star.execute\_query()}}

\end{enumerate}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Provides both full\sphinxhyphen{}pipeline execution and individual stage access
(validate, analyze, combine) for debugging and testing.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Example:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{multi\PYGZus{}query\PYGZus{}executor}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{MultiQueryExecutor}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{relational\PYGZus{}models}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{Context}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{star}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{Star}

\PYG{n}{ctx} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Context}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{star} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{ctx}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{executor} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{MultiQueryExecutor}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{executor}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}multi\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{[}
    \PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{q1}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{CREATE (n:Person }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name: }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Alice}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZcb{})}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{q2}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (n:Person) RETURN n.name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\subsection{Validation}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:validation}}

\subsubsection{Semantic Validator}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:semantic-validator}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Pre\sphinxhyphen{}execution validation: undefined variable detection, aggregation rule
checking, type constraint verification.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.semantic\_validator@\spxentry{pycypher.semantic\_validator}}\index{pycypher.semantic\_validator@\spxentry{pycypher.semantic\_validator}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.semantic_validator}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Semantic validation for Cypher queries.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This module provides semantic analysis beyond syntax checking, including:
\sphinxhyphen{} Variable scope and binding validation
\sphinxhyphen{} Undefined variable detection
\sphinxhyphen{} Aggregation rule validation
\sphinxhyphen{} Function signature validation
\sphinxhyphen{} Type checking for expressions
\subsubsection*{Example}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{semantic\PYGZus{}validator}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{SemanticValidator}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{grammar\PYGZus{}parser}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{GrammarParser}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{parser} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{GrammarParser}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{validator} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{SemanticValidator}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{query} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (n:Person) RETURN m}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} \PYGZsq{}m\PYGZsq{} undefined}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{tree} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{parser}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{parse}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{query}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{errors} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{validator}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{validate}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{tree}\PYG{p}{)}

\subsection{Ingestion Layer}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:ingestion-layer}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The ingestion layer provides Arrow (via PyArrow) as the canonical in\sphinxhyphen{}memory
tabular format and DuckDB as the universal ingestion adapter.


\subsubsection{Context Builder}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:context-builder}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Fluent API for constructing \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Context}} objects from DataFrames, Parquet
files, CSV files, and other tabular sources.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.ingestion.context\_builder@\spxentry{pycypher.ingestion.context\_builder}}\index{pycypher.ingestion.context\_builder@\spxentry{pycypher.ingestion.context\_builder}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.ingestion.context_builder}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Fluent builder that assembles a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Context}} from Arrow\sphinxhyphen{}loaded data.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Usage example:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{ingestion}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}

\PYG{n}{context} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}entity}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{people.csv}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{id\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{person\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}relationship}\PYG{p}{(}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{KNOWS}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{knows.csv}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{n}{source\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{from\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{n}{target\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{to\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{build}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\index{from\_dict() (pycypher.ingestion.context\_builder.ContextBuilder class method)@\spxentry{from\_dict()}\spxextra{pycypher.ingestion.context\_builder.ContextBuilder class method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.context_builder.ContextBuilder.from_dict}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{classmethod}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{from\_dict}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{entity\_frames}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{keyword-only-separator}{\DUrole{o}{\sphinxstyleabbreviation{*}}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{id\_column}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Build a {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.relational_models.Context}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Context}}}}} from a dict of DataFrames.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Each key is a node or relationship label; the corresponding value is a
pandas DataFrame.  DataFrames are automatically classified:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
If the DataFrame contains both \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_SOURCE\_\_}} and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_TARGET\_\_}}
columns it is registered as a \sphinxstylestrong{relationship table}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Otherwise it is registered as an \sphinxstylestrong{entity table}.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
This allows a single \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{from\_dict()}} call to supply both nodes and
edges without needing the verbose \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{add\_entity}} / \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{add\_relationship}}
builder chain:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{ctx} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{from\PYGZus{}dict}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n}{persons\PYGZus{}df}\PYG{p}{,}        \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} entity — no \PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}SOURCE\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}/\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}TARGET\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{KNOWS}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:}  \PYG{n}{knows\PYGZus{}df}\PYG{p}{,}          \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} relationship — has both columns}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{entity\_frames}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{dict}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{[}}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxhref{https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/reference/api/pandas.DataFrame.html\#pandas.DataFrame}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{DataFrame}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{]}}}) – Mapping of label to DataFrame (entity or relationship).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar

\subsubsection{Data Sources}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.ingestion.data_sources}}\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:data-sources}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.ingestion.data\_sources@\spxentry{pycypher.ingestion.data\_sources}}\index{pycypher.ingestion.data\_sources@\spxentry{pycypher.ingestion.data\_sources}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Abstract DataSource hierarchy and URI\sphinxhyphen{}based factory.

\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.data_sources.DataSource}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DataSource}}}}} is the primary abstraction for reading tabular data into
Arrow tables.  Every concrete subclass wraps one kind of external storage and
exposes a single {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.data_sources.DataSource.read}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{read()}}}}} method that returns a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pa.Table}}.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Use {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.data_sources.data_source_from_uri}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{data\_source\_from\_uri()}}}}} to obtain the correct subclass for a given
URI string without writing dispatch code yourself.


\paragraph{Supported URI schemes}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:supported-uri-schemes}}

Abstract base for file\sphinxhyphen{}format strategies.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Each subclass knows how to generate the DuckDB SQL fragment that reads
one specific file format (CSV, Parquet, JSON, …).

{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{view\_sql}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{path}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Generate a DuckDB SQL fragment to read a CSV file with custom options.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Produces a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{read\_csv\_auto()}} call that incorporates this format’s
delimiter, header, and null\_padding settings. The path is validated
to prevent SQL injection attacks.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{path}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Filesystem path or URI to the CSV file to read.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A SQL fragment like \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"read\_csv\_auto('/data/file.csv', delim=';')"}}
that can be embedded in DuckDB queries.

\sphinxlineitem{Raises}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}} – If path or delimiter contains characters unsafe for SQL
    string literals (single quotes or NUL bytes).

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Generate a DuckDB SQL fragment to read a Parquet file.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Produces a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{read\_parquet()}} call for the given path. Parquet files
are self\sphinxhyphen{}describing and require no additional format options. The path
is validated to prevent SQL injection attacks.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{path}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Filesystem path or URI to the Parquet file to read.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A SQL fragment like \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"read\_parquet('/data/file.parquet')"}} that
can be embedded in DuckDB queries.

\sphinxlineitem{Raises}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}} – If path contains characters unsafe for SQL string
    literals (single quotes or NUL bytes).

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{view\_sql() (pycypher.ingestion.data\_sources.JsonFormat method)@\spxentry{view\_sql()}\spxextra{pycypher.ingestion.data\_sources.JsonFormat method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.data_sources.JsonFormat.view_sql}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{view\_sql}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{path}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Generate a DuckDB SQL fragment to read a JSON file with record layout options.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Produces a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{read\_json\_auto()}} call that incorporates this format’s
records layout setting. When records is \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"auto"}}, DuckDB infers the
JSON structure. When set to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"newline\_delimited"}}, forces NDJSON
parsing. The path and records values are validated to prevent SQL
injection attacks.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{path}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Filesystem path or URI to the JSON file to read.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A SQL fragment like \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"read\_json\_auto('/data/file.json')"}} or
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"read\_json\_auto('/data/file.json', format='newline\_delimited')"}}
that can be embedded in DuckDB queries.

\sphinxlineitem{Raises}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}} – If path or records contains characters unsafe for SQL
    string literals (single quotes or NUL bytes).

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/abc.html\#abc.ABC}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ABC}}}

Abstract base for all tabular data sources.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Every concrete subclass implements {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.data_sources.DataSource.read}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{read()}}}}} to load its backing store
and return the result as a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pa.Table}}.  All downstream pipeline
components (normalisation, query execution) consume Arrow tables produced
by {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.data_sources.DataSource.read}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{read()}}}}}.

\index{read\_relation() (pycypher.ingestion.data\_sources.DataSource method)@\spxentry{read\_relation()}\spxextra{pycypher.ingestion.data\_sources.DataSource method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.data_sources.DataSource.read_relation}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{read\_relation}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{con}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Return the source as a lazy DuckDB relation registered on \sphinxstyleemphasis{con}.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This is the out\sphinxhyphen{}of\sphinxhyphen{}core counterpart to {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.data_sources.DataSource.read}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{read()}}}}}: instead of
materialising a full \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pa.Table}}, it yields a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DuckDBLazyFrame}}
wrapping a relation on the caller\sphinxhyphen{}owned persistent connection \sphinxstyleemphasis{con},
so downstream operations can stay inside DuckDB and spill to disk.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The default implementation materialises via {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.data_sources.DataSource.read}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{read()}}}}} and registers
the Arrow table on \sphinxstyleemphasis{con} — correct but not streaming.  File\sphinxhyphen{}backed
sources override this to scan the file directly via DuckDB
(\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{read\_parquet}}/\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{read\_csv\_auto}}) without ever building a full
Arrow table.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{con}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Any}}}) – A persistent \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{duckdb.DuckDBPyConnection}} (see
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.backends.duckdb\_backend.create\_duckdb\_connection()}}).
The caller owns its lifecycle.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DuckDBLazyFrame}} over \sphinxstyleemphasis{con}.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleemphasis{Any}}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}
