{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.data_sources.DataSource}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DataSource}}}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
File\sphinxhyphen{}backed data source that delegates format parsing to a {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.data_sources.Format}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Format}}}}}.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxstyleemphasis{format} object encapsulates the DuckDB read function and its options,
so adding new formats (Avro, ORC, Delta …) requires only a new
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.data_sources.Format}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Format}}}}} subclass without touching this class.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{uri}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Path or URI to the file.  Bare filesystem paths, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{file://}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{s3://}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{gs://}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{https://}}, and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{abfss://}} URIs are
all accepted; DuckDB resolves them at read time.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{format}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.data_sources.Format}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Format}}}}}) – A {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.data_sources.Format}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Format}}}}} instance that supplies the DuckDB SQL
read fragment.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{query}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – Optional DuckDB SQL applied after the file is registered as
the view \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{source}}.  When \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SELECT * FROM source}}
is used.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{schema\_hints}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{dict}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{[}}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{]} }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{| }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – Optional mapping of column name → DuckDB type string.
Applied as explicit \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{CAST}} expressions on the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{source}} view
\sphinxstyleemphasis{before} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{query}} runs against it, so \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{query}} can rely on the
hinted types (e.g. comparing a hinted\sphinxhyphen{}INTEGER column with
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BETWEEN}}) rather than whatever type the file format inferred.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\index{read\_relation() (pycypher.ingestion.data\_sources.FileDataSource method)@\spxentry{read\_relation()}\spxextra{pycypher.ingestion.data\_sources.FileDataSource method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.data_sources.FileDataSource.read_relation}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{read\_relation}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{con}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Scan the file directly via DuckDB, returning a lazy relation.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Streaming counterpart to {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.data_sources.FileDataSource.read}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{read()}}}}}: builds the relation on the
caller\sphinxhyphen{}owned connection \sphinxstyleemphasis{con} without \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{to\_arrow\_table()}}, so the file
is never fully materialised.  Uses a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WITH source AS (…)}} CTE rather
than named views, so any user \sphinxstyleemphasis{query} referencing \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{source}} resolves
locally and multiple sources sharing one connection cannot collide.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{con}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Any}}}) – A persistent \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{duckdb.DuckDBPyConnection}}.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DuckDBLazyFrame}} over \sphinxstyleemphasis{con}.

\sphinxlineitem{Raises}
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.exceptions.SecurityError}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{SecurityError}}}}} – If the URI, query, or schema hints fail validation.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleemphasis{Any}}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Reads from a relational database via a DuckDB connection string.

\sphinxAtStartPar
DuckDB supports connecting to PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, and other DuckDB
files via its scanner extensions.  The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{query}} parameter is required
because there is no meaningful default when targeting a database.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{uri}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{keyword-only-separator}{\DUrole{o}{\sphinxstyleabbreviation{*}}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{query}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{schema\_hints}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Construct the appropriate {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.data_sources.DataSource}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DataSource}}}}} subclass from \sphinxstyleemphasis{uri}.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Dispatch rules (evaluated in order):
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pa.Table}} → {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.data_sources.ArrowDataSource}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ArrowDataSource}}}}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.DataFrame}} → {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.data_sources.DataFrameDataSource}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DataFrameDataSource}}}}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
SQL\sphinxhyphen{}scheme string (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{postgresql:}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{postgres:}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{mysql:}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{sqlite:}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{duckdb:}}) → {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.data_sources.SqlDataSource}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SqlDataSource}}}}}
(\sphinxstyleemphasis{query} is required for SQL sources)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
String ending in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{.csv}} → {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.data_sources.FileDataSource}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{FileDataSource}}}}} with {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.data_sources.CsvFormat}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{CsvFormat}}}}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
String ending in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{.parquet}} → {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.data_sources.FileDataSource}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{FileDataSource}}}}} with {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.data_sources.ParquetFormat}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ParquetFormat}}}}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
String ending in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{.json}} → {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.data_sources.FileDataSource}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{FileDataSource}}}}} with {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.data_sources.JsonFormat}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{JsonFormat}}}}}

\end{enumerate}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The URI may use any scheme that DuckDB supports for steps 4\sphinxhyphen{}6:
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{file://}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{s3://}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{gs://}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{abfss://}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{http://}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{https://}}, or a bare filesystem path.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{uri}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxhref{https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/reference/api/pandas.DataFrame.html\#pandas.DataFrame}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{DataFrame}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Table}}) – Source identifier.  Accepts a URI string, a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.DataFrame}},
or a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pa.Table}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{query}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – Optional SQL override for file\sphinxhyphen{}based sources.  Required for
SQL\sphinxhyphen{}scheme (database) sources.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{schema\_hints}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{dict}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{[}}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{]} }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{| }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – Optional mapping of column name → DuckDB type string,
applied before \sphinxstyleemphasis{query} runs (file and DataFrame sources only —
ignored for SQL and Arrow sources, which have no equivalent
pre\sphinxhyphen{}query step to apply casts to).

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.data_sources.DataSource}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DataSource}}}}} instance of the appropriate subclass.

\sphinxlineitem{Raises}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}} – If \sphinxstyleemphasis{uri} is a SQL\sphinxhyphen{}scheme string but \sphinxstyleemphasis{query} is \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}},
    or if the string’s extension is not recognised.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#TypeError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{TypeError}}} – If \sphinxstyleemphasis{uri} is not a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{str}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.DataFrame}}, or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pa.Table}}.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar

\subsubsection*{Examples}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{src} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{data\PYGZus{}source\PYGZus{}from\PYGZus{}uri}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{s3://bucket/people.parquet}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{src} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{data\PYGZus{}source\PYGZus{}from\PYGZus{}uri}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{postgresql://user:pass@host/db}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{query}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{SELECT * FROM persons WHERE active}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{src} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{data\PYGZus{}source\PYGZus{}from\PYGZus{}uri}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{my\PYGZus{}dataframe}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\end{fulllineitems}



\subsubsection{Pipeline Config}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.ingestion.config}}\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pipeline-config}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.ingestion.config@\spxentry{pycypher.ingestion.config}}\index{pycypher.ingestion.config@\spxentry{pycypher.ingestion.config}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Pydantic models for the pycypher ETL pipeline YAML configuration file.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The top\sphinxhyphen{}level model is {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.config.PipelineConfig}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PipelineConfig}}}}}, loaded from disk via
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.config.load_pipeline_config}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{load\_pipeline\_config()}}}}}.  Every public field that is not required
for the system to function is typed \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Optional}} and defaults to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}}
or an empty collection.


\paragraph{Environment variables}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:id169}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
String values in the YAML file may contain \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\$\{VAR\_NAME\}}} placeholders.
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.config.load_pipeline_config}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{load\_pipeline\_config()}}}}} substitutes them from the process environment
before Pydantic validation.  Unresolved variables (env var not set) are
left as\sphinxhyphen{}is; an error is raised only when the value is actually used (e.g.
when a source is loaded or an output is written).


\paragraph{Supported URI schemes}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:id170}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{uri}} field on source and output records is validated at config\sphinxhyphen{}load
time.  Accepted forms:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{file:///path/to/file.\{csv,parquet,json\}}} — local files

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{/path/to/file.\{csv,parquet,json\}}} — bare filesystem paths

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{s3://}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{gs://}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{abfss://}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{http://}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{https://}} — cloud /
remote files, with a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{.csv}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{.parquet}}, or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{.json}} extension

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{postgresql://}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{postgres://}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{mysql://}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{sqlite://}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{duckdb://}} — SQL databases (a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{query}} field is also required)

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Any URI with an unrecognised scheme + extension combination, or a SQL\sphinxhyphen{}scheme
URI without a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{query}}, raises a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pydantic.ValidationError}} immediately.

\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{(source\_col, target\_col)}} pair are collapsed into a single
edge.  Set to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{True}} for sources where parallel edges carry
distinct meaning (e.g. one row per transaction).

\sphinxAtStartPar
Look up a source by its unique \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{id}}.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Searches both the entity and relationship source lists.  The id
namespace is shared across both lists (enforced by
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.config.SourcesConfig.check_unique_source_ids}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{check\_unique\_source\_ids()}}}}}), so at most one source can match.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{source\_id}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{id}} value to look up.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The matching {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.config.EntitySourceConfig}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{EntitySourceConfig}}}}} or
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.config.RelationshipSourceConfig}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RelationshipSourceConfig}}}}}, or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}} if no source with
that id exists.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.config.EntitySourceConfig}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleemphasis{EntitySourceConfig}}}} | {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.config.RelationshipSourceConfig}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleemphasis{RelationshipSourceConfig}}}} | None

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

Register one or more Python callables as Cypher functions.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Two mutually exclusive forms are supported:

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Module form} — register specific functions (or all public ones) from a
Python module:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{module}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{mypackage.string\PYGZus{}utils}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{n}{names}\PYG{p}{:}
  \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}} \PYG{n}{normalize\PYGZus{}name}
  \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}} \PYG{n}{parse\PYGZus{}phone\PYGZus{}number}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} OR: names: \PYGZdq{}*\PYGZdq{}  (registers all public callables)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Callable form} — register a single function by its fully\sphinxhyphen{}qualified
dotted import path:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n+nb}{callable}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{mypackage.string\PYGZus{}utils.normalize\PYGZus{}name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{source}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
URI or path to a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{.cypher}} file containing the query.
Supported forms:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{file:///abs/path/query.cypher}} — absolute local path

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{s3://bucket/key.cypher}} — Amazon S3 (requires s3fs)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{gs://bucket/key.cypher}} — Google Cloud Storage (requires gcsfs)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{https://host/path.cypher}} — HTTP/HTTPS

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{queries/my\_query.cypher}} — relative path, resolved from the
directory containing the pipeline config file

\end{itemize}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str} | None

\end{description}\end{quote}

\index{load\_with\_sources() (pycypher.ingestion.config.PipelineConfig class method)@\spxentry{load\_with\_sources()}\spxextra{pycypher.ingestion.config.PipelineConfig class method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.config.PipelineConfig.load_with_sources}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{classmethod}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{load\_with\_sources}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{path}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Load a config file and eagerly read all data sources into Arrow tables.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This is the single entry point for pipelines that want both a validated
config and pre\sphinxhyphen{}loaded Arrow tables in one call.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{path}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/pathlib.html\#pathlib.Path}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Path}}}) – Path to the YAML pipeline configuration file.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{(config, sources, skipped)}} tuple where \sphinxstyleemphasis{config} is a
validated {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.config.PipelineConfig}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PipelineConfig}}}}}, \sphinxstyleemphasis{sources} is a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{dict}}
mapping each source \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{id}} to its \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pa.Table}}, and \sphinxstyleemphasis{skipped}
is a list of {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.config.SkippedSource}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SkippedSource}}}}} records for any sources
suppressed by a WARN or SKIP error policy.

\sphinxlineitem{Raises}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#FileNotFoundError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{FileNotFoundError}}} – If \sphinxstyleemphasis{path} does not exist.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{yaml.YAMLError}} – If the file contains invalid YAML.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{pydantic.ValidationError}} – If the configuration is structurally
    invalid.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#tuple}{tuple}{[}{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.config.PipelineConfig}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleemphasis{PipelineConfig}}}}, \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{dict}{[}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}, \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleemphasis{Any}}{]}, \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{list}{[}{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.config.SkippedSource}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleemphasis{SkippedSource}}}}{]}{]}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Load and validate a pipeline configuration from a YAML file.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Processing steps:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Read and parse the YAML file.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Substitute \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\$\{VAR\}}} placeholders from the process environment.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Validate the resulting dict against {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.config.PipelineConfig}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PipelineConfig}}}}}.

\end{enumerate}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{yaml.YAMLError}} – If the file contains invalid YAML.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{pydantic.ValidationError}} – If the configuration is structurally
    invalid (missing required fields, constraint violations, etc.).

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar

\subsubsection{DuckDB Reader}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.ingestion.duckdb_reader}}\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:duckdb-reader}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.ingestion.duckdb\_reader@\spxentry{pycypher.ingestion.duckdb\_reader}}\index{pycypher.ingestion.duckdb\_reader@\spxentry{pycypher.ingestion.duckdb\_reader}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
DuckDB\sphinxhyphen{}backed readers that return Arrow tables.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Every method opens a fresh in\sphinxhyphen{}process DuckDB connection, loads the source,
and returns the result as a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pa.Table}} via \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{duckdb\_relation.to\_arrow\_table()}}.
\index{DuckDBReader (class in pycypher.ingestion.duckdb\_reader)@\spxentry{DuckDBReader}\spxextra{class in pycypher.ingestion.duckdb\_reader}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.duckdb_reader.DuckDBReader}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.ingestion.duckdb\_reader.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{DuckDBReader}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Universal ingestion adapter that normalises any data source to Arrow.

\sphinxAtStartPar
All methods are static.  Each method opens a fresh in\sphinxhyphen{}process DuckDB
connection for the duration of the read and closes it via the context
manager protocol on return or exception.

\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Security:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Never embed credentials directly in connection strings or config
files.}  Use environment variables to supply sensitive values:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{import}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{os}
\PYG{n}{conn} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{postgresql://}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{os}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{environ}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{DB\PYGZus{}USER}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{:}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{os}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{environ}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{DB\PYGZus{}PASSWORD}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{@host/db}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{DuckDBReader}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{from\PYGZus{}sql}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{conn}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{SELECT * FROM t}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
In pipeline YAML configs, reference env vars with \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\$\{DB\_PASSWORD\}}}
syntax.  The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{nmetl security\sphinxhyphen{}check}} command will flag embedded
credentials and insecure connection patterns.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Connection strings are validated for safe URI schemes but are
\sphinxstylestrong{not logged} to prevent credential leakage.  The audit logger
records parameter \sphinxstyleemphasis{keys} only, never values.

\end{description}

\end{fulllineitems}

\subsubsection{Arrow Utilities}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.ingestion.arrow_utils}}\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:arrow-utilities}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.ingestion.arrow\_utils@\spxentry{pycypher.ingestion.arrow\_utils}}\index{pycypher.ingestion.arrow\_utils@\spxentry{pycypher.ingestion.arrow\_utils}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Utilities for normalising Arrow tables before they enter the pycypher pipeline.

\sphinxAtStartPar
All functions return a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pa.Table}} with canonical column names:
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_ID\_\_}} — unique row identifier
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_SOURCE\_\_}} — source node ID for relationships
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_TARGET\_\_}} — target node ID for relationships
\index{normalize\_entity\_table() (in module pycypher.ingestion.arrow\_utils)@\spxentry{normalize\_entity\_table()}\spxextra{in module pycypher.ingestion.arrow\_utils}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.arrow_utils.normalize_entity_table}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.ingestion.arrow\_utils.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{normalize\_entity\_table}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{table}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{id\_col}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Return \sphinxstyleemphasis{table} with an \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_ID\_\_}} column as the first column.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{table}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Table}}) – Source Arrow table.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{id\_col}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – Column to rename to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_ID\_\_}}.  If \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}} or not present,
a sequential integer column is prepended.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Arrow table whose first column is \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_ID\_\_}}.

\sphinxlineitem{Raises}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}} – If \sphinxstyleemphasis{id\_col} is specified but does not exist in \sphinxstyleemphasis{table}.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleemphasis{Table}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{normalize\_relationship\_table() (in module pycypher.ingestion.arrow\_utils)@\spxentry{normalize\_relationship\_table()}\spxextra{in module pycypher.ingestion.arrow\_utils}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.arrow_utils.normalize_relationship_table}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.ingestion.arrow\_utils.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{normalize\_relationship\_table}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{table}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{source\_col}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{target\_col}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{id\_col}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{keyword-only-separator}{\DUrole{o}{\sphinxstyleabbreviation{*}}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{allow\_multi\_edges}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{False}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Return \sphinxstyleemphasis{table} with \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_SOURCE\_\_}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_TARGET\_\_}}, and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_ID\_\_}} columns.

\sphinxAtStartPar
By default, parallel edges (rows with the same \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{(\_\_SOURCE\_\_,
\_\_TARGET\_\_)}} pair) are collapsed to a single edge.  Pass
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{allow\_multi\_edges=True}} to preserve them — this is appropriate when
each row represents a distinct logical relationship instance (e.g.
individual transactions between two accounts).
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{table}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Table}}) – Source Arrow table.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{source\_col}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Column to rename to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_SOURCE\_\_}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{target\_col}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Column to rename to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_TARGET\_\_}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{id\_col}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – Column to rename to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_ID\_\_}}.  If \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}}, a sequential
integer column is prepended (after any dedup).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{allow\_multi\_edges}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}}) – When \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{False}} (default), rows with duplicate
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{(\_\_SOURCE\_\_, \_\_TARGET\_\_)}} pairs are collapsed.  When \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{True}},
parallel edges are preserved.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Arrow table with \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_ID\_\_}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_SOURCE\_\_}}, and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_TARGET\_\_}} columns.

\sphinxlineitem{Raises}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}} – If \sphinxstyleemphasis{source\_col} or \sphinxstyleemphasis{target\_col} are missing from \sphinxstyleemphasis{table}.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleemphasis{Table}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{infer\_attribute\_map() (in module pycypher.ingestion.arrow\_utils)@\spxentry{infer\_attribute\_map()}\spxextra{in module pycypher.ingestion.arrow\_utils}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.arrow_utils.infer_attribute_map}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.ingestion.arrow\_utils.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{infer\_attribute\_map}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{table}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Return \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\{col: col\}}} for every non\sphinxhyphen{}reserved column in \sphinxstyleemphasis{table}.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Reserved columns (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_ID\_\_}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_SOURCE\_\_}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_TARGET\_\_}}) are excluded.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{table}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Table}}) – Arrow table with normalised column names.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Attribute map suitable for passing to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{EntityTable}} or
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RelationshipTable}}.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{dict}{[}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}, \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}{]}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}



\subsubsection{Output Writer}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.ingestion.output_writer}}\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:output-writer}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.ingestion.output\_writer@\spxentry{pycypher.ingestion.output\_writer}}\index{pycypher.ingestion.output\_writer@\spxentry{pycypher.ingestion.output\_writer}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Utilities for writing query result DataFrames to output sinks.

\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.output_writer.write_dataframe_to_uri}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{write\_dataframe\_to\_uri()}}}}} is the single entry point: it accepts a
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.DataFrame}}, a destination URI, and an optional explicit
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.config.OutputFormat}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{OutputFormat}}}}}, then writes the file in the
appropriate format.


\paragraph{Supported URI forms}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:supported-uri-forms}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bare filesystem paths: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{/path/to/output.csv}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{file://}} URIs: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{file:///path/to/output.csv}}

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Cloud URIs (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{s3://}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{gs://}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{https://}}, …) are \sphinxstylestrong{not} currently
supported and raise \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#NotImplementedError}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{NotImplementedError}}}.  Add cloud support by
integrating \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pyarrow.fs}} or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{fsspec}} as a separate enhancement.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Parent directories are created automatically when they do not exist.
\index{write\_dataframe\_to\_uri() (in module pycypher.ingestion.output\_writer)@\spxentry{write\_dataframe\_to\_uri()}\spxextra{in module pycypher.ingestion.output\_writer}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.output_writer.write_dataframe_to_uri}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.ingestion.output\_writer.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{write\_dataframe\_to\_uri}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{df}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{uri}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{fmt}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Write \sphinxstyleemphasis{df} to \sphinxstyleemphasis{uri} in the appropriate format.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Format is determined by \sphinxstyleemphasis{fmt} when provided; otherwise it is inferred
from the file extension of \sphinxstyleemphasis{uri}.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{df}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{pd.DataFrame}}) – The \sphinxhref{https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/reference/api/pandas.DataFrame.html\#pandas.DataFrame}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pandas.DataFrame}}} to serialise.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{uri}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Destination URI.  Bare paths and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{file://}} URIs are accepted.
Cloud URIs raise \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#NotImplementedError}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{NotImplementedError}}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{fmt}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.config.OutputFormat}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{OutputFormat}}}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – Explicit {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.config.OutputFormat}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{OutputFormat}}}}}.
When \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}}, the format is inferred from the URI extension.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Raises}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#NotImplementedError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{NotImplementedError}}} – If \sphinxstyleemphasis{uri} uses a cloud URI scheme
    (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{s3://}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{gs://}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{https://}}, …).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}} – If \sphinxstyleemphasis{fmt} is \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}} and the URI’s extension is not
    \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{.csv}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{.parquet}}, or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{.json}}.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{write\_relation\_to\_uri() (in module pycypher.ingestion.output\_writer)@\spxentry{write\_relation\_to\_uri()}\spxextra{in module pycypher.ingestion.output\_writer}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.output_writer.write_relation_to_uri}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.ingestion.output\_writer.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{write\_relation\_to\_uri}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{relation}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{uri}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{fmt}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Stream a DuckDB relation to \sphinxstyleemphasis{uri} via \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{COPY … TO}} (no pandas frame).

\sphinxAtStartPar
Out\sphinxhyphen{}of\sphinxhyphen{}core counterpart to {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.output_writer.write_dataframe_to_uri}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{write\_dataframe\_to\_uri()}}}}}: instead of
materialising a pandas DataFrame, the relation is streamed straight to disk
by DuckDB, so results larger than RAM can be written.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{relation}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{object}}}) – A \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DuckDBLazyFrame}} (as produced by
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DataSource.read\_relation()}} or the DuckDB backend).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{uri}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Destination URI.  Bare paths and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{file://}} URIs are accepted;
cloud URIs raise \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#NotImplementedError}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{NotImplementedError}}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{fmt}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.config.OutputFormat}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{OutputFormat}}}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – Explicit {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.config.OutputFormat}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{OutputFormat}}}}}.
When \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}}, inferred from the URI extension.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Raises}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#TypeError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{TypeError}}} – If \sphinxstyleemphasis{relation} is not a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DuckDBLazyFrame}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#NotImplementedError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{NotImplementedError}}} – If \sphinxstyleemphasis{uri} uses a cloud URI scheme.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}} – If the format cannot be resolved.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}



\subsubsection{Security}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.ingestion.security}}\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:security}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.ingestion.security@\spxentry{pycypher.ingestion.security}}\index{pycypher.ingestion.security@\spxentry{pycypher.ingestion.security}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Security utilities for input validation and sanitization.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This module provides security functions to prevent SQL injection, path traversal,
and other security vulnerabilities in data source operations.
\index{SECURITY\_LOGGER (in module pycypher.ingestion.security)@\spxentry{SECURITY\_LOGGER}\spxextra{in module pycypher.ingestion.security}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.security.SECURITY_LOGGER}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.ingestion.security.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{SECURITY\_LOGGER}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }<Logger pycypher.security (WARNING)>}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Dedicated security event logger — separate from the query audit logger
so that security events can be routed to a SIEM or alerting pipeline
independently.

\end{fulllineitems}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\index{security\_event\_log() (in module pycypher.ingestion.security)@\spxentry{security\_event\_log()}\spxextra{in module pycypher.ingestion.security}}

\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.security.security_event_log}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.ingestion.security.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{security\_event\_log}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{keyword-only-separator}{\DUrole{o}{\sphinxstyleabbreviation{*}}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{event\_type}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{input\_sample}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{source\_function}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{detail}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Log a security event for incident response.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Emits a structured JSON record to the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.security}} logger
whenever a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SecurityError}} is raised.  Records include enough
context for incident triage without leaking sensitive data.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The logger is \sphinxstylestrong{off by default} and activated by setting the
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_SECURITY\_LOG}} environment variable to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{1}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{true}},
or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{yes}}, or by calling {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.security.enable_security_log}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{enable\_security\_log()}}}}}.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{event\_type}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Category of the blocked threat (e.g.
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"sql\_injection"}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"path\_traversal"}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"ssrf"}}).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{input\_sample}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – The offending input, truncated for safety.
Parameter \sphinxstyleemphasis{values} should never be passed here — use
structural descriptions instead.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{source\_function}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – The validation function that caught the threat.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{detail}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – Optional free\sphinxhyphen{}text detail (e.g. matched pattern name).

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{validate\_sql\_query() (in module pycypher.ingestion.security)@\spxentry{validate\_sql\_query()}\spxextra{in module pycypher.ingestion.security}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.security.validate_sql_query}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.ingestion.security.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{validate\_sql\_query}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{query}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Validate a SQL query using an allowlist approach.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Only \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SELECT}} and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WITH … SELECT}} queries are permitted.  The
query is first stripped of comments (which are a common injection
vector) and then checked against a strict allowlist of permitted
statement prefixes.  Multiple statements are rejected.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{query}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – SQL query to validate.

\sphinxlineitem{Raises}
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.exceptions.SecurityError}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{SecurityError}}}}} – If the query is empty, contains comments, multiple
    statements, or is not an allowed statement type.

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Mask credentials in a URI for safe display in logs and console output.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Replaces the password component of URIs like
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{postgresql://user:s3cret@host/db}} with \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{***}} to prevent credential
leakage in logs, CLI output, and error messages.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{uri}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – The URI string to mask.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The URI with any password replaced by \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{***}}.  If the URI has no
credentials or cannot be parsed, returns the original string unchanged.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}



\subsection{Sinks}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:sinks}}

\subsubsection{Neo4j Sink}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:neo4j-sink}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Write pycypher query results to a Neo4j graph database using idempotent
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MERGE}} semantics.  Requires the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{neo4j}} driver (included in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{uv sync \sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}group dev}}):

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{uv} \PYG{n}{pip} \PYG{n}{install} \PYG{n}{neo4j}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.sinks.neo4j@\spxentry{pycypher.sinks.neo4j}}\index{pycypher.sinks.neo4j@\spxentry{pycypher.sinks.neo4j}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.sinks.neo4j}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Neo4j sink: write pycypher query results to a Neo4j graph database.

\sphinxAtStartPar
All writes use \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MERGE}} semantics, making every call idempotent — safe to
re\sphinxhyphen{}run as part of a recurring ETL pipeline.  Relationship endpoint nodes are
located with \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MATCH}}, so nodes must be written before relationships.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Quick start:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{import}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pandas}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{as}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pd}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{Star}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{sinks}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{neo4j}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{Neo4jSink}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{NodeMapping}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{RelationshipMapping}

\PYG{n}{context} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}entity}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{persons\PYGZus{}df}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{build}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) RETURN p.\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}ID\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{} AS pid, p.name AS name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{k}{with} \PYG{n}{Neo4jSink}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{bolt://localhost:7687}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{neo4j}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{pycypher}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n}{sink}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n}{sink}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{write\PYGZus{}nodes}\PYG{p}{(}
        \PYG{n}{result}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{n}{NodeMapping}\PYG{p}{(}
            \PYG{n}{label}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
            \PYG{n}{id\PYGZus{}column}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{pid}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
            \PYG{n}{property\PYGZus{}columns}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Requires the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{neo4j}} package:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{uv} \PYG{n}{pip} \PYG{n}{install} \PYG{n}{neo4j}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}
\index{Neo4jSink (class in pycypher.sinks.neo4j)@\spxentry{Neo4jSink}\spxextra{class in pycypher.sinks.neo4j}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.sinks.neo4j.Neo4jSink}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.sinks.neo4j.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{Neo4jSink}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{uri}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{user}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{password}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{keyword-only-separator}{\DUrole{o}{\sphinxstyleabbreviation{*}}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{database}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{batch\_size}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{500}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{encrypted}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Write\sphinxhyphen{}only sink that persists pycypher query results to Neo4j.

\sphinxAtStartPar
All writes use \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MERGE}} semantics, making every call idempotent.
Rows are sent to the database in batches via \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{UNWIND}} for efficiency.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The sink is a context manager; the underlying driver is closed on exit:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k}{with} \PYG{n}{Neo4jSink}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{uri}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{user}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{password}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n}{sink}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n}{sink}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{write\PYGZus{}nodes}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{df}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{node\PYGZus{}mapping}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{n}{sink}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{write\PYGZus{}relationships}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{df}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{rel\PYGZus{}mapping}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{uri}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Neo4j Bolt URI, e.g. \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"bolt://localhost:7687"}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{user}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Database username.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{password}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Database password.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{database}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – Target database name.  \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}} uses the server default.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{batch\_size}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Number of rows sent per \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{UNWIND}} transaction.
Tune this down if individual rows are very wide.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{encrypted}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – Explicitly enable (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{True}}) or disable (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{False}}) TLS
encryption for the driver connection.  \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}} (default) defers
to the Neo4j driver’s scheme\sphinxhyphen{}based defaults (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{bolt://}} is
unencrypted, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{bolt+s://}} or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{neo4j+s://}} are encrypted).
Set to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{True}} for production connections to enforce TLS.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Raises}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ImportError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ImportError}}} – If the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{neo4j}} driver package is not installed.

\end{description}\end{quote}

\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Merge rows of \sphinxstyleemphasis{df} as nodes into Neo4j using \sphinxstyleemphasis{mapping}.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{df}} (\sphinxhref{https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/reference/api/pandas.DataFrame.html\#pandas.DataFrame}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{DataFrame}}}) – DataFrame whose rows represent nodes.  Must contain
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{mapping.id\_column}} and all columns referenced by
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{mapping.property\_columns.values()}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{mapping}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.sinks.neo4j.NodeMapping}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{NodeMapping}}}}}) – Declares how columns map onto the target label and
properties.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Number of rows successfully written.  Rows with a null
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{id\_column}} value are skipped and not counted.

\sphinxlineitem{Raises}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}} – If a required column is absent from \sphinxstyleemphasis{df}.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{int}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{write\_relationships() (pycypher.sinks.neo4j.Neo4jSink method)@\spxentry{write\_relationships()}\spxextra{pycypher.sinks.neo4j.Neo4jSink method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.sinks.neo4j.Neo4jSink.write_relationships}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{write\_relationships}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{df}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{mapping}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Merge rows of \sphinxstyleemphasis{df} as relationships into Neo4j using \sphinxstyleemphasis{mapping}.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Both endpoint nodes are located with \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MATCH}}, so they must already
exist in the database.  Write nodes first with {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.sinks.neo4j.Neo4jSink.write_nodes}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{write\_nodes()}}}}}.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{df}} (\sphinxhref{https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/reference/api/pandas.DataFrame.html\#pandas.DataFrame}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{DataFrame}}}) – DataFrame whose rows represent relationships.  Must contain
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{mapping.source\_id\_column}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{mapping.target\_id\_column}},
and all columns referenced by
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{mapping.property\_columns.values()}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{mapping}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.sinks.neo4j.RelationshipMapping}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{RelationshipMapping}}}}}) – Declares how columns map onto the target relationship
type and properties.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Number of rows successfully written.  Rows with null endpoint
IDs are skipped.

\sphinxlineitem{Raises}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}} – If a required column is absent from \sphinxstyleemphasis{df}.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{int}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

Bases: \sphinxhref{https://pydantic.dev/docs/validation/latest/api/pydantic/base\_model/\#pydantic.BaseModel}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BaseModel}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Describes how DataFrame columns map onto a Neo4j node label.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{id\_column}} is used as the MERGE key — its value becomes the
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{id\_property}} on the node.  \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{property\_columns}} maps each Neo4j
property name to the DataFrame column that supplies its value.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Example:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{NodeMapping}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{label}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{id\PYGZus{}column}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{pid}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{property\PYGZus{}columns}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{full\PYGZus{}name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{age}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{age}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

Describes how DataFrame columns map onto a Neo4j relationship type.

\sphinxAtStartPar

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]

    \PYG{n}{rel\PYGZus{}type}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{KNOWS}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}

    \PYG{n}{target\PYGZus{}id\PYGZus{}column}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{tgt\PYGZus{}pid}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{property\PYGZus{}columns}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{since}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{since\PYGZus{}year}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\subsection{Distributed Execution}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:distributed-execution}}

\subsubsection{Cluster}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:cluster}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Coordination protocols, worker registration, query routing, and fault
tolerance for enterprise\sphinxhyphen{}scale distributed execution.  Provides local
implementations for testing; network transport is deferred to Phase 3.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.cluster@\spxentry{pycypher.cluster}}\index{pycypher.cluster@\spxentry{pycypher.cluster}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.cluster}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Distributed execution scaffolding for cluster deployment.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Defines the coordination protocols, worker node registration, query
distribution strategies, and fault tolerance patterns needed for
enterprise\sphinxhyphen{}scale distributed query execution.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This module provides \sphinxstylestrong{interfaces and local implementations} — the
actual network transport and distributed scheduling are deferred to
Phase 3.  The local implementations allow testing the coordination
logic without a real cluster.


\paragraph{Architecture}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:id217}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
┌──────────────────────────────────┐
│         ClusterCoordinator       │
│  (registers workers, routes      │
│   queries, aggregates metrics)   │
└──────┬───────────┬───────────┬───┘
       │           │           │
 ┌─────▼──┐  ┌─────▼──┐  ┌────▼───┐
 │Worker 1│  │Worker 2│  │Worker N│
 │(Star)  │  │(Star)  │  │(Star)  │
 └────────┘  └────────┘  └────────┘
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Each worker wraps a {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.star.Star}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Star}}}}} instance.  The
coordinator selects a worker via a pluggable {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.cluster.QueryRouter}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{QueryRouter}}}}}
strategy (round\sphinxhyphen{}robin, least\sphinxhyphen{}loaded, hash\sphinxhyphen{}based).

\sphinxAtStartPar
Usage:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{cluster}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{ClusterCoordinator}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{LocalWorker}

\PYG{n}{coord} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ClusterCoordinator}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{coord}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{register\PYGZus{}worker}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{LocalWorker}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{w1}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{coord}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{register\PYGZus{}worker}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{LocalWorker}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{w2}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{coord}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) RETURN p.name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{health} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{coord}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{cluster\PYGZus{}health}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

Falls back to round\sphinxhyphen{}robin when all workers have equal load.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Central coordinator for distributed query execution.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Manages worker registration, query routing, and aggregate health
monitoring.  Thread\sphinxhyphen{}safe for concurrent query submission.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{router}} ({\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.cluster.QueryRouter}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{QueryRouter}}}}}) – Query routing strategy.  Defaults to
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.cluster.RoundRobinRouter}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RoundRobinRouter}}}}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{heartbeat\_timeout\_s}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Seconds after which a worker with no
heartbeat is marked unavailable.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\subsection{CLI}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:cli}}

\subsubsection{NMETL CLI}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:nmetl-cli}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Command\sphinxhyphen{}line interface for running Cypher queries against YAML\sphinxhyphen{}configured
data pipelines.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.nmetl\_cli@\spxentry{pycypher.nmetl\_cli}}\index{pycypher.nmetl\_cli@\spxentry{pycypher.nmetl\_cli}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.nmetl_cli}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Command\sphinxhyphen{}line interface for the nmetl ETL pipeline runner.

\sphinxAtStartPar
nmetl is the command\sphinxhyphen{}line entry point for running PyCypher\sphinxhyphen{}based ETL
pipelines defined in a YAML configuration file.  It loads data sources,
executes Cypher queries, and writes results to configured output sinks.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Example usage:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Run a pipeline from a config file}
\PYG{n}{nmetl} \PYG{n}{run} \PYG{n}{pipeline}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{yaml}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Validate a config file without running}
\PYG{n}{nmetl} \PYG{n}{validate} \PYG{n}{pipeline}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{yaml}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} List all queries defined in the config}
\PYG{n}{nmetl} \PYG{n+nb}{list}\PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{n}{queries} \PYG{n}{pipeline}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{yaml}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Dry\PYGZhy{}run: show what would be executed}
\PYG{n}{nmetl} \PYG{n}{run} \PYG{n}{pipeline}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{yaml} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{n}{dry}\PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{n}{run}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}
\index{main() (in module pycypher.nmetl\_cli)@\spxentry{main()}\spxextra{in module pycypher.nmetl\_cli}}

Entry point for the nmetl command\sphinxhyphen{}line tool.

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}



\subsection{Security \& Observability}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:security-observability}}

\subsubsection{Audit Logging}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:audit-logging}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Opt\sphinxhyphen{}in structured query audit logging.  Emits one JSON record per query
execution with query\_id, timing, status, and row counts.  Activated via
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_AUDIT\_LOG}} environment variable.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.audit@\spxentry{pycypher.audit}}\index{pycypher.audit@\spxentry{pycypher.audit}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.audit}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Opt\sphinxhyphen{}in structured query audit logging.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Provides a dedicated \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.audit}} logger that emits one JSON record
per query execution (success or failure).  The audit log is \sphinxstylestrong{off by
default} and activated by setting the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_AUDIT\_LOG}} environment
variable to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{1}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{true}}, or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{yes}}.

\sphinxAtStartPar
When enabled, a \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/logging.handlers.html\#logging.StreamHandler}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{logging.StreamHandler}}} writing to \sphinxstyleemphasis{stderr} is
attached automatically.  For production use, configure the
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.audit}} logger via \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/logging.config.html\#module-logging.config}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{logging.config}}} or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{dictConfig}} to
route records to a file, syslog, or log aggregator.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Each record contains:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{query\_id}} — unique correlation ID (hex string).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{timestamp}} — ISO\sphinxhyphen{}8601 UTC timestamp.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{query}} — the query text (truncated to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{max\_query\_length}}).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{status}} — \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"ok"}} or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"error"}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{elapsed\_ms}} — wall\sphinxhyphen{}clock execution time in milliseconds.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{rows}} — number of result rows (success only).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{error\_type}} — exception class name (failure only).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{parameter\_keys}} — list of parameter names (never values).

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Security note: parameter \sphinxstyleemphasis{values} and full result data are \sphinxstylestrong{never}
logged.  Query text is truncated to prevent unbounded log growth.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Usage:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{export} \PYG{n}{PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}AUDIT\PYGZus{}LOG}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Queries are now logged to stderr via pycypher.audit logger.}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Programmatic activation:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{audit}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{enable\PYGZus{}audit\PYGZus{}log}
\PYG{n}{enable\PYGZus{}audit\PYGZus{}log}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Log a mutation operation (CREATE, SET, DELETE, MERGE, REMOVE).

\sphinxAtStartPar
Emits a structured JSON record for each mutation clause execution,
enabling audit trails for all write operations.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{query\_id}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – Unique correlation ID linking to the parent query.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{operation}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Mutation type (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"CREATE"}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"SET"}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"DELETE"}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"DETACH\_DELETE"}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"MERGE"}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"REMOVE"}}).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{entity\_type}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – The entity or relationship type affected.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{affected\_count}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Number of rows/entities affected.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{elapsed\_s}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Wall\sphinxhyphen{}clock execution time in seconds.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{details}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{dict}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{[}}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Any}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{]} }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{| }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – Optional extra context (e.g. property keys modified,
detached relationship count).  Values must be JSON\sphinxhyphen{}serializable.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Security note: entity \sphinxstyleemphasis{data} (property values, IDs) is \sphinxstylestrong{never} logged.
Only structural metadata (counts, types, property names) is recorded.

\end{fulllineitems}
