\subsubsection{Rate Limiter}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:rate-limiter}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Thread\sphinxhyphen{}safe token\sphinxhyphen{}bucket rate limiter for resource protection in
multi\sphinxhyphen{}tenant deployments.  Configured via \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_RATE\_LIMIT\_QPS}}
and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_RATE\_LIMIT\_BURST}}.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.rate\_limiter@\spxentry{pycypher.rate\_limiter}}\index{pycypher.rate\_limiter@\spxentry{pycypher.rate\_limiter}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.rate_limiter}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Query rate limiting for resource protection in multi\sphinxhyphen{}tenant deployments.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Provides a thread\sphinxhyphen{}safe token\sphinxhyphen{}bucket rate limiter that can be applied
per\sphinxhyphen{}session or per\sphinxhyphen{}caller to prevent query abuse.  Integrates with the
existing audit logging and configuration infrastructure.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Configuration is via environment variables:
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_RATE\_LIMIT\_QPS}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Maximum sustained queries per second (token refill rate).
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{0}} (default) disables rate limiting entirely.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_RATE\_LIMIT\_BURST}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Maximum burst size (bucket capacity).  Allows short bursts above the
sustained rate.  Defaults to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{10}}.

\end{description}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Usage:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{rate\PYGZus{}limiter}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}global\PYGZus{}limiter}

\PYG{n}{limiter} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}global\PYGZus{}limiter}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{limiter}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{acquire}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} raises RateLimitError if over limit}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Programmatic usage with custom settings:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{limiter} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{QueryRateLimiter}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{qps}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{5.0}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{burst}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{20}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{limiter}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{acquire}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{caller\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{user\PYGZhy{}123}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}
\index{QueryRateLimiter (class in pycypher.rate\_limiter)@\spxentry{QueryRateLimiter}\spxextra{class in pycypher.rate\_limiter}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.rate_limiter.QueryRateLimiter}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.rate\_limiter.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{QueryRateLimiter}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{qps}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{0.0}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{burst}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{10}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{keyword-only-separator}{\DUrole{o}{\sphinxstyleabbreviation{*}}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{per\_caller}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{False}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Configurable query rate limiter with optional per\sphinxhyphen{}caller tracking.

\sphinxAtStartPar
When \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{qps=0}} (the default from environment), the limiter is disabled
and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{acquire()}} is a no\sphinxhyphen{}op.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{qps}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Sustained queries per second (token refill rate).
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{0}} disables rate limiting.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{burst}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Maximum burst size (token bucket capacity).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{per\_caller}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}}) – If \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{True}}, maintain separate buckets per \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{caller\_id}}.
If \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{False}} (default), use a single shared bucket.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\subsection{Utilities}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:utilities}}

\subsubsection{Types}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:types}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Type aliases and type definitions used across the codebase.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.cypher\_types@\spxentry{pycypher.cypher\_types}}\index{pycypher.cypher\_types@\spxentry{pycypher.cypher\_types}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.cypher_types}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Backend\sphinxhyphen{}agnostic type aliases for the pycypher query engine.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This module defines type aliases that decouple the evaluator stack from
concrete pandas types.  Today they resolve to pandas types (zero overhead);
tomorrow they can be swapped to protocol\sphinxhyphen{}based abstractions that support
Dask, Polars, or DuckDB backends.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Usage:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{cypher\PYGZus{}types}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{FrameSeries}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{FrameDataFrame}

\PYG{k}{def}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nf}{evaluate}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n+nb+bp}{self}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{expr}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n}{Expression}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{o}{\PYGZgt{}} \PYG{n}{FrameSeries}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The aliases live here — not in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{backend\_engine.py}} — to avoid circular
imports (evaluators import types; backend\_engine imports relational\_models
which evaluators also need).
\index{FrameSeries (in module pycypher.cypher\_types)@\spxentry{FrameSeries}\spxextra{in module pycypher.cypher\_types}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.cypher_types.FrameSeries}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.cypher\_types.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{FrameSeries}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
A 1\sphinxhyphen{}D array of values aligned with a BindingFrame.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Today: literally \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.Series}}.
Future: a protocol that pd.Series, dask Series, and Polars Series satisfy.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{FrameDataFrame (in module pycypher.cypher\_types)@\spxentry{FrameDataFrame}\spxextra{in module pycypher.cypher\_types}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.cypher_types.FrameDataFrame}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.cypher\_types.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{FrameDataFrame}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
A 2\sphinxhyphen{}D table of bindings or results.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Today: literally \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.DataFrame}}.
Future: a protocol that pd.DataFrame, dask DataFrame, and Polars DataFrame
satisfy.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{BackendFrame (in module pycypher.cypher\_types)@\spxentry{BackendFrame}\spxextra{in module pycypher.cypher\_types}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.cypher_types.BackendFrame}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.cypher\_types.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{BackendFrame}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Opaque handle to a backend\sphinxhyphen{}specific DataFrame.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Used in the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BackendEngine}} protocol and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{InstrumentedBackend}} wrapper
to annotate frame parameters.  Each concrete backend returns its own type
(\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.DataFrame}} for pandas, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DuckDBLazyFrame}} for DuckDB, etc.) but
callers of the protocol treat frames as opaque handles — they are only
passed back into backend methods or materialised via \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{to\_pandas()}}.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Today: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Any}} (since the protocol is heterogeneous across backends).
Future: a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{TypeVar}} bound to a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Frame}} protocol once all backends
conform to a structural frame interface.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{BackendMask (in module pycypher.cypher\_types)@\spxentry{BackendMask}\spxextra{in module pycypher.cypher\_types}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.cypher_types.BackendMask}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.cypher\_types.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{BackendMask}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Opaque handle to a backend\sphinxhyphen{}specific boolean mask.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Used in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BackendEngine.filter()}} to annotate the mask parameter.
Today \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.Series{[}bool{]}}} for pandas, but other backends may use their
own mask representations.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{ColumnValues (in module pycypher.cypher\_types)@\spxentry{ColumnValues}\spxextra{in module pycypher.cypher\_types}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.cypher_types.ColumnValues}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.cypher\_types.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{ColumnValues}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Values for a new or replaced column in a backend frame.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Passed to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BackendEngine.assign\_column()}}.  May be a Series, list,
scalar, or backend\sphinxhyphen{}specific column representation.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{SourceObject (in module pycypher.cypher\_types)@\spxentry{SourceObject}\spxextra{in module pycypher.cypher\_types}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.cypher_types.SourceObject}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.cypher\_types.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{SourceObject}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Raw data source passed to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BackendEngine.scan\_entity()}}.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Typically a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.DataFrame}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pa.Table}}, or file path, depending on
ingestion context.

\end{fulllineitems}



\subsubsection{Exceptions}
\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:exceptions}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Complete exception hierarchy.  All exceptions are importable from the
top\sphinxhyphen{}level \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher}} package.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!pycypher.exceptions@\spxentry{pycypher.exceptions}}\index{pycypher.exceptions@\spxentry{pycypher.exceptions}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.exceptions}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Custom exceptions for the PyCypher package.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This package defines custom exception classes used throughout the PyCypher
package for handling specific error conditions during query parsing and
execution.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The module is split into three submodules for maintainability:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.exceptions.base}} — Infrastructure (docs links, environment detection, sanitization)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.exceptions.parse}} — Parse\sphinxhyphen{}time errors (syntax, AST conversion)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.exceptions.runtime}} — Runtime errors (type, variable, function, resource, security)

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Return a user\sphinxhyphen{}safe error string from \sphinxstyleemphasis{exc}.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Strips internal file paths and traceback fragments that could leak
implementation details.  Masks URI credentials (passwords) to prevent
credential exposure in error output.  The exception \sphinxstyleemphasis{type} name is
preserved so the user can still identify the category of error.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret

{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{message}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{query\_fragment}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{''}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{node\_type}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{''}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Exception raised when AST conversion from grammar parse tree fails.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This exception is thrown when the grammar parser successfully parses a query
but the conversion to typed AST models fails, typically due to grammar/AST
model synchronization issues or unsupported syntax constructs.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#SyntaxError}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SyntaxError}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
User\sphinxhyphen{}friendly wrapper around Lark parse errors.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Wraps low\sphinxhyphen{}level Lark \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{UnexpectedInput}} exceptions into a message that
shows the offending line, column position, and what the parser expected,
without leaking internal terminal names like \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_ANON\_0}}.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Includes keyword typo detection: when the token at the error position is
close to a known Cypher keyword, a “Did you mean …?” suggestion is
appended automatically.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}

{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#TimeoutError}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{TimeoutError}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Raised when a cache lock cannot be acquired within the timeout.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This prevents deadlocks in the result cache from hanging query execution
indefinitely.  When raised, the cache operation is skipped and query
execution proceeds without caching.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\end{description}\end{quote}
\index{timeout\_seconds (pycypher.exceptions.CacheLockTimeoutError attribute)@\spxentry{timeout\_seconds}\spxextra{pycypher.exceptions.CacheLockTimeoutError attribute}}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{timeout\_seconds}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
The lock acquisition timeout that was exceeded.

\end{fulllineitems}

Raised when a node label or relationship type is not registered in the context.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This is a \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}} subclass so that existing code catching
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}} continues to work.  New code that only wants to handle the
“entity/relationship type absent from context” case (e.g. MERGE’s create
fallback) can catch this specific subclass without accidentally swallowing
unrelated errors.

\end{description}\end{quote}

the configured timeout, preventing runaway queries from consuming resources
indefinitely.

Raised when a query fails inside a cluster worker.

\sphinxAtStartPar

so that distributed failures can be diagnosed without correlating
separate log streams.

\end{description}\end{quote}

    \PYG{n}{worker}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (n) RETURN n}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{except} \PYG{n}{WorkerExecutionError} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n}{exc}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Worker }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{exc}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{worker\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ failed after }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{exc}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{elapsed\PYGZus{}ms}\PYG{l+s+si}{:}\PYG{l+s+s2}{.0f}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ms}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}

Exception raised when a Cypher expression has an unexpected type.

\sphinxAtStartPar

but the resulting type is not what was expected for the given context.

\section{Shared API}
\label{\detokenize{api/shared:shared-api}}\label{\detokenize{api/shared::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The Shared package contains common utilities used across all PyCypher packages.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!shared@\spxentry{shared}}\index{shared@\spxentry{shared}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/shared:module-shared}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Shared utilities and common functionality for PyCypher\sphinxhyphen{}NMETL.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This package provides shared utilities, logging configuration, and helper
functions used across the PyCypher\sphinxhyphen{}NMETL ecosystem.


\subsection{Submodules}
\label{\detokenize{api/shared:submodules}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{shared.helpers}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Common utility functions: null checks, string matching, URI handling,
base64 encoding/decoding.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{shared.logger}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Centralised logging configuration with Rich console formatting and
optional JSON output.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{shared.metrics}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Lightweight in\sphinxhyphen{}process query metrics collector for diagnostic access.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{shared.telemetry}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Optional Pyroscope continuous profiling configuration.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{shared.otel}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
OpenTelemetry distributed tracing integration (optional dependency).

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{shared.exporters}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Pluggable metrics export adapters (Prometheus, StatsD, JSON).

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{shared.deprecation}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Standardised deprecation warnings with \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{deprecated()}} decorator and
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{emit\_deprecation()}} helper for graceful API evolution.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{shared.compat}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
API surface snapshot/diff tools and Neo4j Cypher compatibility notes
for detecting breaking changes and guiding migrations.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{shared.file\_intent}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
File Intent Registration System for multi\sphinxhyphen{}agent coordination.
Agents register intent before editing shared files; the system
classifies risk levels and warns on conflicts.

\end{description}


\subsection{Core Modules}
\label{\detokenize{api/shared:core-modules}}

\subsubsection{Logger}
\label{\detokenize{api/shared:logger}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Structured logging configuration shared across all packages.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!shared.logger@\spxentry{shared.logger}}\index{shared.logger@\spxentry{shared.logger}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/shared:module-shared.logger}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Shared logging configuration module.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This module provides a centralized logger configuration using Rich for
enhanced console output formatting. The logger level defaults to WARNING
but can be overridden via the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_LOG\_LEVEL}} environment variable
(e.g. \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_LOG\_LEVEL=DEBUG}}).

\sphinxAtStartPar
Set \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_LOG\_FORMAT=json}} for machine\sphinxhyphen{}readable JSON\sphinxhyphen{}lines output
suitable for log aggregation pipelines (ELK, Datadog, Splunk).


\paragraph{Query correlation}
\label{\detokenize{api/shared:query-correlation}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/shared:shared.logger.set_query_id}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{set\_query\_id()}}}}} / {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/shared:shared.logger.get_query_id}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{get\_query\_id()}}}}} to propagate a query
correlation ID through the call stack via \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/contextvars.html\#module-contextvars}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{contextvars}}}.  The JSON
formatter automatically includes the active \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{query\_id}} in every log
line, eliminating the need to pass \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{extra=\{"query\_id": ...\}}} manually.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{shared}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{logger}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{set\PYGZus{}query\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{reset\PYGZus{}query\PYGZus{}id}

\PYG{n}{token} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{set\PYGZus{}query\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{abc123}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{try}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n}{LOGGER}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{info}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{processing}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} JSON output includes \PYGZdq{}query\PYGZus{}id\PYGZdq{}: \PYGZdq{}abc123\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{k}{finally}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n}{reset\PYGZus{}query\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{token}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}
\index{LOGGING\_LEVEL (in module shared.logger)@\spxentry{LOGGING\_LEVEL}\spxextra{in module shared.logger}}

\begin{fulllineitems}

\subsubsection{Metrics}
\label{\detokenize{api/shared:metrics}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Query execution metrics collection, snapshots, and diagnostic summaries.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!shared.metrics@\spxentry{shared.metrics}}\index{shared.metrics@\spxentry{shared.metrics}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/shared:module-shared.metrics}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Lightweight in\sphinxhyphen{}process query metrics collector.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Aggregates query execution statistics — counts, timing percentiles, error
rates, and slow\sphinxhyphen{}query detection — without requiring an external metrics
backend.  All data lives in\sphinxhyphen{}process and is designed for diagnostic access
via \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{QueryMetrics.snapshot()}}.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The collector is \sphinxstylestrong{thread\sphinxhyphen{}safe} (uses a \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/threading.html\#threading.Lock}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{threading.Lock}}}) for
compatibility with the free\sphinxhyphen{}threaded Python 3.14t build.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Enable/disable via the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_METRICS\_ENABLED}} environment variable
(default: enabled).  When disabled, all recording methods are no\sphinxhyphen{}ops.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Usage:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{shared}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{metrics}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{QUERY\PYGZus{}METRICS}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Record a completed query}
\PYG{n}{QUERY\PYGZus{}METRICS}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{record\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{query\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{abc123}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{elapsed\PYGZus{}s}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.042}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{rows}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{150}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{clauses}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Match}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Return}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Record an error}
\PYG{n}{QUERY\PYGZus{}METRICS}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{record\PYGZus{}error}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{query\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{def456}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{error\PYGZus{}type}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{TypeError}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{elapsed\PYGZus{}s}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.003}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Get a point\PYGZhy{}in\PYGZhy{}time snapshot}
\PYG{n}{stats} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{QUERY\PYGZus{}METRICS}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{snapshot}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{stats}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Immutable point\sphinxhyphen{}in\sphinxhyphen{}time view of collected metrics.

Return automated health classification.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"healthy"}} — error rate < 5\% and slow query rate < 10\%.
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"degraded"}} — error rate 5–20\% or slow query rate 10–30\%.
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"unhealthy"}} — error rate > 20\% or slow query rate > 30\%.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Return a detailed diagnostic report with actionable recommendations.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Analyses performance degradation, error patterns, clause hotspots,
cache efficiency, and resource pressure.  Designed for production
debugging — each section includes concrete recommendations when
problems are detected.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}

\sphinxAtStartPar

\end{description}\end{quote}

\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{memory\_delta\_mb}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – RSS memory change during query execution (MB).

\sphinxAtStartPar
Update the latest result cache statistics.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Called after each query execution with the output of
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ResultCache.stats()}}.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}

\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{stats}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{dict}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{[}}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Any}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{]}}}) – Dict with keys like \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{result\_cache\_hits}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{result\_cache\_misses}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{result\_cache\_hit\_rate}}, etc.

\end{description}\end{quote}

\subsubsection{Helpers}
\label{\detokenize{api/shared:helpers}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Serialisation and utility functions.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!shared.helpers@\spxentry{shared.helpers}}\index{shared.helpers@\spxentry{shared.helpers}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/shared:module-shared.helpers}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Shared utility functions used across the PyCypher\sphinxhyphen{}NMETL project.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This module provides common helper functions for URI handling, encoding/decoding
objects, data type conversions, and user\sphinxhyphen{}experience helpers that are used
throughout the project.
\index{is\_null\_value() (in module shared.helpers)@\spxentry{is\_null\_value()}\spxextra{in module shared.helpers}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/shared:shared.helpers.is_null_value}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{shared.helpers.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{is\_null\_value}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{x}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Return \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{True}} if \sphinxstyleemphasis{x} is \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}} or a floating\sphinxhyphen{}point \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{NaN}}.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This is the canonical null\sphinxhyphen{}check used throughout the PyCypher evaluator
pipeline.  Cypher treats both \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}} and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{NaN}} as null, so every
point that needs to distinguish “has a value” from “is missing” should
call this function rather than re\sphinxhyphen{}implementing the check inline.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{x}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{object}}}) – The value to test.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{True}} when \sphinxstyleemphasis{x} is \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}} or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{float('nan')}}.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{bool}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{is\_null\_raw\_list() (in module shared.helpers)@\spxentry{is\_null\_raw\_list()}\spxextra{in module shared.helpers}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/shared:shared.helpers.is_null_raw_list}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{shared.helpers.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{is\_null\_raw\_list}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{value}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Return \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{True}} if \sphinxstyleemphasis{value} is null, empty, or not a valid iterable collection.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Used by collection evaluators to detect when a row’s list expression
produced a missing/non\sphinxhyphen{}list value so each caller can handle the
degenerate case (skip iteration, treat as empty list, or return the
accumulator unchanged).

\sphinxAtStartPar
Handles \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}}, empty pandas arrays/Series, empty Python lists/tuples,
and scalar pandas null values (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{NaN}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.NA}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.NaT}}).
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{value}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{object}}}) – A single Python value from a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.Series}} iteration.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{True}} if the value should be treated as an empty/missing list.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{bool}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{suggest\_close\_match() (in module shared.helpers)@\spxentry{suggest\_close\_match()}\spxextra{in module shared.helpers}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/shared:shared.helpers.suggest_close_match}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{shared.helpers.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{suggest\_close\_match}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{target}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{candidates}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{cutoff}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{0.6}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Return a “Did you mean ‘…’?” hint string when a close match is found.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Uses \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/difflib.html\#difflib.get\_close\_matches}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{difflib.get\_close\_matches()}}} to find the single best candidate
within \sphinxstyleemphasis{cutoff} similarity.  Returns an empty string when the target already
appears verbatim in \sphinxstyleemphasis{candidates} (no hint needed for correct input) or when
no candidate is close enough.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{target}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – The misspelled or unknown name entered by the user.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{candidates}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Iterable}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{[}}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{]}}}) – Iterable of valid names to compare against.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{cutoff}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Minimum similarity ratio in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[}0, 1{]}}}.  Defaults to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{0.6}},
matching the project\sphinxhyphen{}wide convention used at all three call sites.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A hint string such as \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"  Did you mean 'tolower'?"}} (with a leading
two\sphinxhyphen{}space indent for inline appending to error messages), or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{""}} when
no close match exists.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Examples}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{suggest\PYGZus{}close\PYGZus{}match}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{persn}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{company}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{\PYGZdq{}  Did you mean \PYGZsq{}person\PYGZsq{}?\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{suggest\PYGZus{}close\PYGZus{}match}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{xyz}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{company}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{\PYGZsq{}\PYGZsq{}}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{suggest\PYGZus{}close\PYGZus{}match}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{)}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} exact match — no hint}
\PYG{g+go}{\PYGZsq{}\PYGZsq{}}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{decode() (in module shared.helpers)@\spxentry{decode()}\spxextra{in module shared.helpers}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/shared:shared.helpers.decode}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{shared.helpers.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{decode}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{encoded\_data}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Decode a base64\sphinxhyphen{}encoded JSON object.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Accepts JSON\sphinxhyphen{}serializable Python values (dicts, lists, strings, numbers,
booleans, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}}).  Raises \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}} for any input that is not
valid base64\sphinxhyphen{}encoded JSON — including pickle payloads, which are rejected
to prevent deserialisation of arbitrary code.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{encoded\_data}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Base64\sphinxhyphen{}encoded UTF\sphinxhyphen{}8 JSON string, as produced by
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/shared:shared.helpers.encode}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{encode()}}}}}.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The decoded Python value.

\sphinxlineitem{Raises}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}} – If the input is not valid base64\sphinxhyphen{}encoded JSON.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleemphasis{Any}}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{encode() (in module shared.helpers)@\spxentry{encode()}\spxextra{in module shared.helpers}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/shared:shared.helpers.encode}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{shared.helpers.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{encode}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{source\_object}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{to\_bytes}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{False}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Encode a JSON\sphinxhyphen{}serializable Python object as a base64 string.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Accepts dicts, lists, strings, numbers, booleans, and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}}.  Raises
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}} for types that are not JSON\sphinxhyphen{}serializable (e.g. sets,
custom class instances, bytes).  Using JSON rather than \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pickle}} prevents
deserialisation of arbitrary code in {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/shared:shared.helpers.decode}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{decode()}}}}}.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{source\_object}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Any}}}) – A JSON\sphinxhyphen{}serializable Python value.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{to\_bytes}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}}) – If \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{True}}, return the base64 string as \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#bytes}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{bytes}}}
instead of \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{str}}}.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Base64\sphinxhyphen{}encoded representation as \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{str}}} (default) or
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#bytes}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{bytes}}}.

\sphinxlineitem{Raises}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}} – If \sphinxstyleemphasis{source\_object} is not JSON\sphinxhyphen{}serializable or if
    encoding fails for any other reason.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str} | \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#bytes}{bytes}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\subsubsection{Telemetry}
\label{\detokenize{api/shared:telemetry}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Optional telemetry integration (Pyroscope profiling).
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!shared.telemetry@\spxentry{shared.telemetry}}\index{shared.telemetry@\spxentry{shared.telemetry}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/shared:module-shared.telemetry}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Configuration for Pyroscope continuous profiling.

\sphinxAtStartPar
All settings are configurable via environment variables:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYROSCOPE\_SERVER}}  – Pyroscope endpoint (default \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{http://localhost:4040}})

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYROSCOPE\_APP\_NAME}} – application name tag (default \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{nmetl}})

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYROSCOPE\_SAMPLE\_RATE}} – samples per second (default \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{100}})

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYROSCOPE\_ENABLED}}  – set to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{0}} or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{false}} to disable (default enabled)

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
If Pyroscope is not installed or the server is unreachable the module
silently degrades — no import\sphinxhyphen{}time crash.


\subsection{Observability}
\label{\detokenize{api/shared:observability}}

\subsubsection{OpenTelemetry}
\label{\detokenize{api/shared:opentelemetry}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Tracing and span instrumentation for query execution phases.
Falls back to no\sphinxhyphen{}op stubs when \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{opentelemetry}} is not installed.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!shared.otel@\spxentry{shared.otel}}\index{shared.otel@\spxentry{shared.otel}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/shared:module-shared.otel}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
OpenTelemetry integration for PyCypher distributed tracing.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Wraps query execution with OpenTelemetry spans for end\sphinxhyphen{}to\sphinxhyphen{}end
distributed tracing.  All configuration is via standard \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{OTEL\_*}}
environment variables.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Zero\sphinxhyphen{}config design}: if \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{opentelemetry\sphinxhyphen{}api}} is not installed, every
public function is a silent no\sphinxhyphen{}op — no import errors, no runtime cost.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Enable via:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_OTEL\_ENABLED=1}} (default: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{0}})

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Standard OTEL env vars (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{OTEL\_SERVICE\_NAME}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{OTEL\_EXPORTER\_*}}, etc.)

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Usage:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{shared}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{otel}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{trace\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}tracer}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Context\PYGZhy{}manager style}
\PYG{k}{with} \PYG{n}{trace\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) RETURN p.name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{query\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{abc}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n}{span}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{query}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{n}{span}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{set\PYGZus{}attribute}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{result.rows}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n+nb}{len}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{result}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Manual tracer access}
\PYG{n}{tracer} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}tracer}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{with} \PYG{n}{tracer}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{start\PYGZus{}as\PYGZus{}current\PYGZus{}span}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{custom\PYGZhy{}operation}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n}{span}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\index{trace\_query() (in module shared.otel)@\spxentry{trace\_query()}\spxextra{in module shared.otel}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/shared:shared.otel.trace_query}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{shared.otel.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{trace\_query}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{query}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{keyword-only-separator}{\DUrole{o}{\sphinxstyleabbreviation{*}}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{query\_id}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{parameters}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Wrap a query execution in an OpenTelemetry span.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Sets standard attributes on the span:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{db.system}} = \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"pycypher"}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{db.statement}} = query text (truncated to 500 chars)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{db.operation}} = first Cypher keyword (MATCH, CREATE, etc.)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.query\_id}} = correlation ID (if provided)

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
On exception, the span is marked ERROR and the exception is recorded.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{query}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – The Cypher query string.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{query\_id}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – Optional correlation ID for cross\sphinxhyphen{}system tracing.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{parameters}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{dict}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{[}}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Any}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{]} }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{| }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – Optional query parameters (keys logged, values omitted
for security).

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Yields}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The active span (real or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_NullSpan}}).

\end{description}\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Example:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k}{with} \PYG{n}{trace\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (n) RETURN n}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{query\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{q\PYGZhy{}123}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n}{span}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{query}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{n}{span}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{set\PYGZus{}attribute}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{result.rows}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n+nb}{len}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{result}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\end{fulllineitems}

\subsubsection{Exporters}
\label{\detokenize{api/shared:exporters}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Metrics export adapters: Prometheus, StatsD, and JSON file exporters
with a unified \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MetricsExporter}} protocol.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!shared.exporters@\spxentry{shared.exporters}}\index{shared.exporters@\spxentry{shared.exporters}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/shared:module-shared.exporters}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Pluggable metrics export adapters for external monitoring systems.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Bridges the in\sphinxhyphen{}process {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/shared:shared.metrics.QueryMetrics}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{QueryMetrics}}}}} collector to
external observability backends — Prometheus, StatsD/Datadog, and JSON file
export.  All exporters work with zero external dependencies by default
(Prometheus text format and JSON need only stdlib).

\sphinxAtStartPar
Configuration is entirely via environment variables:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_METRICS\_EXPORT}} — comma\sphinxhyphen{}separated list of active exporters
(e.g. \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{prometheus,statsd,json}}).  Default: none.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_METRICS\_PREFIX}} — metric name prefix (default: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher}}).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_STATSD\_HOST}} / \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_STATSD\_PORT}} — StatsD endpoint.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_METRICS\_JSON\_PATH}} — path for JSON file export.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_METRICS\_EXPORT\_INTERVAL\_S}} — push interval (default: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{60}}).

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Usage:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{shared}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{exporters}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}exporters}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{export\PYGZus{}once}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Auto\PYGZhy{}configured from env vars}
\PYG{n}{exporters} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}exporters}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Push current metrics to all configured backends}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{shared}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{metrics}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{QUERY\PYGZus{}METRICS}
\PYG{n}{export\PYGZus{}once}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{QUERY\PYGZus{}METRICS}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{snapshot}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Or get Prometheus text format directly}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{shared}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{exporters}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{PrometheusExporter}
\PYG{n}{exporter} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{PrometheusExporter}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{exporter}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{render}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{QUERY\PYGZus{}METRICS}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{snapshot}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Export metrics in Prometheus text exposition format.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Generates \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{text/plain; version=0.0.4}} output suitable for scraping
by Prometheus, VictoriaMetrics, or any OpenMetrics\sphinxhyphen{}compatible collector.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Does not require any external dependencies — generates the text format
directly.  For a full Prometheus client with push\sphinxhyphen{}gateway support, see
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{prometheus\_client}}.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{prefix}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Metric name prefix.  Default: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher}}.

\end{description}\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Push metrics to a StatsD\sphinxhyphen{}compatible daemon via UDP.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Uses a plain UDP socket (stdlib only) — no external dependencies.
Compatible with StatsD, Datadog Agent, Telegraf, and similar collectors.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{host}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – StatsD host.  Default from \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_STATSD\_HOST}} or
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{127.0.0.1}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{port}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – StatsD port.  Default from \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_STATSD\_PORT}} or
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{8125}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{prefix}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Metric name prefix.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Append metrics snapshots to a JSON\sphinxhyphen{}lines file.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Each export appends a single JSON line with a timestamp, suitable for
ingestion by ELK, Datadog Logs, Loki, or any log aggregation pipeline.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{path}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – Output file path.  Default from \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_METRICS\_JSON\_PATH}}
or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{metrics.jsonl}}.

\end{description}\end{quote}

Auto\sphinxhyphen{}configure exporters from \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_METRICS\_EXPORT}} env var.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
List of configured {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/shared:shared.exporters.MetricsExporter}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MetricsExporter}}}}} instances.
Empty list if no exporters are configured.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{list}{[}{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/shared:shared.exporters.MetricsExporter}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleemphasis{MetricsExporter}}}}{]}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Example:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} In .env:  PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}METRICS\PYGZus{}EXPORT=prometheus,json}
\PYG{n}{exporters} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}exporters}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Returns [PrometheusExporter(), JSONFileExporter()]}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\end{fulllineitems}

\subsection{Compatibility}
\label{\detokenize{api/shared:compatibility}}

\subsubsection{API Surface Compatibility}
\label{\detokenize{api/shared:api-surface-compatibility}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Snapshot and diff the public API surface for backward\sphinxhyphen{}compatibility
checking between releases.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!shared.compat@\spxentry{shared.compat}}\index{shared.compat@\spxentry{shared.compat}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/shared:module-shared.compat}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
API surface snapshot and compatibility checking utilities.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Provides tools for detecting breaking changes between PyCypher releases by
capturing and comparing public API surfaces.  This module supports two
primary workflows:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Snapshot} — capture the current public API to a JSON file for
future comparison:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{shared}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{compat}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{snapshot\PYGZus{}api\PYGZus{}surface}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{save\PYGZus{}snapshot}

\PYG{n}{surface} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{snapshot\PYGZus{}api\PYGZus{}surface}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{pycypher}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{save\PYGZus{}snapshot}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{surface}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{api\PYGZus{}v0.0.19.json}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Diff} — compare two snapshots to find added, removed, and changed
symbols:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{shared}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{compat}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{load\PYGZus{}snapshot}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{diff\PYGZus{}surfaces}

\PYG{n}{old} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{load\PYGZus{}snapshot}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{api\PYGZus{}v0.0.18.json}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{new} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{load\PYGZus{}snapshot}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{api\PYGZus{}v0.0.19.json}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{report} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{diff\PYGZus{}surfaces}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{old}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{new}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{report}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{summary}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\end{enumerate}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The snapshot captures every name in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_all\_\_}} (or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{dir()}} filtered to
public names), along with its kind (function, class, exception, constant)
and call signature where available.


\paragraph{Neo4j Cypher compatibility}
\label{\detokenize{api/shared:neo4j-cypher-compatibility}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/shared:shared.compat.NEO4J_COMPAT_NOTES}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{NEO4J\_COMPAT\_NOTES}}}}} dictionary documents known syntax
differences between standard Neo4j Cypher and the PyCypher dialect so
that users migrating queries can check compatibility up front.
\index{NEO4J\_COMPAT\_NOTES (in module shared.compat)@\spxentry{NEO4J\_COMPAT\_NOTES}\spxextra{in module shared.compat}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/shared:shared.compat.NEO4J_COMPAT_NOTES}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{shared.compat.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{NEO4J\_COMPAT\_NOTES}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{dict}\DUrole{p}{{[}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}\DUrole{p}{,}\DUrole{w}{ }\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{dict}\DUrole{p}{{[}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}\DUrole{p}{,}\DUrole{w}{ }\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{Any}\DUrole{p}{{]}}\DUrole{p}{{]}}}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\{'APOC functions': \{'notes': 'APOC is Neo4j\sphinxhyphen{}specific. PyCypher has its own scalar function library.', 'supported': False, 'workaround': 'Check `nmetl functions` for available PyCypher functions.'\}, 'CALL procedure()': \{'notes': 'CALL clause supported for registered procedures.', 'supported': True\}, 'CALL \{ ... \} IN TRANSACTIONS': \{'notes': 'PyCypher operates in\sphinxhyphen{}memory; transaction batching is not applicable.', 'supported': False\}, 'CASE expressions': \{'notes': 'Both simple and generic CASE WHEN forms supported.', 'supported': True\}, 'CREATE': \{'notes': 'Supported for node and relationship creation.', 'supported': True\}, 'CREATE CONSTRAINT': \{'notes': 'Constraints are enforced at the DataFrame level.', 'supported': False\}, 'CREATE INDEX': \{'notes': 'PyCypher uses DataFrame column access; no index DDL needed.', 'supported': False\}, 'DELETE': \{'notes': 'Supported for node and relationship deletion.', 'supported': True\}, 'DETACH DELETE': \{'notes': 'Not yet supported. Use explicit relationship deletion first.', 'supported': False, 'workaround': 'DELETE relationships manually before deleting nodes.'\}, 'EXISTS subqueries': \{'notes': 'Supported in WHERE clauses.', 'supported': True\}, 'FOREACH': \{'notes': 'Supported for mutation within loops.', 'supported': True\}, 'LOAD CSV': \{'notes': 'Use ContextBuilder with pandas DataFrames or DuckDBReader instead.', 'supported': False, 'workaround': 'pd.read\_csv() → ContextBuilder().add\_entity(...)'\}, 'List comprehensions': \{'notes': 'Supported: {[}x IN list WHERE pred | expr{]}', 'supported': True\}, 'MERGE': \{'notes': 'Supported with ON CREATE SET / ON MATCH SET.', 'supported': True\}, 'Map projections': \{'notes': 'Supported: node \{ .prop1, .prop2, key: expr \}', 'supported': True\}, 'OPTIONAL MATCH': \{'notes': 'Left\sphinxhyphen{}outer\sphinxhyphen{}join semantics, same as Neo4j.', 'supported': True\}, 'Parameters (\$param)': \{'notes': 'Supported via execute\_query(params=\{...\}).', 'supported': True\}, 'Pattern comprehensions': \{'notes': 'Supported: {[}(a)\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}>(b) | b.name{]}', 'supported': True\}, 'Temporal types': \{'notes': 'date(), time(), datetime(), duration() supported.', 'supported': True\}, 'UNION / UNION ALL': \{'notes': 'Both variants supported.', 'supported': True\}, 'UNWIND': \{'notes': 'Supported for list expansion.', 'supported': True\}, 'Variable\sphinxhyphen{}length paths {[}*m..n{]}': \{'notes': 'Supported with safety limits (max 20 unbounded hops, 1M frontier rows).', 'supported': True\}, 'WITH': \{'notes': 'Projection and aggregation piping supported.', 'supported': True\}, 'allShortestPaths': \{'notes': 'Returns all minimum\sphinxhyphen{}hop paths between node pairs.', 'supported': True\}, 'shortestPath': \{'notes': 'BFS\sphinxhyphen{}based implementation with configurable hop limits.', 'supported': True\}\}}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Known syntax/feature differences between Neo4j Cypher and PyCypher.
Keys are feature names; values are dicts with \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{supported}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{notes}},
and optional \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{workaround}} fields.

\end{fulllineitems}

\subsubsection{Deprecation Utilities}
\label{\detokenize{api/shared:deprecation-utilities}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Emit structured deprecation warnings with configurable severity and
suggested replacements.
\index{module@\spxentry{module}!shared.deprecation@\spxentry{shared.deprecation}}\index{shared.deprecation@\spxentry{shared.deprecation}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/shared:module-shared.deprecation}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Lightweight deprecation utilities for PyCypher API evolution.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Provides a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{deprecated()}} decorator and a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{emit\_deprecation()}} helper
that standardise deprecation warnings across the codebase.  All warnings
include a removal version, a migration hint, and are emitted via the
standard \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/warnings.html\#module-warnings}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{warnings}}} module so that users can filter them with
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/warnings.html\#warnings.filterwarnings}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{warnings.filterwarnings()}}}.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Usage:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{shared}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{deprecation}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{deprecated}

\PYG{n+nd}{@deprecated}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{since}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{0.0.19}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{removed\PYGZus{}in}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{0.1.0}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{alternative}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{new\PYGZus{}func}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{def}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nf}{old\PYGZus{}func}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{x}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n+nb}{int}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{o}{\PYGZgt{}} \PYG{n+nb}{int}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{k}{return} \PYG{n}{x} \PYG{o}{+} \PYG{l+m+mi}{1}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Calling old\PYGZus{}func() emits:}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} DeprecationWarning: old\PYGZus{}func is deprecated since v0.0.19 and will}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} be removed in v0.1.0. Use new\PYGZus{}func instead.}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
For non\sphinxhyphen{}decorator contexts (e.g. \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_getattr\_\_}} module\sphinxhyphen{}level hooks),
use {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/shared:shared.deprecation.emit_deprecation}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{emit\_deprecation()}}}}} directly:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{shared}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{deprecation}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{emit\PYGZus{}deprecation}

\PYG{k}{def}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nf+fm}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}getattr\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n+nb}{str}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{o}{\PYGZgt{}} \PYG{n+nb}{type}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{k}{if} \PYG{n}{name} \PYG{o}{==} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{OldClass}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:}
        \PYG{n}{emit\PYGZus{}deprecation}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{OldClass}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{since}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{0.0.19}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
                         \PYG{n}{removed\PYGZus{}in}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{0.1.0}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{alternative}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{NewClass}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
        \PYG{k}{return} \PYG{n}{NewClass}
    \PYG{k}{raise} \PYG{n+ne}{AttributeError}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Decorator that marks a function or class as deprecated.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Emits a \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#DeprecationWarning}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DeprecationWarning}}} on every call with a standardised
message including version timeline and migration guidance.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{since}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Version where deprecation was introduced (e.g. \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"0.0.19"}}).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{removed\_in}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Version where the symbol will be removed (e.g. \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"0.1.0"}}).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{alternative}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Name of the replacement (e.g. \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"new\_func"}}).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{detail}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Additional migration context.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A decorator that wraps the target with a deprecation warning.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Any}{\sphinxstyleemphasis{Any}}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Example:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n+nd}{@deprecated}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{since}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{0.0.19}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{removed\PYGZus{}in}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{0.1.0}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{alternative}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ContextBuilder}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{class}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nc}{OldBuilder}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\end{fulllineitems}



\section{Package Overview}
\label{\detokenize{api/index:package-overview}}

\subsection{PyCypher}
\label{\detokenize{api/index:pycypher}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The core package — Cypher query parsing, AST processing, and BindingFrame
execution.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Entry points:}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.star.Star}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Star}}}}} — execute Cypher queries against DataFrames

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.context_builder.ContextBuilder}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ContextBuilder}}}}} — build graph contexts from tabular data

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.semantic_validator.validate_query}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{validate\_query()}}}}} — pre\sphinxhyphen{}execution query validation

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Execution pipeline:}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{grammar\_parser}} / \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{grammar\_transformers}} — Cypher → Lark → AST

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ast\_models}} / \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ast\_converter}} / \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ast\_rewriter}} — immutable Pydantic AST, conversion, and rewriting

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pattern\_matcher}} / \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{path\_expander}} — MATCH clause evaluation

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{binding\_frame}} / \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{binding\_evaluator}} — vectorised expression execution

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pipeline}} — stage\sphinxhyphen{}based parse → validate → plan → execute pipeline

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{backend\_engine}} — pluggable backends (Pandas, DuckDB, Polars)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{query\_planner}} / \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{query\_optimizer}} / \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{query\_profiler}} — planning, optimization, and profiling

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Evaluators:}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{arithmetic\_evaluator}} / \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{boolean\_evaluator}} / \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{comparison\_evaluator}} — operator evaluation

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{collection\_evaluator}} / \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{string\_predicate\_evaluator}} / \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{exists\_evaluator}} — complex expressions

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{aggregation\_evaluator}} / \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{aggregation\_planner}} — grouped and full\sphinxhyphen{}table aggregation

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{scalar\_function\_evaluator}} / \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{scalar\_functions}} — 131 built\sphinxhyphen{}in scalar functions

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Multi\sphinxhyphen{}query composition:}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{multi\_query\_analyzer}} / \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{multi\_query\_executor}} — dependency\sphinxhyphen{}aware ETL pipelines

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Write path:}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{mutation\_engine}} — CREATE, SET, DELETE, MERGE, FOREACH

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{sinks.neo4j}} — write results to Neo4j

\end{itemize}


\subsection{Shared}
\label{\detokenize{api/index:shared}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Common utilities: structured logging, query metrics, serialisation helpers,
observability, and compatibility checking.
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{logger}} — \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{LOGGER}} singleton for structured output

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{metrics}} — \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{QUERY\_METRICS}} collector and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MetricsSnapshot}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{helpers}} — serialisation and utility functions

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{telemetry}} — optional Pyroscope profiling integration

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{otel}} — OpenTelemetry tracing (no\sphinxhyphen{}op fallback when not installed)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{exporters}} — Prometheus, StatsD, and JSON metrics export

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{compat}} — API surface snapshot and backward\sphinxhyphen{}compatibility diffing

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{deprecation}} — structured deprecation warnings

\end{itemize}

\sphinxstepscope
