\subsection{Why Does PyCypher Need \sphinxstyleliteralintitle{\sphinxupquote{\_\_ID\_\_}} Columns?}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/data_model:why-does-pycypher-need-id-columns}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
If you’ve tried loading data and been confused by \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_ID\_\_}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_SOURCE\_\_}},
or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_TARGET\_\_}} — you’re not alone.  Here’s what they are and why they exist.


\subsubsection{The Short Answer}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/data_model:the-short-answer}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
PyCypher executes \sphinxstylestrong{graph queries} against \sphinxstylestrong{tabular data}.  Graph queries
traverse connections between things (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MATCH (a)\sphinxhyphen{}{[}:KNOWS{]}\sphinxhyphen{}>(b)}}).  For
that to work, every row needs a unique identity so relationships can
reference it.
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_ID\_\_}} — uniquely identifies each entity (node) within its type

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_SOURCE\_\_}} — which entity a relationship starts from

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_TARGET\_\_}} — which entity a relationship points to

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
These are the structural glue that turns flat tables into a queryable graph.

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{note}{Note:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{You do not need to rename your columns.}  PyCypher accepts your
existing column names via the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{id\_col}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{source\_col}}, and
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{target\_col}} parameters.  The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_ID\_\_}}/\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_SOURCE\_\_}}/\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_TARGET\_\_}}
names are internal — PyCypher renames them for you.
\end{sphinxadmonition}


\subsubsection{Why Not Just Use Row Position?}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/data_model:why-not-just-use-row-position}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
You might wonder: “Why can’t PyCypher just use row 0, row 1, row 2?”
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Relationships need stable references.}  If you say “Person 42 KNOWS
Person 17”, you need those IDs to look up the actual rows.  Row position
can change if the data is filtered, sorted, or reloaded.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Cross\sphinxhyphen{}type joins.}  When a relationship connects two different entity
types (e.g., \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Person \sphinxhyphen{}{[}:WORKS\_AT{]}\sphinxhyphen{}> Company}}), the source and target
IDs come from different tables.  Row positions would be meaningless
across tables.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Mutation correctness.}  \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SET}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{CREATE}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MERGE}}, and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DELETE}}
operations must track which exact row to update.  A stable identity
column guarantees this.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Performance.}  During query execution, PyCypher stores only ID columns
in intermediate results (BindingFrames).  Full row data is fetched
on\sphinxhyphen{}demand only when needed — typically at \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN}} or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WHERE}}.  This
dramatically reduces memory usage and speeds up multi\sphinxhyphen{}hop traversals.

\end{enumerate}


\subsection{Using Your Existing Column Names}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/data_model:using-your-existing-column-names}}

\subsubsection{The Most Common Pattern}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/data_model:the-most-common-pattern}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
You already have a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{customer\_id}} column.  Don’t rename it — tell
PyCypher which column to use:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{import}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pandas}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{as}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pd}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{ingestion}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{Star}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Your existing data — no \PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}ID\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{} column needed}
\PYG{n}{customers} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{customer\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{101}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{102}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{103}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Alice}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Bob}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Carol}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{email}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{alice@example.com}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{bob@example.com}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{carol@example.com}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{n}{orders} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{order\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1001}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{1002}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{1003}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{1004}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{customer\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{101}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{102}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{101}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{103}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{product}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Widget}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Gadget}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Gizmo}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Widget}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{amount}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mf}{29.99}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mf}{49.99}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mf}{19.99}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mf}{29.99}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{n}{context} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}entity}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Customer}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{customers}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{id\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{customer\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}entity}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Order}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{orders}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{id\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{order\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}relationship}\PYG{p}{(}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{PLACED}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{n}{orders}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{n}{source\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{customer\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}    \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} which customer placed it}
        \PYG{n}{target\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{order\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}       \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} which order was placed}
    \PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{build}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{n}{star} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (c:Customer)\PYGZhy{}[:PLACED]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(o:Order) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN c.name, o.product, o.amount}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Behind the scenes, PyCypher renames \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{customer\_id}} → \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_ID\_\_}} in the
Customer table, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{order\_id}} → \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_ID\_\_}} in the Order table, and
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{customer\_id}} → \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_SOURCE\_\_}} / \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{order\_id}} → \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_TARGET\_\_}} in the
relationship table.  \sphinxstylestrong{Your original column names remain accessible as
properties} (e.g., \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{c.customer\_id}} still works in queries).


\subsubsection{From the CLI}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/data_model:from-the-cli}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{nmetl query}} command accepts the same column mapping:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Entity: Label=path[:id\PYGZus{}col]}
nmetl\PYG{+w}{ }query\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}MATCH (c:Customer) RETURN c.name\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s+se}{\PYGZbs{}}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}entity\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}Customer=customers.csv:customer\PYGZus{}id\PYGZdq{}}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Relationship: Type=path:source\PYGZus{}col:target\PYGZus{}col}
nmetl\PYG{+w}{ }query\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}MATCH (c:Customer)\PYGZhy{}[:PLACED]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(o:Order) RETURN c.name, o.product\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s+se}{\PYGZbs{}}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}entity\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}Customer=customers.csv:customer\PYGZus{}id\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s+se}{\PYGZbs{}}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}entity\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}Order=orders.csv:order\PYGZus{}id\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s+se}{\PYGZbs{}}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}rel\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}PLACED=orders.csv:customer\PYGZus{}id:order\PYGZus{}id\PYGZdq{}}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{In YAML Pipeline Config}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/data_model:in-yaml-pipeline-config}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n+nt}{sources}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{  }\PYG{n+nt}{entities}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{p+pIndicator}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nt}{id}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{customers}
\PYG{+w}{      }\PYG{n+nt}{uri}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{data/customers.csv}
\PYG{+w}{      }\PYG{n+nt}{entity\PYGZus{}type}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{Customer}
\PYG{+w}{      }\PYG{n+nt}{id\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{customer\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{+w}{       }\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} your column name}

\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{p+pIndicator}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nt}{id}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{orders}
\PYG{+w}{      }\PYG{n+nt}{uri}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{data/orders.csv}
\PYG{+w}{      }\PYG{n+nt}{entity\PYGZus{}type}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{Order}
\PYG{+w}{      }\PYG{n+nt}{id\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{order\PYGZus{}id}

\PYG{+w}{  }\PYG{n+nt}{relationships}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{p+pIndicator}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nt}{id}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{placed}
\PYG{+w}{      }\PYG{n+nt}{uri}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{data/orders.csv}
\PYG{+w}{      }\PYG{n+nt}{relationship\PYGZus{}type}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{PLACED}
\PYG{+w}{      }\PYG{n+nt}{source\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{customer\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{+w}{   }\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} your column name}
\PYG{+w}{      }\PYG{n+nt}{target\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{order\PYGZus{}id}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{When No ID Column Exists}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/data_model:when-no-id-column-exists}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
If your data has no natural identifier, \sphinxstylestrong{omit} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{id\_col}} and PyCypher
auto\sphinxhyphen{}generates sequential integer IDs (0, 1, 2, …):

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} No id\PYGZus{}col — PyCypher assigns \PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}ID\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{} = 0, 1, 2, ...}
\PYG{n}{products} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Widget}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Gadget}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Gizmo}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{price}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mf}{9.99}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mf}{49.99}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mf}{19.99}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{context} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}entity}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Product}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{products}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{build}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
This works for entities that are only queried by properties (not
referenced by relationships).  If you need to connect them via
relationships, auto\sphinxhyphen{}generated IDs are positional and fragile — prefer
explicit IDs.


\subsection{Real\sphinxhyphen{}World Examples}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/data_model:real-world-examples}}

\subsubsection{Example 1: E\sphinxhyphen{}Commerce (Customer → Order → Product)}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/data_model:example-1-e-commerce-customer-order-product}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Starting data — three CSVs as they might come from a database export:

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{customers.csv}}:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
customer\PYGZus{}id,name,email,city
C001,Alice,alice@co.com,NYC
C002,Bob,bob@co.com,LA
C003,Carol,carol@co.com,NYC
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{products.csv}}:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
sku,title,price,category
SKU\PYGZhy{}100,Widget,29.99,Hardware
SKU\PYGZhy{}200,Gadget,49.99,Electronics
SKU\PYGZhy{}300,Gizmo,19.99,Hardware
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{orders.csv}}:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
order\PYGZus{}id,customer\PYGZus{}id,sku,quantity,order\PYGZus{}date
ORD\PYGZhy{}1,C001,SKU\PYGZhy{}100,2,2024\PYGZhy{}01\PYGZhy{}15
ORD\PYGZhy{}2,C002,SKU\PYGZhy{}200,1,2024\PYGZhy{}01\PYGZhy{}16
ORD\PYGZhy{}3,C001,SKU\PYGZhy{}300,3,2024\PYGZhy{}01\PYGZhy{}17
ORD\PYGZhy{}4,C003,SKU\PYGZhy{}100,1,2024\PYGZhy{}01\PYGZhy{}18
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Loading into PyCypher:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{context} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}entity}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Customer}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{customers.csv}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{id\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{customer\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}entity}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Product}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{products.csv}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{id\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{sku}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}relationship}\PYG{p}{(}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ORDERED}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{orders.csv}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{n}{source\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{customer\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{n}{target\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{sku}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{build}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{n}{star} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} \PYGZdq{}What hardware did NYC customers order?\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{l+s+s2}{    MATCH (c:Customer)\PYGZhy{}[:ORDERED]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(p:Product)}
\PYG{l+s+s2}{    WHERE c.city = }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{NYC}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ AND p.category = }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Hardware}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}
\PYG{l+s+s2}{    RETURN c.name AS customer, p.title AS product}
\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Example 2: Social Network (User follows User)}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/data_model:example-2-social-network-user-follows-user}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Same entity type on both sides of the relationship:

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{users.csv}}:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
user\PYGZus{}id,username,joined
1,alice,2023\PYGZhy{}01\PYGZhy{}01
2,bob,2023\PYGZhy{}02\PYGZhy{}15
3,carol,2023\PYGZhy{}03\PYGZhy{}10
4,dave,2023\PYGZhy{}04\PYGZhy{}20
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{follows.csv}}:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
follower\PYGZus{}id,followee\PYGZus{}id,since
1,2,2023\PYGZhy{}03\PYGZhy{}01
1,3,2023\PYGZhy{}04\PYGZhy{}01
2,3,2023\PYGZhy{}05\PYGZhy{}01
3,4,2023\PYGZhy{}06\PYGZhy{}01
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{context} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}entity}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{User}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{users.csv}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{id\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{user\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}relationship}\PYG{p}{(}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{FOLLOWS}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{follows.csv}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{n}{source\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{follower\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{n}{target\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{followee\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{build}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{n}{star} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} \PYGZdq{}Who does Alice follow, and who do they follow?\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{l+s+s2}{    MATCH (a:User }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{username: }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{alice}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZcb{})\PYGZhy{}[:FOLLOWS]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(b:User)\PYGZhy{}[:FOLLOWS]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(c:User)}
\PYG{l+s+s2}{    RETURN a.username AS user, b.username AS follows, c.username AS follows\PYGZus{}of\PYGZus{}follows}
\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Example 3: Multi\sphinxhyphen{}Entity (Person + Company + Employment)}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/data_model:example-3-multi-entity-person-company-employment}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{import}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pandas}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{as}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pd}

\PYG{n}{people} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{emp\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{E001}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{E002}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{E003}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Alice}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Bob}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Carol}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{department}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Engineering}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Marketing}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Engineering}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{n}{companies} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{company\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ACME}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{GLOB}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Acme Corp}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Globex Inc}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{industry}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Tech}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Finance}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{n}{employment} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{emp\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{E001}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{E002}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{E003}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{company\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ACME}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{GLOB}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ACME}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{role}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Senior Engineer}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Marketing Lead}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Junior Engineer}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{start\PYGZus{}year}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2020}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2021}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2023}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{n}{context} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}entity}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{people}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{id\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{emp\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}entity}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Company}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{companies}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{id\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{company\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}relationship}\PYG{p}{(}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{WORKS\PYGZus{}AT}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{n}{employment}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{n}{source\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{emp\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{n}{target\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{company\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{build}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{n}{star} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} \PYGZdq{}Who works at Acme Corp, and what\PYGZsq{}s their role?\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{l+s+s2}{    MATCH (p:Person)\PYGZhy{}[r:WORKS\PYGZus{}AT]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(c:Company }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name: }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Acme Corp}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZcb{})}
\PYG{l+s+s2}{    RETURN p.name AS employee, r.role AS role, r.start\PYGZus{}year AS since}
\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Data Preparation Guide}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/data_model:data-preparation-guide}}

\subsubsection{Converting Existing Tables}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/data_model:converting-existing-tables}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Most real\sphinxhyphen{}world data needs minimal preparation.  The key question is:
\sphinxstylestrong{which column uniquely identifies each row?}


\begin{savenotes}\sphinxattablestart
\sphinxthistablewithglobalstyle
\centering
\begin{tabular}[t]{\X{30}{100}\X{30}{100}\X{40}{100}}
\sphinxtoprule
\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Source data
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
ID column
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
PyCypher setup
\\
\sphinxmidrule
\sphinxtableatstartofbodyhook
\sphinxAtStartPar
Database table with primary key
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
The PK column
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{id\_col="pk\_column"}}
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
CSV with a unique name/code
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
The unique column
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{id\_col="name"}} or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{id\_col="code"}}
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
CSV with no unique column
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Omit \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{id\_col}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Auto\sphinxhyphen{}generated sequential IDs
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
Data with composite keys
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Create a combined column
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
See below
\\
\sphinxbottomrule
\end{tabular}
\sphinxtableafterendhook\par
\sphinxattableend\end{savenotes}


\subsubsection{Handling Composite Keys}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/data_model:handling-composite-keys}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
If uniqueness comes from multiple columns (e.g., \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{(year, department)}}),
create a combined ID before loading:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{df} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{year}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2023}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2023}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2024}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{dept}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Eng}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Sales}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Eng}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{budget}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{100000}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{80000}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{120000}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Create a composite ID}
\PYG{n}{df}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{budget\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{df}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{year}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{astype}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n+nb}{str}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{o}{+} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}} \PYG{o}{+} \PYG{n}{df}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{dept}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}

\PYG{n}{context} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}entity}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Budget}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{df}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{id\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{budget\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{build}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{ID Generation Strategies}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/data_model:id-generation-strategies}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
When your data has no natural identifier:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{import}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{uuid}

\PYG{n}{df} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{event}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{login}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{click}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{purchase}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{timestamp}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{2024\PYGZhy{}01\PYGZhy{}01 10:00}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{2024\PYGZhy{}01\PYGZhy{}01 10:05}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{2024\PYGZhy{}01\PYGZhy{}01 10:12}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Option 1: Sequential integers (simplest)}
\PYG{n}{df}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n+nb}{range}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n+nb}{len}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{df}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Option 2: UUIDs (globally unique)}
\PYG{n}{df}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{n+nb}{str}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{uuid}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{uuid4}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{k}{for} \PYG{n}{\PYGZus{}} \PYG{o+ow}{in} \PYG{n+nb}{range}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n+nb}{len}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{df}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{]}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Option 3: Hash of content (deterministic)}
\PYG{n}{df}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{df}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{apply}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{k}{lambda} \PYG{n}{r}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{r}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{event}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{r}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{timestamp}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{axis}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{n}{context} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}entity}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Event}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{df}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{id\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{build}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Type Coercion}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/data_model:type-coercion}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{ID types must match between entity and relationship tables.}  If your
entity has integer IDs but your relationship CSV loads them as strings,
the join will produce zero results.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Entity: integer IDs}
\PYG{n}{people} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{person\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{3}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{A}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{B}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{C}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Relationship: string IDs (common after CSV loading)}
\PYG{n}{knows} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{from}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{1}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{2}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{to}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{2}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{3}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Fix: convert to matching types before loading}
\PYG{n}{knows}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{from}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{knows}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{from}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{astype}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n+nb}{int}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{knows}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{to}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{knows}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{to}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{astype}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n+nb}{int}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Troubleshooting Common Data Issues}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/data_model:troubleshooting-common-data-issues}}

\subsubsection{Empty Query Results}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/data_model:empty-query-results}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Symptom}: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MATCH (a)\sphinxhyphen{}{[}:REL{]}\sphinxhyphen{}>(b)}} returns an empty DataFrame.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Most likely cause}: ID type mismatch between entity and relationship.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Diagnose: check the dtypes}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{entity\PYGZus{}df}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{customer\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{dtype}\PYG{p}{)}      \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} int64}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{relationship\PYGZus{}df}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{customer\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{dtype}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} object (string!)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Fix: align the types}
\PYG{n}{relationship\PYGZus{}df}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{customer\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{relationship\PYGZus{}df}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{customer\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{astype}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n+nb}{int}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Other causes}:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Relationship \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{source\_col}}/\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{target\_col}} point to wrong columns

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
IDs in the relationship don’t exist in the entity table (dangling references)

\end{itemize}


\subsubsection{KeyError: \sphinxstyleliteralintitle{\sphinxupquote{\_\_ID\_\_}}}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/data_model:keyerror-id}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Symptom}: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{KeyError: '\_\_ID\_\_'}} when loading data.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Cause}: Using \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{from\_dict()}} or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{from\_dataframe()}} without providing
an \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_ID\_\_}} column or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{id\_col}} parameter.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} This fails — no \PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}ID\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{} and no id\PYGZus{}col}
\PYG{n}{df} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Alice}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Bob}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{table} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{EntityTable}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{from\PYGZus{}dataframe}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{df}\PYG{p}{)}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} KeyError!}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Fix option 1: add \PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}ID\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{} column}
\PYG{n}{df}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}ID\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{]}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Fix option 2: use ContextBuilder with id\PYGZus{}col}
\PYG{n}{context} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}entity}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{df}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{id\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{build}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Fix option 3: let ContextBuilder auto\PYGZhy{}generate IDs (omit id\PYGZus{}col)}
\PYG{n}{context} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}entity}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{df}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{build}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Duplicate ID Warning}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/data_model:duplicate-id-warning}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Symptom}: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MATCH}} returns unexpected extra rows (cross\sphinxhyphen{}join behavior).

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Cause}: Multiple rows share the same \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_ID\_\_}} within an entity type.
Each duplicate multiplies the result rows.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Diagnose: check for duplicates}
\PYG{n}{dupes} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{df}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{n}{df}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{customer\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{duplicated}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{keep}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k+kc}{False}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{]}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Found }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n+nb}{len}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{dupes}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ duplicate IDs}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Fix: deduplicate before loading}
\PYG{n}{df} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{df}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{drop\PYGZus{}duplicates}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{subset}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{customer\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{keep}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{first}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Missing Relationship Columns}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/data_model:missing-relationship-columns}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Symptom}: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}} or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{KeyError}} when adding a relationship.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Cause}: The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{source\_col}} or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{target\_col}} name doesn’t match any
column in the data.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Check actual column names}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{df}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{columns}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{tolist}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} [\PYGZsq{}cust\PYGZus{}id\PYGZsq{}, \PYGZsq{}prod\PYGZus{}id\PYGZsq{}, \PYGZsq{}qty\PYGZsq{}]  — not \PYGZdq{}customer\PYGZus{}id\PYGZdq{}!}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Fix: use the actual column names}
\PYG{n}{context} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}relationship}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{BOUGHT}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{df}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{source\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{cust\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}      \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} matches the actual column}
    \PYG{n}{target\PYGZus{}col}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{prod\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Quick Reference}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/data_model:quick-reference}}

\begin{savenotes}\sphinxattablestart
\sphinxthistablewithglobalstyle
\centering
\begin{tabular}[t]{\X{25}{100}\X{25}{100}\X{50}{100}}
\sphinxtoprule
\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Internal column
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Your parameter
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Purpose
\\
\sphinxmidrule
\sphinxtableatstartofbodyhook
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_ID\_\_}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{id\_col="your\_column"}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Unique identifier for each entity row
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_SOURCE\_\_}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{source\_col="your\_column"}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Which entity a relationship starts from
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_TARGET\_\_}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{target\_col="your\_column"}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Which entity a relationship points to
\\
\sphinxbottomrule
\end{tabular}
\sphinxtableafterendhook\par
\sphinxattableend\end{savenotes}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Rules:}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Entity IDs must be unique within each entity type

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Relationship source/target values must match entity IDs (same type)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
If \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{id\_col}} is omitted, sequential integers are auto\sphinxhyphen{}generated

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{source\_col}} and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{target\_col}} are required for relationships

\end{itemize}

\sphinxstepscope


\section{Variables in PyCypher}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/variables:variables-in-pycypher}}\label{\detokenize{user_guide/variables::doc}}

\subsection{Understanding Variable Representation}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/variables:understanding-variable-representation}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
As of the latest version, PyCypher uses a consistent \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Variable}} class throughout the AST to represent all variable references. This ensures type safety and makes variable tracking more robust.


\subsection{The Variable Class}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/variables:the-variable-class}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{ast\PYGZus{}models}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{Variable}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Create a variable}
\PYG{n}{var} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Variable}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Access the name}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{var}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{p}{)}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} \PYGZdq{}person\PYGZdq{}}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Where Variables Are Used}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/variables:where-variables-are-used}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Variables appear in many AST node types:


\subsubsection{Node Patterns}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/variables:node-patterns}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{ast\PYGZus{}models}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{NodePattern}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{Variable}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Node pattern with variable}
\PYG{n}{node} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{NodePattern}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{variable}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{Variable}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{n}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{labels}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{properties}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k+kc}{None}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Relationship Patterns}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/variables:relationship-patterns}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{ast\PYGZus{}models}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{RelationshipPattern}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{Variable}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Relationship pattern with variable}
\PYG{n}{rel} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{RelationshipPattern}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{variable}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{Variable}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{r}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{types}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{KNOWS}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{properties}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k+kc}{None}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{direction}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{outgoing}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Property Lookups}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/variables:property-lookups}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{ast\PYGZus{}models}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{PropertyLookup}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{Variable}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Property lookup on a variable}
\PYG{n}{prop} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{PropertyLookup}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{expression}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{Variable}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{property\PYGZus{}name}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{age}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{List Comprehensions}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/variables:list-comprehensions}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{ast\PYGZus{}models}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{ListComprehension}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{Variable}

\PYG{n}{comp} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ListComprehension}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{variable}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{Variable}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{x}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{list\PYGZus{}expression}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{some\PYGZus{}list}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{filter\PYGZus{}expression}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{some\PYGZus{}filter}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{map\PYGZus{}expression}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{some\PYGZus{}map}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Migration from String Variables}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/variables:migration-from-string-variables}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
If you have code that previously used string variables, you need to update it:

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Old code:}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{node} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{NodePattern}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{variable}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{n}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} String}
    \PYG{n}{labels}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{New code:}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{ast\PYGZus{}models}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{Variable}

\PYG{n}{node} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{NodePattern}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{variable}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{Variable}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{n}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Variable instance}
    \PYG{n}{labels}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Benefits}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/variables:benefits}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Using \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Variable}} instances provides several advantages:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Type Safety}: Pydantic validates that variables are proper Variable instances

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Consistency}: Same representation everywhere in the AST

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Extensibility}: Can add metadata to variables (scope, type, etc.)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Better Validation}: Can track variable definitions and references more easily

\end{enumerate}


\subsection{See Also}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/variables:see-also}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{PyCypher API}}}} \sphinxhyphen{} Complete API reference for Variable class

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{tutorials/basic_query_parsing::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Basic Query Execution}}}} \sphinxhyphen{} Tutorial on working with variables

\end{itemize}

\sphinxstepscope


\section{AST Nodes}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/ast_nodes:ast-nodes}}\label{\detokenize{user_guide/ast_nodes::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Complete reference for all AST node types in PyCypher.


\subsection{Overview}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/ast_nodes:overview}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
PyCypher uses strongly\sphinxhyphen{}typed AST nodes based on Pydantic models. All AST nodes inherit from the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ASTNode}} base class and provide validation, serialization, and tree traversal capabilities.


\subsection{Node Hierarchy}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/ast_nodes:node-hierarchy}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The AST follows the openCypher grammar structure:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Query} \sphinxhyphen{} Top\sphinxhyphen{}level query container

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Clause} \sphinxhyphen{} MATCH, CREATE, RETURN, WITH, WHERE, etc.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Expression} \sphinxhyphen{} All expressions (arithmetic, comparison, literals, etc.)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Pattern} \sphinxhyphen{} Graph patterns for matching and creation

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Literal} \sphinxhyphen{} Literal values (integers, strings, booleans, lists, maps)

\end{itemize}


\subsection{Core Node Types}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/ast_nodes:core-node-types}}

\subsubsection{Query}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/ast_nodes:query}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The root node for all Cypher queries:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{ast\PYGZus{}models}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{Query}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{Match}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{Return}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} A Query contains clauses}
\PYG{n}{query} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{clauses}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}
    \PYG{n}{Match}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{Return}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Clauses}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/ast_nodes:clauses}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Match Clause}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{ast\PYGZus{}models}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{Match}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{Pattern}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{NodePattern}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{Variable}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} MATCH (n:Person)}
\PYG{n}{match} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Match}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{pattern}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{Pattern}\PYG{p}{(}
        \PYG{n}{pattern\PYGZus{}paths}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}
            \PYG{n}{PatternPath}\PYG{p}{(}
                \PYG{n}{node\PYGZus{}pattern}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{NodePattern}\PYG{p}{(}
                    \PYG{n}{variable}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{Variable}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{n}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
                    \PYG{n}{labels}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}
                \PYG{p}{)}
            \PYG{p}{)}
        \PYG{p}{]}
    \PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Return Clause}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{ast\PYGZus{}models}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{Return}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{ReturnBody}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{ReturnItem}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{Variable}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} RETURN n.name}
\PYG{n}{return\PYGZus{}clause} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Return}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{return\PYGZus{}body}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{ReturnBody}\PYG{p}{(}
        \PYG{n}{return\PYGZus{}items}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}
            \PYG{n}{ReturnItem}\PYG{p}{(}
                \PYG{n}{expression}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{PropertyLookup}\PYG{p}{(}
                    \PYG{n}{expression}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{Variable}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{n}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
                    \PYG{n}{property\PYGZus{}name}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
                \PYG{p}{)}
            \PYG{p}{)}
        \PYG{p}{]}
    \PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Expressions}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/ast_nodes:expressions}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Comparison}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{ast\PYGZus{}models}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{Comparison}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{Variable}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{IntegerLiteral}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} n.age \PYGZgt{} 30}
\PYG{n}{comparison} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Comparison}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{operator}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZgt{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{left}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{PropertyLookup}\PYG{p}{(}
        \PYG{n}{expression}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{Variable}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{n}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{n}{property\PYGZus{}name}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{age}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{right}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{IntegerLiteral}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{value}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{30}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Arithmetic}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{ast\PYGZus{}models}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{Arithmetic}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} a + b}
\PYG{n}{addition} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Arithmetic}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{operator}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{+}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{left}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{Variable}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{a}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{right}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{Variable}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{b}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{List Comprehension}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{ast\PYGZus{}models}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{ListComprehension}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} [x IN items WHERE x \PYGZgt{} 5 | x * 2]}
\PYG{n}{list\PYGZus{}comp} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ListComprehension}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{variable}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{Variable}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{x}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{list\PYGZus{}expr}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{Variable}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{items}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{where}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{Comparison}\PYG{p}{(}
        \PYG{n}{operator}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZgt{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{n}{left}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{Variable}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{x}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{n}{right}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{IntegerLiteral}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{value}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{5}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{map\PYGZus{}expr}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{Arithmetic}\PYG{p}{(}
        \PYG{n}{operator}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{*}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{n}{left}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{Variable}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{x}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{n}{right}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{IntegerLiteral}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{value}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Patterns}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/ast_nodes:patterns}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Node Pattern}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{ast\PYGZus{}models}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{NodePattern}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{Variable}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{MapLiteral}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} (person:Person \PYGZob{}name: \PYGZsq{}Alice\PYGZsq{}\PYGZcb{})}
\PYG{n}{node} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{NodePattern}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{variable}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{Variable}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{labels}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{properties}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{MapLiteral}\PYG{p}{(}
        \PYG{n}{entries}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n}{StringLiteral}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{value}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Alice}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}
    \PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Relationship Pattern}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{ast\PYGZus{}models}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{RelationshipPattern}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} \PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS \PYGZob{}since: 2020\PYGZcb{}]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}}
\PYG{n}{rel} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{RelationshipPattern}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{variable}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{Variable}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{r}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{types}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{KNOWS}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{properties}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{MapLiteral}\PYG{p}{(}
        \PYG{n}{entries}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{since}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n}{IntegerLiteral}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{value}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2020}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}
    \PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{direction}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{OUTGOING}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Literals}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/ast_nodes:literals}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
All literal types:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{ast\PYGZus{}models}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{IntegerLiteral}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{FloatLiteral}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{StringLiteral}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{BooleanLiteral}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{NullLiteral}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{ListLiteral}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{MapLiteral}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Integer}
\PYG{n}{int\PYGZus{}lit} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{IntegerLiteral}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{value}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{42}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} String}
\PYG{n}{str\PYGZus{}lit} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{StringLiteral}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{value}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Hello}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Boolean}
\PYG{n}{bool\PYGZus{}lit} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{BooleanLiteral}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{value}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k+kc}{True}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Null}
\PYG{n}{null\PYGZus{}lit} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{NullLiteral}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} List}
\PYG{n}{list\PYGZus{}lit} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ListLiteral}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{elements}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}
        \PYG{n}{IntegerLiteral}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{value}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{n}{IntegerLiteral}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{value}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{p}{]}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Map}
\PYG{n}{map\PYGZus{}lit} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{MapLiteral}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{entries}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{key1}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n}{StringLiteral}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{value}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{value1}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{key2}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n}{IntegerLiteral}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{value}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{123}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Variables}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/ast_nodes:variables}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
All variable references use the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Variable}} class:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{ast\PYGZus{}models}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{Variable}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Create a variable}
\PYG{n}{var} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Variable}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Variables are used throughout the AST}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} \PYGZhy{} In patterns}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} \PYGZhy{} In expressions}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} \PYGZhy{} In comprehensions}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} \PYGZhy{} In return items}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Validation}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/ast_nodes:validation}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
All AST nodes support validation:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{grammar\PYGZus{}parser}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{GrammarParser}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{ast\PYGZus{}models}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{ASTConverter}

\PYG{n}{parser} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{GrammarParser}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{converter} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ASTConverter}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{n}{query} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (n:Person) WHERE n.age \PYGZgt{} 30 RETURN n.name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{n}{raw\PYGZus{}ast} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{parser}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{parse\PYGZus{}to\PYGZus{}ast}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{query}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{typed\PYGZus{}ast} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{converter}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{convert}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{raw\PYGZus{}ast}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Validate the AST}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{typed\PYGZus{}ast}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{validate}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{k}{if} \PYG{n}{result}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{is\PYGZus{}valid}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Query is valid!}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{else}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{k}{for} \PYG{n}{issue} \PYG{o+ow}{in} \PYG{n}{result}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{issues}\PYG{p}{:}
        \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Validation issue: }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{issue}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Tree Traversal}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/ast_nodes:tree-traversal}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
AST nodes inherit from \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{TreeMixin}} providing tree traversal methods:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Find all variables in the AST}
\PYG{n}{variables} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{typed\PYGZus{}ast}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{find\PYGZus{}all}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{Variable}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Find all node patterns}
\PYG{n}{node\PYGZus{}patterns} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{typed\PYGZus{}ast}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{find\PYGZus{}all}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{NodePattern}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Get all children of a node}
\PYG{n}{children} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{node}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{children}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Pydantic Integration}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/ast_nodes:pydantic-integration}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
All AST nodes are Pydantic models:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Serialize to dict}
\PYG{n}{node\PYGZus{}dict} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{node}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{model\PYGZus{}dump}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Serialize to JSON}
\PYG{n}{node\PYGZus{}json} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{node}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{model\PYGZus{}dump\PYGZus{}json}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Load from dict}
\PYG{n}{node} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{NodePattern}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{o}{*}\PYG{o}{*}\PYG{n}{node\PYGZus{}dict}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Validation is automatic}
\PYG{k}{try}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n}{invalid\PYGZus{}node} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{NodePattern}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{variable}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{not a variable}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{except} \PYG{n}{ValidationError} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n}{e}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Validation error: }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{e}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{For More Information}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/ast_nodes:for-more-information}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
See {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{PyCypher API}}}} for complete API reference

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
See {\hyperref[\detokenize{tutorials/ast_manipulation::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{AST Manipulation}}}} for examples

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
See {\hyperref[\detokenize{user_guide/variables::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Variables in PyCypher}}}} for details on variable handling

\end{itemize}

\sphinxstepscope


\section{Query Processing}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/query_processing:query-processing}}\label{\detokenize{user_guide/query_processing::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Understanding query parsing, validation, and execution in PyCypher.


\subsection{Overview}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/query_processing:overview}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
PyCypher processes Cypher queries through a multi\sphinxhyphen{}stage pipeline:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Parsing}: Convert Cypher text to a raw AST using the Lark grammar

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Conversion}: Transform the raw AST to typed Pydantic models via \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ASTConverter}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Validation}: Check query semantics and structure (via \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SemanticValidator}})

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Execution}: Translate and execute against a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}} IR

\end{enumerate}


\subsection{Parsing Pipeline}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/query_processing:parsing-pipeline}}

\subsubsection{Raw Parsing}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/query_processing:raw-parsing}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{GrammarParser}} uses Lark to parse Cypher queries:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{grammar\PYGZus{}parser}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{GrammarParser}

\PYG{n}{parser} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{GrammarParser}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{n}{query} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (person:Person)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(friend)}
\PYG{l+s+s2}{WHERE person.age \PYGZgt{} 30}
\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN person.name, friend.name}
\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Parse to raw AST (nested dicts and lists)}
\PYG{n}{raw\PYGZus{}ast} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{parser}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{parse\PYGZus{}to\PYGZus{}ast}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{query}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n+nb}{type}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{raw\PYGZus{}ast}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} dict}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The raw AST is a nested structure of dictionaries matching the Lark grammar.


\subsubsection{AST Conversion}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/query_processing:ast-conversion}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ASTConverter}} transforms raw AST to typed nodes:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{ast\PYGZus{}models}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{ASTConverter}

\PYG{n}{converter} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ASTConverter}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{typed\PYGZus{}ast} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{converter}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{convert}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{raw\PYGZus{}ast}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Now we have strongly\PYGZhy{}typed Pydantic models}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n+nb}{type}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{typed\PYGZus{}ast}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Query}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n+nb}{type}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{typed\PYGZus{}ast}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{clauses}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{0}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Match}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Benefits of typed AST:}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Type safety with mypy/ty

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Automatic validation

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
IDE autocomplete

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Serialization support

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Tree traversal methods

\end{itemize}


\subsection{Validation}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/query_processing:validation}}

\subsubsection{Semantic Validation}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/query_processing:semantic-validation}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SemanticValidator}} checks query correctness:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{semantic\PYGZus{}validator}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{SemanticValidator}

\PYG{n}{validator} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{SemanticValidator}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{validator}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{validate}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{typed\PYGZus{}ast}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{k}{if} \PYG{n}{result}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{is\PYGZus{}valid}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Query is valid!}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{else}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{k}{for} \PYG{n}{issue} \PYG{o+ow}{in} \PYG{n}{result}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{issues}\PYG{p}{:}
        \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Issue: }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{issue}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{message}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
        \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{  Location: }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{issue}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{location}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
        \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{  Severity: }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{issue}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{severity}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{What gets validated:}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Variable scoping and references

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Type compatibility

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Required vs optional clauses

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Pattern structure

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Function signatures

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Aggregation rules

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
For the simplest use case, the top\sphinxhyphen{}level \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{validate\_query()}}
convenience function parses and validates in a single call:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{validate\PYGZus{}query}

\PYG{n}{errors} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{validate\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (n:Person) RETURN m}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{for} \PYG{n}{error} \PYG{o+ow}{in} \PYG{n}{errors}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{error}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
See the {\hyperref[\detokenize{tutorials/query_validation::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Query Validation}}}} tutorial for a detailed walkthrough.


\subsection{Query Execution}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/query_processing:query-execution}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Star}} class parses, translates, and executes a Cypher query against a
populated \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Context}} in a single call:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{relational\PYGZus{}models}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{Context}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{star}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{Star}

\PYG{n}{star} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Execute a complete query and get a pandas DataFrame}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) RETURN p.name AS name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{BindingFrame IR}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/query_processing:bindingframe-ir}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The execution engine uses a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}} as its internal representation.
A BindingFrame is a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.DataFrame}} whose \sphinxstylestrong{columns are Cypher variable names}
(e.g. \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"p"}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"r"}}) and whose values are entity IDs.  Attributes are never
stored in the frame — they are fetched on demand from the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Context}}.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{binding\PYGZus{}frame}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{BindingFrame}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{EntityScan}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} EntityScan produces a frame with one column per variable}
\PYG{n}{scan} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{EntityScan}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{entity\PYGZus{}type}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{var\PYGZus{}name}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{p}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{frame} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{scan}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{scan}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Fetch an attribute — ID\PYGZhy{}keyed lookup against the entity table}
\PYG{n}{names} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{frame}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}property}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{p}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
This design eliminates three sources of fragility in earlier approaches:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Opaque 32\sphinxhyphen{}hex HASH\_ID column names

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{EntityType\_\_propertyname}} column prefixes bleeding into operator logic

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{variable\_map}} metadata that had to be threaded through every operator

\end{enumerate}


\subsection{Expression Evaluation}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/query_processing:expression-evaluation}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingExpressionEvaluator}} computes expression values vectorially
against a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}}.  It returns a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.Series}} aligned with the frame:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{binding\PYGZus{}evaluator}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{BindingExpressionEvaluator}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{ast\PYGZus{}models}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{Arithmetic}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{IntegerLiteral}

\PYG{n}{evaluator} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{BindingExpressionEvaluator}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{frame}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{n}{expr} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Arithmetic}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{operator}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{+}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{left}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{IntegerLiteral}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{value}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{right}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{IntegerLiteral}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{value}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{3}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{result\PYGZus{}series} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{evaluator}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{evaluate}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{expr}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{result\PYGZus{}series}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{iloc}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{0}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{)}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} 5}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Supported expression types:}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Arithmetic: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{+}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\sphinxhyphen{}}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{*}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{/}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\%}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\textasciicircum{}}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Comparison: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{=}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{<>}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{<}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{<=}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{>}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{>=}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Logical: AND, OR, NOT, XOR (Kleene three\sphinxhyphen{}valued logic, fully vectorised)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Null checks: IS NULL, IS NOT NULL

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Label predicates}: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{n:Label}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{n:Label1:Label2}} — test whether a node
variable belongs to a specific entity type.  The compound form (colon\sphinxhyphen{}separated
labels) applies AND semantics: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{n:Person:Employee}} is true only if the node
has both labels simultaneously.  Fully composable with AND/OR/NOT:
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WHERE n:Person AND NOT n:Manager}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{String predicates}: STARTS WITH, ENDS WITH, CONTAINS, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{=\textasciitilde{}}} (regex), IN.
