When used with a non\sphinxhyphen{}string left\sphinxhyphen{}hand operand (integer, float, boolean),
PyCypher raises a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{TypeError}} identifying the operator, the actual type,
and a suggested remedy:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} p.age is integer — raises TypeError:}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} \PYGZdq{}Operator \PYGZsq{}STARTS WITH\PYGZsq{} requires a string left\PYGZhy{}hand operand,}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{}  but got \PYGZsq{}int64\PYGZsq{}. Use toString() to convert if needed.\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{n}{WHERE} \PYG{n}{p}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{age} \PYG{n}{STARTS} \PYG{n}{WITH} \PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{3}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Fix:}
\PYG{n}{WHERE} \PYG{n}{toString}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{p}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{age}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{n}{STARTS} \PYG{n}{WITH} \PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{3}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
All\sphinxhyphen{}null columns pass through without error (null IS NOT a non\sphinxhyphen{}string value).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
CASE expressions (both searched and simple, vectorised)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
List index access: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{list{[}i{]}}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
List slicing: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{list{[}start..end{]}}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{List comprehensions}: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[}x IN list WHERE cond | expr{]}}} — filter and/or
transform a list per row.  The WHERE and map expression are each evaluated
once over all (row, element) pairs (vectorised).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Pattern comprehensions}: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[}(p)\sphinxhyphen{}{[}:REL{]}\sphinxhyphen{}>(q) WHERE cond | expr{]}}} —
collect graph neighbours into a per\sphinxhyphen{}row list.  Evaluated via a single
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.merge}} across all anchor rows (vectorised).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Quantifier predicates}: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{any(x IN list WHERE cond)}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{all(x IN list WHERE cond)}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{none(x IN list WHERE cond)}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{single(x IN list WHERE cond)}} — return a Boolean per row, vectorised
via the same explode\sphinxhyphen{}evaluate\sphinxhyphen{}regroup pipeline as list comprehensions.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{EXISTS subqueries}: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{EXISTS \{ MATCH (p)\sphinxhyphen{}{[}:REL{]}\sphinxhyphen{}>(q) WHERE cond \}}} —
returns \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{true}} if the inner pattern matches at least one row.  The
subquery is executed once for the entire outer frame using a sentinel\sphinxhyphen{}column
batch technique (not once per row).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{REDUCE expressions}: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{reduce(acc = init, x IN list | step)}} — fold a
list into a scalar using an accumulator.  The step expression is evaluated
once per step position, batched across all active rows (rows whose list
has not yet been exhausted).  The accumulator dependency within a single
row remains sequential, but rows at the same step position are evaluated
together in one \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}}, reducing allocations from
O(rows × max\_items) to O(max\_items).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Scalar functions: via \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ScalarFunctionRegistry}} — 131 functions across
string (toUpper, toLower, trim, substring, startsWith, endsWith,
contains, byteSize, …), math (abs, ceil, floor, sqrt, log, log10, pow,
hypot, fmod, gcd, lcm, …), bitwise (bitAnd, bitOr, bitXor, bitNot,
bitShiftLeft, bitShiftRight), trigonometric (sin, cos, tan, asin, acos,
atan, atan2, sinh, cosh, tanh, …), angle conversion (degrees, radians),
constants (pi, e), list (head, last, tail, range, toList, …), type
predicates (isString, isInteger, isFloat, …), temporal (date, datetime,
localdatetime, duration, date.truncate, datetime.truncate,
localdatetime.truncate, …), hash \& encoding (md5, sha256,
encodeBase64, …), and utility (coalesce, toString, toInteger,
randomUUID, …).  Math, trig, bitwise, and string predicate functions
are fully vectorised using numpy or pandas \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{str}} operations — no
per\sphinxhyphen{}row Python overhead for \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{abs(n.score)}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{sin(n.angle)}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{bitAnd(n.flags, 0xFF)}}, or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{startsWith(n.name, 'A')}}.
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{round()}} supports an optional third argument to select any of the
seven Neo4j 5.x rounding modes: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{HALF\_UP}} (default, ties away from
zero), \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{HALF\_DOWN}} (ties toward zero), \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{HALF\_EVEN}} (banker’s
rounding), \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{CEILING}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{FLOOR}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{UP}} (always away from zero),
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DOWN}} (truncation toward zero).

\end{itemize}


\subsection{Cross\sphinxhyphen{}Product MATCH}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/query_processing:cross-product-match}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
When two \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MATCH}} patterns share no common variables, pycypher produces the
Cartesian product (SQL CROSS JOIN) of both scans:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} All 3×2=6 (person, product) pairs}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person), (pr:Product) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN p.name AS person, pr.title AS product}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Filter: who can afford what?}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person), (pr:Product) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{WHERE p.budget \PYGZgt{}= pr.price }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN p.name AS person, pr.title AS product}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Self cross\PYGZhy{}join: all ordered (A, B) person pairs where A ≠ B}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person), (q:Person) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{WHERE p.name \PYGZlt{}\PYGZgt{} q.name }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN p.name AS pname, q.name AS qname}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Aggregation}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/query_processing:aggregation}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Aggregation functions (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{count}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{sum}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{avg}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{min}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{max}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{collect}}) are evaluated by \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{evaluate\_aggregation()}}, which returns a
scalar suitable for use in a WITH or RETURN clause:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Full\PYGZhy{}table aggregation (no GROUP BY)}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) RETURN count(*) AS n}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Grouped aggregation}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) RETURN p.dept AS dept, count(p) AS n}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DISTINCT}} modifier is supported for all aggregation functions:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Count unique departments (not total people)}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) RETURN count(DISTINCT p.dept) AS dept\PYGZus{}count}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Collect unique scores only}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) RETURN collect(DISTINCT p.score) AS unique\PYGZus{}scores}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Sum of unique scores}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) RETURN sum(DISTINCT p.score) AS total}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Projection Modifiers: ORDER BY, LIMIT, SKIP, DISTINCT}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/query_processing:projection-modifiers-order-by-limit-skip-distinct}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
RETURN and WITH both support the standard Cypher projection modifiers.
They are applied after aggregation in the following order:
DISTINCT → ORDER BY → SKIP → LIMIT.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Sort by age descending, return the top 3}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) RETURN p.name, p.age ORDER BY p.age DESC LIMIT 3}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Remove duplicate departments}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) RETURN DISTINCT p.dept}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Paginate: skip the first 10 rows, return the next 5}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) RETURN p.name SKIP 10 LIMIT 5}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} WITH pipeline: sort and cap rows before the next stage}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{WITH p.name AS name, p.age AS age ORDER BY age ASC LIMIT 1 }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN name, age}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
ORDER BY expressions may reference any property of the matched nodes,
including properties that are \sphinxstyleemphasis{not} in the RETURN item list.  If an
ORDER BY expression cannot be evaluated against the projected columns it
is automatically evaluated against the pre\sphinxhyphen{}projection binding frame.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Null placement} — By default PyCypher matches Neo4j 5.x semantics: nulls
sort \sphinxstyleemphasis{last} for both ASC and DESC.  Use the optional \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{NULLS FIRST}} /
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{NULLS LAST}} suffix to override this per sort key:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Put rows with null scores first, then ascending non\PYGZhy{}nulls}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (n:Person) RETURN n.name, n.score }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ORDER BY n.score ASC NULLS FIRST}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Descending by score; explicit NULLS LAST (same as default)}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (n:Person) RETURN n.name, n.score }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ORDER BY n.score DESC NULLS LAST}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Multi\PYGZhy{}column sort with mixed null placement}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (n:Item) RETURN n.val, n.tag }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ORDER BY n.val ASC NULLS FIRST, n.tag ASC NULLS LAST}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Each sort key carries its own NULLS directive independently.  Without a
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{NULLS}} keyword the default is \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{NULLS LAST}} (Neo4j 5.x default).


\subsubsection{Auto\sphinxhyphen{}generated Column Names}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/query_processing:auto-generated-column-names}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
When a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN}} item has no explicit \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{AS}} alias, PyCypher infers a
display name from the expression (matching Neo4j’s behaviour):
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN p.name}} → column \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{name}} \sphinxstyleemphasis{(unqualified property name)}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN p}} → column \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{p}} \sphinxstyleemphasis{(variable name)}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN toUpper(p.name)}} → column \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{toUpper(name)}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN p.age + 1}} → column \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{age + 1}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN 42}} → column \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{42}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN true}} → column \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{true}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN p.age > 25}} → column \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{age > 25}} \sphinxstyleemphasis{(comparison)}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN p.name STARTS WITH 'A'}} → column \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{name STARTS WITH A}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN p.name IS NULL}} → column \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{name IS NULL}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN NOT p.active}} → column \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{NOT active}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN p.age > 18 AND p.active}} → column \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{age > 18 AND active}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN \sphinxhyphen{}p.age}} → column \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\sphinxhyphen{}age}} \sphinxstyleemphasis{(unary negation)}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN \$limit}} → column \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\$limit}} \sphinxstyleemphasis{(query parameter)}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN count(*)}} → column \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{count(*)}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN xs{[}0{]}}} → column \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{xs{[}0{]}}} \sphinxstyleemphasis{(index lookup)}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN xs{[}1..3{]}}} → column \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{xs{[}1..3{]}}} \sphinxstyleemphasis{(slicing)}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN {[}x IN xs | x * 2{]}}} → column \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[}x IN xs | x * 2{]}}} \sphinxstyleemphasis{(list comprehension)}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN CASE WHEN … END}} → column \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{case}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN reduce(s = 0, x IN ns | s + x)}} → column \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{reduce(s, ns)}}

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Two or more aliasless items always receive distinct column names:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) RETURN toUpper(p.name), p.age + 1}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Columns: [\PYGZsq{}toUpper(name)\PYGZsq{}, \PYGZsq{}age + 1\PYGZsq{}]}

\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) RETURN p.age \PYGZgt{} 30, p.name IS NOT NULL}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Columns: [\PYGZsq{}age \PYGZgt{} 30\PYGZsq{}, \PYGZsq{}name IS NOT NULL\PYGZsq{}]}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
An explicit \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{AS}} alias always overrides the auto\sphinxhyphen{}generated name.


\subsection{OPTIONAL MATCH — Left\sphinxhyphen{}Join Semantics}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/query_processing:optional-match-left-join-semantics}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{OPTIONAL MATCH}} is the Cypher analogue of a SQL LEFT JOIN.  Rows from the
preceding \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MATCH}} are always preserved; when the optional pattern finds no
match, the unbound variables take \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{null}} values.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Everyone returned; fname is null for those with no KNOWS edge}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH (p:Person)}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    OPTIONAL MATCH (p)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(f:Person)}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    WITH p.name AS pname, f.name AS fname}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    RETURN pname, fname}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    \PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Filter to those who have no outgoing KNOWS relationship}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH (p:Person)}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    OPTIONAL MATCH (p)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(f:Person)}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    WITH p.name AS pname, f.name AS fname}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    WHERE fname IS NULL}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    RETURN pname}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    \PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Combine with coalesce for null\PYGZhy{}safe display}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH (p:Person)}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    OPTIONAL MATCH (p)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(f:Person)}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    WITH p.name AS pname, coalesce(f.name, \PYGZsq{}nobody\PYGZsq{}) AS friend}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    RETURN pname, friend}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    \PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
When \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{OPTIONAL MATCH}} appears as the first clause of a query (no preceding
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MATCH}}), it behaves like a regular \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MATCH}} except that an unknown entity
type produces 0 rows instead of raising an error.


\subsection{UNWIND — List Explosion}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/query_processing:unwind-list-explosion}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{UNWIND}} takes a list expression and produces one row per list element,
binding each element to an alias variable.  It is commonly used after
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{collect()}} to re\sphinxhyphen{}expand aggregated lists:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Collect names then unwind into individual rows}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH (p:Person)}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    WITH collect(p.name) AS names}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    UNWIND names AS name}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    RETURN name}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    \PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Explode a list property (one row per tag per person)}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH (p:Person)}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    WITH p.name AS name, p.tags AS tags}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    UNWIND tags AS tag}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    RETURN name, tag}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    \PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Filter after UNWIND using WITH ... WHERE}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH (p:Person)}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    WITH p.name AS name, p.tags AS tags}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    UNWIND tags AS tag}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    WITH name, tag WHERE tag = \PYGZsq{}python\PYGZsq{}}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    RETURN name, tag}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    \PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{WITH * — Pass\sphinxhyphen{}Through Projection}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/query_processing:with-pass-through-projection}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WITH *}} passes all variables from the preceding \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MATCH}} (or earlier pipeline
stage) into subsequent clauses unchanged.  No explicit alias list is needed:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Access any matched property after WITH *}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) WITH * RETURN p.name AS name, p.age AS age}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Filter using WITH * WHERE}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) WITH * WHERE p.age \PYGZgt{} 30 RETURN p.name AS name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Sort and cap rows without enumerating aliases}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) WITH * ORDER BY p.age ASC LIMIT 3 RETURN p.name AS name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Chain: use WITH * to pass variables, then project explicitly in the next WITH}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) WITH * WITH p.name AS name, p.dept AS dept RETURN name, dept}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Variable\sphinxhyphen{}Length Paths}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/query_processing:variable-length-paths}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Relationship patterns support \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{*min..max}} hop\sphinxhyphen{}depth syntax for reachability
queries.  The BFS traversal honours relationship direction:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} All paths of exactly 1–3 KNOWS hops}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (a:Person)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS*1..3]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(b:Person) RETURN a.name, b.name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Unbounded — any positive number of hops}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (a:Person)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS*]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(b:Person) RETURN a.name, b.name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Exactly N hops}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (a:Person)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS*2..2]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(b:Person) RETURN a.name, b.name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{length()}} function returns the number of relationships (hops) in a
named path variable:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH p = (a:Person)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS*1..4]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(b:Person) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN a.name, b.name, length(p) AS hops}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{shortestPath and allShortestPaths}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/query_processing:shortestpath-and-allshortestpaths}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{shortestPath(pattern)}} returns one row per (start, end) pair at the
minimum hop count.  \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{allShortestPaths(pattern)}} returns every path that
ties for the minimum — there may be more than one:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} One row per (start, end) pair: minimum hops}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH (a:Person \PYGZob{}name: \PYGZsq{}Alice\PYGZsq{}\PYGZcb{}), (b:Person \PYGZob{}name: \PYGZsq{}Dave\PYGZsq{}\PYGZcb{})}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH p = shortestPath((a)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS*]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(b))}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    RETURN a.name AS start, b.name AS end, length(p) AS hops}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    \PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} All minimum\PYGZhy{}hop paths between the same pair}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH (a:Person \PYGZob{}name: \PYGZsq{}Alice\PYGZsq{}\PYGZcb{}), (b:Person \PYGZob{}name: \PYGZsq{}Dave\PYGZsq{}\PYGZcb{})}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH p = allShortestPaths((a)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS*]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(b))}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    RETURN a.name AS start, b.name AS end, length(p) AS hops}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    \PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Both functions return an empty result (not an error) when no path exists.


\subsection{SET Clause and Atomicity}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/query_processing:set-clause-and-atomicity}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The SET clause writes property updates back to entity and relationship tables
via \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame.mutate()}}.  All mutations within a query are buffered in a
shadow layer and only promoted to the canonical tables on successful
completion.  If the query raises an exception, all pending mutations are
discarded:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Successful SET — changes persist for subsequent queries}
\PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) SET p.score = 99 RETURN p.name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Failed SET — context remains unchanged}
\PYG{k}{try}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) SET p.name = }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Changed}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ RETURN nonexistent.x}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{except} \PYG{n+ne}{Exception}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{k}{pass}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Person.name is still the original value}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Set a relationship property}
\PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (a:Person)\PYGZhy{}[r:KNOWS]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(b:Person) SET r.since = 2020}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{REMOVE Clause}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/query_processing:remove-clause}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{REMOVE}} deletes a property from matching entities.  Property deletion is
also buffered and only committed on success:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Remove a property from all matching nodes}
\PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) WHERE p.temp IS NOT NULL REMOVE p.temp}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Remove from a relationship}
\PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (a:Person)\PYGZhy{}[r:KNOWS]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(b:Person) REMOVE r.since}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{CREATE and MERGE Clauses}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/query_processing:create-and-merge-clauses}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
CREATE inserts new nodes and relationships.  MERGE is an upsert: it first
tries to match the pattern; if no match is found, it creates the pattern.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Create a node}
\PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{CREATE (p:Person }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name: }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Eve}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{, age: 27\PYGZcb{})}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Create a relationship between matched nodes}
\PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH (a:Person \PYGZob{}name: \PYGZsq{}Alice\PYGZsq{}\PYGZcb{}), (b:Person \PYGZob{}name: \PYGZsq{}Eve\PYGZsq{}\PYGZcb{})}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    CREATE (a)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(b)}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    \PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} MERGE with ON CREATE / ON MATCH actions}
\PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MERGE (p:Person \PYGZob{}name: \PYGZsq{}Alice\PYGZsq{}\PYGZcb{})}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    ON CREATE SET p.created\PYGZus{}at = timestamp()}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    ON MATCH  SET p.updated\PYGZus{}at = timestamp()}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    \PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{MERGE error semantics} — when the entity type in a MERGE pattern is not
registered in the context, MERGE interprets the absence as “no match” and
creates the pattern.  This is the expected Cypher upsert behaviour for
bootstrapping new entity types.  Internally, the engine catches only
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.exceptions.GraphTypeNotFoundError}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{GraphTypeNotFoundError}}}}} during the match phase;
any other exception propagates normally so genuine bugs are never silently
swallowed.  \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{GraphTypeNotFoundError}} is exported from the top\sphinxhyphen{}level
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher}} package and can be caught directly:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{GraphTypeNotFoundError}

\PYG{k}{try}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n}{scan} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{EntityScan}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{entity\PYGZus{}type}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Ghost}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{var\PYGZus{}name}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{g}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{n}{scan}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{scan}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{except} \PYG{n}{GraphTypeNotFoundError} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n}{e}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Entity type }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{e}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{type\PYGZus{}name}\PYG{l+s+si}{!r\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ is not in the context}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{DELETE Clause}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/query_processing:delete-clause}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
DELETE removes matched nodes from the entity table:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Delete a specific node}
\PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name: }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Eve}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZcb{}) DELETE p}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{FOREACH Clause}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/query_processing:foreach-clause}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{FOREACH}} applies a write clause to every element of a list.  The loop
variable is scoped to the FOREACH body; variables in the outer query are
visible but cannot be assigned:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Activate every person in a collected list}
\PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH (p:Person)}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    WITH collect(p) AS people}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    FOREACH (person IN people | SET person.active = true)}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    \PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Create nodes from a literal list}
\PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    FOREACH (name IN [\PYGZsq{}Alice\PYGZsq{}, \PYGZsq{}Bob\PYGZsq{}, \PYGZsq{}Carol\PYGZsq{}] |}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{        MERGE (p:Person \PYGZob{}name: name\PYGZcb{})}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    )}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    \PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{UNION — Combining Results}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/query_processing:union-combining-results}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{UNION}} merges two or more sub\sphinxhyphen{}query result sets and deduplicates the
combined rows.  \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{UNION ALL}} skips deduplication:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Deduplicated union}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH (p:Person) WHERE p.dept = \PYGZsq{}Eng\PYGZsq{} RETURN p.name AS name}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    UNION}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH (p:Person) WHERE p.age \PYGZlt{} 30  RETURN p.name AS name}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    \PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Keep all rows}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH (p:Person) RETURN p.name AS name}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    UNION ALL}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH (p:Person) WHERE p.active = true RETURN p.name AS name}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    \PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
All sub\sphinxhyphen{}queries in a UNION must project the same column names.


\subsubsection{End\sphinxhyphen{}to\sphinxhyphen{}End Processing}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/query_processing:end-to-end-processing}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Complete query processing example:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{relational\PYGZus{}models}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{Context}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{star}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{Star}

\PYG{n}{context} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Context}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} populate with EntityTable / RelationshipTable objects}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Parse, translate, and execute in one call}
\PYG{n}{star} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) WHERE p.age \PYGZgt{} 30 RETURN p.name AS name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} result is a pandas DataFrame with a \PYGZsq{}name\PYGZsq{} column}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Error Handling}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/query_processing:error-handling}}

\subsubsection{Handling Parse Errors}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/query_processing:handling-parse-errors}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Lark parse errors are raised as \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{lark.exceptions.UnexpectedInput}}
subclasses (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{UnexpectedToken}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{UnexpectedCharacters}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{UnexpectedEOF}}).

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{lark}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{exceptions}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{UnexpectedInput}

\PYG{k}{try}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n}{raw\PYGZus{}ast} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{parser}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{parse\PYGZus{}to\PYGZus{}ast}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{invalid\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{except} \PYG{n}{UnexpectedInput} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n}{e}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Parse error: }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{e}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
To check syntax without raising, use {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.grammar_parser.GrammarParser.validate}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{validate()}}}}}:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{parser} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{GrammarParser}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Returns True for valid Cypher, False for genuine syntax errors.}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} NOTE: internal transformer bugs (VisitError) are NOT caught and will}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} propagate — this is intentional so bugs surface rather than being masked.}
\PYG{k}{if} \PYG{n}{parser}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{validate}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (n:Person) RETURN n.name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Valid query}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{else}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Syntax error in query}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Performance Considerations}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/query_processing:performance-considerations}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Parse caching (automatic)}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The Earley\sphinxhyphen{}based grammar parser is compiled once per process and cached as a
singleton.  AST parse results are additionally cached by query string using an
LRU cache (capacity 512 entries).  Identical query strings hit an O(1) cache
lookup on all calls after the first — no Cypher parsing overhead for repeated
executions of the same query.

\sphinxAtStartPar
For an ETL pipeline executing 5 queries across 1 000 data batches this means
roughly 5 cold parses (\textasciitilde{}56 ms each) rather than 5 000 (\textasciitilde{}280 s total).  No
manual caching is required.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Vectorised execution}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
BindingFrame operations execute against pandas DataFrames using vectorised
pandas operations.  The following paths are fully vectorised (no per\sphinxhyphen{}row
Python loops):
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Boolean logic (AND, OR, NOT, XOR) — Kleene three\sphinxhyphen{}valued numpy operations.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Property lookups — two\sphinxhyphen{}level ID\sphinxhyphen{}keyed index cache:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Arrow→pandas conversion cache} — for \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ContextBuilder}}\sphinxhyphen{}loaded data
(Arrow\sphinxhyphen{}backed tables), the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pyarrow.Table``→``pd.DataFrame}} conversion
is performed once per entity type and cached in
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Context.\_property\_lookup\_cache}}.  Subsequent property lookups on the
same entity type skip the Arrow conversion entirely.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{set\_index cache} — the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{raw\_df.set\_index(ID\_COLUMN)}} indexed
DataFrame is reused across all property accesses in the same query.
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{EntityScan.scan()}} pre\sphinxhyphen{}warms the cache so all subsequent lookups in
the same query are guaranteed hits.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Cross\sphinxhyphen{}query persistence} — the cache is retained across read\sphinxhyphen{}only
queries; it is only cleared when a mutation (SET/CREATE/DELETE) is
committed.

\end{enumerate}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Combined effect: 8.4× speedup on repeated read\sphinxhyphen{}only queries vs. the
uncached baseline (0.805s → 0.096s for 20 warm queries on a 2 000\sphinxhyphen{}row
Arrow\sphinxhyphen{}backed context).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
List comprehensions — all (row, element) pairs exploded into one flat
DataFrame; WHERE and map expressions evaluated once each.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Pattern comprehensions — single \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pd.merge}} across all anchor rows;
WHERE and map evaluated once over all pairs.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Quantifier predicates (any/all/none/single) — same explode pipeline as
list comprehensions.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
EXISTS subqueries — sentinel\sphinxhyphen{}column batch: the inner query executes once
with all outer rows rather than once per row.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Grouped aggregations (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{count}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{sum}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{avg}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{min}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{max}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{stdev}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{stdevp}}) — pandas native Cython aggregation.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Scalar math functions (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{abs}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ceil}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{floor}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{sign}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{sqrt}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{cbrt}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{log}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{log2}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{log10}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{exp}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pow}}) — numpy C\sphinxhyphen{}level
array operations via \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{np.abs}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{np.log}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{np.power}}, etc.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Trigonometric functions (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{sin}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{cos}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{tan}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{asin}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{acos}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{atan}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{atan2}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{sinh}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{cosh}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{tanh}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{degrees}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{radians}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{cot}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{haversin}}) — numpy C\sphinxhyphen{}level.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
String predicate functions (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{startsWith}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{endsWith}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{contains}}) —
pandas \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{.str}} accessor (Cython\sphinxhyphen{}level); pattern is always treated as a
literal string, not a regex.

\end{itemize}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{reduce(acc = init, x IN list | step)}} uses a \sphinxstylestrong{batch\sphinxhyphen{}per\sphinxhyphen{}step} approach:
all rows that are at the same step position are evaluated together in a
single \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BindingFrame}}.  Accumulator state within a row is sequential
(step \sphinxstyleemphasis{i} uses the value from step \sphinxstyleemphasis{i−1}), but the step expression itself
is only called O(max\_items) times regardless of row count — 50 calls for
200 rows × 50\sphinxhyphen{}element lists instead of 10 000.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Scaling}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Performance scales linearly with the number of matching rows in a MATCH
clause.  For very large tables (>1 M rows), push selective filters as early
as possible using WHERE clauses immediately after MATCH.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Variable\sphinxhyphen{}length paths (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[}:REL*{]}}}) perform a BFS over the relationship
table; execution time is proportional to the number of reachable edges.
Bound hop limits (e.g. \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{*1..3}}) significantly reduce the search space
compared to unbounded \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{*}}.

\end{itemize}


\subsection{For More Information}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/query_processing:for-more-information}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
See {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{PyCypher API}}}} for complete API reference

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
See {\hyperref[\detokenize{tutorials/query_validation::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Query Validation}}}} for validation examples

\end{itemize}

\sphinxstepscope


\section{Error Handling Patterns}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/error_handling:error-handling-patterns}}\label{\detokenize{user_guide/error_handling::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
PyCypher uses a structured exception hierarchy that lets you catch errors
at the granularity you need.  All exceptions are importable from the
top\sphinxhyphen{}level \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher}} package.


\subsection{Exception Hierarchy}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/error_handling:exception-hierarchy}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
SyntaxError
└── CypherSyntaxError          — invalid Cypher syntax

ValueError
├── ASTConversionError          — grammar parsed but AST build failed
│   └── GrammarTransformerSyncError  — grammar/AST model mismatch
├── GraphTypeNotFoundError      — unknown entity label or relationship type
├── VariableNotFoundError       — variable not in scope
├── VariableTypeMismatchError   — variable exists but wrong type
├── UnsupportedFunctionError    — unknown function name
├── FunctionArgumentError       — wrong argument count
├── MissingParameterError       — query parameter not provided
└── InvalidCastError            — failed type cast (e.g. toInteger(\PYGZdq{}abc\PYGZdq{}))

TypeError
├── WrongCypherTypeError        — unexpected expression type
└── IncompatibleOperatorError   — operator/type mismatch
    └── TemporalArithmeticError — date/time arithmetic error

TimeoutError
└── QueryTimeoutError           — query exceeded wall\PYGZhy{}clock budget

MemoryError
└── QueryMemoryBudgetError      — estimated memory exceeds budget

RuntimeError
└── RateLimitError              — query rate limit exceeded
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Every custom exception inherits from a Python built\sphinxhyphen{}in, so existing
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{except ValueError}} or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{except TypeError}} handlers continue to work
without modification.


\subsection{Catching Parse Errors}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/error_handling:catching-parse-errors}}\label{\detokenize{user_guide/error_handling:cyphersyntaxerror}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.exceptions.CypherSyntaxError}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{CypherSyntaxError}}}}} for user\sphinxhyphen{}provided
queries that might have typos:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{CypherSyntaxError}

\PYG{n}{star} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{try}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{user\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{except} \PYG{n}{CypherSyntaxError} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n}{e}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Syntax error at line }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{e}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{line}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{, column }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{e}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{column}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} e.query contains the original query string}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Catching Runtime Errors}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/error_handling:catching-runtime-errors}}\label{\detokenize{user_guide/error_handling:graphtypenotfounderror}}\label{\detokenize{user_guide/error_handling:unsupportedfunctionerror}}\label{\detokenize{user_guide/error_handling:variablenotfounderror}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
For dynamic queries, catch the specific exception types:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{VariableNotFoundError}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{UnsupportedFunctionError}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{GraphTypeNotFoundError}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{k}{try}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{query}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{except} \PYG{n}{VariableNotFoundError} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n}{e}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} e.variable\PYGZus{}name, e.available\PYGZus{}variables, e.hint}
    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Unknown variable }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{e}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{variable\PYGZus{}name}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Available: }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{e}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{available\PYGZus{}variables}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{except} \PYG{n}{UnsupportedFunctionError} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n}{e}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} e.function\PYGZus{}name, e.supported\PYGZus{}functions, e.category}
    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{No such function }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{e}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{function\PYGZus{}name}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{except} \PYG{n}{GraphTypeNotFoundError} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n}{e}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} e.type\PYGZus{}name}
    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{No entity type }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{e}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{type\PYGZus{}name}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ registered}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Resource Limit Errors}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/error_handling:resource-limit-errors}}\label{\detokenize{user_guide/error_handling:querycomplexityerror}}\label{\detokenize{user_guide/error_handling:querymemorybudgeterror}}\label{\detokenize{user_guide/error_handling:querytimeouterror}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Protect against runaway queries in production:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{QueryTimeoutError}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{QueryMemoryBudgetError}

\PYG{k}{try}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
        \PYG{n}{query}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{n}{timeout\PYGZus{}seconds}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{10.0}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{n}{memory\PYGZus{}budget\PYGZus{}bytes}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{500} \PYG{o}{*} \PYG{l+m+mi}{1024} \PYG{o}{*} \PYG{l+m+mi}{1024}\PYG{p}{,}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} 500 MB}
    \PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{except} \PYG{n}{QueryTimeoutError} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n}{e}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Query timed out after }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{e}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{elapsed\PYGZus{}seconds}\PYG{l+s+si}{:}\PYG{l+s+s2}{.1f}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{s }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
          \PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{(budget: }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{e}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{timeout\PYGZus{}seconds}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{s)}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{except} \PYG{n}{QueryMemoryBudgetError} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n}{e}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Estimated }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{e}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{estimated\PYGZus{}bytes}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{o}{/}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+m+mf}{1e6}\PYG{l+s+si}{:}\PYG{l+s+s2}{.0f}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MB exceeds }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
          \PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{budget }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{e}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{budget\PYGZus{}bytes}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{o}{/}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+m+mf}{1e6}\PYG{l+s+si}{:}\PYG{l+s+s2}{.0f}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MB}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Rate Limit Errors}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/error_handling:rate-limit-errors}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
When rate limiting is enabled via \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_RATE\_LIMIT\_QPS}}, queries
that exceed the sustained rate raise
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.exceptions.RateLimitError}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RateLimitError}}}}}:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{RateLimitError}

\PYG{k}{try}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{query}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{except} \PYG{n}{RateLimitError}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Back off and retry, or return a 429 to the caller}
    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Rate limit exceeded — try again shortly}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Pre\sphinxhyphen{}execution Validation}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/error_handling:pre-execution-validation}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.semantic_validator.validate_query}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{validate\_query()}}}}} to catch errors
\sphinxstyleemphasis{before} executing the query:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{validate\PYGZus{}query}

\PYG{n}{errors} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{validate\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (n:Person) RETURN m.name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{for} \PYG{n}{error} \PYG{o+ow}{in} \PYG{n}{errors}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{error}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{severity}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{value}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{: }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{error}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{message}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} \PYGZdq{}error: Variable \PYGZsq{}m\PYGZsq{} is not defined...\PYGZdq{}}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Security and Pipeline Errors}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/error_handling:security-and-pipeline-errors}}\label{\detokenize{user_guide/error_handling:securityerror}}\label{\detokenize{user_guide/error_handling:cyclicdependencyerror}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.exceptions.SecurityError}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SecurityError}}}}} is raised when SQL injection,
path traversal, or SSRF attempts are detected in data source URIs or
DuckDB queries.

\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.exceptions.CyclicDependencyError}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{CyclicDependencyError}}}}} is raised when a
multi\sphinxhyphen{}query pipeline contains circular dependencies that prevent
topological ordering.


\subsection{Best Practices}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/error_handling:best-practices}}\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Catch specific exceptions first}, then broad ones:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k}{try}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{query}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{except} \PYG{n}{CypherSyntaxError}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} user typo}
\PYG{k}{except} \PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{VariableNotFoundError}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{GraphTypeNotFoundError}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} schema mismatch}
\PYG{k}{except} \PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{QueryTimeoutError}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{QueryMemoryBudgetError}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} resource limits}
\PYG{k}{except} \PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n+ne}{TypeError}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n+ne}{ValueError}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} catch\PYGZhy{}all for remaining pycypher errors}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Use structured attributes} rather than parsing error messages.
Every exception exposes the relevant data as named attributes.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Use pre\sphinxhyphen{}execution validation} for user\sphinxhyphen{}submitted queries to give
faster, friendlier feedback without the cost of execution.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Set timeouts in production} — either via \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{timeout\_seconds}} parameter
or the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_QUERY\_TIMEOUT\_S}} environment variable.

\end{enumerate}


\subsection{Troubleshooting Quick Reference}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/error_handling:troubleshooting-quick-reference}}\label{\detokenize{user_guide/error_handling:troubleshooting}}

\begin{savenotes}\sphinxattablestart
\sphinxthistablewithglobalstyle
\centering
\sphinxcapstartof{table}
\sphinxthecaptionisattop
\sphinxcaption{Common Errors and Fixes}\label{\detokenize{user_guide/error_handling:id1}}
\sphinxaftertopcaption
\begin{tabular}[t]{\X{30}{100}\X{30}{100}\X{40}{100}}
\sphinxtoprule
\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Error
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Likely Cause
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Fix
\\
\sphinxmidrule
\sphinxtableatstartofbodyhook
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{CypherSyntaxError}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Typo in Cypher keyword or missing clause
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Check the line/column pointer in the error. Look for the “Did you mean …?” suggestion. Ensure MATCH has a RETURN clause and all quotes/brackets are closed.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{GraphTypeNotFoundError}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Entity label or relationship type not loaded
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Check \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{e.available\_types}} for registered types. Verify your \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}entity}} / \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}rel}} flags match the labels in your query.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{VariableNotFoundError}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Variable used in RETURN/WHERE not bound in MATCH
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Check \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{e.available\_variables}} for what’s in scope. Ensure variables are defined in a MATCH or WITH clause before use. Look for the “Did you mean …?” hint.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{UnsupportedFunctionError}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Function name not recognised
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Check \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{e.supported\_functions}} for available functions. Function names are case\sphinxhyphen{}insensitive (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{count}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{COUNT}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Count}} all work).
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{FunctionArgumentError}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Wrong number of arguments to a function
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Check \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{e.expected\_args}} vs \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{e.actual\_args}}. Refer to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{e.argument\_description}} for the correct signature.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{IncompatibleOperatorError}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Operator applied to incompatible types (e.g. string + integer)
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
The error message includes a type\sphinxhyphen{}specific suggestion. Common fixes: use \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{coalesce()}} for NULLs, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{toString()}}/\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{toInteger()}} for type conversion.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{QueryTimeoutError}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Query exceeded wall\sphinxhyphen{}clock time budget
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Add \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{LIMIT}} to reduce result set. Add \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WHERE}} filters to reduce scan scope. Increase \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{timeout\_seconds}} if the query is genuinely large.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{QueryMemoryBudgetError}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Estimated memory exceeds configured budget
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Add \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{LIMIT}}/\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WHERE}} to reduce working set. Process in batches with \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SKIP}}/\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{LIMIT}}. Increase \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{memory\_budget\_bytes}} if resources allow.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{QueryComplexityError}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Query complexity score exceeds limit
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Check \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{e.breakdown}} for top contributors. Reduce MATCH clauses, shorten variable\sphinxhyphen{}length paths, add join conditions. Increase \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_MAX\_COMPLEXITY\_SCORE}} if needed.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{InvalidCastError}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Type cast failed (e.g. \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{toInteger("abc")}})
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Verify the source value is castable. Use \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{CASE WHEN}} to handle non\sphinxhyphen{}castable values gracefully.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MissingParameterError}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Parameterised query missing a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\$param}} value
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Pass the parameter: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{execute\_query(query, parameters=\{'param': value\})}}. The error message shows the exact usage.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{CyclicDependencyError}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Circular dependency in multi\sphinxhyphen{}query pipeline
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Check \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{e.remaining\_nodes}} for the cycle. Break the cycle by splitting a query into separate read/write steps.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SecurityError}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
SQL injection, path traversal, or SSRF attempt detected
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Review the data source URI or query for suspicious patterns. Use parameterised queries instead of string interpolation.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RateLimitError}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Query rate limit exceeded
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Back off and retry. Check \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{e.qps}} and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{e.burst}} for current limits. Adjust \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_RATE\_LIMIT\_QPS}} if needed.
\\
\sphinxbottomrule
\end{tabular}
\sphinxtableafterendhook\par
\sphinxattableend\end{savenotes}


\subsubsection{Debugging Commands}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/error_handling:debugging-commands}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
When troubleshooting, these patterns help identify the root cause:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Check available entity types and relationship types}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{entity\PYGZus{}types}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{relationship\PYGZus{}types}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Inspect DataFrame columns for property errors}
\PYG{k}{for} \PYG{n}{name}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{df} \PYG{o+ow}{in} \PYG{n}{context}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{entities}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{items}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{: }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n+nb}{list}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{df}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{columns}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Validate a query before executing it}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{validate\PYGZus{}query}
\PYG{n}{errors} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{validate\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{query\PYGZus{}string}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{for} \PYG{n}{e} \PYG{o+ow}{in} \PYG{n}{errors}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{e}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{severity}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{value}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{: }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{e}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{message}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Profile a slow query}
\PYG{n}{star} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{query}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{profile}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k+kc}{True}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{explain\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{query}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxstepscope


\section{Configuration Reference}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/configuration:configuration-reference}}\label{\detokenize{user_guide/configuration::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
PyCypher is configured entirely through environment variables and
constructor parameters.  All environment variables are read once at import
time from {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.config}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.config}}}}}.


\subsection{Query Execution}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/configuration:query-execution}}

\begin{savenotes}\sphinxattablestart
\sphinxthistablewithglobalstyle
\centering
\begin{tabular}[t]{\X{35}{100}\X{15}{100}\X{50}{100}}
\sphinxtoprule
\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Environment Variable
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Default
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Description
\\
\sphinxmidrule
\sphinxtableatstartofbodyhook
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_QUERY\_TIMEOUT\_S}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
None
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Wall\sphinxhyphen{}clock timeout (seconds) for a single query.  \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}} means no
limit.  Can also be set per\sphinxhyphen{}query via \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{execute\_query(timeout\_seconds=...)}}.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_MAX\_CROSS\_JOIN\_ROWS}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
10,000,000
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Hard ceiling on cross\sphinxhyphen{}join result size.  Prevents accidental Cartesian
explosions when MATCH patterns share no variables.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_MAX\_UNBOUNDED\_PATH\_HOPS}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
20
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Maximum BFS hops for unbounded variable\sphinxhyphen{}length paths (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[}*{]}}}).
Higher values risk exponential memory growth in dense graphs.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_MAX\_COMPLEXITY\_SCORE}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
None
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Hard ceiling on query complexity score.  Queries exceeding this are
rejected before execution with {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.exceptions.QueryComplexityError}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{QueryComplexityError}}}}}.
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}} (or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{0}}) disables the gate.  Typical production values: 50–200.
Can also be set per\sphinxhyphen{}query via \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{execute\_query(max\_complexity\_score=...)}}.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_COMPLEXITY\_WARN\_THRESHOLD}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
None
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Soft threshold for query complexity warnings.  Queries scoring above
this emit a warning but still execute.  \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}} (or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{0}}) disables
warnings.  Set lower than \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_MAX\_COMPLEXITY\_SCORE}} for early alerts.
\\
\sphinxbottomrule
\end{tabular}
\sphinxtableafterendhook\par
\sphinxattableend\end{savenotes}


\subsection{Caching}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/configuration:caching}}

\begin{savenotes}\sphinxattablestart
\sphinxthistablewithglobalstyle
\centering
\begin{tabular}[t]{\X{35}{100}\X{15}{100}\X{50}{100}}
\sphinxtoprule
\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Environment Variable
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Default
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Description
\\
\sphinxmidrule
\sphinxtableatstartofbodyhook
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_RESULT\_CACHE\_MAX\_MB}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
100
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Maximum memory (MB) for the LRU query result cache.  Set to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{0}}
to disable result caching.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_RESULT\_CACHE\_TTL\_S}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
0
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Time\sphinxhyphen{}to\sphinxhyphen{}live (seconds) for cached results.  \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{0}} means entries never
expire — only evicted by size pressure.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_AST\_CACHE\_MAX}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
1024
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Maximum parsed ASTs cached per \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{GrammarParser}} instance.  LRU
eviction when full.  \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{0}} disables caching.
\\
\sphinxbottomrule
\end{tabular}
\sphinxtableafterendhook\par
\sphinxattableend\end{savenotes}


\subsection{Logging and Metrics}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/configuration:logging-and-metrics}}

\begin{savenotes}\sphinxattablestart
\sphinxthistablewithglobalstyle
\centering
\begin{tabular}[t]{\X{35}{100}\X{15}{100}\X{50}{100}}
\sphinxtoprule
\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Environment Variable
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Default
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Description
\\
\sphinxmidrule
\sphinxtableatstartofbodyhook
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_LOG\_LEVEL}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
WARNING
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Log level: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DEBUG}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{INFO}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WARNING}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ERROR}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{CRITICAL}}.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_LOG\_FORMAT}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
rich
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Log format: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{rich}} (human\sphinxhyphen{}readable) or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{json}} (machine\sphinxhyphen{}readable,
suitable for ELK/Datadog/Splunk).
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_METRICS\_ENABLED}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
1
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Enable query metrics collection.  Set to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{0}} or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{false}} to disable.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_SLOW\_QUERY\_MS}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
1000
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Threshold (milliseconds) for slow\sphinxhyphen{}query warnings in the metrics
collector.
\\
\sphinxbottomrule
\end{tabular}
\sphinxtableafterendhook\par
\sphinxattableend\end{savenotes}


\subsection{Telemetry}

\begin{savenotes}\sphinxattablestart

nmetl

Application name tag in Pyroscope.
\\

\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYROSCOPE\_SAMPLE\_RATE}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
100

Samples per second.
\\

\sphinxtableafterendhook\par

\subsection{Per\sphinxhyphen{}Query Overrides}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/configuration:per-query-overrides}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Many configuration options can be overridden per\sphinxhyphen{}query via
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.star.Star.execute_query}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{execute\_query()}}}}} parameters:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (a)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS*]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(b) RETURN a, b}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{timeout\PYGZus{}seconds}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{5.0}\PYG{p}{,}            \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} overrides PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}QUERY\PYGZus{}TIMEOUT\PYGZus{}S}
    \PYG{n}{memory\PYGZus{}budget\PYGZus{}bytes}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{256} \PYG{o}{*} \PYG{l+m+mi}{1024} \PYG{o}{*} \PYG{l+m+mi}{1024}\PYG{p}{,}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} 256 MB hard limit}
    \PYG{n}{max\PYGZus{}complexity\PYGZus{}score}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{100}\PYG{p}{,}        \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} reject overly complex queries}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Production Configuration Example}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/configuration:production-configuration-example}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} .env for production deployment}
\PYG{n+nb}{export}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}QUERY\PYGZus{}TIMEOUT\PYGZus{}S}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m}{30}
\PYG{n+nb}{export}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}MAX\PYGZus{}CROSS\PYGZus{}JOIN\PYGZus{}ROWS}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m}{1000000}
\PYG{n+nb}{export}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}MAX\PYGZus{}UNBOUNDED\PYGZus{}PATH\PYGZus{}HOPS}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m}{10}
\PYG{n+nb}{export}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}RESULT\PYGZus{}CACHE\PYGZus{}MAX\PYGZus{}MB}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m}{500}
\PYG{n+nb}{export}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}RESULT\PYGZus{}CACHE\PYGZus{}TTL\PYGZus{}S}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m}{300}
\PYG{n+nb}{export}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}LOG\PYGZus{}LEVEL}\PYG{o}{=}INFO
\PYG{n+nb}{export}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}LOG\PYGZus{}FORMAT}\PYG{o}{=}json
\PYG{n+nb}{export}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}SLOW\PYGZus{}QUERY\PYGZus{}MS}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m}{500}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxstepscope
