\subsection{Environment Variables at a Glance}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/performance_tuning:environment-variables-at-a-glance}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
All runtime tuning knobs are read from environment variables at import time
and centralised in {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:module-pycypher.config}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.config}}}}}.


\subsection{Query Timeouts}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/performance_tuning:query-timeouts}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Prevent runaway queries from consuming resources indefinitely.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Per\sphinxhyphen{}query timeout} (programmatic):

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (a)\PYGZhy{}[*]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(b) RETURN a, b}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{timeout\PYGZus{}seconds}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{5.0}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Default timeout} (environment):

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n+nb}{export}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}QUERY\PYGZus{}TIMEOUT\PYGZus{}S}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m}{10}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
When a query exceeds its timeout, PyCypher raises
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.exceptions.QueryTimeoutError}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{QueryTimeoutError}}}}} with the elapsed time and
a query fragment for diagnostics:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{QueryTimeoutError}

\PYG{k}{try}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{slow\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{timeout\PYGZus{}seconds}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{2.0}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{except} \PYG{n}{QueryTimeoutError} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n}{e}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Timed out after }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{e}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{elapsed\PYGZus{}seconds}\PYG{l+s+si}{:}\PYG{l+s+s2}{.1f}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{s}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The timeout is enforced cooperatively between clauses \sphinxstylestrong{and} via
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SIGALRM}} on Unix (main thread only) so that queries stuck inside C
extensions (e.g. a large pandas merge) are still interrupted.


\subsection{Memory Budget}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/performance_tuning:memory-budget}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Cap the peak memory a single query is allowed to consume.  The query planner
estimates memory requirements before execution begins and rejects queries
that exceed the budget:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person), (q:Person) RETURN p.name, q.name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{memory\PYGZus{}budget\PYGZus{}bytes}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{500} \PYG{o}{*} \PYG{l+m+mi}{1024} \PYG{o}{*} \PYG{l+m+mi}{1024}\PYG{p}{,}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} 500 MB}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
If the planner’s estimate exceeds the budget, a
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.exceptions.QueryMemoryBudgetError}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{QueryMemoryBudgetError}}}}} is raised \sphinxstylestrong{before}
any execution begins, avoiding wasted work:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{QueryMemoryBudgetError}

\PYG{k}{try}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{big\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{memory\PYGZus{}budget\PYGZus{}bytes}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{100\PYGZus{}000\PYGZus{}000}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{except} \PYG{n}{QueryMemoryBudgetError} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n}{e}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Estimated }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{e}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{estimated\PYGZus{}bytes}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{o}{/}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+m+mf}{1e6}\PYG{l+s+si}{:}\PYG{l+s+s2}{.0f}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ MB exceeds budget}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Cross\sphinxhyphen{}Join Limits}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/performance_tuning:cross-join-limits}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Cartesian products (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MATCH (a:X), (b:Y)}}) can produce explosive row counts.
PyCypher enforces a configurable hard ceiling:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Reject cross\PYGZhy{}joins exceeding 1 million rows}
\PYG{n+nb}{export}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}MAX\PYGZus{}CROSS\PYGZus{}JOIN\PYGZus{}ROWS}\PYG{o}{=}1\PYGZus{}000\PYGZus{}000
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The default is 10 million rows.  When exceeded, the query fails immediately
with a clear error rather than consuming all available memory.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Best practice}: avoid unfiltered cross\sphinxhyphen{}joins.  Push WHERE predicates as
early as possible:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} BAD: cross\PYGZhy{}join first, filter later (produces N×M rows)}
\PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person), (c:Company) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{WHERE p.company\PYGZus{}id = c.id }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN p.name, c.name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} BETTER: use a relationship to join directly}
\PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person)\PYGZhy{}[:WORKS\PYGZus{}AT]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(c:Company) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN p.name, c.name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Result Caching}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/performance_tuning:result-caching}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
PyCypher caches query results in an LRU cache keyed by query string +
parameters.  Repeated identical read\sphinxhyphen{}only queries skip parsing and execution
entirely.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Configuration}:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Programmatic: set cache size and TTL per Star instance}
\PYG{n}{star} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{context}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{result\PYGZus{}cache\PYGZus{}max\PYGZus{}mb}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{200}\PYG{p}{,}          \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} 200 MB cache}
    \PYG{n}{result\PYGZus{}cache\PYGZus{}ttl\PYGZus{}seconds}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{300.0}\PYG{p}{,}   \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} 5\PYGZhy{}minute TTL}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Or via environment variables (affects all Star instances)}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} export PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}RESULT\PYGZus{}CACHE\PYGZus{}MAX\PYGZus{}MB=200}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} export PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}RESULT\PYGZus{}CACHE\PYGZus{}TTL\PYGZus{}S=300}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Disable caching} (useful for mutation\sphinxhyphen{}heavy workloads):

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{star} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{result\PYGZus{}cache\PYGZus{}max\PYGZus{}mb}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{0}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Smart invalidation}: the cache is automatically invalidated whenever a
mutation query (SET, CREATE, DELETE, MERGE, REMOVE, FOREACH) commits.
Mutation queries are never cached.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Monitoring cache effectiveness}:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}cache\PYGZus{}stats}

\PYG{n}{stats} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}cache\PYGZus{}stats}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{star}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Hit rate: }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{stats}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{result\PYGZus{}cache\PYGZus{}hit\PYGZus{}rate}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{l+s+si}{:}\PYG{l+s+s2}{.0\PYGZpc{}}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Entries:  }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{stats}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{result\PYGZus{}cache\PYGZus{}entries}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Size:     }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{stats}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{result\PYGZus{}cache\PYGZus{}size\PYGZus{}mb}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{l+s+si}{:}\PYG{l+s+s2}{.1f}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ MB}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Evictions: }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{stats}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{result\PYGZus{}cache\PYGZus{}evictions}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
A hit rate below 50\% on analytical workloads may indicate:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Queries with unique literal values that prevent cache matches

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Frequent mutations invalidating the cache

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Cache too small for the working set — increase \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{result\_cache\_max\_mb}}

\end{itemize}


\subsubsection{Parse Caching}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/performance_tuning:parse-caching}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Independent of the result cache, PyCypher maintains an LRU cache (capacity
512) for parsed AST trees.  Identical query strings hit an O(1) lookup on
all calls after the first.  No configuration is needed — this is automatic.

\sphinxAtStartPar
For an ETL pipeline executing 5 queries across 1 000 batches, this means
roughly 5 cold parses (\textasciitilde{}56 ms each) rather than 5 000.


\subsection{Graph\sphinxhyphen{}Native Indexes}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/performance_tuning:graph-native-indexes}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
PyCypher builds graph\sphinxhyphen{}native indexes lazily on first access, accelerating
pattern matching from O(E) full table scans to O(degree) neighbor lookups.
Indexes are managed through the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Context}} and invalidated automatically
when mutations commit.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Index types}:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{AdjacencyIndex}} — per\sphinxhyphen{}relationship\sphinxhyphen{}type adjacency lists for fast
neighbor lookups

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PropertyValueIndex}} — per\sphinxhyphen{}(entity\_type, property) hash index for
value\sphinxhyphen{}to\sphinxhyphen{}ID resolution in O(1)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{EntityLabelIndex}} — per\sphinxhyphen{}label sorted ID arrays for O(log n) membership
tests

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Usage} (indexes are automatic, but you can inspect them):

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{ctx} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ContextBuilder}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}entity}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{people}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{build}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{manager} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ctx}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{index\PYGZus{}manager}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Neighbor lookup — O(degree) instead of O(E)}
\PYG{n}{neighbors} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{manager}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}neighbors}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{KNOWS}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{source\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{42}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{direction}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{outgoing}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Property index — O(1) instead of O(N)}
\PYG{n}{ids} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{manager}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{lookup\PYGZus{}property}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Alice}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{When indexes help most}: queries with selective relationship traversals
or property equality filters.  Indexes are rebuilt automatically after
mutations, so there is no manual maintenance.


\subsection{LeapfrogTriejoin (Multi\sphinxhyphen{}Way Joins)}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/performance_tuning:leapfrogtriejoin-multi-way-joins}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
For queries joining 3+ patterns on a shared variable, PyCypher uses the
LeapfrogTriejoin algorithm (Veldhuizen 2014) which achieves worst\sphinxhyphen{}case
optimal time complexity O(N\textasciicircum{}\{w/2\}) — exponentially better than iterated
binary joins at O(N\textasciicircum{}\{w\sphinxhyphen{}1\}).

\sphinxAtStartPar
This is particularly effective for \sphinxstylestrong{cyclic query patterns} such as
triangle queries:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Triangle query — LeapfrogTriejoin automatically used}
\PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{l+s+s2}{    MATCH (a:Person)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(b:Person),}
\PYG{l+s+s2}{          (b)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(c:Person),}
\PYG{l+s+s2}{          (c)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(a)}
\PYG{l+s+s2}{    RETURN a.name, b.name, c.name}
\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The algorithm activates automatically when 3+ frames share a common join
variable.  For 2\sphinxhyphen{}frame joins, standard hash or sort\sphinxhyphen{}merge joins are used
instead.


\subsection{Cardinality Feedback Loops}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/performance_tuning:cardinality-feedback-loops}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The query planner maintains a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{CardinalityFeedbackStore}} that learns from
execution history to improve future query plans.  After each query, actual
vs. estimated cardinalities are recorded.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{How it works}:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Stores rolling window of (estimated, actual) cardinality pairs per entity
type (max 32 observations)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Computes geometric mean correction factors, clamped to {[}0.01, 100{]}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Applies corrections to future cardinality estimates for better join
ordering and algorithm selection

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
This system is automatic — no configuration needed.  Over time, query plans
improve as the planner accumulates execution statistics.


\subsection{Rate Limiting}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/performance_tuning:rate-limiting}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Protect shared deployments from query abuse with the built\sphinxhyphen{}in rate limiter.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{rate\PYGZus{}limiter}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{QueryRateLimiter}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}global\PYGZus{}limiter}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Global limiter (configured via environment variables)}
\PYG{n}{limiter} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}global\PYGZus{}limiter}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{limiter}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{acquire}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} raises RateLimitError if over limit}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Custom per\PYGZhy{}caller limiter}
\PYG{n}{limiter} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{QueryRateLimiter}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{qps}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{5.0}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{burst}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{20}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{limiter}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{acquire}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{caller\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{user\PYGZhy{}123}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
When the rate limit is exceeded, a {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.exceptions.RateLimitError}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RateLimitError}}}}}
is raised immediately (non\sphinxhyphen{}blocking fail\sphinxhyphen{}fast).


\subsection{Audit Logging}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/performance_tuning:audit-logging}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Enable structured query audit logging for compliance and debugging:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n+nb}{export}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}AUDIT\PYGZus{}LOG}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m}{1}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Each query execution emits a JSON record to the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.audit}} logger
containing: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{query\_id}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{timestamp}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{query}} (truncated), \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{status}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{elapsed\_ms}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{rows}}, and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{parameter\_keys}}.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Security}: parameter \sphinxstyleemphasis{values} and result data are never logged.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Programmatic activation}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{audit}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{enable\PYGZus{}audit\PYGZus{}log}
\PYG{n}{enable\PYGZus{}audit\PYGZus{}log}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
For production, configure the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.audit}} logger via
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/logging.config.html\#module-logging.config}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{logging.config}}} to route records to a file, syslog, or log
aggregator.


\subsection{Query Structure Best Practices}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/performance_tuning:query-structure-best-practices}}

\subsubsection{Push Filters Early}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/performance_tuning:push-filters-early}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The most impactful optimisation is to filter rows as early as possible.
WHERE clauses immediately after MATCH reduce the working set before
subsequent joins and aggregations:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} GOOD: filter before aggregation}
\PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) WHERE p.active = true }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RETURN p.dept AS dept, count(p) AS n}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} LESS EFFICIENT: aggregate all rows, then filter}
\PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{WITH p.dept AS dept, count(p) AS n }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{WHERE n \PYGZgt{} 5 RETURN dept, n}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Bound Variable\sphinxhyphen{}Length Paths}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/performance_tuning:bound-variable-length-paths}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Unbounded paths (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[}*{]}}}) are capped at 20 hops by default, but even this
can produce large intermediate results on dense graphs.  Always specify
explicit bounds when possible:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} GOOD: bounded path — predictable performance}
\PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (a:Person)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS*1..3]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(b:Person) RETURN a.name, b.name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} RISKY: unbounded path — BFS explores up to 20 hops}
\PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (a:Person)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS*]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(b:Person) RETURN a.name, b.name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Reduce the hop cap globally if your graph has long chains:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n+nb}{export}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}MAX\PYGZus{}UNBOUNDED\PYGZus{}PATH\PYGZus{}HOPS}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m}{5}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Use WITH to Pipeline Results}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/performance_tuning:use-with-to-pipeline-results}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use WITH to narrow the working set between query stages.  Only project
the columns you need for subsequent clauses:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    MATCH (p:Person)\PYGZhy{}[:WORKS\PYGZus{}AT]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(c:Company)}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    WITH c.name AS company, count(p) AS headcount}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    WHERE headcount \PYGZgt{} 10}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    RETURN company, headcount ORDER BY headcount DESC}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{    \PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Prefer Relationship Patterns over Cross\sphinxhyphen{}Joins}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/performance_tuning:prefer-relationship-patterns-over-cross-joins}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Relationship patterns (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\sphinxhyphen{}{[}:REL{]}\sphinxhyphen{}>}}) scan only related rows.  Cross\sphinxhyphen{}joins
(\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MATCH (a), (b)}}) produce the full Cartesian product.  The difference is
orders of magnitude on large tables.


\subsection{Monitoring and Diagnostics}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/performance_tuning:monitoring-and-diagnostics}}

\subsubsection{Query Metrics}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/performance_tuning:query-metrics}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
PyCypher collects execution metrics automatically:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{shared}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{metrics}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{QUERY\PYGZus{}METRICS}

\PYG{n}{stats} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{QUERY\PYGZus{}METRICS}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{snapshot}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Total queries:     }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{stats}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{total\PYGZus{}queries}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Timing p50:        }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{stats}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{timing\PYGZus{}p50\PYGZus{}ms}\PYG{l+s+si}{:}\PYG{l+s+s2}{.1f}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ ms}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Timing p99:        }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{stats}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{timing\PYGZus{}p99\PYGZus{}ms}\PYG{l+s+si}{:}\PYG{l+s+s2}{.1f}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ ms}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Slow queries:      }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{stats}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{slow\PYGZus{}queries}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Error rate:        }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{stats}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{error\PYGZus{}rate}\PYG{l+s+si}{:}\PYG{l+s+s2}{.1\PYGZpc{}}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Memory delta p50:  }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{stats}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{memory\PYGZus{}delta\PYGZus{}p50\PYGZus{}mb}\PYG{l+s+si}{:}\PYG{l+s+s2}{.1f}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ MB}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The slow\sphinxhyphen{}query threshold is configurable:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n+nb}{export}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}SLOW\PYGZus{}QUERY\PYGZus{}MS}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m}{500}\PYG{+w}{   }\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} 500ms threshold}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Query Execution Logging}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/performance_tuning:query-execution-logging}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
PyCypher logs every query execution at the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{INFO}} level with row count,
elapsed time, and memory delta.  Enable debug logging for parse timing and
clause\sphinxhyphen{}by\sphinxhyphen{}clause breakdown:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{import}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{logging}
\PYG{n}{logging}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{getLogger}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{pycypher}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{setLevel}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{logging}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DEBUG}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Common Bottlenecks}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/performance_tuning:common-bottlenecks}}

\subsection{Production Deployment Checklist}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/performance_tuning:production-deployment-checklist}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} 1. Set a default timeout to prevent runaway queries}
\PYG{n+nb}{export}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}QUERY\PYGZus{}TIMEOUT\PYGZus{}S}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m}{30}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} 2. Limit cross\PYGZhy{}join size for safety}
\PYG{n+nb}{export}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}MAX\PYGZus{}CROSS\PYGZus{}JOIN\PYGZus{}ROWS}\PYG{o}{=}1\PYGZus{}000\PYGZus{}000

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} 3. Size the result cache for your working set}
\PYG{n+nb}{export}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}RESULT\PYGZus{}CACHE\PYGZus{}MAX\PYGZus{}MB}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m}{200}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} 4. Set a TTL if data changes externally}
\PYG{n+nb}{export}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}RESULT\PYGZus{}CACHE\PYGZus{}TTL\PYGZus{}S}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m}{60}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} 5. Bound variable\PYGZhy{}length paths for dense graphs}
\PYG{n+nb}{export}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}MAX\PYGZus{}UNBOUNDED\PYGZus{}PATH\PYGZus{}HOPS}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m}{10}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} 6. Set slow\PYGZhy{}query threshold for alerting}
\PYG{n+nb}{export}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}SLOW\PYGZus{}QUERY\PYGZus{}MS}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m}{500}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} 7. Enable audit logging for compliance}
\PYG{n+nb}{export}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}AUDIT\PYGZus{}LOG}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m}{1}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} 8. Rate limit for multi\PYGZhy{}tenant deployments}
\PYG{n+nb}{export}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}RATE\PYGZus{}LIMIT\PYGZus{}QPS}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m}{50}
\PYG{n+nb}{export}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}RATE\PYGZus{}LIMIT\PYGZus{}BURST}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m}{100}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\item {}

\section{Constants and Magic Values Reference}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/constants_reference:constants-and-magic-values-reference}}\label{\detokenize{user_guide/constants_reference::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This document explains every configurable constant and internal magic value
in PyCypher, including the rationale for each default.

\begin{sphinxcontents}
\sphinxstylecontentstitle{On this page}
\begin{itemize}
\item {}

\subsection{Environment\sphinxhyphen{}Configurable Constants}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/constants_reference:environment-configurable-constants}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
These constants are read from environment variables at import time via
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.config}}.  All can be overridden without code changes.


\subsubsection{Query Execution Limits}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/constants_reference:query-execution-limits}}

\begin{savenotes}\sphinxattablestart
\sphinxthistablewithglobalstyle
\centering
\begin{tabular}[t]{\X{20}{100}\X{10}{100}\X{10}{100}\X{60}{100}}
\sphinxtoprule
\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Env Variable
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Default
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Module
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Rationale
\\
\sphinxmidrule
\sphinxtableatstartofbodyhook
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_QUERY\_TIMEOUT\_S}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
None
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{config}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Wall\sphinxhyphen{}clock budget for a single query (seconds).  None means no limit.
Uses \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{signal.SIGALRM}} on Unix.  Set this in production to prevent
runaway queries from holding resources indefinitely.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_MAX\_CROSS\_JOIN\_ROWS}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
1,000,000
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{config}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Hard ceiling on cross\sphinxhyphen{}join (Cartesian product) result rows.  Prevents
accidental memory exhaustion from \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MATCH (a), (b)}} patterns.
1M rows is \textasciitilde{}8 MB for ID\sphinxhyphen{}only frames; well within typical memory budgets.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_MAX\_UNBOUNDED\_PATH\_HOPS}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
20
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{config}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Maximum BFS depth for unbounded variable\sphinxhyphen{}length paths (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[}*{]}}}).
20 hops prevents runaway expansion on cyclic graphs while allowing
traversal of most practical graph structures.  Social networks
(diameter \textasciitilde{}6) and supply chains (depth \textasciitilde{}10) are well within this limit.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_MAX\_COMPLEXITY\_SCORE}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
None (0)
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{config}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Hard gate on query complexity score.  Queries exceeding this are
rejected before execution.  None disables the gate.  Typical
production values: 50\sphinxhyphen{}200.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_COMPLEXITY\_WARN\_THRESHOLD}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
None (0)
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{config}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Soft threshold for complexity warnings.  Queries scoring above this
emit a warning but still execute.  Set lower than
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MAX\_COMPLEXITY\_SCORE}} for early alerts.
\\
\sphinxbottomrule
\end{tabular}
\sphinxtableafterendhook\par
\sphinxattableend\end{savenotes}


\subsubsection{Security Limits}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/constants_reference:security-limits}}

\begin{savenotes}\sphinxattablestart
\sphinxthistablewithglobalstyle
\centering
\begin{tabular}[t]{\X{20}{100}\X{10}{100}\X{10}{100}\X{60}{100}}
\sphinxtoprule
\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Env Variable
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Default
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Module
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Rationale
\\
\sphinxmidrule
\sphinxtableatstartofbodyhook
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_MAX\_QUERY\_SIZE\_BYTES}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
1,048,576 (1 MiB)
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{config}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Rejects queries exceeding this size before parsing.  Prevents DoS
via oversized query strings.  1 MiB accommodates even very large
Cypher queries while protecting against adversarial payloads.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_MAX\_QUERY\_NESTING\_DEPTH}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
200
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{config}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Maximum AST traversal depth.  Prevents stack exhaustion on deeply
nested WHERE clauses or subqueries.  200 is generous for legitimate
queries (typical nesting: 5\sphinxhyphen{}20 levels).
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_MAX\_COLLECTION\_SIZE}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
1,000,000
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{config}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Ceiling on generated collection/string sizes from \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{range()}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{repeat()}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{lpad()}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{rpad()}}, and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{UNWIND}}.  Prevents
memory exhaustion from adversarial inputs like \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{range(0, 999999999)}}.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_RATE\_LIMIT\_QPS}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
0 (disabled)
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{config}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Maximum sustained queries per second.  When enabled, excess queries
receive a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RateLimitError}}.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_RATE\_LIMIT\_BURST}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
10
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{config}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Burst allowance for rate limiting.  Allows short bursts above the
sustained QPS rate.  Only meaningful when rate limiting is enabled.
\\
\sphinxbottomrule
\end{tabular}
\sphinxtableafterendhook\par
\sphinxattableend\end{savenotes}


\subsubsection{Caching}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/constants_reference:caching}}

\begin{savenotes}\sphinxattablestart
\sphinxthistablewithglobalstyle
\centering
\begin{tabular}[t]{\X{20}{100}\X{10}{100}\X{10}{100}\X{60}{100}}
\sphinxtoprule
\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Env Variable
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Default
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Module
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Rationale
\\
\sphinxmidrule
\sphinxtableatstartofbodyhook
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_RESULT\_CACHE\_MAX\_MB}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
100
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{config}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Maximum in\sphinxhyphen{}memory size for the query result cache.  100 MB balances
cache hit rates against memory pressure.  LRU eviction removes
least\sphinxhyphen{}recently\sphinxhyphen{}used entries when the limit is reached.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_RESULT\_CACHE\_TTL\_S}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
0 (no expiry)
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{config}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Time\sphinxhyphen{}to\sphinxhyphen{}live for cached results.  0 means entries are only evicted by
size pressure.  Set a TTL when the underlying data changes between
queries.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_AST\_CACHE\_MAX}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
1,024
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{config}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
LRU cache size for parsed ASTs.  1,024 entries accommodate typical
application workloads with repeated query patterns.  Set to 0 to
disable caching (useful during grammar development).
\\
\sphinxbottomrule
\end{tabular}
\sphinxtableafterendhook\par
\sphinxattableend\end{savenotes}


\subsection{Internal Constants (Not Configurable)}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/constants_reference:internal-constants-not-configurable}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
These constants are defined in source code and require code changes to
modify.


\subsubsection{Cardinality Estimator}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/constants_reference:cardinality-estimator}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Constants defined in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{cardinality\_estimator.py}} (extracted from
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{query\_planner.py}} in 2026\sphinxhyphen{}03, see {\hyperref[\detokenize{adr/adr-007-cardinality-estimator-extraction::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{ADR\sphinxhyphen{}007: Extract Cardinality Estimator from Query Planner}}}}).


\begin{savenotes}\sphinxattablestart
\sphinxthistablewithglobalstyle
\centering
\begin{tabular}[t]{\X{25}{100}\X{10}{100}\X{65}{100}}
\sphinxtoprule
\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Constant
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Value
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Rationale
\\
\sphinxmidrule
\sphinxtableatstartofbodyhook
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{STATS\_SAMPLE\_SIZE}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
10,000
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Maximum rows sampled when computing column statistics.  10K rows
provides statistically meaningful NDV and null\sphinxhyphen{}fraction estimates
while keeping computation fast on large tables.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{HISTOGRAM\_BINS}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
64
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Number of equi\sphinxhyphen{}width bins for histogram\sphinxhyphen{}based range selectivity.
64 bins balances granularity against memory overhead (\textasciitilde{}512 bytes
per histogram).
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{HISTOGRAM\_MIN\_ROWS}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
10
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Minimum non\sphinxhyphen{}null rows required to build a histogram.  Below this
count, histograms are unreliable and the estimator falls back to
uniform distribution assumptions.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DEFAULT\_FILTER\_SELECTIVITY}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
0.33
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Default fraction of rows passing a WHERE filter when no statistics
are available.  Same value as \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_DEFAULT\_FILTER\_SELECTIVITY}} in the
query planner (see below).
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{AVG\_BYTES\_PER\_CELL}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
64
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Conservative per\sphinxhyphen{}cell memory estimate for mixed\sphinxhyphen{}type columns when
the actual DataFrame is unavailable.  Used for memory budgeting in
query planning.
\\
\sphinxbottomrule
\end{tabular}
\sphinxtableafterendhook\par
\sphinxattableend\end{savenotes}


\subsubsection{Query Planner}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/constants_reference:query-planner}}

\begin{savenotes}\sphinxattablestart
\sphinxthistablewithglobalstyle
\centering
\begin{tabular}[t]{\X{25}{100}\X{10}{100}\X{65}{100}}
\sphinxtoprule
\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Constant
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Value
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Rationale
\\
\sphinxmidrule
\sphinxtableatstartofbodyhook
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_DEFAULT\_FILTER\_SELECTIVITY}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
0.33
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Default fraction of rows that pass a WHERE filter when no statistics
are available.  0.33 (one\sphinxhyphen{}third) is a standard database heuristic:
conservative enough to avoid over\sphinxhyphen{}optimistic plan choices, while not
so pessimistic that it prevents useful optimisations.  Used for
equality checks, comparisons, string predicates, and IS NULL tests.
Override by recording actual selectivity via the query learning system.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_BROADCAST\_THRESHOLD}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
10,000
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Row count below which the smaller side of a join is broadcast to
all partitions.  10K rows fit comfortably in L2 cache (\textasciitilde{}256 KB for
8\sphinxhyphen{}byte IDs), making broadcast join faster than hash partitioning
for small\sphinxhyphen{}vs\sphinxhyphen{}large joins.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_MERGE\_SORTED\_THRESHOLD}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
0.8
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Fraction of data that must be pre\sphinxhyphen{}sorted to trigger a merge\sphinxhyphen{}sort join
instead of hash join.  0.8 means at least 80\% sorted — merge join
only wins when data is mostly ordered.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_STREAMING\_AGG\_THRESHOLD}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
10,000,000
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Row count above which aggregation switches to streaming (chunked)
mode.  10M rows is approximately when a single\sphinxhyphen{}pass aggregation
starts competing with available memory.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_CROSS\_JOIN\_WARNING\_THRESHOLD}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
100,000
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Output row count above which the planner emits a warning for
cross\sphinxhyphen{}join operations.  Separate from the hard ceiling
(\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MAX\_CROSS\_JOIN\_ROWS}}) — this is an advisory threshold.
\\
\sphinxbottomrule
\end{tabular}
\sphinxtableafterendhook\par
\sphinxattableend\end{savenotes}


\subsubsection{Path Expansion (BFS)}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/constants_reference:path-expansion-bfs}}

\begin{savenotes}\sphinxattablestart
\sphinxthistablewithglobalstyle
\centering
\begin{tabular}[t]{\X{25}{100}\X{10}{100}\X{65}{100}}
\sphinxtoprule
\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Constant
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Value
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Rationale
\\
\sphinxmidrule
\sphinxtableatstartofbodyhook
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_MAX\_UNBOUNDED\_PATH\_HOPS}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
20
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Module\sphinxhyphen{}level default for the configurable env var.  See above.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_MAX\_FRONTIER\_ROWS}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
1,000,000
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Maximum BFS frontier size (rows) at any single hop level.
Prevents memory explosion during breadth\sphinxhyphen{}first path expansion.
1M rows is \textasciitilde{}8 MB of IDs, keeping BFS within reasonable memory bounds.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_MAX\_BFS\_TOTAL\_ROWS}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
5,000,000
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Maximum total accumulated result rows across all BFS hops.
5M rows (\textasciitilde{}40 MB of IDs) is the overall safety valve for path
expansion.
\\
\sphinxbottomrule
\end{tabular}
\sphinxtableafterendhook\par
\sphinxattableend\end{savenotes}


\subsubsection{Query Learning}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/constants_reference:query-learning}}

\begin{savenotes}\sphinxattablestart
\sphinxthistablewithglobalstyle
\centering
\begin{tabular}[t]{\X{25}{100}\X{10}{100}\X{65}{100}}
\sphinxtoprule
\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Constant
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Value
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Rationale
\\
\sphinxmidrule
\sphinxtableatstartofbodyhook
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_MAX\_SELECTIVITY\_HISTORY}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
64
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Rolling window size for selectivity observations per
(entity\_type, property, operator) triple.  64 provides enough
samples for stable exponential moving averages without unbounded
memory growth.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_MAX\_JOIN\_HISTORY}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
64
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Rolling window size for join performance observations per
(strategy, size\_bucket) pair.  Same rationale as selectivity history.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_MAX\_PLAN\_CACHE}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
256
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Maximum entries in the adaptive plan cache (keyed by query
fingerprint).  256 covers typical application workloads with
\textasciitilde{}50\sphinxhyphen{}100 distinct query shapes.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_CONFIDENCE\_THRESHOLD}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
0.5
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Minimum confidence score before learned selectivity overrides the
default heuristic.  0.5 ensures at least moderate statistical
evidence before changing plan decisions.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_MAX\_HISTORY}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
32
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Cardinality estimator rolling window per entity type.  32
observations balance recency with stability.
\\
\sphinxbottomrule
\end{tabular}
\sphinxtableafterendhook\par
\sphinxattableend\end{savenotes}


\subsubsection{Other Internal Limits}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/constants_reference:other-internal-limits}}

\begin{savenotes}\sphinxattablestart
\sphinxthistablewithglobalstyle
\centering
\begin{tabular}[t]{\X{25}{100}\X{10}{100}\X{15}{100}\X{50}{100}}
\sphinxtoprule
\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Constant
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Value
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Module
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Rationale
\\
\sphinxmidrule
\sphinxtableatstartofbodyhook
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_MAX\_REGEX\_PATTERN\_LENGTH}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
1,000
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{binding\_evaluator}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{string\_predicate\_evaluator}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Maximum regex pattern length for \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{=\textasciitilde{}}} operator.  Prevents ReDoS
(Regular Expression Denial of Service) attacks via adversarial
patterns.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_MAX\_QUERIES}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
1,000
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{multi\_query\_analyzer}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Maximum queries in a multi\sphinxhyphen{}query dependency analysis batch.  Bounds
the O(n\textasciicircum{}2) dependency inference cost.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_MAX\_NAME\_GENERATION\_ATTEMPTS}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
10,000
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{variable\_manager}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Safety limit for generating unique variable names.  10K attempts
ensures name generation succeeds even in heavily aliased queries.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_MAX\_CONTENT\_LENGTH}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
10 MiB
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{cypher\_lsp}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Maximum LSP JSON\sphinxhyphen{}RPC payload size.  Rejects oversized payloads
before parsing to prevent memory exhaustion.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_MAX\_DOCUMENTS}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
128
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{cypher\_lsp}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Maximum concurrent open documents in the LSP server.  Evicts
oldest documents beyond this limit.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_MAX\_QUERY\_LOG\_LEN}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
200
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{grammar\_parser}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Truncation limit for query strings in log messages.  Keeps logs
readable without losing diagnostic value.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_MAX\_HISTORY}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
1,000
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{repl}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Maximum readline history entries in the REPL.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_MAX\_QUERY\_LENGTH}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
2,048
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{audit}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Truncation limit for query strings in audit log records.  Longer
than log truncation to preserve audit trail fidelity.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{CROSS\_JOIN\_WARN\_THRESHOLDS}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
(100K, 1M)
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{config}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Progressive warning thresholds for cross\sphinxhyphen{}join cardinality.
Warnings at 100K and 1M rows give users early notice before
hitting the hard ceiling.
\\
\sphinxbottomrule
\end{tabular}
\sphinxtableafterendhook\par
\sphinxattableend\end{savenotes}

50

\\

\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_DEFAULT\_LOCK\_TIMEOUT}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
5.0s

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{star}} (ResultCache)
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Timeout for acquiring cache locks.  5 seconds is long enough for
normal contention, short enough to detect deadlocks.

\section{Overview}
\label{\detokenize{user_guide/index:overview}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The user guide provides in\sphinxhyphen{}depth reference material for PyCypher’s core
abstractions:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{user_guide/data_model::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Data Model Requirements}}}} — Why PyCypher needs ID columns, how to use your existing
column names, real\sphinxhyphen{}world data loading patterns, and troubleshooting
common data issues.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{user_guide/variables::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Variables in PyCypher}}}} — How the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Variable}} class represents named references
throughout the AST and execution engine.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{user_guide/ast_nodes::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{AST Nodes}}}} — Complete catalogue of all AST node types with field
descriptions and construction examples.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{user_guide/query_processing::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Query Processing}}}} — The full query lifecycle: parsing, AST conversion,
semantic validation, BindingFrame execution, expression evaluation,
aggregation, mutation, and performance characteristics.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{user_guide/error_handling::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Error Handling Patterns}}}} — Exception hierarchy, catching patterns, and
best practices for robust query error handling.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{user_guide/configuration::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Configuration Reference}}}} — Complete reference for all environment variables
and per\sphinxhyphen{}query configuration options.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{user_guide/performance_tuning::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Performance Tuning}}}} — Practical strategies for optimising query
execution: timeouts, memory budgets, cross\sphinxhyphen{}join limits, result caching,
parse caching, query structure best practices, and a production deployment
checklist.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{user_guide/constants_reference::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Constants and Magic Values Reference}}}} — Complete reference for all configurable constants
and internal magic values, with rationale for each default.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxstepscope


\chapter{Deployment \& Scaling}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/index:deployment-scaling}}\label{\detokenize{deployment/index::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This guide covers production deployment, container orchestration, scaling
strategies, monitoring, and operational best practices for PyCypher.

\sphinxstepscope


\section{Security Hardening}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/security:security-hardening}}\label{\detokenize{deployment/security::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This guide covers security hardening for production PyCypher deployments.
It is based on the internal security audit that identified and mitigated
vulnerabilities in the data ingestion pipeline, query engine, and external
integrations.

be deferred.

\subsection{Input Validation}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/security:input-validation}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
PyCypher applies defence\sphinxhyphen{}in\sphinxhyphen{}depth to every user\sphinxhyphen{}controlled input.
Understanding the validation layers helps operators diagnose rejected queries
and tune security policy.


\subsubsection{SQL Query Validation}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/security:sql-query-validation}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
User\sphinxhyphen{}supplied SQL queries (for data source ingestion) pass through four
validation phases:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Comment stripping} – removes \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\#}}, and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{/* */}} comments
to prevent comment\sphinxhyphen{}based injection.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Statement multiplicity} – rejects queries with more than one
semicolon (multi\sphinxhyphen{}statement injection).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Keyword allowlist} – only \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SELECT}} and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WITH}} are permitted as
the first keyword.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Table function blocklist} – dangerous DuckDB table functions are
rejected even inside valid \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SELECT}} queries.

\end{enumerate}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The following DuckDB functions are blocked:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
read\PYGZus{}csv, read\PYGZus{}csv\PYGZus{}auto, read\PYGZus{}parquet, read\PYGZus{}json, read\PYGZus{}json\PYGZus{}auto,
read\PYGZus{}json\PYGZus{}objects, read\PYGZus{}json\PYGZus{}objects\PYGZus{}auto, read\PYGZus{}blob, read\PYGZus{}text,
read\PYGZus{}ndjson, read\PYGZus{}ndjson\PYGZus{}auto, read\PYGZus{}ndjson\PYGZus{}objects, glob,
parquet\PYGZus{}scan, parquet\PYGZus{}metadata, parquet\PYGZus{}schema,
parquet\PYGZus{}file\PYGZus{}metadata, parquet\PYGZus{}kv\PYGZus{}metadata, sniff\PYGZus{}csv,
query\PYGZus{}table, query
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Functions with these prefixes are also blocked: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{duckdb\_}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pg\_}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pragma\_}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{information\_schema}}.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Why this matters:} Without the table function blocklist, a query such as
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SELECT * FROM read\_csv('/etc/passwd')}} would pass the keyword allowlist
(it starts with \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SELECT}}) and read arbitrary files from the filesystem.


\subsubsection{Path Validation}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/security:path-validation}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
File paths are validated against:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Path traversal} – \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{..}} components are rejected.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Sensitive prefixes} – \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{/etc/}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{/proc/}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{/sys/}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{/dev/}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{/root/}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{/var/run/}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{/var/lib/}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{/boot/}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{/sbin/}} are
blocked.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{SQL string literal safety} – single quotes, NUL bytes, URL\sphinxhyphen{}encoded
attacks (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\%27}}), and Unicode normalisation attacks are rejected when
paths are interpolated into DuckDB SQL.

\end{itemize}


\subsubsection{SSRF Protection}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/security:ssrf-protection}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
HTTP/HTTPS URIs are checked for Server\sphinxhyphen{}Side Request Forgery (SSRF):
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Blocked hostnames:} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{localhost}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ip6\sphinxhyphen{}localhost}}, etc.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Blocked IP ranges:} RFC 1918 private, loopback, link\sphinxhyphen{}local, reserved.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{DNS resolution:} hostnames are resolved and the resulting IP is
checked against the same blocklist.

\end{itemize}

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{warning}{Warning:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
DNS rebinding attacks (TOCTOU) are a known limitation.  The hostname is
resolved once during validation, but the downstream HTTP library may
resolve it again.  For maximum protection in untrusted environments,
use network\sphinxhyphen{}level egress controls (see {\hyperref[\detokenize{deployment/security:network-security}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{std}{\DUrole{std-ref}{Network Security}}}}} below).
\end{sphinxadmonition}


\subsubsection{Regex Safety}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/security:regex-safety}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The Cypher \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{=\textasciitilde{}}} regex operator validates patterns before execution:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Length limit} – patterns longer than 1,000 characters are rejected.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Syntax check} – invalid regex is rejected before reaching the engine.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{ReDoS detection} – patterns with nested quantifiers (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{(a+)+}}),
overlapping alternation (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{(a|a)*}}), or chained quantifiers
(\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{a\{n\}\{m\}}}) are rejected to prevent catastrophic backtracking.

\end{itemize}


\subsection{Environment Hardening}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/security:environment-hardening}}

\subsubsection{Secrets}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/security:secrets}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Generate strong random values for all credentials:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Generate secrets}
\PYG{n+nb}{export}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{NEO4J\PYGZus{}PASSWORD}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k}{\PYGZdl{}(}openssl\PYG{+w}{ }rand\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}hex\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+m}{32}\PYG{k}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{export}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{SPARK\PYGZus{}RPC\PYGZus{}SECRET}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k}{\PYGZdl{}(}openssl\PYG{+w}{ }rand\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}hex\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+m}{32}\PYG{k}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{export}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{NOMINATIM\PYGZus{}PASSWORD}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k}{\PYGZdl{}(}openssl\PYG{+w}{ }rand\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}hex\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+m}{32}\PYG{k}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Write to .env (never commit this file)}
cat\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZgt{}\PYG{+w}{ }.env\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s}{\PYGZlt{}\PYGZlt{} EOF}
\PYG{l+s}{NEO4J\PYGZus{}USER=neo4j}
\PYG{l+s}{NEO4J\PYGZus{}PASSWORD=\PYGZdl{}\PYGZob{}NEO4J\PYGZus{}PASSWORD\PYGZcb{}}
\PYG{l+s}{SPARK\PYGZus{}RPC\PYGZus{}SECRET=\PYGZdl{}\PYGZob{}SPARK\PYGZus{}RPC\PYGZus{}SECRET\PYGZcb{}}
\PYG{l+s}{NOMINATIM\PYGZus{}PASSWORD=\PYGZdl{}\PYGZob{}NOMINATIM\PYGZus{}PASSWORD\PYGZcb{}}
\PYG{l+s}{EOF}
chmod\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+m}{600}\PYG{+w}{ }.env
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Security\sphinxhyphen{}Sensitive Environment Variables}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/security:security-sensitive-environment-variables}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
These variables control security\sphinxhyphen{}relevant behaviour:


\begin{savenotes}\sphinxattablestart
\sphinxthistablewithglobalstyle
\centering
\begin{tabular}[t]{\X{30}{100}\X{15}{100}\X{55}{100}}
\sphinxtoprule
\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Variable
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Default
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Security Notes
\\
\sphinxmidrule
\sphinxtableatstartofbodyhook
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_AST\_CACHE\_MAX}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{1024}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Maximum cached AST entries.  Set lower in memory\sphinxhyphen{}constrained
environments.  Set higher for ETL pipelines with many unique queries.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_QUERY\_TIMEOUT\_MS}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
(none)
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Per\sphinxhyphen{}query timeout in milliseconds.  \sphinxstylestrong{Strongly recommended} for
production to prevent runaway queries from consuming resources.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_LOG\_LEVEL}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WARNING}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Set to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{INFO}} for audit trails.  Avoid \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DEBUG}} in production
(logs query text which may contain sensitive data).
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_LOG\_FORMAT}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
text
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Set to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{json}} for structured logging with log aggregators.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYROSCOPE\_SERVER}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
(disabled)
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Profiling data endpoint.  \sphinxstylestrong{Must} point to a trusted internal host.
An attacker who controls this variable can exfiltrate performance
data to an external server.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYROSCOPE\_ENABLED}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{false}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Disable in production unless actively profiling.  Profiling adds
CPU overhead and exposes timing information.
\\
\sphinxbottomrule
\end{tabular}
\sphinxtableafterendhook\par
\sphinxattableend\end{savenotes}


\subsection{Network Security}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/security:network-security}}\label{\detokenize{deployment/security:id1}}

\subsubsection{Egress Restrictions}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/security:egress-restrictions}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
PyCypher containers should have restricted egress to prevent data
exfiltration via cloud read functions or SSRF bypasses:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} docker\PYGZhy{}compose.override.yml — production egress restrictions}
\PYG{n+nt}{services}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{  }\PYG{n+nt}{pycypher}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{n+nt}{networks}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{      }\PYG{p+pIndicator}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{internal}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} No access to external networks}
\PYG{+w}{  }\PYG{n+nt}{neo4j}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{n+nt}{networks}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{      }\PYG{p+pIndicator}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{internal}
\PYG{+w}{  }\PYG{n+nt}{spark\PYGZhy{}master}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{n+nt}{networks}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{      }\PYG{p+pIndicator}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{internal}

\PYG{n+nt}{networks}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{  }\PYG{n+nt}{internal}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{n+nt}{internal}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{true}\PYG{+w}{  }\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} No external connectivity}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
If the pipeline must read from cloud storage (S3, GCS, Azure), use a
proxy or IAM role with minimum required permissions rather than granting
broad network access.


\subsubsection{Neo4j Connection Security}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/security:neo4j-connection-security}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Always enable TLS for Neo4j connections in production:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{import}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{os}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{sinks}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{neo4j}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{Neo4jSink}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Use bolt+s:// scheme for TLS}
\PYG{k}{with} \PYG{n}{Neo4jSink}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{bolt+s://neo4j\PYGZhy{}host:7687}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{neo4j}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{os}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{environ}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{NEO4J\PYGZus{}PASSWORD}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n}{sink}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n}{sink}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{write\PYGZus{}nodes}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{result}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{mapping}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The Neo4j sink validates all identifiers (labels, property names,
relationship types) against Cypher injection, including:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Backtick escaping attacks

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
NUL byte injection

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Unicode normalisation confusables (e.g., fullwidth grave accent)

\end{itemize}


\subsection{Resource Limits}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/security:resource-limits}}

\subsubsection{Query Timeout}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/security:query-timeout}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Set a per\sphinxhyphen{}query timeout to prevent runaway queries:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Via CLI}
nmetl\PYG{+w}{ }query\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}timeout\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+m}{30000}\PYG{+w}{ }config.yaml\PYG{+w}{  }\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} 30 second limit}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Via environment}
\PYG{n+nb}{export}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}QUERY\PYGZus{}TIMEOUT\PYGZus{}MS}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m}{30000}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{BFS Path Expansion}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/security:bfs-path-expansion}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Variable\sphinxhyphen{}length path patterns (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[}*1..10{]}}}) use BFS expansion with
two safety limits:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Hop limit:} unbounded paths (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[}*{]}}}) are capped at 20 hops.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Frontier limit:} the BFS frontier is truncated at 1,000,000 rows
to prevent memory exhaustion in highly\sphinxhyphen{}connected graphs.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
If your graph has high average degree (>100 edges per node), consider
adding explicit hop bounds to variable\sphinxhyphen{}length patterns.


\subsubsection{AST Cache}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/security:ast-cache}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The query parser caches AST results with LRU eviction:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Default size:} 1,024 entries.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Configuration:} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_AST\_CACHE\_MAX}} environment variable.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Monitoring:} cache hit rate, eviction count, and current size are
available via \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{parser.cache\_stats}}.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
For ETL pipelines with a fixed set of queries, the default is sufficient.
For applications generating many unique queries (e.g., parameterised by
user input), monitor eviction rates and increase the limit if hit rate
drops below 80\%.


\subsection{Monitoring and Alerting}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/security:monitoring-and-alerting}}

\subsubsection{Security Events to Monitor}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/security:security-events-to-monitor}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Configure alerts for these log patterns:


\begin{savenotes}\sphinxattablestart
\sphinxthistablewithglobalstyle
\centering
\begin{tabular}[t]{\X{40}{100}\X{30}{100}\X{30}{100}}
\sphinxtoprule
\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Log Pattern
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Indicates
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Action
\\
\sphinxmidrule
\sphinxtableatstartofbodyhook
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SecurityError}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Blocked SQL injection, path traversal, or SSRF attempt
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Investigate source IP / user
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Dangerous DuckDB table function}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Attempted file system access via SQL query
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Block source, review query logs
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SSRF protection}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Attempted access to private/internal network
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Review URI source, tighten egress
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BFS frontier.*exceeds safety limit}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Possible resource exhaustion attack via complex queries
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Review query patterns, add hop bounds
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{catastrophic backtracking}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
ReDoS attack via regex \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{=\textasciitilde{}}} operator
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Block pattern, review query source
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Query.*timed out}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Runaway query (accidental or malicious)
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Review query, tune timeout
\\
\sphinxbottomrule
\end{tabular}
\sphinxtableafterendhook\par
\sphinxattableend\end{savenotes}


\subsubsection{Structured Logging}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/security:structured-logging}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Enable JSON logging for machine\sphinxhyphen{}parseable security audit trails:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n+nb}{export}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}LOG\PYGZus{}FORMAT}\PYG{o}{=}json
\PYG{n+nb}{export}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}LOG\PYGZus{}LEVEL}\PYG{o}{=}INFO
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Docker Security}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/security:docker-security}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The production Dockerfile follows container security best practices:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Non\sphinxhyphen{}root user:} runs as UID 1000 (not root).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Minimal base image:} Python slim variant.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{No shell access needed:} consider using \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}read\sphinxhyphen{}only}} filesystem.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Health checks:} Docker health checks verify the engine is responsive.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Additional hardening for production:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n+nt}{services}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{  }\PYG{n+nt}{pycypher}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{n+nt}{read\PYGZus{}only}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{true}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{n+nt}{tmpfs}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{      }\PYG{p+pIndicator}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{/tmp}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{n+nt}{security\PYGZus{}opt}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{      }\PYG{p+pIndicator}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{no\PYGZhy{}new\PYGZhy{}privileges:true}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{n+nt}{cap\PYGZus{}drop}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{      }\PYG{p+pIndicator}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{ALL}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{n+nt}{mem\PYGZus{}limit}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{4g}\PYG{+w}{          }\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Prevent OOM from taking down the host}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{n+nt}{memswap\PYGZus{}limit}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{4g}\PYG{+w}{      }\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Disable swap to avoid performance collapse}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Function Registration Security}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/security:function-registration-security}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The YAML configuration system supports registering custom Python functions
as Cypher scalar functions.  This is protected by:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Module blocklist:} imports from \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{os}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{subprocess}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{sys}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{socket}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pickle}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ctypes}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{importlib}}, and 13 other
dangerous modules are rejected.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Import path validation:} only dotted Python identifiers
(\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{package.module.function}}) are accepted.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Top\sphinxhyphen{}level check:} the first component of the import path is checked
against the blocklist.

\end{itemize}

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{warning}{Warning:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Custom function registration executes arbitrary Python code from the
imported module.  Only register functions from trusted, audited packages.
Never allow end users to specify import paths directly.
\end{sphinxadmonition}


\subsection{Incident Response}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/security:incident-response}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
If a security event is detected:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Contain} – stop the affected container immediately:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
docker\PYG{+w}{ }compose\PYG{+w}{ }stop\PYG{+w}{ }pycypher
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Preserve evidence} – save logs before they rotate:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
docker\PYG{+w}{ }compose\PYG{+w}{ }logs\PYG{+w}{ }pycypher\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZgt{}\PYG{+w}{ }incident\PYGZus{}\PYG{k}{\PYGZdl{}(}date\PYG{+w}{ }+\PYGZpc{}s\PYG{k}{)}.log
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Investigate} – search logs for the triggering event:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
grep\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}i\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}SecurityError\PYGZbs{}|SSRF\PYGZbs{}|injection\PYGZbs{}|backtracking\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{+w}{ }incident\PYGZus{}*.log
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Remediate} – apply the appropriate fix:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
SQL injection attempt → review and tighten query validation

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
SSRF attempt → add network egress restrictions

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Resource exhaustion → lower timeout, add hop bounds

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
ReDoS → the pattern is already blocked; check for bypass attempts

\end{itemize}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Post\sphinxhyphen{}incident} – update monitoring rules and review access controls.

\end{enumerate}

\sphinxstepscope


\section{Docker Containerisation}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/docker:docker-containerisation}}\label{\detokenize{deployment/docker::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
PyCypher ships two Dockerfiles and a Docker Compose configuration that
orchestrates the full development and integration stack.


\subsection{Images}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/docker:images}}

\subsubsection{Production Image (\sphinxstyleliteralintitle{\sphinxupquote{Dockerfile}})}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/docker:production-image-dockerfile}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The production image uses a \sphinxstylestrong{multi\sphinxhyphen{}stage build} to minimize image size.
Stage 1 installs dependencies with build tools (compilers, git).  Stage 2
copies only the virtual environment into a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{python:3.14\sphinxhyphen{}slim}} runtime
image, excluding all build\sphinxhyphen{}only tooling.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
docker\PYG{+w}{ }build\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}t\PYG{+w}{ }pycypher:latest\PYG{+w}{ }.
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Key characteristics:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Multi\sphinxhyphen{}stage build} – \textasciitilde{}400 MB final image (vs \textasciitilde{}1.2 GB single\sphinxhyphen{}stage)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Non\sphinxhyphen{}root user} (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{appuser}}, UID 1000) for security hardening

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Built\sphinxhyphen{}in health check} – \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{HEALTHCHECK}} directive runs \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{nmetl health}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Health server default} – \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{CMD}} starts the HTTP health endpoint on port 8079

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Layer\sphinxhyphen{}cache\sphinxhyphen{}optimized – dependency manifests copied before source code

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Running the production image:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Start with health server (default CMD)}
docker\PYG{+w}{ }run\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}p\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+m}{8079}:8079\PYG{+w}{ }pycypher:latest

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Run a one\PYGZhy{}off query instead}
docker\PYG{+w}{ }run\PYG{+w}{ }pycypher:latest\PYG{+w}{ }nmetl\PYG{+w}{ }query\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}inline\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}MATCH (n) RETURN n\PYGZdq{}}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Start health server on all interfaces}
docker\PYG{+w}{ }run\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}p\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+m}{8079}:8079\PYG{+w}{ }pycypher:latest\PYG{+w}{ }nmetl\PYG{+w}{ }health\PYGZhy{}server\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}bind\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+m}{0}.0.0.0
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Development Image (\sphinxstyleliteralintitle{\sphinxupquote{Dockerfile.dev}})}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/docker:development-image-dockerfile-dev}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The development image adds interactive tooling on top of the same Python base.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
docker\PYG{+w}{ }build\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}f\PYG{+w}{ }Dockerfile.dev\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}t\PYG{+w}{ }pycypher\PYGZhy{}dev:latest\PYG{+w}{ }.
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Additional tools included:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ipython}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ipdb}} for interactive debugging

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pytest}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pytest\sphinxhyphen{}cov}} for testing

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{sudo}} access for the developer user

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
VS Code Dev Container compatible

\end{itemize}


\subsection{Docker Compose}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/docker:docker-compose}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{docker\sphinxhyphen{}compose.yml}} defines the full multi\sphinxhyphen{}service stack.  All services
share the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher\sphinxhyphen{}dev\sphinxhyphen{}network}} bridge network.


\subsubsection{Quick Start}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/docker:quick-start}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} 1. Copy and populate the environment file}
cp\PYG{+w}{ }.env.example\PYG{+w}{ }.env
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Edit .env with real credentials (see Environment Configuration)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} 2. Start the development stack}
make\PYG{+w}{ }dev\PYGZhy{}up

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} 3. Access the dev container}
make\PYG{+w}{ }dev\PYGZhy{}shell

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} 4. Run the test suite inside the container}
\PYG{n+nb}{cd}\PYG{+w}{ }/workspace\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{o}{\PYGZam{}\PYGZam{}}\PYG{+w}{ }uv\PYG{+w}{ }run\PYG{+w}{ }pytest
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Services}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/docker:services}}

\begin{savenotes}\sphinxattablestart
