\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
fastopendata\sphinxhyphen{}api
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
pycypher\sphinxhyphen{}fastopendata\sphinxhyphen{}api
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
8093
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
REST API (Swagger at /docs)
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
code\sphinxhyphen{}server
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
pycypher\sphinxhyphen{}code\sphinxhyphen{}server
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
8080
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
VS Code in browser (profile: code\sphinxhyphen{}server)
\\
\sphinxbottomrule
\end{tabular}
\sphinxtableafterendhook\par
\sphinxattableend\end{savenotes}


\subsubsection{Optional Profiles}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/docker:optional-profiles}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Some services are behind Docker Compose profiles to avoid starting them by
default:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Start with Jupyter Lab}
make\PYG{+w}{ }dev\PYGZhy{}jupyter
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} → http://localhost:8888}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Start with browser\PYGZhy{}based VS Code}
make\PYG{+w}{ }dev\PYGZhy{}vscode
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} → http://localhost:8080}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Volumes}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/docker:volumes}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Persistent volumes ensure data survives container restarts:


\begin{savenotes}\sphinxattablestart
\sphinxthistablewithglobalstyle
\centering
\begin{tabular}[t]{\X{30}{100}\X{70}{100}}
\sphinxtoprule
\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Volume
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Purpose
\\
\sphinxmidrule
\sphinxtableatstartofbodyhook
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{uv\sphinxhyphen{}cache}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Python package cache (speeds up rebuilds)
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pytest\sphinxhyphen{}cache}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Test result cache
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{neo4j\sphinxhyphen{}data}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Neo4j graph database files
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{neo4j\sphinxhyphen{}logs}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Neo4j server logs
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{neo4j\sphinxhyphen{}import}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Neo4j CSV import directory
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{spark\sphinxhyphen{}events}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Spark event history for the History Server UI
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{fastopendata\sphinxhyphen{}raw}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Downloaded raw datasets (OSM PBF, TIGER shapefiles, etc.);
bind\sphinxhyphen{}mounted directly to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\$\{DATA\_DIR\}}} on the host — the same flat
location the native Snakemake pipeline (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{make fod\sphinxhyphen{}data}}) writes to,
so a file downloaded either way is visible to both
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{nominatim\sphinxhyphen{}data}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
PostGIS database for Nominatim; bind\sphinxhyphen{}mounted to
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\$\{NOMINATIM\_PGDATA\_DIR\}}} on the host (not Docker\sphinxhyphen{}managed storage,
and deliberately \sphinxstylestrong{not} nested under \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DATA\_DIR}} — see
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{docs/deployment/troubleshooting.rst}} for why nesting corrupts the
import) so the multi\sphinxhyphen{}GB database lives on the data disk
\\
\sphinxbottomrule
\end{tabular}
\sphinxtableafterendhook\par
\sphinxattableend\end{savenotes}


\subsubsection{Building Custom Images}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/docker:building-custom-images}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
When adding new Python dependencies:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Rebuild just the dev container}
make\PYG{+w}{ }dev\PYGZhy{}rebuild

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Rebuild all images}
docker\PYG{+w}{ }compose\PYG{+w}{ }build

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Rebuild with no cache (after Dockerfile changes)}
docker\PYG{+w}{ }compose\PYG{+w}{ }build\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}no\PYGZhy{}cache\PYG{+w}{ }pycypher\PYGZhy{}dev
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Health Checks}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/docker:health-checks}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
All key containers include health checks:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{pycypher\sphinxhyphen{}dev}: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{nmetl health}} every 30s (60s start grace period)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Neo4j}: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wget http://localhost:7474}} every 10s (10 retries)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Nominatim}: geocode query every 30s (20 retries, 300s start grace period)

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{nmetl health}} command returns exit code 0 (healthy), 1 (degraded), or
2 (unhealthy), making it compatible with Docker \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{HEALTHCHECK}} and Kubernetes
liveness/readiness probes.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Check health status:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
docker\PYG{+w}{ }compose\PYG{+w}{ }ps
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Shows health status for each service}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Detailed health report from the dev container}
docker\PYG{+w}{ }compose\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nb}{exec}\PYG{+w}{ }pycypher\PYGZhy{}dev\PYG{+w}{ }uv\PYG{+w}{ }run\PYG{+w}{ }nmetl\PYG{+w}{ }health\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}json
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Kubernetes Deployment}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/docker:kubernetes-deployment}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The production image works directly with Kubernetes.  The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{nmetl health\sphinxhyphen{}server}}
command provides standard HTTP endpoints for liveness and readiness probes:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n+nt}{apiVersion}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{apps/v1}
\PYG{n+nt}{kind}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{Deployment}
\PYG{n+nt}{metadata}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{  }\PYG{n+nt}{name}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{pycypher}
\PYG{n+nt}{spec}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{  }\PYG{n+nt}{replicas}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{1}
\PYG{+w}{  }\PYG{n+nt}{selector}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{n+nt}{matchLabels}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{      }\PYG{n+nt}{app}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{pycypher}
\PYG{+w}{  }\PYG{n+nt}{template}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{n+nt}{metadata}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{      }\PYG{n+nt}{labels}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{        }\PYG{n+nt}{app}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{pycypher}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{n+nt}{spec}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{      }\PYG{n+nt}{containers}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{        }\PYG{p+pIndicator}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nt}{name}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{pycypher}
\PYG{+w}{          }\PYG{n+nt}{image}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{pycypher:latest}
\PYG{+w}{          }\PYG{n+nt}{ports}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{            }\PYG{p+pIndicator}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nt}{containerPort}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{8079}
\PYG{+w}{          }\PYG{n+nt}{livenessProbe}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{            }\PYG{n+nt}{httpGet}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{              }\PYG{n+nt}{path}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{/health}
\PYG{+w}{              }\PYG{n+nt}{port}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{8079}
\PYG{+w}{            }\PYG{n+nt}{initialDelaySeconds}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{10}
\PYG{+w}{            }\PYG{n+nt}{periodSeconds}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{30}
\PYG{+w}{          }\PYG{n+nt}{readinessProbe}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{            }\PYG{n+nt}{httpGet}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{              }\PYG{n+nt}{path}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{/ready}
\PYG{+w}{              }\PYG{n+nt}{port}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{8079}
\PYG{+w}{            }\PYG{n+nt}{initialDelaySeconds}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{5}
\PYG{+w}{            }\PYG{n+nt}{periodSeconds}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{10}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{/metrics}} endpoint serves Prometheus text format, ready for scraping
by Prometheus or any OpenMetrics\sphinxhyphen{}compatible collector.

\sphinxstepscope


\section{Environment Configuration}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/environment:environment-configuration}}\label{\detokenize{deployment/environment::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
PyCypher uses environment variables for service credentials and connection
strings.  All required variables must be set before running \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{docker compose}}.


\subsection{Setup}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/environment:setup}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Copy the template}
cp\PYG{+w}{ }.env.example\PYG{+w}{ }.env

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Edit with real values}
\PYG{n+nv}{\PYGZdl{}EDITOR}\PYG{+w}{ }.env
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Required Variables}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/environment:required-variables}}

\begin{savenotes}\sphinxattablestart
\sphinxthistablewithglobalstyle

&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Example
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Description
\\

\sphinxtableatstartofbodyhook
\sphinxAtStartPar

&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Neo4j database username
\\

\sphinxAtStartPar

Neo4j database password
\\

\sphinxAtStartPar

\sphinxAtStartPar
Variable
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Default
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Description

Application name for Pyroscope
\\

\sphinxtableafterendhook\par
\sphinxattableend\end{savenotes}


\subsection{Python Environment}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/environment:python-environment}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
PyCypher requires Python 3.14+ and uses \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{uv}} for dependency management.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Install uv}
curl\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}LsSf\PYG{+w}{ }https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{p}{|}\PYG{+w}{ }sh

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Sync all workspace dependencies}
uv\PYG{+w}{ }sync

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Verify installation}
uv\PYG{+w}{ }run\PYG{+w}{ }python\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}c\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}from pycypher import Star; print(\PYGZsq{}OK\PYGZsq{})\PYGZdq{}}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The workspace is structured as a monorepo with cross\sphinxhyphen{}package dependencies:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{packages/shared}} – common utilities (no dependencies on other packages)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{packages/pycypher}} – core engine (depends on \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{shared}})

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{packages/fastopendata}} – data pipeline (depends on \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher}})

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
After editing any \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pyproject.toml}}, always re\sphinxhyphen{}sync:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
uv\PYG{+w}{ }sync
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Security Notes}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/environment:security-notes}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Never commit \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{.env}} files to version control (already in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{.gitignore}})

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Generate strong random secrets for \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SPARK\_RPC\_SECRET}} and passwords

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The production Dockerfile runs as a non\sphinxhyphen{}root user (UID 1000)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Spark RPC authentication and encryption are enabled by default

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Neo4j heap and page cache are capped to prevent OOM (1 GB heap, 512 MB page cache)

\end{itemize}

\sphinxstepscope


\section{Scaling \& Performance}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/scaling:scaling-performance}}\label{\detokenize{deployment/scaling::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
PyCypher supports several strategies for scaling query execution from
single\sphinxhyphen{}machine development to multi\sphinxhyphen{}node production workloads.


\subsection{Backend Selection}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/scaling:backend-selection}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
PyCypher’s query engine uses pandas DataFrames as the default execution
backend.  The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{backend\_engine}} module provides an abstraction layer for
alternative backends:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{backend\PYGZus{}engine}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{select\PYGZus{}backend}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Select a backend programmatically}
\PYG{n}{backend} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{select\PYGZus{}backend}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{pandas}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\begin{savenotes}\sphinxattablestart
\sphinxthistablewithglobalstyle
\centering
\begin{tabular}[t]{\X{15}{100}\X{30}{100}\X{30}{100}\X{25}{100}}
\sphinxtoprule
\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Backend
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Status
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Best for
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Notes
\\
\sphinxmidrule
\sphinxtableatstartofbodyhook
\sphinxAtStartPar
Pandas
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Default, fully integrated}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
All workloads, widest compatibility
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Used by \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Star.execute\_query()}}
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
DuckDB
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Integrated for data ingestion
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Large CSV/Parquet scans, SQL sources
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Used by \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ContextBuilder}} for loading
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
Polars
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Optional (included in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{uv sync \sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}group dev}})
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
CPU\sphinxhyphen{}bound transforms
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Backend engine support, not default path
\\
\sphinxbottomrule
\end{tabular}
\sphinxtableafterendhook\par
\sphinxattableend\end{savenotes}

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{note}{Note:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ContextBuilder.from\_dict()}} does not accept a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{backend}} parameter.
Backend selection is handled by the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{backend\_engine}} module, which is
used internally by the query planner.
\end{sphinxadmonition}


\subsubsection{Health Checks and Circuit Breaker}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/scaling:health-checks-and-circuit-breaker}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The backend engine includes a health check probe that validates scan, filter,
join, and materialise operations before committing to a backend:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{backend\PYGZus{}engine}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{check\PYGZus{}backend\PYGZus{}health}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{PandasBackend}

\PYG{n}{backend} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{PandasBackend}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{healthy} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{check\PYGZus{}backend\PYGZus{}health}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{backend}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The circuit breaker uses a three\sphinxhyphen{}state model:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{CLOSED} (normal) – requests flow through; failures increment a counter

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{OPEN} (tripped) – backend is skipped; checked again after a recovery timeout

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{HALF\_OPEN} (probing) – one test request; success resets, failure re\sphinxhyphen{}opens

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Default thresholds: 3 consecutive failures to open, 60\sphinxhyphen{}second recovery timeout.


\subsection{Spark Cluster (Docker Infrastructure)}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/scaling:spark-cluster-docker-infrastructure}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The Docker Compose setup includes an optional Spark cluster for external
data processing.  This is part of the deployment infrastructure, not the
PyCypher query engine.  To scale:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Start with default 1 worker}
make\PYG{+w}{ }spark\PYGZhy{}up

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Scale to 5 workers}
make\PYG{+w}{ }spark\PYGZhy{}scale\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{WORKERS}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m}{5}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} View the Spark UI}
make\PYG{+w}{ }spark\PYGZhy{}ui
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} → http://localhost:8090}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Worker Configuration}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/scaling:worker-configuration}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Each Spark worker is configured with:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{2 GB memory} (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SPARK\_WORKER\_MEMORY=2G}})

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{2 CPU cores} (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SPARK\_WORKER\_CORES=2}})

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{RPC authentication} enabled with shared secret

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{RPC encryption} and local storage encryption enabled

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
To change worker resources, modify \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{docker\sphinxhyphen{}compose.yml}}:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n+nt}{spark\PYGZhy{}worker}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{  }\PYG{n+nt}{environment}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{p+pIndicator}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{SPARK\PYGZus{}WORKER\PYGZus{}MEMORY=4G}\PYG{+w}{   }\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} increase per\PYGZhy{}worker memory}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{p+pIndicator}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{SPARK\PYGZus{}WORKER\PYGZus{}CORES=4}\PYG{+w}{     }\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} increase per\PYGZhy{}worker cores}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Neo4j Tuning (Docker Infrastructure)}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/scaling:neo4j-tuning-docker-infrastructure}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Default Neo4j memory settings are conservative:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n+nt}{neo4j}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{  }\PYG{n+nt}{environment}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{p+pIndicator}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{NEO4J\PYGZus{}dbms\PYGZus{}memory\PYGZus{}heap\PYGZus{}max\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}size=1G}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{p+pIndicator}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{NEO4J\PYGZus{}dbms\PYGZus{}memory\PYGZus{}pagecache\PYGZus{}size=512m}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
For production workloads with larger graphs, increase these values.  A common
guideline: allocate 50\% of available RAM to page cache and 25\% to heap.


\subsection{Query\sphinxhyphen{}Level Performance}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/scaling:query-level-performance}}

\subsubsection{Memory Budget}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/scaling:memory-budget}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The query planner estimates memory usage and enforces a configurable budget:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{Star}

\PYG{n}{star} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (a)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(b) RETURN a.name, b.name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{memory\PYGZus{}budget\PYGZus{}mb}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{512}\PYG{p}{,}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} cap at 512 MB}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Cross\sphinxhyphen{}Join Limits}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/scaling:cross-join-limits}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Unbounded cross\sphinxhyphen{}products (e.g., \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MATCH (a), (b)}}) are capped by default to
prevent resource exhaustion.  The limit is configurable:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (a:Person), (b:Person) RETURN count(*)}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{max\PYGZus{}cross\PYGZus{}join\PYGZus{}rows}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1\PYGZus{}000\PYGZus{}000}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Query Timeout}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/scaling:query-timeout}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Set a timeout to abort runaway queries:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (a)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS*1..10]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(b) RETURN a, b}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{timeout\PYGZus{}seconds}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{30}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Lazy Evaluation}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/scaling:lazy-evaluation}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Variable\sphinxhyphen{}length path queries and large scans benefit from lazy evaluation,
which defers computation until results are materialised:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Lazy evaluation is automatic for variable\PYGZhy{}length paths}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (a)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS*1..5]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(b) RETURN a.name, b.name LIMIT 100}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Only paths that survive the LIMIT are fully materialised}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Performance Testing}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/scaling:performance-testing}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Run the built\sphinxhyphen{}in performance benchmarks:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Large\PYGZhy{}dataset integration tests (120s timeout)}
make\PYG{+w}{ }test\PYGZhy{}large\PYGZhy{}dataset

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Backend compatibility tests}
make\PYG{+w}{ }test\PYGZhy{}backends

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Full test suite in parallel}
make\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nb}{test}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Resource Planning}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/scaling:resource-planning}}

\begin{savenotes}\sphinxattablestart
\sphinxthistablewithglobalstyle
\centering
\begin{tabular}[t]{\X{25}{100}\X{25}{100}\X{25}{100}\X{25}{100}}
\sphinxtoprule
\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Dataset size
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Recommended backend
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
RAM per worker
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Workers
\\
\sphinxmidrule
\sphinxtableatstartofbodyhook
\sphinxAtStartPar
< 100K rows
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Pandas
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
2 GB
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
1
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
100K – 10M rows
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
DuckDB for loading, Pandas for execution
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
4 GB
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
1
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
10M – 100M rows
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
DuckDB for loading, tune memory budget
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
8 GB
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
1
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
> 100M rows
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Partition data, batch processing
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
16 GB+
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
1+
\\
\sphinxbottomrule
\end{tabular}
\sphinxtableafterendhook\par
\sphinxattableend\end{savenotes}

\sphinxAtStartPar
These are guidelines.  Actual requirements depend on query complexity (number
of joins, variable\sphinxhyphen{}length paths, aggregation cardinality).  Use the query
planner’s memory estimation to calibrate for your workload.

\sphinxstepscope


\section{Monitoring \& Operations}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/monitoring:monitoring-operations}}\label{\detokenize{deployment/monitoring::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
PyCypher includes built\sphinxhyphen{}in metrics collection, structured logging, and
diagnostic tools for production observability.


\subsection{Query Metrics}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/monitoring:query-metrics}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{shared.metrics}} module collects per\sphinxhyphen{}query execution metrics
automatically:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{shared}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{metrics}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{QUERY\PYGZus{}METRICS}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Execute some queries...}
\PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) RETURN p.name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Get a point\PYGZhy{}in\PYGZhy{}time snapshot}
\PYG{n}{snapshot} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{QUERY\PYGZus{}METRICS}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{snapshot}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Queries executed: }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{snapshot}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{total\PYGZus{}queries}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Total time: }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{snapshot}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{total\PYGZus{}time\PYGZus{}ms}\PYG{l+s+si}{:}\PYG{l+s+s2}{.1f}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ ms}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Error count: }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{snapshot}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{error\PYGZus{}count}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Metrics Snapshot}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/monitoring:metrics-snapshot}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MetricsSnapshot}} includes:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Query counts} – total, successful, failed

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Timing} – total, mean, p50, p95, p99 latencies

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Throughput} – queries per second

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Error rates} – error count and error rate trend

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Per\sphinxhyphen{}clause breakdown} – time spent in MATCH, WHERE, RETURN, etc.

\end{itemize}


\subsubsection{Diagnostic Report}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/monitoring:diagnostic-report}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
For operator dashboards, use the diagnostic report on a metrics snapshot:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{snapshot} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{QUERY\PYGZus{}METRICS}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{snapshot}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{report} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{snapshot}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{diagnostic\PYGZus{}report}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Returns a human\PYGZhy{}readable multi\PYGZhy{}section report with hotspot}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} identification, error analysis, and cache efficiency}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Cache Statistics}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/monitoring:cache-statistics}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Monitor AST parse cache and result cache pressure:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}cache\PYGZus{}stats}

\PYG{n}{stats} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}cache\PYGZus{}stats}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{AST cache hits: }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{stats}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{ast\PYGZus{}hits}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{AST cache misses: }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{stats}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{ast\PYGZus{}misses}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{AST cache size: }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{stats}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{ast\PYGZus{}size}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Result cache hit rate: }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{stats}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{get}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{result\PYGZus{}cache\PYGZus{}hit\PYGZus{}rate}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+m+mi}{0}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{l+s+si}{:}\PYG{l+s+s2}{.1\PYGZpc{}}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Metrics Export}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/monitoring:metrics-export}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
PyCypher includes pluggable metrics exporters for Prometheus, StatsD, and
JSON file output.  All exporters use stdlib only — no external dependencies.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Configure via the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_METRICS\_EXPORT}} environment variable:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Enable one or more exporters (comma\PYGZhy{}separated)}
\PYG{n+nb}{export}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}METRICS\PYGZus{}EXPORT}\PYG{o}{=}prometheus,statsd,json
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Prometheus}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/monitoring:prometheus}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Generates \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{text/plain; version=0.0.4}} output compatible with Prometheus,
VictoriaMetrics, or any OpenMetrics scraper.  The health server at
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{/metrics}} uses this exporter automatically.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{shared}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{exporters}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{PrometheusExporter}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{shared}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{metrics}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{QUERY\PYGZus{}METRICS}

\PYG{n}{exporter} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{PrometheusExporter}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{prefix}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{pycypher}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{text} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{exporter}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{render}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{QUERY\PYGZus{}METRICS}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{snapshot}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Returns multi\PYGZhy{}line Prometheus text exposition format}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Exported metrics include \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher\_queries\_total}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher\_query\_duration\_p50\_ms}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher\_cache\_hit\_rate}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher\_health\_status}}, per\sphinxhyphen{}clause timings, and per\sphinxhyphen{}error\sphinxhyphen{}type counters.


\subsubsection{StatsD / Datadog}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/monitoring:statsd-datadog}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Pushes metrics via UDP to any StatsD\sphinxhyphen{}compatible daemon (StatsD, Datadog Agent,
Telegraf).

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n+nb}{export}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}METRICS\PYGZus{}EXPORT}\PYG{o}{=}statsd
\PYG{n+nb}{export}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}STATSD\PYGZus{}HOST}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m}{127}.0.0.1\PYG{+w}{   }\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} default}
\PYG{n+nb}{export}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}STATSD\PYGZus{}PORT}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m}{8125}\PYG{+w}{         }\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} default}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{JSON File}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/monitoring:json-file}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Appends JSON\sphinxhyphen{}lines snapshots for ingestion by ELK, Datadog Logs, or Loki.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n+nb}{export}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}METRICS\PYGZus{}EXPORT}\PYG{o}{=}json
\PYG{n+nb}{export}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}METRICS\PYGZus{}JSON\PYGZus{}PATH}\PYG{o}{=}metrics.jsonl\PYG{+w}{   }\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} default}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Programmatic Export}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/monitoring:programmatic-export}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Push metrics to all configured exporters in one call:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{shared}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{exporters}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{export\PYGZus{}once}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{shared}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{metrics}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{QUERY\PYGZus{}METRICS}

\PYG{n}{export\PYGZus{}once}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{QUERY\PYGZus{}METRICS}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{snapshot}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Additional configuration:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_METRICS\_PREFIX}} — metric name prefix (default: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher}})

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_METRICS\_EXPORT\_INTERVAL\_S}} — push interval in seconds (default: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{60}})

\end{itemize}


\subsection{OpenTelemetry Distributed Tracing}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/monitoring:opentelemetry-distributed-tracing}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
PyCypher supports OpenTelemetry distributed tracing for end\sphinxhyphen{}to\sphinxhyphen{}end query
visibility across services.  If \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{opentelemetry\sphinxhyphen{}api}} is not installed, all
tracing calls are silent no\sphinxhyphen{}ops with zero runtime cost.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Enable tracing}
\PYG{n+nb}{export}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}OTEL\PYGZus{}ENABLED}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m}{1}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Standard OTel configuration}
\PYG{n+nb}{export}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{OTEL\PYGZus{}SERVICE\PYGZus{}NAME}\PYG{o}{=}pycypher
\PYG{n+nb}{export}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{OTEL\PYGZus{}EXPORTER\PYGZus{}OTLP\PYGZus{}ENDPOINT}\PYG{o}{=}http://localhost:4317
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Wrap query execution in traced spans:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{shared}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{otel}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{trace\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{trace\PYGZus{}phase}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Trace an entire query}
\PYG{k}{with} \PYG{n}{trace\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) RETURN p.name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{query\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{q\PYGZhy{}123}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n}{span}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{query}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{n}{span}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{set\PYGZus{}attribute}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{result.rows}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n+nb}{len}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{result}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Trace individual phases (parse, plan, execute)}
\PYG{k}{with} \PYG{n}{trace\PYGZus{}phase}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{parse}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{query\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{q\PYGZhy{}123}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n}{span}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n}{ast} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{parser}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{parse}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{query}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Span attributes set automatically:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{db.system}} = \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"pycypher"}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{db.statement}} = query text (truncated to 500 chars)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{db.operation}} = first Cypher keyword (MATCH, CREATE, etc.)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.query\_id}} = correlation ID

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Exceptions are recorded on the span and the span status is set to ERROR.


\subsection{Structured Logging}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/monitoring:structured-logging}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
PyCypher uses the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{shared.logger}} module for structured output:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{shared}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{logger}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{LOGGER}

\PYG{n}{LOGGER}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{info}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Query executed}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{query}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (n) RETURN n}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{rows}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{42}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{LOGGER}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{debug}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Pattern match}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{entity\PYGZus{}type}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{matches}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1000}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Query execution is automatically logged with:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Query text (truncated for security)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Execution time

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Row count

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Error details (on failure)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Query ID for correlation

\end{itemize}


\subsubsection{Query ID Correlation}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/monitoring:query-id-correlation}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Each query execution is assigned a unique ID for end\sphinxhyphen{}to\sphinxhyphen{}end tracing:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) RETURN p.name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Log output: \PYGZob{}\PYGZdq{}query\PYGZus{}id\PYGZdq{}: \PYGZdq{}abc\PYGZhy{}123\PYGZdq{}, \PYGZdq{}time\PYGZus{}ms\PYGZdq{}: 12.3, \PYGZdq{}rows\PYGZdq{}: 100\PYGZcb{}}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Container Logs}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/monitoring:container-logs}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
View logs for any service:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Development container}
make\PYG{+w}{ }dev\PYGZhy{}logs

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Spark cluster}
make\PYG{+w}{ }spark\PYGZhy{}logs

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Neo4j}
make\PYG{+w}{ }neo4j\PYGZhy{}logs

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Nominatim}
make\PYG{+w}{ }nominatim\PYGZhy{}logs

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} FastOpenData}
make\PYG{+w}{ }fod\PYGZhy{}logs
make\PYG{+w}{ }fod\PYGZhy{}api\PYGZhy{}logs
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Service Health}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/monitoring:service-health}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Check container status and health:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} All services}
docker\PYG{+w}{ }compose\PYG{+w}{ }ps

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Nominatim import/service status}
make\PYG{+w}{ }nominatim\PYGZhy{}status

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Neo4j health (via browser endpoint)}
curl\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}s\PYG{+w}{ }http://localhost:7474

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Spark cluster status}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} → open http://localhost:8090}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Backend Health Monitoring}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/monitoring:backend-health-monitoring}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Monitor backend health programmatically:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{backend\PYGZus{}engine}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{check\PYGZus{}backend\PYGZus{}health}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}circuit\PYGZus{}breaker}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{PandasBackend}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Probe a specific backend}
\PYG{n}{backend} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{PandasBackend}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{healthy} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{check\PYGZus{}backend\PYGZus{}health}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{backend}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Pandas backend healthy: }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{healthy}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Check circuit breaker state}
\PYG{n}{cb} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}circuit\PYGZus{}breaker}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{for} \PYG{n}{name} \PYG{o+ow}{in} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{pandas}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{duckdb}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{polars}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n}{state} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{cb}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{state}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{n}{available} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{cb}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{is\PYGZus{}available}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{: state=}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{state}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{value}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{, available=}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{available}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Operational Runbook}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/monitoring:operational-runbook}}

\subsubsection{Restart a Stuck Container}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/monitoring:restart-a-stuck-container}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Restart just the dev container}
docker\PYG{+w}{ }compose\PYG{+w}{ }restart\PYG{+w}{ }pycypher\PYGZhy{}dev

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Full rebuild if state is corrupted}
make\PYG{+w}{ }dev\PYGZhy{}rebuild
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Clear Neo4j Data}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/monitoring:clear-neo4j-data}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} WARNING: deletes all graph data (3s abort window)}
make\PYG{+w}{ }neo4j\PYGZhy{}reset
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Reset Spark State}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/monitoring:reset-spark-state}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
make\PYG{+w}{ }spark\PYGZhy{}down
docker\PYG{+w}{ }volume\PYG{+w}{ }rm\PYG{+w}{ }pycypher\PYGZhy{}nmetl\PYGZus{}spark\PYGZhy{}events
make\PYG{+w}{ }spark\PYGZhy{}up
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Check Nominatim Import Progress}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/monitoring:check-nominatim-import-progress}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The first Nominatim start imports US OSM data, which takes several hours:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Watch import progress}
make\PYG{+w}{ }nominatim\PYGZhy{}logs

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Check status endpoint}
make\PYG{+w}{ }nominatim\PYGZhy{}status

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Test geocoding}
make\PYG{+w}{ }nominatim\PYGZhy{}search
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxstepscope


\section{Troubleshooting}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/troubleshooting:troubleshooting}}\label{\detokenize{deployment/troubleshooting::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Common issues and their solutions when deploying and operating PyCypher.


\subsection{Docker Issues}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/troubleshooting:docker-issues}}

\subsubsection{\sphinxstyleliteralintitle{\sphinxupquote{docker compose up}} fails with “variable not set”}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/troubleshooting:docker-compose-up-fails-with-variable-not-set}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Cause}: Required environment variables are missing.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Fix}: Copy and populate the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{.env}} file:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
cp\PYG{+w}{ }.env.example\PYG{+w}{ }.env
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Set NEO4J\PYGZus{}USER, NEO4J\PYGZus{}PASSWORD, SPARK\PYGZus{}RPC\PYGZus{}SECRET, NOMINATIM\PYGZus{}PASSWORD}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Container starts but \sphinxstyleliteralintitle{\sphinxupquote{uv sync}} fails}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/troubleshooting:container-starts-but-uv-sync-fails}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Cause}: uv cache is stale or corrupted.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Fix}:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Clear the uv cache volume}
docker\PYG{+w}{ }compose\PYG{+w}{ }down
docker\PYG{+w}{ }volume\PYG{+w}{ }rm\PYG{+w}{ }pycypher\PYGZhy{}nmetl\PYGZus{}uv\PYGZhy{}cache
make\PYG{+w}{ }dev\PYGZhy{}up
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Port conflicts}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/troubleshooting:port-conflicts}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Cause}: Another service is using the same port.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Fix}: Check which ports are in use:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} macOS / Linux}
lsof\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}i\PYG{+w}{ }:7474\PYG{+w}{   }\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Neo4j}
lsof\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}i\PYG{+w}{ }:7077\PYG{+w}{   }\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Spark}
lsof\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}i\PYG{+w}{ }:8090\PYG{+w}{   }\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Spark UI}
lsof\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}i\PYG{+w}{ }:8092\PYG{+w}{   }\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Nominatim}
lsof\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}i\PYG{+w}{ }:8093\PYG{+w}{   }\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} FastOpenData API}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Stop the conflicting service or change the port mapping in
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{docker\sphinxhyphen{}compose.yml}}.


\subsection{Python / Dependency Issues}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/troubleshooting:python-dependency-issues}}

\subsubsection{\sphinxstyleliteralintitle{\sphinxupquote{ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'pycypher'}}}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/troubleshooting:modulenotfounderror-no-module-named-pycypher}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Cause}: Workspace not synced after changes.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Fix}:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
uv\PYG{+w}{ }sync
uv\PYG{+w}{ }run\PYG{+w}{ }python\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}c\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}from pycypher import Star; print(\PYGZsq{}OK\PYGZsq{})\PYGZdq{}}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{\sphinxstyleliteralintitle{\sphinxupquote{Python >= 3.14 required}}}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/troubleshooting:python-3-14-required}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Cause}: System Python is too old.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Fix}: Install Python 3.14+ via \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{uv}}:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
uv\PYG{+w}{ }python\PYG{+w}{ }install\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+m}{3}.14
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Backend failures}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/troubleshooting:backend-failures}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Cause}: Optional backend dependency not installed.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Fix}:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Install DuckDB backend}
uv\PYG{+w}{ }pip\PYG{+w}{ }install\PYG{+w}{ }duckdb

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Install Polars backend}
uv\PYG{+w}{ }pip\PYG{+w}{ }install\PYG{+w}{ }polars

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Verify}
uv\PYG{+w}{ }run\PYG{+w}{ }python\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}c\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}from pycypher.backend\PYGZus{}engine import select\PYGZus{}backend; print(select\PYGZus{}backend(hint=\PYGZsq{}pandas\PYGZsq{}).name)\PYGZdq{}}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Spark Issues}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/troubleshooting:spark-issues}}

\subsubsection{Spark worker cannot connect to master}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/troubleshooting:spark-worker-cannot-connect-to-master}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Cause}: RPC secret mismatch or network issue.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Fix}: Verify the secret is consistent:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Both master and worker must use the same SPARK\PYGZus{}RPC\PYGZus{}SECRET}
docker\PYG{+w}{ }compose\PYG{+w}{ }logs\PYG{+w}{ }spark\PYGZhy{}master\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{p}{|}\PYG{+w}{ }grep\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}i\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}auth\PYGZdq{}}
docker\PYG{+w}{ }compose\PYG{+w}{ }logs\PYG{+w}{ }spark\PYGZhy{}worker\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{p}{|}\PYG{+w}{ }grep\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}i\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}auth\PYGZdq{}}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Spark job fails with OOM}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/troubleshooting:spark-job-fails-with-oom}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Cause}: Worker memory is insufficient.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Fix}: Increase worker memory in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{docker\sphinxhyphen{}compose.yml}}:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n+nt}{spark\PYGZhy{}worker}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{  }\PYG{n+nt}{environment}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{p+pIndicator}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+lScalar+lScalarPlain}{SPARK\PYGZus{}WORKER\PYGZus{}MEMORY=4G}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Or scale out with more workers:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
make\PYG{+w}{ }spark\PYGZhy{}scale\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{WORKERS}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m}{4}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Neo4j Issues}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/troubleshooting:neo4j-issues}}

\subsubsection{Neo4j authentication failure}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/troubleshooting:neo4j-authentication-failure}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Cause}: Credentials in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{.env}} do not match the Neo4j volume state.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Fix}: If you changed the password after first setup, reset the volume:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
make\PYG{+w}{ }neo4j\PYGZhy{}down
docker\PYG{+w}{ }volume\PYG{+w}{ }rm\PYG{+w}{ }pycypher\PYGZhy{}nmetl\PYGZus{}neo4j\PYGZhy{}data
make\PYG{+w}{ }neo4j\PYGZhy{}up
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Neo4j APOC procedures not available}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/troubleshooting:neo4j-apoc-procedures-not-available}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Cause}: APOC plugin failed to install.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Fix}: Check logs and ensure the plugin config is correct:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
make\PYG{+w}{ }neo4j\PYGZhy{}logs
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Look for APOC\PYGZhy{}related messages}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Nominatim Issues}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/troubleshooting:nominatim-issues}}

\subsubsection{Nominatim import takes hours}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/troubleshooting:nominatim-import-takes-hours}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Expected}: The first\sphinxhyphen{}start import of the US OSM extract takes several hours
and requires approximately 32 GB of RAM.  Subsequent starts skip the import.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Watch progress}
make\PYG{+w}{ }nominatim\PYGZhy{}logs

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Check status}
make\PYG{+w}{ }nominatim\PYGZhy{}status
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Nominatim fails with “could not open file … Permission denied”}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/troubleshooting:nominatim-fails-with-could-not-open-file-permission-denied}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Two independent causes can produce this; check both if the import gets
partway through (roles created, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DROP DATABASE}} succeeds) and then fails
specifically on \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{createdb}}.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Cause 1 — bind\sphinxhyphen{}mount nesting.} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{nominatim\sphinxhyphen{}data}} (Postgres’s \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PGDATA}})
must \sphinxstylestrong{not} be a subdirectory of \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DATA\_DIR}}. \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DATA\_DIR}} is separately
bind\sphinxhyphen{}mounted read\sphinxhyphen{}write into the same container at \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{/nominatim/data}}, and
the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{mediagis/nominatim}} entrypoint runs \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{chown \sphinxhyphen{}R nominatim:nominatim
/nominatim}} on every start. If \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PGDATA}} lives underneath \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DATA\_DIR}}, it
is reachable from \sphinxstyleemphasis{both} mount points inside the container, so that chown
silently walks into it too and reassigns ownership away from the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{postgres}}
system account — breaking access moments later when \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{nominatim import}}
tries to create the database. This is why \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{NOMINATIM\_PGDATA\_DIR}} in the
Makefile is a \sphinxstylestrong{sibling} of \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DATA\_DIR}}
(\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{.../fastopendata/nominatim\sphinxhyphen{}postgres}}, not
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{.../fastopendata/data/nominatim/postgres}}) — keep it that way.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Cause 2 — restrictive mount\sphinxhyphen{}point permissions.} The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{mediagis/nominatim}}
image runs PostgreSQL as a dedicated non\sphinxhyphen{}root OS account (uid 101 / gid 103
in the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{4.4}} tag — check with \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{docker compose run \sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}rm \sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}entrypoint sh
nominatim \sphinxhyphen{}c 'getent passwd postgres'}} if the image is ever bumped).
Postgres refuses to start unless it strictly owns \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PGDATA}}, and if the
drive is auto\sphinxhyphen{}mounted by udisks2 under \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{/run/media/<user>/...}}, that mount
point is typically \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{root:root}} with an ACL granting only the logged\sphinxhyphen{}in
desktop user — Postgres’s uid can’t even traverse into it. Mount the drive at
a stable, non\sphinxhyphen{}udisks2 location via \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{/etc/fstab}} instead (e.g. \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{/mnt/2tb}}),
then run once:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Let non\PYGZhy{}owner uids traverse the filesystem\PYGZsq{}s own root dir}
sudo\PYG{+w}{ }chmod\PYG{+w}{ }o+rx\PYG{+w}{ }/mnt/2tb

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Postgres must literally own its data directory}
sudo\PYG{+w}{ }chown\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}R\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+m}{101}:103\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZdl{}\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n+nv}{NOMINATIM\PYGZus{}PGDATA\PYGZus{}DIR}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Both are one\sphinxhyphen{}time fixes tied to the filesystem itself (not the fragile
udisks2 mount point), so they survive reboots/remounts as long as \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{fstab}}
is used instead of the desktop auto\sphinxhyphen{}mounter. If \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{NOMINATIM\_PGDATA\_DIR}}
ever ends up in a broken half\sphinxhyphen{}initialized state (partial \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{chown}} applied,
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{initdb}} refusing to run because the directory isn’t empty), wipe it and
let the entrypoint reinitialize from scratch:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
make\PYG{+w}{ }nominatim\PYGZhy{}down
docker\PYG{+w}{ }compose\PYG{+w}{ }run\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}rm\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}entrypoint\PYG{+w}{ }sh\PYG{+w}{ }nominatim\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s+se}{\PYGZbs{}}
\PYG{+w}{  }\PYGZhy{}c\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}find /var/lib/postgresql/14/main \PYGZhy{}mindepth 1 \PYGZhy{}delete\PYGZsq{}}
make\PYG{+w}{ }nominatim\PYGZhy{}up
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Nominatim returns empty results}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/troubleshooting:nominatim-returns-empty-results}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Cause}: Import is incomplete or PBF file is missing.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Fix}: Both the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{fastopendata}} and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{nominatim}} containers bind\sphinxhyphen{}mount
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{fastopendata\sphinxhyphen{}raw}} to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\$\{DATA\_DIR\}}} on the host (see \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{docker\sphinxhyphen{}compose.yml}}),
so check for the PBF there rather than under the git\sphinxhyphen{}tracked
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{packages/fastopendata/raw\_data/}} path:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
ls\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZdl{}\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n+nv}{DATA\PYGZus{}DIR}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{/us\PYGZhy{}latest.osm.pbf}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
If missing, download it either natively or via the fastopendata container —
either way it lands in the same place, so Nominatim will pick it up without
re\sphinxhyphen{}downloading:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
make\PYG{+w}{ }fod\PYGZhy{}data\PYGZhy{}osm
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} OR: make fod\PYGZhy{}shell, then follow DATASETS.md \PYGZsh{}16 to download the PBF}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Query Execution Issues}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/troubleshooting:query-execution-issues}}

\subsubsection{Query hangs or runs too long}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/troubleshooting:query-hangs-or-runs-too-long}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Fix}: Set a timeout:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{...}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{timeout\PYGZus{}seconds}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{30}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Cross\sphinxhyphen{}product produces too many rows}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/troubleshooting:cross-product-produces-too-many-rows}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Fix}: The cross\sphinxhyphen{}join limit prevents runaway cartesian products:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Default limit applies; increase if needed}
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{...}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{max\PYGZus{}cross\PYGZus{}join\PYGZus{}rows}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{500\PYGZus{}000}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{\sphinxstyleliteralintitle{\sphinxupquote{GraphTypeNotFoundError}}}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/troubleshooting:graphtypenotfounderror}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Cause}: Query references an entity or relationship type not in the context.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Fix}: Check available types:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n+nb}{repr}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Context(backend=\PYGZsq{}pandas\PYGZsq{}, entities=\PYGZob{}\PYGZsq{}Person\PYGZsq{}: 4\PYGZcb{}, relationships=\PYGZob{}\PYGZsq{}KNOWS\PYGZsq{}: 3\PYGZcb{})}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Getting Help}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/troubleshooting:getting-help}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Issue tracker}: Report bugs at the GitHub repository

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Test suite}: Run \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{uv run pytest \sphinxhyphen{}x}} to identify failures

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Logs}: Check \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{make dev\sphinxhyphen{}logs}} for container output

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Metrics}: Use \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{QUERY\_METRICS.snapshot().diagnostic\_report()}} for execution stats

\end{itemize}


\section{Architecture Overview}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/index:architecture-overview}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
PyCypher deployments consist of these components:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{PyCypher core} – the Cypher query engine (always required)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Spark cluster} – optional distributed compute for large datasets

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Neo4j} – optional graph database sink for writing query results

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Nominatim} – optional geocoding service for geospatial ETL

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{FastOpenData API} – optional REST API for data ingestion

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
All components are containerised and orchestrated via Docker Compose.  The
Makefile provides convenience targets for every operational task.


\subsection{Deployment Modes}
\label{\detokenize{deployment/index:deployment-modes}}

\begin{savenotes}\sphinxattablestart
