\subsection{Development Environment}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/contributing:development-environment}}

\subsubsection{Requirements}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/contributing:requirements}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Python 3.14 or higher (free\sphinxhyphen{}threaded build recommended: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{3.14t}})

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.astral.sh/uv/}{uv} for dependency management

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Docker (via Rancher Desktop or Docker Desktop) for containerised testing

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Git

\end{itemize}


\subsubsection{Initial Setup}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/contributing:initial-setup}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Clone the repository}
git\PYG{+w}{ }clone\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZlt{}repository\PYGZhy{}url\PYGZgt{}
\PYG{n+nb}{cd}\PYG{+w}{ }pycypher\PYGZhy{}nmetl

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Install dependencies (uv manages the workspace virtualenv)}
uv\PYG{+w}{ }sync

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Verify the setup}
uv\PYG{+w}{ }run\PYG{+w}{ }pytest\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}co\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}q\PYG{+w}{   }\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} list collected tests without running}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
All Python operations go through \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{uv}} — it manages the workspace virtual
environment automatically.


\subsubsection{Project Structure}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/contributing:project-structure}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This is a \sphinxstylestrong{monorepo workspace} with two interdependent packages:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
packages/
├── pycypher/    \PYGZsh{} Cypher parser, AST, relational algebra, ETL
└── shared/      \PYGZsh{} Common utilities, logging, telemetry

tests/           \PYGZsh{} All tests live at the repo root
docs/            \PYGZsh{} Sphinx documentation
examples/        \PYGZsh{} Runnable example scripts
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Dependency order: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{shared}} → \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher}}.  The Makefile handles build
ordering automatically.


\subsection{Code Style}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/contributing:code-style}}

\subsubsection{Formatting}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/contributing:formatting}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
make\PYG{+w}{ }format\PYG{+w}{   }\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Runs isort + ruff format}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Ruff is configured in the root \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pyproject.toml}} with \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{select = {[}"ALL"{]}}}
(highly strict linting).  Fix any issues before committing.


\subsubsection{Type Annotations}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/contributing:type-annotations}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{All functions and methods must have type annotations.}  Use \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ty}} (not
mypy) for type checking:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
uv\PYG{+w}{ }run\PYG{+w}{ }ty\PYG{+w}{ }check
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Docstrings}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/contributing:docstrings}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use \sphinxhref{https://google.github.io/styleguide/pyguide.html\#38-comments-and-docstrings}{Google\sphinxhyphen{}style docstrings}.
Sphinx autodoc extracts documentation from source, so always update docstrings
when modifying public APIs.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k}{def}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nf}{example\PYGZus{}function}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{name}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n+nb}{str}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{count}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n+nb}{int} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{o}{\PYGZgt{}} \PYG{n+nb}{list}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{n+nb}{str}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}Short summary of what this function does.}

\PYG{l+s+sd}{    Args:}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{        name: Description of name parameter.}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{        count: Description of count parameter.}

\PYG{l+s+sd}{    Returns:}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{        Description of return value.}

\PYG{l+s+sd}{    Raises:}
\PYG{l+s+sd}{        ValueError: When name is empty.}

\PYG{l+s+sd}{    \PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Running Tests}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/contributing:running-tests}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Quick iteration during development (parallel, minimal output, skips slow tests)}
make\PYG{+w}{ }test\PYGZhy{}quick

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Run all tests (parallel, 8 threads)}
make\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nb}{test}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Run a specific test file}
make\PYG{+w}{ }test\PYGZhy{}file\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{FILE}\PYG{o}{=}tests/test\PYGZus{}ast\PYGZus{}models.py

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Stop on first failure}
make\PYG{+w}{ }test\PYGZhy{}fast

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Re\PYGZhy{}run only previously failed tests}
make\PYG{+w}{ }test\PYGZhy{}failed

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Run with coverage report}
make\PYG{+w}{ }coverage
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
See {\hyperref[\detokenize{developer_guide/testing::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Testing}}}} for the full testing guide.


\subsection{Making Changes}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/contributing:making-changes}}

\subsubsection{Branch Naming}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/contributing:branch-naming}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use descriptive branch names that indicate the type of change:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{feature/add\sphinxhyphen{}temporal\sphinxhyphen{}functions}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{fix/undefined\sphinxhyphen{}variable\sphinxhyphen{}error}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{docs/update\sphinxhyphen{}contributing\sphinxhyphen{}guide}}

\end{itemize}


\subsubsection{Commit Messages}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/contributing:commit-messages}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Write clear, concise commit messages:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Start with an imperative verb (Add, Fix, Update, Remove, Refactor)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Keep the first line under 72 characters

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Reference issues or context in the body when relevant

\end{itemize}


\subsection{Pull Request Process}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/contributing:pull-request-process}}\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Create a branch} from \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{main}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Make your changes} — keep PRs focused on a single concern

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Run the pre\sphinxhyphen{}PR check} — a single command that formats, lints, type\sphinxhyphen{}checks,
and runs the test suite:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
make\PYG{+w}{ }check\PYG{+w}{   }\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} runs: lock\PYGZhy{}check → format → lint → typecheck → test\PYGZhy{}fast}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Push and open a PR} against \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{main}}

\end{enumerate}

\sphinxAtStartPar
PR descriptions should include:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
A summary of what changed and why

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
How to test the changes

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Any breaking changes or migration notes

\end{itemize}


\subsubsection{Code Review}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/contributing:code-review}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
All PRs require review before merging

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Reviewers check for correctness, test coverage, and adherence to project
conventions

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Address review feedback promptly; use the conversation to discuss trade\sphinxhyphen{}offs

\end{itemize}


\subsection{Adding Dependencies}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/contributing:adding-dependencies}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Edit the relevant package\PYGZsq{}s pyproject.toml, then:}
uv\PYG{+w}{ }sync
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Never use \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pip install}} directly — the workspace relies on \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{uv}} for
consistent dependency resolution.


\subsection{Documentation}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/contributing:documentation}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Build docs}
make\PYG{+w}{ }docs

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Or directly:}
uv\PYG{+w}{ }run\PYG{+w}{ }sphinx\PYGZhy{}build\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}b\PYG{+w}{ }html\PYG{+w}{ }docs\PYG{+w}{ }docs/\PYGZus{}build/html
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Update documentation alongside code changes.  See {\hyperref[\detokenize{tutorials/index::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Tutorials}}}}
for tutorial conventions.


\subsection{Editor Integration (LSP)}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/contributing:editor-integration-lsp}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
PyCypher includes a built\sphinxhyphen{}in Language Server Protocol (LSP) server that
provides real\sphinxhyphen{}time Cypher query assistance in any LSP\sphinxhyphen{}capable editor.  No
external extensions are required — the server uses only the standard library.


\subsubsection{Features}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/contributing:features}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Diagnostics} — parse errors and semantic validation as you type

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Completion} — keywords, functions, and entity labels

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Hover} — function documentation and signatures

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Signature Help} — parameter hints inside function calls

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Go to Definition} — jump to variable definitions

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Formatting} — auto\sphinxhyphen{}format Cypher queries

\end{itemize}


\subsubsection{Starting the Server}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/contributing:starting-the-server}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
python\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}m\PYG{+w}{ }pycypher.cypher\PYGZus{}lsp
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The server communicates via JSON\sphinxhyphen{}RPC over stdin/stdout per the LSP
specification.


\subsubsection{VS Code}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/contributing:vs-code}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Add to your \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{.vscode/settings.json}}:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{n+nt}{\PYGZdq{}pycypher.lspServer.path\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}python \PYGZhy{}m pycypher.cypher\PYGZus{}lsp\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
For workspace\sphinxhyphen{}specific configuration, create \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{.vscode/settings.json}} at
the repository root.


\subsubsection{Neovim (nvim\sphinxhyphen{}lspconfig)}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/contributing:neovim-nvim-lspconfig}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Add to your Neovim configuration (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{init.lua}} or equivalent):

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kd}{local}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{lspconfig}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nb}{require}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{lspconfig}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k+kd}{local}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{configs}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nb}{require}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{lspconfig.configs}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{k+kr}{if}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{o+ow}{not}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{configs}\PYG{p}{.}\PYG{n+py}{pycypher}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kr}{then}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{n+nv}{configs}\PYG{p}{.}\PYG{n+py}{pycypher}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
\PYG{+w}{        }\PYG{n+nv}{default\PYGZus{}config}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
\PYG{+w}{            }\PYG{n+nv}{cmd}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{python}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZhy{}m}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{pycypher.cypher\PYGZus{}lsp}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{},}
\PYG{+w}{            }\PYG{n+nv}{filetypes}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{cypher}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{},}
\PYG{+w}{            }\PYG{n+nv}{root\PYGZus{}dir}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{lspconfig}\PYG{p}{.}\PYG{n+py}{util}\PYG{p}{.}\PYG{n+py}{find\PYGZus{}git\PYGZus{}ancestor}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{+w}{            }\PYG{n+nv}{settings}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}\PYGZcb{},}
\PYG{+w}{        }\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{},}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}
\PYG{k+kr}{end}

\PYG{n+nv}{lspconfig}\PYG{p}{.}\PYG{n+py}{pycypher}\PYG{p}{.}\PYG{n+nf}{setup}\PYG{p}{(\PYGZob{}\PYGZcb{})}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Emacs (lsp\sphinxhyphen{}mode)}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/contributing:emacs-lsp-mode}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Add to your Emacs configuration:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n+nb}{with\PYGZhy{}eval\PYGZhy{}after\PYGZhy{}load}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s+ss}{\PYGZsq{}lsp\PYGZhy{}mode}
\PYG{+w}{  }\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n+nv}{add\PYGZhy{}to\PYGZhy{}list}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s+ss}{\PYGZsq{}lsp\PYGZhy{}language\PYGZhy{}id\PYGZhy{}configuration}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{o}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n+nv}{cypher\PYGZhy{}mode}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s}{cypher}\PYG{l+s}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{+w}{  }\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n+nv}{lsp\PYGZhy{}register\PYGZhy{}client}
\PYG{+w}{   }\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n+nv}{make\PYGZhy{}lsp\PYGZhy{}client}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{n+nb}{:new\PYGZhy{}connection}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n+nv}{lsp\PYGZhy{}stdio\PYGZhy{}connection}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{o}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s}{python}\PYG{l+s}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s}{\PYGZhy{}m}\PYG{l+s}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s}{pycypher.cypher\PYGZus{}lsp}\PYG{l+s}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{n+nb}{:activation\PYGZhy{}fn}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n+nv}{lsp\PYGZhy{}activate\PYGZhy{}on}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s}{cypher}\PYG{l+s}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{n+nb}{:server\PYGZhy{}id}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s+ss}{\PYGZsq{}pycypher\PYGZhy{}lsp}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{File Type Detection}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/contributing:file-type-detection}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Most editors need to associate \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{.cypher}} files with the Cypher file type.
For Neovim, add:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n+nv}{vim}\PYG{p}{.}\PYG{n+py}{filetype}\PYG{p}{.}\PYG{n+nf}{add}\PYG{p}{(\PYGZob{}}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{extension}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{cypher}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{cypher}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{})}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
For VS Code, add to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{settings.json}}:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{n+nt}{\PYGZdq{}files.associations\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
\PYG{+w}{        }\PYG{n+nt}{\PYGZdq{}*.cypher\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}cypher\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxstepscope

\subsection{Running Tests}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/testing:running-tests}}

\subsubsection{Quick Reference}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/testing:quick-reference}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
All commands use \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{uv run}} to execute within the workspace virtual environment.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Run all tests (parallel)}
make\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nb}{test}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Run all tests (serial, useful for debugging)}
make\PYG{+w}{ }test\PYGZhy{}serial

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Fast: stop on first failure, parallel}
make\PYG{+w}{ }test\PYGZhy{}fast

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Quick: minimal output, no coverage}
make\PYG{+w}{ }test\PYGZhy{}quick

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Re\PYGZhy{}run only previously failed tests}
make\PYG{+w}{ }test\PYGZhy{}failed

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Re\PYGZhy{}run failed tests first, then all}
make\PYG{+w}{ }test\PYGZhy{}changed
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Targeting Specific Tests}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/testing:targeting-specific-tests}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Single file}
uv\PYG{+w}{ }run\PYG{+w}{ }pytest\PYG{+w}{ }tests/test\PYGZus{}ast\PYGZus{}models.py

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Single test class}
uv\PYG{+w}{ }run\PYG{+w}{ }pytest\PYG{+w}{ }tests/test\PYGZus{}ast\PYGZus{}models.py::TestPatterns

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Single test method}
uv\PYG{+w}{ }run\PYG{+w}{ }pytest\PYG{+w}{ }tests/test\PYGZus{}ast\PYGZus{}models.py::TestPatterns::test\PYGZus{}node\PYGZus{}pattern\PYGZus{}basic

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} By keyword pattern (matches test names)}
uv\PYG{+w}{ }run\PYG{+w}{ }pytest\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}k\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}aggregation and not percentile\PYGZdq{}}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} By marker}
uv\PYG{+w}{ }run\PYG{+w}{ }pytest\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}m\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}not integration\PYGZdq{}}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Coverage}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/testing:coverage}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} HTML report in coverage\PYGZus{}report/}
make\PYG{+w}{ }coverage

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Detailed terminal + HTML report}
make\PYG{+w}{ }coverage\PYGZhy{}detailed

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Inline coverage for a specific file}
uv\PYG{+w}{ }run\PYG{+w}{ }pytest\PYG{+w}{ }tests/test\PYGZus{}star\PYGZus{}gaps.py\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}cov\PYG{o}{=}pycypher.star\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}cov\PYGZhy{}report\PYG{o}{=}term\PYGZhy{}missing
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Test Architecture}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/testing:test-architecture}}

\subsubsection{Directory Layout}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/testing:directory-layout}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
All tests live in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{tests/}} at the repository root. The project uses a flat
directory structure — each test file targets a specific module or feature area:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
tests/
├── conftest.py                     \PYGZsh{} Shared fixtures, auto\PYGZhy{}markers, isolation
├── fixtures/data/                  \PYGZsh{} Static test data files (Parquet, CSV)
├── benchmarks/                     \PYGZsh{} Performance benchmark tests (auto: performance)
├── large\PYGZus{}dataset/                  \PYGZsh{} Large\PYGZhy{}dataset integration tests (auto: integration+slow)
├── load\PYGZus{}testing/                   \PYGZsh{} Load and stress tests (auto: performance+slow)
├── property\PYGZus{}based/                 \PYGZsh{} Property\PYGZhy{}based tests (auto: unit)
├── test\PYGZus{}ast\PYGZus{}models.py              \PYGZsh{} AST node construction and validation
├── test\PYGZus{}grammar\PYGZus{}parser.py          \PYGZsh{} Lark grammar → raw AST parsing
├── test\PYGZus{}semantic\PYGZus{}validator.py       \PYGZsh{} Static semantic analysis
├── test\PYGZus{}binding\PYGZus{}frame.py           \PYGZsh{} BindingFrame IR operations
└── ...                             \PYGZsh{} \PYGZti{}300 test files covering all features
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Automatic Test Markers}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/testing:automatic-test-markers}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Tests are \sphinxstylestrong{automatically marked} based on their file location and name,
so you rarely need to add \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{@pytest.mark}} decorators manually.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Directory\sphinxhyphen{}based auto\sphinxhyphen{}markers:}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{tests/benchmarks/}} → \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{performance}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{tests/large\_dataset/}} → \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{integration}} + \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{slow}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{tests/load\_testing/}} → \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{performance}} + \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{slow}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{tests/property\_based/}} → \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{unit}}

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Filename\sphinxhyphen{}based auto\sphinxhyphen{}markers:}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Files containing \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_e2e\_}} or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_end\_to\_end}} → \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{integration}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Files containing \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_performance\_}} → \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{performance}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Files containing \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_security\_}} → \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{security}}

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Explicit \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{@pytest.mark.X}} decorators always take precedence over auto\sphinxhyphen{}markers.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Per\sphinxhyphen{}marker timeouts} are also applied automatically:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{integration}} tests: 120 seconds (vs 30s default)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{slow}} tests: 300 seconds (vs 30s default)

\end{itemize}


\subsubsection{Test Isolation}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/testing:test-isolation}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Two auto\sphinxhyphen{}use fixtures in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{conftest.py}} prevent cross\sphinxhyphen{}test contamination:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{SIGALRM cleanup} — Clears pending alarm signals after each test,
preventing spurious \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{QueryTimeoutError}} in unrelated tests.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Logger level restoration} — Restores \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{shared.logger.LOGGER}} level
after each test, preventing \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{caplog.at\_level()}} from leaving loggers
in DEBUG mode.

\end{itemize}


\subsubsection{Shared Fixtures}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/testing:shared-fixtures}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{conftest.py}} provides reusable fixtures for the most common test patterns.
Always prefer these over creating one\sphinxhyphen{}off test data:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k}{def}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nf}{test\PYGZus{}basic\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{person\PYGZus{}star}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{o}{\PYGZgt{}} \PYG{k+kc}{None}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}person\PYGZus{}star provides a Star with 4 Person entities.\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{person\PYGZus{}star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) RETURN p.name AS name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{k}{assert} \PYG{n+nb}{len}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{result}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{o}{==} \PYG{l+m+mi}{4}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Available fixtures:}
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{people\_df}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
4\sphinxhyphen{}row Person DataFrame (Alice, Bob, Carol, Dave) with name, age, dept, salary.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{knows\_df}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
3\sphinxhyphen{}row KNOWS relationship DataFrame with since attribute.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{person\_star}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Star with Person entities only.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{social\_star}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Star with Person entities and KNOWS relationships.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{empty\_star}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Star with empty context (no entities or relationships).

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{scalar\_registry}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Shared \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ScalarFunctionRegistry}} singleton.

\end{description}


\subsection{Writing Tests}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/testing:writing-tests}}

\subsubsection{Test Structure}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/testing:test-structure}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
All test functions and methods \sphinxstylestrong{must} have type annotations.  Use
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pytest}}\sphinxhyphen{}style classes to group related tests:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}future\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{annotations}

\PYG{k+kn}{import}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pandas}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k}{as}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pd}
\PYG{k+kn}{import}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pytest}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{Star}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{relational\PYGZus{}models}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{Context}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{EntityMapping}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{EntityTable}

\PYG{n}{ID\PYGZus{}COLUMN} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}ID\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}


\PYG{n+nd}{@pytest}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{fixture}
\PYG{k}{def}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nf}{product\PYGZus{}star}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{o}{\PYGZgt{}} \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}Star with a Product entity table.\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{n}{df} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
        \PYG{n}{ID\PYGZus{}COLUMN}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{3}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Widget}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Gadget}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Doohickey}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{price}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mf}{9.99}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mf}{19.99}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mf}{4.99}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{n}{table} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{EntityTable}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{from\PYGZus{}dataframe}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Product}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{df}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{n}{ctx} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Context}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{entity\PYGZus{}mapping}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{EntityMapping}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{mapping}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Product}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n}{table}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{k}{return} \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{ctx}\PYG{p}{)}


\PYG{k}{class}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nc}{TestProductQueries}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}Tests for product catalog queries.\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}

    \PYG{k}{def}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nf}{test\PYGZus{}return\PYGZus{}all\PYGZus{}products}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n+nb+bp}{self}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{product\PYGZus{}star}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{o}{\PYGZgt{}} \PYG{k+kc}{None}\PYG{p}{:}
        \PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{product\PYGZus{}star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
            \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Product) RETURN p.name AS name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
        \PYG{p}{)}
        \PYG{k}{assert} \PYG{n+nb}{len}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{result}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{o}{==} \PYG{l+m+mi}{3}
        \PYG{k}{assert} \PYG{n+nb}{set}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{result}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{o}{==} \PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Widget}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Gadget}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Doohickey}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}

    \PYG{k}{def}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nf}{test\PYGZus{}filter\PYGZus{}by\PYGZus{}price}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n+nb+bp}{self}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{product\PYGZus{}star}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{o}{\PYGZgt{}} \PYG{k+kc}{None}\PYG{p}{:}
        \PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{product\PYGZus{}star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
            \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Product) WHERE p.price \PYGZgt{} 10 RETURN p.name AS name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
        \PYG{p}{)}
        \PYG{k}{assert} \PYG{n+nb}{set}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{result}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{o}{==} \PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Gadget}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Testing Query Execution (End\sphinxhyphen{}to\sphinxhyphen{}End)}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/testing:testing-query-execution-end-to-end}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The most common test pattern exercises the full pipeline — parsing, planning,
and execution — via \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Star.execute\_query()}}:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k}{def}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nf}{test\PYGZus{}aggregation\PYGZus{}with\PYGZus{}grouping}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{social\PYGZus{}star}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{o}{\PYGZgt{}} \PYG{k+kc}{None}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}Grouped COUNT aggregation returns correct counts per department.\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{social\PYGZus{}star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) RETURN p.dept AS dept, count(p) AS cnt}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Convert to dict for order\PYGZhy{}independent assertion}
    \PYG{n}{dept\PYGZus{}counts} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n+nb}{dict}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n+nb}{zip}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{result}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{dept}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{result}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{cnt}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{strict}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k+kc}{False}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{k}{assert} \PYG{n}{dept\PYGZus{}counts}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{eng}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]} \PYG{o}{==} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2}
    \PYG{k}{assert} \PYG{n}{dept\PYGZus{}counts}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{mktg}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]} \PYG{o}{==} \PYG{l+m+mi}{1}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Testing AST Construction}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/testing:testing-ast-construction}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
For parser and AST tests, validate structure without executing queries:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{ast\PYGZus{}models}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{ASTConverter}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{Query}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{Match}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{Return}

\PYG{k}{def}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nf}{test\PYGZus{}parse\PYGZus{}match\PYGZus{}return}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{o}{\PYGZgt{}} \PYG{k+kc}{None}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n}{query} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ASTConverter}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{from\PYGZus{}cypher}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (n:Person) RETURN n.name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}

    \PYG{k}{assert} \PYG{n+nb}{isinstance}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{query}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{Query}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{k}{assert} \PYG{n+nb}{len}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{query}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{clauses}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{o}{==} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2}
    \PYG{k}{assert} \PYG{n+nb}{isinstance}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{query}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{clauses}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{0}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{Match}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{k}{assert} \PYG{n+nb}{isinstance}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{query}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{clauses}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{Return}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Testing Error Paths}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/testing:testing-error-paths}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Always verify that errors produce clear, actionable messages:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{import}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pytest}

\PYG{k}{def}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nf}{test\PYGZus{}empty\PYGZus{}query\PYGZus{}raises}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{person\PYGZus{}star}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{o}{\PYGZgt{}} \PYG{k+kc}{None}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{k}{with} \PYG{n}{pytest}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{raises}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n+ne}{ValueError}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{match}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{must not be empty}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{:}
        \PYG{n}{person\PYGZus{}star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{k}{def}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nf}{test\PYGZus{}unknown\PYGZus{}function\PYGZus{}suggests\PYGZus{}correction}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{person\PYGZus{}star}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{o}{\PYGZgt{}} \PYG{k+kc}{None}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{k}{with} \PYG{n}{pytest}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{raises}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n+ne}{Exception}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{match}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{(?i)did you mean}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{:}
        \PYG{n}{person\PYGZus{}star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
            \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) RETURN tUpperr(p.name)}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
        \PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Testing Mutations (CREATE, SET, DELETE)}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/testing:testing-mutations-create-set-delete}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Mutation tests should verify both the returned result and the context state:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k}{def}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nf}{test\PYGZus{}set\PYGZus{}updates\PYGZus{}context}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{person\PYGZus{}star}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{o}{\PYGZgt{}} \PYG{k+kc}{None}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}SET clause persists changes to the underlying context.\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{n}{person\PYGZus{}star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) WHERE p.name = }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Alice}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ SET p.age = 31 RETURN p}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Verify the mutation persisted in the context}
    \PYG{n}{person\PYGZus{}df} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{person\PYGZus{}star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{entity\PYGZus{}mapping}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{source\PYGZus{}obj}
    \PYG{n}{alice\PYGZus{}row} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{person\PYGZus{}df}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{n}{person\PYGZus{}df}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]} \PYG{o}{==} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Alice}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{iloc}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{0}\PYG{p}{]}
    \PYG{k}{assert} \PYG{n}{alice\PYGZus{}row}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{age}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]} \PYG{o}{==} \PYG{l+m+mi}{31}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Testing Shadow State Atomicity}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/testing:testing-shadow-state-atomicity}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Mutations use a shadow\sphinxhyphen{}write pattern (begin → execute → commit/rollback).
Verify that failed queries leave the context unchanged:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k}{def}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nf}{test\PYGZus{}failed\PYGZus{}mutation\PYGZus{}rolls\PYGZus{}back}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{person\PYGZus{}star}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n}{Star}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{o}{\PYGZgt{}} \PYG{k+kc}{None}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}A query that fails mid\PYGZhy{}execution must not leave partial mutations.\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{n}{original\PYGZus{}names} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n+nb}{set}\PYG{p}{(}
        \PYG{n}{person\PYGZus{}star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{entity\PYGZus{}mapping}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{source\PYGZus{}obj}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}
    \PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{k}{with} \PYG{n}{pytest}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{raises}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n+ne}{Exception}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{:}
        \PYG{n}{person\PYGZus{}star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) SET p.INVALID RETURN p}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{n}{current\PYGZus{}names} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n+nb}{set}\PYG{p}{(}
        \PYG{n}{person\PYGZus{}star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{entity\PYGZus{}mapping}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Person}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{source\PYGZus{}obj}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}
    \PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{k}{assert} \PYG{n}{current\PYGZus{}names} \PYG{o}{==} \PYG{n}{original\PYGZus{}names}
    \PYG{k}{assert} \PYG{n}{person\PYGZus{}star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{context}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{\PYGZus{}shadow} \PYG{o}{==} \PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Performance Testing}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/testing:performance-testing}}

\subsubsection{Writing Robust Performance Tests}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/testing:writing-robust-performance-tests}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Performance assertions are inherently noisy.  Use statistical approaches
to avoid flaky tests:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{import}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{statistics}
\PYG{k+kn}{import}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{time}

\PYG{k}{def}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nf}{test\PYGZus{}view\PYGZus{}faster\PYGZus{}than\PYGZus{}copy}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{o}{\PYGZgt{}} \PYG{k+kc}{None}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{+w}{    }\PYG{l+s+sd}{\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}Verify that DataFrame view is faster than copy for read\PYGZhy{}only access.\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{n}{n\PYGZus{}trials} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{l+m+mi}{7}
    \PYG{n}{copy\PYGZus{}times}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n+nb}{list}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{n+nb}{float}\PYG{p}{]} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{p}{]}
    \PYG{n}{view\PYGZus{}times}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n+nb}{list}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{n+nb}{float}\PYG{p}{]} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{p}{]}

    \PYG{n}{df} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pd}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{DataFrame}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{a}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n+nb}{range}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+m+mi}{10\PYGZus{}000}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{b}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n+nb}{range}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+m+mi}{10\PYGZus{}000}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}

    \PYG{k}{for} \PYG{n}{\PYGZus{}} \PYG{o+ow}{in} \PYG{n+nb}{range}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{n\PYGZus{}trials}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{:}
        \PYG{n}{t0} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{time}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{perf\PYGZus{}counter}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
        \PYG{k}{for} \PYG{n}{\PYGZus{}} \PYG{o+ow}{in} \PYG{n+nb}{range}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+m+mi}{50}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{:}
            \PYG{n}{\PYGZus{}} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{df}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{a}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{copy}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
        \PYG{n}{copy\PYGZus{}times}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{append}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{time}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{perf\PYGZus{}counter}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}} \PYG{n}{t0}\PYG{p}{)}

        \PYG{n}{t0} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{time}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{perf\PYGZus{}counter}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
        \PYG{k}{for} \PYG{n}{\PYGZus{}} \PYG{o+ow}{in} \PYG{n+nb}{range}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+m+mi}{50}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{:}
            \PYG{n}{\PYGZus{}} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{df}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{a}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{]}
        \PYG{n}{view\PYGZus{}times}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{append}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{time}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{perf\PYGZus{}counter}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}} \PYG{n}{t0}\PYG{p}{)}

    \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Use median and generous tolerance (3x) for CI stability}
    \PYG{k}{assert} \PYG{n}{statistics}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{median}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{view\PYGZus{}times}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{o}{\PYGZlt{}}\PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{statistics}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{median}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{copy\PYGZus{}times}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{o}{*} \PYG{l+m+mf}{3.0}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Rules for performance tests:}
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use \sphinxstylestrong{median of multiple trials} (≥5) instead of single measurements

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Apply \sphinxstylestrong{generous tolerance} (2–3x) — test for regressions, not exact speedups

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Include a \sphinxstylestrong{descriptive assertion message} with actual values for debugging

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Never assert exact timings — only relative comparisons or order\sphinxhyphen{}of\sphinxhyphen{}magnitude

\end{enumerate}


\subsubsection{Benchmark Tests}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/testing:benchmark-tests}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{tests/benchmarks/}} directory contains pytest\sphinxhyphen{}benchmark tests:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
uv\PYG{+w}{ }run\PYG{+w}{ }pytest\PYG{+w}{ }tests/benchmarks/\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}benchmark\PYGZhy{}only
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Integration Tests}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/testing:integration-tests}}

\subsubsection{Spark Integration}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/testing:spark-integration}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Spark tests require a running Spark cluster or use \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{local{[}*{]}}} mode:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Run Spark tests (requires Docker)}
make\PYG{+w}{ }test\PYGZhy{}spark

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Or directly with local mode}
uv\PYG{+w}{ }run\PYG{+w}{ }pytest\PYG{+w}{ }tests/\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}k\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}spark\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}v
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Neo4j Integration}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/testing:neo4j-integration}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Neo4j tests require a running Neo4j instance:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Start Neo4j via Docker}
docker\PYG{+w}{ }compose\PYG{+w}{ }up\PYG{+w}{ }neo4j\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}d

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Run Neo4j tests}
make\PYG{+w}{ }test\PYGZhy{}neo4j

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Or with custom connection}
\PYG{n+nv}{NEO4J\PYGZus{}URI}\PYG{o}{=}bolt://localhost:7687\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{NEO4J\PYGZus{}PASSWORD}\PYG{o}{=}secret\PYG{+w}{ }uv\PYG{+w}{ }run\PYG{+w}{ }pytest\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}k\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}neo4j\PYGZdq{}}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Large Dataset Tests}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/testing:large-dataset-tests}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Tests that exercise distributed backends (Dask, DuckDB, Polars):

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
make\PYG{+w}{ }test\PYGZhy{}large\PYGZhy{}dataset
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Configuration for Testing}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/testing:configuration-for-testing}}

\subsubsection{Environment Variables}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/testing:environment-variables}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Tests respect the same \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_*}} environment variables as production
(see \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pycypher.config}}):
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_QUERY\_TIMEOUT\_S}} (default: None)}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Default query timeout in seconds.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_MAX\_CROSS\_JOIN\_ROWS}} (default: 10,000,000)}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Hard ceiling on cross\sphinxhyphen{}join result size (rows).

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_RESULT\_CACHE\_MAX\_MB}} (default: 100)}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Maximum result cache size in megabytes.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_RESULT\_CACHE\_TTL\_S}} (default: 0)}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Result cache TTL in seconds (0 = no expiry).

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PYCYPHER\_MAX\_UNBOUNDED\_PATH\_HOPS}} (default: 20)}
\sphinxAtStartPar
BFS hop limit for unbounded variable\sphinxhyphen{}length paths (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[}*{]}}}).

\end{description}


\subsubsection{Timeout Configuration}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/testing:timeout-configuration}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
All tests have a 30\sphinxhyphen{}second timeout enforced by \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pytest\sphinxhyphen{}timeout}} (configured
in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pyproject.toml}}).  Tests that need longer (e.g. Spark integration) should
use:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n+nd}{@pytest}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{mark}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{timeout}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+m+mi}{120}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{def}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nf}{test\PYGZus{}large\PYGZus{}spark\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{spark\PYGZus{}session}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Continuous Integration}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/testing:continuous-integration}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The CI pipeline (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{.github/workflows/ci.yml}}) runs three jobs:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Tests}: Full test suite with \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pytest \sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}timeout=30}} on Python 3.14t

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Dependency Compatibility}: Verifies all backend imports (Dask, DuckDB, etc.)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Documentation}: Builds Sphinx docs to catch broken references

\end{enumerate}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Tests must pass before merging to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{main}}.


\subsection{Type Checking}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/testing:type-checking}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Run type checks with \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ty}} (not mypy):

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
uv\PYG{+w}{ }run\PYG{+w}{ }ty\PYG{+w}{ }check
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
All functions and methods \sphinxstylestrong{must} have type annotations.  The project uses
Python 3.14.0 (free\sphinxhyphen{}threaded build).

\sphinxstepscope


\section{Release Process}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/release:release-process}}\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/release::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
How PyCypher versions are numbered, built, and published.

\begin{sphinxcontents}
\sphinxstylecontentstitle{In this guide}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/release:id1}}{\hyperref[\detokenize{developer_guide/release:version-numbering}]{\sphinxcrossref{Version Numbering}}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/release:id2}}{\hyperref[\detokenize{developer_guide/release:pre-release-checklist}]{\sphinxcrossref{Pre\sphinxhyphen{}Release Checklist}}}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/release:id3}}{\hyperref[\detokenize{developer_guide/release:bump-version}]{\sphinxcrossref{Bump Version}}}

\end{itemize}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/release:id4}}{\hyperref[\detokenize{developer_guide/release:building-packages}]{\sphinxcrossref{Building Packages}}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/release:id5}}{\hyperref[\detokenize{developer_guide/release:publishing}]{\sphinxcrossref{Publishing}}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/release:id6}}{\hyperref[\detokenize{developer_guide/release:post-release}]{\sphinxcrossref{Post\sphinxhyphen{}Release}}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/release:id7}}{\hyperref[\detokenize{developer_guide/release:ci-cd}]{\sphinxcrossref{CI/CD}}}

\end{itemize}
\end{sphinxcontents}


\subsection{Version Numbering}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/release:version-numbering}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
PyCypher follows \sphinxhref{https://semver.org/}{Semantic Versioning} (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH}}):
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{MAJOR} — incompatible API changes

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{MINOR} — new functionality, backwards\sphinxhyphen{}compatible

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{PATCH} — backwards\sphinxhyphen{}compatible bug fixes

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The current version is declared in:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pyproject.toml}} (root workspace)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{packages/pycypher/pyproject.toml}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{packages/shared/pyproject.toml}}

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
All three must be updated in sync for a release.


\subsection{Pre\sphinxhyphen{}Release Checklist}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/release:pre-release-checklist}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Before cutting a release, verify:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{All tests pass}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
uv\PYG{+w}{ }run\PYG{+w}{ }pytest
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Type checking passes}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
uv\PYG{+w}{ }run\PYG{+w}{ }ty\PYG{+w}{ }check
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Formatting is clean}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
make\PYG{+w}{ }format
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Documentation builds without errors}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
make\PYG{+w}{ }docs
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{CHANGELOG or commit history} documents all notable changes since the
last release

\end{enumerate}


\subsubsection{Bump Version}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/release:bump-version}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The Makefile provides a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BUMP}} variable (default: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{micro}}):

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Patch bump (0.0.1 → 0.0.2)}
make\PYG{+w}{ }bump\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{BUMP}\PYG{o}{=}micro

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Minor bump (0.0.1 → 0.1.0)}
make\PYG{+w}{ }bump\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{BUMP}\PYG{o}{=}minor

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Major bump (0.0.1 → 1.0.0)}
make\PYG{+w}{ }bump\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nv}{BUMP}\PYG{o}{=}major
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
If the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{bump}} target is not available, update the version strings in the
three \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pyproject.toml}} files manually.


\subsection{Building Packages}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/release:building-packages}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Build wheel and sdist for each package}
uv\PYG{+w}{ }build\PYG{+w}{ }packages/shared
uv\PYG{+w}{ }build\PYG{+w}{ }packages/pycypher
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Built artefacts are placed in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{dist/}}.


\subsection{Publishing}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/release:publishing}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Publish to PyPI (requires credentials)}
uv\PYG{+w}{ }publish\PYG{+w}{ }dist/*

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Or publish to a test index first}
uv\PYG{+w}{ }publish\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}index\PYG{+w}{ }testpypi\PYG{+w}{ }dist/*
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Ensure your PyPI credentials are configured (e.g. via \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\textasciitilde{}/.pypirc}} or
environment variables \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{UV\_PUBLISH\_TOKEN}}).


\subsection{Post\sphinxhyphen{}Release}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/release:post-release}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
After a successful publish:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Tag the release in git: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{git tag v0.1.0 \&\& git push origin v0.1.0}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Create a GitHub Release from the tag with release notes

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bump the version in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pyproject.toml}} files to the next development
version (e.g. \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{0.1.1.dev0}}) if desired

\end{enumerate}


\subsection{CI/CD}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/release:ci-cd}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The GitHub Actions workflow (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{.github/workflows/ci.yml}}) runs on every push
and PR:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Tests on Python 3.14 (free\sphinxhyphen{}threaded build)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Linting and formatting checks

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Type checking with \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ty}}

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Releases can be automated by configuring a publish workflow triggered on
tagged commits.

\sphinxstepscope


\section{Security Hardening \& Compliance}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/security:security-hardening-compliance}}\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/security::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This guide documents PyCypher’s security architecture, threat mitigation
strategies, and best practices for secure deployment.

\begin{sphinxcontents}
\sphinxstylecontentstitle{On this page}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/security:id1}}{\hyperref[\detokenize{developer_guide/security:threat-model}]{\sphinxcrossref{Threat Model}}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/security:id2}}{\hyperref[\detokenize{developer_guide/security:input-validation}]{\sphinxcrossref{Input Validation}}}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/security:id3}}{\hyperref[\detokenize{developer_guide/security:cypher-query-parameters}]{\sphinxcrossref{Cypher query parameters}}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/security:id4}}{\hyperref[\detokenize{developer_guide/security:sql-data-source-queries}]{\sphinxcrossref{SQL data\sphinxhyphen{}source queries}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar

\sphinxAtStartPar

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/security:id7}}{\hyperref[\detokenize{developer_guide/security:resource-limits}]{\sphinxcrossref{Resource Limits}}}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/security:id8}}{\hyperref[\detokenize{developer_guide/security:cross-join-row-limit}]{\sphinxcrossref{Cross\sphinxhyphen{}join row limit}}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/security:id9}}{\hyperref[\detokenize{developer_guide/security:query-timeout}]{\sphinxcrossref{Query timeout}}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/security:id10}}{\hyperref[\detokenize{developer_guide/security:memory-budget}]{\sphinxcrossref{Memory budget}}}

\end{itemize}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/security:id11}}{\hyperref[\detokenize{developer_guide/security:internal-column-protection}]{\sphinxcrossref{Internal Column Protection}}}

\item {}

\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/security:id14}}{\hyperref[\detokenize{developer_guide/security:logging-and-audit}]{\sphinxcrossref{Logging and Audit}}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/security:id15}}{\hyperref[\detokenize{developer_guide/security:deployment-checklist}]{\sphinxcrossref{Deployment Checklist}}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/security:id16}}{\hyperref[\detokenize{developer_guide/security:security-testing}]{\sphinxcrossref{Security Testing}}}

\end{sphinxcontents}


\subsection{Threat Model}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/security:threat-model}}

\begin{sphinxseealso}{See also:}

\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{developer_guide/threat_model::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Threat Model \& Trust Boundaries}}}} for the comprehensive trust boundary mapping,
data flow analysis, and coordination requirements.


\end{sphinxseealso}


\sphinxAtStartPar
PyCypher processes user\sphinxhyphen{}supplied Cypher queries against in\sphinxhyphen{}process data.
The primary threat vectors are:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Query injection} — malicious Cypher or SQL embedded in user input.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Resource exhaustion} — unbounded cross\sphinxhyphen{}joins, runaway queries, or
memory bombs that crash the host process.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Path traversal} — data\sphinxhyphen{}source URIs or file paths that escape the
intended directory.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Data leakage} — returning internal columns (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_ID\_\_}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_SOURCE\_\_}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_TARGET\_\_}}) or properties from unintended entity
types.

\end{enumerate}


\subsection{Input Validation}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/security:input-validation}}

\subsubsection{Cypher query parameters}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/security:cypher-query-parameters}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Always use parameterised queries instead of string interpolation:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} GOOD — parameters are never interpolated into the query string}
\PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) WHERE p.age \PYGZgt{} \PYGZdl{}min\PYGZus{}age RETURN p.name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{parameters}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{min\PYGZus{}age}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{l+m+mi}{18}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} BAD — opens the door to injection}
\PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (p:Person) WHERE p.age \PYGZgt{} }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{user\PYGZus{}input}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ RETURN p.name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{SQL data\sphinxhyphen{}source queries}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/security:sql-data-source-queries}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The ingestion layer uses DuckDB for reading external files.  All SQL
passed to DuckDB is validated by
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.security.validate_sql_query}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{validate\_sql\_query()}}}}}, which rejects:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Multiple statements (semicolon stacking).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
DDL keywords (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DROP}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ALTER}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{CREATE}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{TRUNCATE}}).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
DML mutation keywords (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{INSERT}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{UPDATE}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DELETE}}).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Comment markers (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{/* */}}) that could hide payloads.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Use {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.security.sanitize_sql_identifier}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{sanitize\_sql\_identifier()}}}}} for any
user\sphinxhyphen{}supplied table or column names, and
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.security.parameterize_duckdb_query}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{parameterize\_duckdb\_query()}}}}} for safe
value substitution.


\subsubsection{File path sanitisation}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/security:file-path-sanitisation}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.security.sanitize_file_path}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{sanitize\_file\_path()}}}}} prevents path
traversal by rejecting \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{..}} components and blocking access to sensitive
system directories (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{/etc/}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{/root/}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{/proc/}}).


\subsubsection{URI scheme validation}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/security:uri-scheme-validation}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.ingestion.security.validate_uri_scheme}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{validate\_uri\_scheme()}}}}} restricts data
source URIs to an allow\sphinxhyphen{}list of safe schemes: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{file}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{http}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{https}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{s3}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{gs}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{postgresql}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{mysql}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{sqlite}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{duckdb}}.


\subsection{Resource Limits}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/security:resource-limits}}

\subsubsection{Cross\sphinxhyphen{}join row limit}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/security:cross-join-row-limit}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Unbounded \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MATCH (a), (b), (c)}} patterns can produce Cartesian
explosions.  PyCypher enforces a configurable row ceiling:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Default: 10,000,000 rows}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Override via environment variable:}
\PYG{k+kn}{import}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{os}
\PYG{n}{os}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{environ}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{PYCYPHER\PYGZus{}MAX\PYGZus{}CROSS\PYGZus{}JOIN\PYGZus{}ROWS}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{1000000}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Exceeding the limit raises \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MemoryError}} with a descriptive message.


\subsubsection{Query timeout}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/security:query-timeout}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Arm a per\sphinxhyphen{}query wall\sphinxhyphen{}clock budget to prevent runaway execution:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (a)\PYGZhy{}[*1..10]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(b) RETURN a, b}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{timeout\PYGZus{}seconds}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{5.0}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Exceeding the timeout raises \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{QueryTimeoutError}}.


\subsubsection{Memory budget}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/security:memory-budget}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The query planner estimates memory usage before execution begins.  Set
an explicit budget to reject queries that would exceed it:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{star}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{execute\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (a)\PYGZhy{}[:KNOWS]\PYGZhy{}\PYGZgt{}(b) RETURN a, b}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{memory\PYGZus{}budget\PYGZus{}bytes}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{512} \PYG{o}{*} \PYG{l+m+mi}{1024} \PYG{o}{*} \PYG{l+m+mi}{1024}\PYG{p}{,}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} 512 MB}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Exceeding the budget raises \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{QueryMemoryBudgetError}}.


\subsection{Internal Column Protection}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/security:internal-column-protection}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
PyCypher’s data model uses internal columns (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_ID\_\_}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_SOURCE\_\_}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_TARGET\_\_}}) to track entity identity and relationship endpoints.
These are automatically excluded from user\sphinxhyphen{}visible results:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{properties(n)}} omits internal columns.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{keys(n)}} omits internal columns.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RETURN *}} omits internal columns.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MapProjection}} with \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{.*}} omits internal columns.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
If you build custom functions that access raw DataFrames, filter out
columns whose names start and end with \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_}}.


\subsection{Semantic Validation}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/security:semantic-validation}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/pycypher:pycypher.semantic_validator.SemanticValidator}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SemanticValidator}}}}} performs
static analysis on parsed queries before execution:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Detects undefined variables.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Validates aggregation/non\sphinxhyphen{}aggregation mixing.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Checks function arity and argument types.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Reports unused variables.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Always validate user\sphinxhyphen{}supplied queries before execution in
security\sphinxhyphen{}sensitive contexts:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{pycypher}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{validate\PYGZus{}query}

\PYG{n}{issues} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{validate\PYGZus{}query}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MATCH (n) RETURN m}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{for} \PYG{n}{issue} \PYG{o+ow}{in} \PYG{n}{issues}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{k}{if} \PYG{n}{issue}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{severity} \PYG{o}{==} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{error}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:}
        \PYG{k}{raise} \PYG{n+ne}{ValueError}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Query validation failed: }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{issue}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{message}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Error Handling}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/security:error-handling}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
PyCypher raises typed exceptions rather than generic \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Exception}}:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{GraphTypeNotFoundError}} — unknown entity or relationship type.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{UndefinedVariableError}} — reference to unbound variable.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{CypherSyntaxError}} — malformed Cypher query.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{QueryTimeoutError}} — wall\sphinxhyphen{}clock budget exceeded.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{QueryMemoryBudgetError}} — estimated memory exceeds budget.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SecurityError}} — SQL injection or path traversal detected.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Catch specific exceptions rather than broad \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{except Exception}} to
avoid masking security\sphinxhyphen{}relevant errors.


\subsection{Logging and Audit}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/security:logging-and-audit}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
PyCypher logs query execution through the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{shared.logger}} module:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Query start/end}: query text, elapsed time, row count, RSS delta.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Query plan}: node count, memory estimate, join presence.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Per\sphinxhyphen{}clause timing}: individual clause execution durations.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Errors}: full traceback with query context.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
For audit compliance, configure the logger to write to a persistent
destination (file, syslog, or structured logging service).  Query
correlation IDs (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_query\_id}}) enable tracing individual queries across
log entries.


\subsection{Deployment Checklist}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/security:deployment-checklist}}

\begin{savenotes}\sphinxattablestart
\sphinxthistablewithglobalstyle
\centering
\begin{tabular}[t]{\X{10}{100}\X{60}{100}\X{30}{100}}

P0

your hardware.

Run \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{validate\_query()}} on user\sphinxhyphen{}supplied Cypher before execution.

Configure persistent audit logging for query execution events.

Pin dependency versions with upper bounds (already done in

\sphinxAtStartPar
Done

\subsection{Security Testing}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/security:security-testing}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
PyCypher includes comprehensive security test suites:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{tests/test\_security\_sql\_injection\_tdd.py}} — SQL injection prevention.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{tests/test\_security\_sql\_injection\_proof.py}} — SQL injection proof tests.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{tests/test\_backend\_engine\_security.py}} — backend engine security.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{tests/test\_data\_source\_security.py}} — data source URI validation.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{tests/test\_duckdb\_reader\_security.py}} — DuckDB reader security.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{tests/test\_helpers\_serialization\_security.py}} — serialisation safety.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{tests/test\_neo4j\_sink\_security.py}} — Neo4j sink security.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{tests/test\_dask\_distributed\_security.py}} — distributed security.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{tests/test\_distributed\_security\_contracts.py}} — distributed contracts.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Run the full security suite:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
uv\PYG{+w}{ }run\PYG{+w}{ }pytest\PYG{+w}{ }tests/\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}m\PYG{+w}{ }security\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}q
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Or run all security\sphinxhyphen{}named test files:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
uv\PYG{+w}{ }run\PYG{+w}{ }pytest\PYG{+w}{ }tests/test\PYGZus{}security*.py\PYG{+w}{ }tests/test\PYGZus{}*security*.py\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}q
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxstepscope


\section{Threat Model \& Trust Boundaries}
\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/threat_model:threat-model-trust-boundaries}}\label{\detokenize{developer_guide/threat_model::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This document defines the formal trust boundaries, data flow analysis,
and security\sphinxhyphen{}sensitive operations in the PyCypher\sphinxhyphen{}nmetl system. It serves
as the baseline for systematic security review per Amendment Cycle 2.

\begin{sphinxcontents}
\sphinxstylecontentstitle{On this page}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar

\sphinxAtStartPar

\sphinxAtStartPar
